college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text: The total cost of four years of education at our college, including hostel and other miscellaneous expenses, amounts to approximately 10 lakhs. While the fees can be considered average in terms of value for money, there is certainly room for improvement in certain areas.The staff over here in the college is really good and supportive. The don't give us the holiday on each Saturday but it's okk, they conduct so many activities in the campus Rather than getting board just by doing academics.

Course curriculum is not so good. Only IT companies comes and student who has done good performance in coding and computer science then those students gets placed through oncampus .this is darker side of wb colleges.exam are taken half yearly as semester.teacher are moderate .Campus Life is moderate , this college has not so big campus but it's ok . The annual festival gravest is conducted in November. And a summer fest is also conducted in June. And a lot of tech workshops and fest are conducted throughout the year. There is a huge main library and another library, especially for cs-it students. So there are a lot of books available. We can issue any book we want without any problem. The classrooms are huge and spacious. The only facility missing is ac in classrooms. The labs are good. football, basketball, cricket, badminton and many more tournaments are conducted. Though a swimming pool is missing. A lot of tech societies and other societies like debating, drama, writing, singing etc are run by students and they organize events every now and then.I have been programming since class 11 and wanted to be a Software engineer. I can confidently say that course curriculum and faculty members are well sought out. the curriculum is well designed to provide a balanced mix of theory and practical knowledge.
I choose this course due to its application in various fields, like- Chips, Processors, Communication, 5G, transmission and keeping a window open to computer science fields also. 
The student to faculty ratio is little higher when compared to others colleges of this Tier, ie of 12-17 students per Faculty. Most of the Faculties have excellent qualification, with Ph.D. degrees from reputed universities all over India. They are well versed in the courses they teach and have drafted research papers as well.
In terms of acadamics the college is very strict. there is exams/quiz every month and difficulty is moderate as well.Campus Life is well versed as well due to students from all over india and various co-culicular and extracuricular activitiees happening all over the campus.
There are mainly 2 extravagant Fest which are TechnoVIT (tech fest) and Vibrance (Cultural fest), which happen in fall and winter semester respectively,
also the library is very big having 3 floors ,for books relateed to all courses and journals and 1 lab for video assisatance.
classes are well ventilated with big boards, projector and speakers on both sides.
it has various facilities for sports like tennis court, basketball court, swimming pool, vollyball court, cricket/football ground with practice net.
there are various social groups as well which are mainted by students.

The campus life is quite good here at the bit. The annual cultural fest is named bitotsav and is held around February end of March, and the main tech fest is named Pantheon, held around the end of October. There are plenty of books for each subject for all the subjects. The classrooms are also well equipped with good lighting, AC, and fans. There are good sports and co-curricular facilities available on campus. There is a dedicated sports complex with grounds and courts for various sports and a well-equipped gym too. There are many clubs related to many activities like music, sports, drama, tech, etc.Students become eligible for placements in the seventh semester. Many big softech companies visit the campus like Amazon, Microsoft, Walmart, Accenture, and many more. The highest package for my branch went up to 50 lakhs last year. Almost all the students of the circuital branches get placed. Although the placement in non-circuital branches isn't good. After the degree, i plan on getting placed in a good company and working there.

The interim placement report has been released and according to the report, students received the highest CTC of INR 1.09 CPA and the average CTC stood at INR 13.24 LPA. During its summer internship placement drive of the 2020-21 batch, students received the highest stipend of INR 80K per month.The students at DTU Diplom have a mandatory 20-week internship at a company. During the internship, the student participates in the daily activities of the company just like any other employee.
The placement of the IT branch at DTU is at par with that of COE. More than 95% of companies.

I chose this course because of the high placement percentage and good placement packages. There are enough faculty members for all the students. Most of the faculties are highly skilled in their subjects and are very good at making the students understand the concept. The faculties are all PhD holders in their respective subjects and have good ranks in gate exams too. Initially, due to late admission because of COVID, the terms were short and so was the time gap between the semester exams. This, it was a bit hard to cope with the speed. But after the first 2 semesters, everything went to normal and we do get around 3.5 to 4 months for each semester. The end-semester exams are relatively easy but the quizzes are set by your class teachers and can differ based on difficulty.The annual fest is named bitotsav and it is conducted around March or February the name of the tech-fest is pantheon and it is considered in around September or October. 
The library has most of the books available in sufficient quantities. Especially, for reserved categories, there are extra books reserved for them. All the hostels have their separate badminton and volleyball courts too. There are many clubs run by students in many fields like sports, programming, music, drama, art, etc.

The college is affiliated with VTU. They still follow the old pattern. The syllabus is very outdated and lengthy. There are two sems per year and total 8 sems. In each sem there are three internal exams and two lab exams. You have to pass in all the exams. There are other clubs also like dhwani for music, cybotrox for robotics, and RSC which is a sports club. After these, there is a coding club named BIGO. The demand for this club is maximum. You have to be extraordinary for getting into this club. There are auditions every year for being a part of clubs. And all the branch websites are maintained by students of a particular branch only.

Placements are good at Amity University Gwalior. If you study hard then you can get up to 30lacs per year, but if you are average in your study then also you will easily get a 5lacs per year job very easily. They have different specialization courses also for students which will help them in their placements. Faculty members are also very concerned about students.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text: The course curriculum is similar to the general cse curriculum and the faculty is highly qualified but they are strict in attendance and giving grades. Exams are very frequent and they happen around every two months in a semester. The exams are tough and there is a system of relative grading which somewhat balances the tough exams.The fees are around 2.25 lakhs every semester including hostel and mess fees. And every year fees are increased by around 10% every year there are very few scholarships available based on the low family income of students but they are very hard to avail.

The placement of the college is good but purely skill-based. It's obvious that if you don't work hard, you'll have to rely on mass recruiters.
The students become eligible for campus placements from the 5-6th semester onwards, starting from the internships to the placements in the 7-8th semester.
The highest package offered this year was 62 lakhs per annum with an average of 8.5 lakhs per annum.
The percentage of students getting placed was around 90-95.
My plan after getting a degree is to acquire a job with a minimum pay of 15-20 lakhs per annum.
After having a few years of experience in the field, I may prepare for MBA.Life at the campus is amazing. The university recently conduct its carnival on its 25th anniversary and it was a blast.
There are many societies and the interaction is also great. You'll get to learn a lot from these societies.
There is a central library building in the University and also, and each branch has its libraries, many of the hostels also have a reading room.
Sports and extra curricular activities are also highly supported. A student will get a lot of opportunities to show their skills.

The course curriculum of Chemical engineering is quite easier as compared to other branches like Electrical engineering, ECE, etc.
The faculty members here at DTU are quite supportive as well.
Most of the faculty members of CHE here at DTU have done Ph.D. either in Chemical Engineering or Chemistry.
It is quite easy to pass the exams if a student has paid attention in class and has studied properly for 1 week just before the exams.Campus life is quite chill here at DTU.

The National Institutes of Technology (NITs) are the premier engineering colleges in India. The fees for studying in these institutes are relatively low compared to private engineering colleges, but they have been increasing over the years.
In 2021, the Ministry of Education increased the fees for undergraduate courses in all the NITs, including NIT Meghalaya. The hike was in the range of 10-20%, and it was done to cover the increased costs of running the institutes.

fees are pretty much average at 125000 /yr only, still, if u need financial independence then u could contact the state bank nitw branch which will guide you to take loans at a very minimal interest rate.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text:
The course I chose was CSE because I like this field and also the placement package is nice. The curriculum, at first might seem unnecessary but as you study more you find that more or less everything in the course is necessary.
There are two groups in each branch, i.e, about 60 students and there are two professors for each group for a particular subject. Also, both the groups are further divided into three sub-groups, i.e, a total of six sub-groups for labs, and a professor is assigned to each sub-group.
All the professors are not too good, not too bad just like the normal stereotypical image of a professor in an engineering college. 
The college conducts mid-term and end-term examinations in a semester. Also, quizzes and lab assignments are also conducted. We have midterms after two and a half months after the start of the semester and end terms after the same amount of time.
Passing exams is easy if you just study occasionally even before the exams, you can pass.PECFEST(cultural) is conducted at the end of the year and PECFEST(technical) is conducted at around mid-year. Both are great and basically the same for someone who wants to enjoy. 
There are many books available in the library and you can even ask for them if a book is not there. 
Classrooms have projectors, ACs (not all), and normal chalk/whiteboards.
There are plenty of extra-curricular activities like cricket, football, basketball, tennis, Dramatics(dance+acting), singing, bhangra, editorial boards, and societies for each branch-specific thing. 
Most of the clubs are run by students only and they provide good opportunities for someone to grow in that field.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Our College also provides a good amount of internship opportunities, and the ones worthy can easily make it. Companies like JPMC, and Maruti (in mechanical), offer internships, and a nice stipend is also provided. Students are provided a project semester for the internship, and in the end, they have to prepare a detailed report, telling about their learnings and outcomes.The course curriculum according to me is quite up to the industry standards. I chose the course because of my personal interest, and I don't regret my decision curriculum-wise. The faculty-student ratio in a lecture class is about 1:100, and 1:30 in a subgroup. All the faculties are highly qualified. Exams are also moderately difficult, absolutely not a cakewalk.

All faculties they provide are PHD scholars. There are 3 exams in each semester. The core branch theory subjects are pretty difficult to pass.You will not able to enjoy much due to their academic schedule. But you will have pretty much yearly events to participate in and enjoy.
There are so many clubs including technical clubs, cultural clubs, and official college clubs that organised events and hackathons.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
The annual fest's name is Vivacity and Tech fest is Plinth they are a nice fest and are organized on a higher level.
they are conducted in October / November generally but this time they are organized in January and February.
Books and journals are available in the most updated version in the library most of the time so it is more useful for students.
Sports facilities are good in the college but the administration spends a very less amount of money because of which students have to manage by themselves only.
multiple extracurricular activities happen in college regularly so that students can learn and explore something new which they have never done before.

faculty to student ratio is about 20:1 Ph.D. faculty ratio

Difficulty level of exams depends on teacher to teacher some make it a little difficult and some make it very difficult.
Overall exam and academic processes are on the tougher side.

There are two annual fests, Riviera and Gravitas. Gravitas is the tech fest where various clubs conduct different events ranging from tech events like hackathons and coding competitions to fun events like laser tag and go-karting.
The library is huge with 6 floors. A vast array of academic books can be found here and you can borrow any three books at maximum for a maximum period of 14 days.
Classrooms are big and are equipped with smart board projectors and biometric fingerprint scanners to take students' attendance. Some smart classrooms have air conditioning too.
Sports facilities are plenty ranging from basketball, football, badminton, tennis and table tennis courts to swimming pools, outdoor running tracks, and gyms, for both boys and girls.Fees are decent prices for a private university and the cost to study is good when you take the huge campus and all the facilities it offers into consideration. Placement offers will be given to students from the start of their third year onwards. You'll have to mandatorily either participate in an internship or get involved in some other project like the capstone project. Financial assistance from the university's side for research and competitive projects is also provided for talented students who need funds for their work.

almost all books are available in our 4 floored library,
classrooms are medium, they have essential things that it.
sports and extracurricular activities are constant and good.

classroom do not have ac, AC rooms are only for First-year Students.

BIT Mesra has an active internship program that offers students the opportunity to gain practical work experience and apply their classroom knowledge in real-world settings. Some of the companies that have offered internships to BIT Mesra students in the past include TATA Steel, IBM, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Cognizant, L&T, and many more. For example, a computer science student might work on a project that involves developing a new software application or a website. college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:

The infrastructure of Delhi Technological University is very good as it maintains it's natural beauty and well planned infrastructure. The library is huge but there will be difficulty to find a particular book during exams. Sports curriculum is not so great but ok. There were many society's that were created by students which will help in socializing with others.Most of the companies which give internship opportunities are only available for CS, IT, and Electrical departments. And the lower department students didn't get that many opportunities. Many companies came for internship programs like google, Microsoft, adobe etc.

The syllabus of computer engineering and Information Technology is almost the same at DTU except with a difference of one compulsory subject in 4th year. The faculties are average. A Technical and Cultural fest is organized in the institute every year - CORONA and MILANGE at around December. An annual fest, ECCENTRICA is also organized every year around October. Apart from these, many other festivals are enjoyed by students which are organized by different clubs.
The library includes all the books necessary during the course for every year, every branch. Journals and newpapers are also available in the latest editions.
Most of the classrooms are smart classes with projectors, screens, and microphones. All the classes have Air Conditioning and good ventilation.
Cricket, Football, Badminton, and other games are played on campus and students participate in different games in Inter-NIT sports meet. There is also a gym, music room, badminton court, and cafeteria to enjoy and chill.
NSS has a branch in NIT Patna that is run by the students themselves. There are many clubs robotics, coding, entrepreneurship etc.Mechanical engineering opens a gate for a number of opportunities that include GATE, PSUs, Indian Engineering Services, and many other government services. Students can also go for private companies.
The faculty-student ratio in NIT Patna is around 1:20. There are adequate faculty members in the college.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Placement for our college is good, placement percentages for the different departments are different for example for the cse department it is approx 95%, for ece is also 90% and other branches also get good placement whose percentage is greater than 70%. All the things in college are done timely, they release time table for the semester at the start of the semester which includes classes, holidays, exams, etc everything, so everything is on time.

The library needs a serious update but yes there are books available in the library and dates are given for the collection of books from the library. There are some social groups run by the students of our college along with a lot of clubs namely SOVIC, Coding club, Robotics Club etc.I chose Btech in electrical engineering because this allows me to go to core sector jobs as well as IT jobs. The student-to-faculty ratio is 18:1 in our department. The professors are all PhD and master qualified. Just like any other college, some teachers are very good at their skills while others struggle a bit at teaching but overall it depends on the student. The class tests carry high importance and are done timely and the finals are also done timely and can be passed with a decent effort but for good marks, more than a decent effort is required.

Campus Life of BIT MESRA is awesome. You have a lush green campus to live and enjoy. The campus is just beautiful and very large. No pollution, no noise just pure vibes of a green campus. 
The infrastructure is also beautiful. Classes are smart, we'll be equipped and highly maintained.
We have high-class lab facilities.
The canteen area is nice.
The hostel infrastructure is also good.
And sports facilities are also very decent in BIT MESRA. A proper stadium is there for Football, Badminton, Basketball, and Volleyball. 
The gym facility is also very good.Course Curriculum is excellent in BIT MESRA. Faculty members are highly qualified and knowledgeable. Good care is taken toward academics. All exams and quizzes are held timely and teachers are very strict. It's difficult to score well but you can manage if you are little smart. 
Attendance is taken very seriously. You need to have more than 75% attendance to appear in exams or else you will get an F in the subject which means you have to attend classes again in summer break to pass.

You can assume that every course fees are same
Of Rs 2.20 lakh for the first year.
Yes, University provides scholarships for students who had a family income less than from benchmark mark of the rupee just they have to show valid documents that show their eligibility to grant scholarships.
On campus, you could have lots of opportunities to grab internships and also gets a chance to work with startupsYes, you will experience a very good life on the campus as talking about social life you will interact with International students and also the student from the master's background and from the design background there are lots of students to talk to elaborate your skills. 
 one of the most famous  annual fests is engifest and recently we had a literature and film fest we called it YUVAAN23

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Fees per semester are nearly 1.2 lakhs for the general category whose family income is more than 5 lakhs. And for general category students with a family income between 1 to 5 lakhs and all OBC students get a remission of 2/3 of tuition fees which are 1 lakh per semester. And whose family income is less than 1 lakh will get full remission of tuition fees and a 100 per cent tuition fee waiver for sc /st / pwd students. 
And there are lots of scholarship here for students.Life on campus is fun but academics here at IITK are more difficult than at other IITs. Fests time is too much enjoyable there is a total of 3 fests but something keeps going on campus for a year. Lecture Hall has AC and a lot of places on campus to study. Sports and other facilities are great and there are too many clubs, societies, and teams run by the student body to discover and live their passion.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Annual fest - Antaragni in October, Tech fest - techkriti in March. 
Almost all curriculum books are available in the library.
All classrooms have AC and projectors
One of the best sports infrastructures in our college. Almost all sports are played

Student Gymkhana website of iitk and all other club websitesThere is an organisation called the student's placement office - which manages all placements and internships given to students. Amazon, Mahindra, Microsoft and many more companies recruit for internships
I haven't sit for an internship or placement yet so can't say much about it

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Placement season comes every year likely for last year's students. The company from various fields including cooperate and research-based companies are present. 
The average package is like 10 to 15 lakhs CTC and the highest fits cores.
Almost everybody gets placed though depending on the skill of the person also.Companies come to grant internship opportunities after every summer break.
Moreover, information about internships is also circulated via mail between semesters. 
Companies for both research and industrial sectors grant internship opportunities.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: I chose this course because of my rank. The ratio here is around 300-400 students for first-year students but after that, it reduces to around 50-100 students depending on the department they are enrolled in here. The faculty are highly qualified and great at teaching. But exams are tough here so you need to study regularly.The fee is around 1.3 lac per semester which is similar to other IITs as well. Being a general category student I don't get to avail any of the scholarships or financial aid provided but still, loans are available if students find it difficult to pay.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
IIT Kanpur is a big campus with an area of over 1000 acres. The library is old and has lakhs of books. Udghosh is the annual Sports fest of IIT Kanpur.
Techkriti is the annual technical fest. Antaragni is the annual Cultural Fest. Students from all parts of the country participate in all the festsThis was the best option for me according to my rank. IIT Kanpur is considered one of the best colleges for electrical engineering. The lab facilities at IIT Kanpur are great. The most important reason for choosing IIT Kanpur for me was the culture and the facilities provided here.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: Antragni  udgosh techkriti are the cultural and tech fedt of college. It is Asia's famous fest. the classroom has full facilities like a projector, speaker, and big screen. in IIT there are classrooms with 600 student capacity.Job opportunity is good. The average package is 17 lakh. IIT Kanpur gives scholarships to needy ones. So one can easily go for that course. Fees are approx 1 lakh but one with an income lower than 1 lakh needs no tuition fees

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Btech- Materials Science and Engineering is a good course, You get to learn many theoretical as well as numerical subjects.
Topics like the solid state are Important.
I have chosen this course because I preferred college over a branch, but now I can say the branch is also good. Most people apply for software-related jobs. Almost all students get placed. Getting an internship is quite a difficult task, but that's for every branch. The class size is not too large, so if you like to interact with professors, you can easily do it. Most of the professors are Ph.D. from foreign universities. They teach quite well, students usually study a few days before the exam and can pass the course by just seeing the slides provided by the professors.Tuition Fees are about 1 lakh per semester. Mess establishment charges are over 13k per semester. Roughly, the semester fee is around 1.25 lakh per semester. i.e. 2.5lakh per year.
Tuition fees depend on your income.
For income greater than 5lakh per annum, Tuition fees are 1 lakh per semester.
For income between 1lakh and 5 lakhs, the Tuition fee is Rs 33,000 per semester and for Income less than 1lakh per annum, There are no Tuition Fees. 
There are many scholarships available like Merit-cum Means Scholarship, Donor Scholarships, and external scholarships.

college_name: IIT Ropar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
The fest is divided into 3 categories: cultural, techno, and sports, and their names are Zeitgeist, Advitya, and Aarohan respectively.
The campus has a big library that has a variety of books from different fields and the library, the building has a high capacity for students to study and for group discussion areas.
The campus has a large sports complex for all sports and the ground has proper lighting for practicing at night. The college has so many clubs which include sports, dance, painting, photography, etc. for extracurricular activities.Firstly tuition fees are divided into 3 categories according to family income.  107150 per sem, whose family income is above 5 lakh. 40484 per sem, whose family income is between 1 and 5 lakh.
 7150 per sem, whose family income is below 1 lakh.
The hostel fee is 1500 per sem and the mess has a daily fee coupon which price is 130/day.
The above tuition fees are for gen. and obc students.

The annual cultural fest is named Blitzchlag
Which is generally organised in the month of Feb.
The annual tech fest is named by Sphinx
Which is organised in the month of March.
There is a sports ground gym basketball court, badminton court, volleyball court, tennis court etc.
Social groups in this college are CACS, MC??, THINK INDIA, BLISS, F&P, UBA etc.Since this is a government engineering college so its fee is stable at approx 80000/- per semester for college.
Different state candidates can apply for their state government scholarship.
As well as those candidates whose family income is below 5 lac per annum they will get fee remission.

The course curriculum is current and the syllabus is continuously revised and made relevant according to the industrial skills needed. Some of the faculties are the best you can get and they motivate us to pursue and be deeply engrossed in the subject of their expertise. All faculties are PHD holders from reputed institutions. The exams are of moderate level and one could pass the exams with regular and sincere studies.The club's cultural and technical fest is spectacular and fun to be a part of. The opportunities one gets in pursuing extra activities and club work add to one's experience. The library is fully stocked with all the books. Classrooms are standard with a smart board and a projector. All kinds of sports facilities are available inside the campus. There are over 200 clubs and chapters run by students in VIT.

There is Daksh the technical fest, Eclecia the cultural fest, the Nascent fest for freshers. All the sem books are provided by the library. Classrooms are well equipped with projectors for laptop presentations. 
Basketball, swimming, cricket, football, and other sports are held between streams and colleges. 
Clubs are there in college like science, robotics and all.ECE is a very great course in the engineering field. As the stream is good for both core and non-core fields. The faculty is nice and helpful. Every student can contact the professors personally for any doubts. All the professors are Ph.D. in their respective fields. The term exams are held once per 5-6 months and the mid sems are held in between the sems.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
The annual fest happens in the month of March. Tech fest and other cultural fest are also held timely by the clubs
At Jaypee institute of information technology, the library is very good and has world-class collection of the both Indian and international authors
No proper sports facilities are present at Jaypee institute of information technologyTalking about Jaypee institute of information technology, the placement in the department of biotechnology of Jaypee institute of information technology is very poor, only Nestle is the renowned company which comes and that to hires only 2 students and there is only 80% placement in the Jaypee institute of information technology biotech

There are many fests which keep happening, like anadyanta, hackathons, flashmob and many more, most of the books are available in the library with ease, they're are many clubs, and lots of exposure to the outside world, like Ecell, flashmobs, music clubs, sports clubs, tech clubs and you can name it all. Amenities in classrooms are good too, have the basic stuff.I chose this course cause I'm really interested in it and hope to see a bright future in aviation and design, there are good enough faculties and many faculties in fact who'll be ready to help yall out anytime and very understanding.
Most of the facilities will be at least holding a PhD or master in it, exams are well planned and passing is a bit hard

Campus Life is indescribable. Engifest, Techfest, and various cultural fests are conducted from time to time. Recently Engifest was conducted.
The college library is very big, with thousands of books and the best infrastructure. Classrooms have good infrastructure and all are equipped with ACs and smartboards.
The College Sports complex is very big and has facilities for Cricket, Football, Badminton, Tennis, Table Tennis,  Athletics, Volleyball, etc.
There are Clubs and societies for almost all fields ranging from AI-Robotics to singing.Students at the end of 3rd year appear for interviews in campus placement. DTU has one of the best placements records. The highest placement overseas is 1.84 crore and domestic is 80 lakhs. Around 100% of student gets placed in the computer science branch. This year around 384 companies visited for placements. The average package for Computer Science is 17-18 LPA. FAANG companies also visit the college. I plan to take the best possible placement.

DTU has one of the best fests in India. It is called ENGIFEST. There are many small and medium-level fests organized are other societies.
Sports is one of the best things about dtu . There are 2 full basketball courts. 1 cricket field, 1 football field, and an indoor stadium also.Avg package last year was around Rs 15 l.p.a and the highest package was Rs . 1 . 8 cr.
200 + companies visit the campus and overall placements are good.
There are 100 % placements at dtu.
But there is a lot of competition due high number of students

Life of campus is quite cheerful as there are many events and workshops conducted time to time for refreshing+ there are many tech and nontech societies also Annual fest is ENGIFEST and Tech fest is INVICTUS. Books, Magazines and various kinds of journals are always available ranging on different topics in the CENTRAL LIBRARY. Our classroom as well maintained and fully air conditioned and they have many charging ports also. There are many sports facilities also both indoor and outdoor sports. Fresources is a website run by the students and for the studentsFees structure for the first year is around 2.2lac for academic year 2022-23. Fee hike is around 5-10% for every academic year. There are many scholarships Offered to the students some are merit based while others are category based there are many private scholarships also including Reliance Foundation Scholarship and Fast retailing scholarship. There are many on campus internship and job offers which goes upto 1cr per annum (for placements) and 2lac/month( For internship at Sprinkler)

The annual tech fest is Invictus and the annual cultural fest happens in the month of February. There are a large number of books in the library. The ground and sports infra are pretty good as compared to other colleges.
You can find a society relating to every activity within this college.The fees are around 2.15 lakhs per annum which are somewhat high given that it is a government university. There are various scholarships available based on different eligibilities. The placements and internships though are pretty great. The stipend for interns goes up to 2 lahks per month.

The teacher in our institution is very helpful well qualified and have good knowledge and some of them are qualified from iits, dtu is considered better than other collage as it provides a lot of opportunities when it comes to placement or other Personality development. Many students get on-campus placement.There are many fests conducted by the societies and the biggest fest in north India is engifest which is conducted in dtu, there is a very good library, a good ac classroom sports complex and a mic Mac. There are many socities like madhurima, yuvaan, wave society and many more, that conduct many activities.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The course was well prepared as per corporate culture. It provides exposure to analytical skills, coding, communication, innovation, and entrepreneurship. The Faculty to student ratio was around 1:120 in class and for tutorials and practicals, it was 1:30. The faculties are pioneers in their domain and studied for IITs and abroad reputed universities. The exam was easy to pass as the whole curriculum is majorly created on a hands-on learning approach.There was a Satanalia fest conducted each year and induction is well planned with huge activities from societies and events. The library is huge every kind of book or novel is available. The classrooms were good including all the amenities. The sports ground activities are well organized and courts and grounds are well maintained. There is a Fross week team for new admission students detail are available on the college website.

This year kiit celebrated KIIT SILVER JUBILEE
it was a 3 months celebration that included tech fest, star night, sports fest, and many more events 
This place is known for sports kiit has produced many Olympians 
Dutee Chand who is the fastest runner of India in 100 meters do practice on the track of kiit itselfI choose this course because of the expected job growth in the Computer science sector
Faculty: Students ratio is 1:40 Most of the teachers have done a PhD 
And are qualified from IITs and Nits
Exams are conducted twice a semester and there is also the option for improvement exams

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
I chose this course because I had a keen interest in both Mathematics and Computer Science. I was more interested in learning the mathematical foundations of computer science, rather than just the application of it.
faculty: student ratio is very low. There are generally more than 100 students in a usual class.
Faculty members are highly qualified and have done their Ph.D. and post Docs from renowned Indian and international universities. The teaching method is basically going through slides in subjects other than maths and for maths, they write on the projectors and boards.
There is 1 Minor and 1 Major evaluation. In some semesters, there can be 2 minors and 1 major, it is announced beforehand. Exams are very difficult to score and it is quite hard to maintain a decent GPA here.Annual cultural fest - Rendezvous
Tech fest - Tryst
There are more than 6000 books and more than 100000 journals in the library of IIT Delhi and books can be made available in 1 monthly period on demand.
The lecture hall complex is very big and all the classes are held there. There are lifts, washrooms, and drinking water on each floor. Also, all the rooms have centralized ac.
There are big sports grounds. There is a separate court for each sport and a newly built Mittal sports complex for Indoor games as well.
All the clubs are maintained by students. In some big clubs like eDC, SInC, Robotics, etc., a professor is also assigned to supervision. Other clubs are FACC, PFC, DevClub, ANCC, Dance, Music, Drama, etc. To conclude, there is something for everybody.

I chose this course as I'm interested in the communication sector. Faculty: student ratio is for each subject 4:55 . All the faculties are well qualified and well experienced. There will be continuous assessment tests every month followed by semester exams. Anyone who listens to class and properly follows the homework and tests shall score more than a pass mark in the final examination.There will be two intercollege events: kriya and shristi. There'll be a variety of books for all departments and all subjects. Books for gate and other exam preparation are also available. We have projectors in the classroom for advanced learning. Intramurals - inter-department sports cultural. We have more than 25+ clubs and 10+ associations in our college.

Campus life is the best. DTU has one of the best cultures and societies. 
The Tech Fest (Invictus) and Cultural Fest ( Engifest) are one of the largest fests in North India. They were conducted in February. 
The library is really big and contains books for every subject and interest. 
Classrooms are nice and AC occupied, professors teach on the Whiteboard and the classes are clean too. 
There are a lot of societies run by students like Ecell DTU, Biosoc DTU, Cultural Council DTU, SCEE DTU, etc. 
There are a lot of startups and websites run by students like resources websites.The fees increase by 10 percent every year. 
The cost of study comes to around 9 lakh for me. 
You can get scholarships if you are from a reservation or your family income is less than 4.5 lakhs per annum. 
You can get student loans for cheap rates from the bank and DTU assists you with that. 
You can get internships on campus, ranging from 30,000 to 70,000. But, the competition is tough to get internships.

There are 2 grand fests Incandesence and Tecnoesis and the other 2 regionals Posua and Oikyotan. Incandescence is conducted in February and tech noesis in October. Most books and journals are available in the library. The library at NIT Silchar is the largest digital library in Asia. The classrooms are provided desk benches and the class dimensions are gallery structured and provided with projectors for better visualization.  For sports, there is a sports complex that consists of a tennis court, badminton court, basketball court, and a gym. There are two grounds, one for football and another for cricket. There are numerous clubs in the college where you can show your talent. There is a singing club, dancing club, drama club, coding club, literary club, development club, eco club, etc. There are branch websites that are made by students for students and run by students.In the 7th semester, students become eligible for placements and can sit in placement rounds till the end of the 8th semester if they are not yet placed.
A lot of tech companies like Microsoft, amazon visit during campus placement and they recruit a lot of students. The highest placement is 44 LPA and the average package is 15 LPA . 74% of the students eligible for placement got placed. After getting the degree I would like to work at a tech company and grow along my career.

From the past few years, the placements are quite good. This year the maximum placement till now is 28LPA which is again a very good ROI as seen from tier 3 colleges. Most of the students get placed and there are mass recruiters to give placements to plenty of students.Fees are quite high and also there are scholarship opportunities for those who have secured more than 85% in class 12. They provide up to 100% scholarships to those who have secured 95% in 12th. Also, there are plenty of Campus opportunities.

After the 6th semester, students are eligible for placement. In my department, there are several companies like maruti suzuki, hero motorcop, Reliance, valency international etc are coming for placement. Reliance gave 10 offers hero gave 8 offers Maruti gave 7 offers etc. The highest package is 22lpa in valency international. The average package is 7.5lpa.more than 80% of students got placed. My plan is to opt for placement and preparation for the gate.The annual festival is aadhay and the technical fest is technika. Aadhay was held in June and technika was held in May. There are some monthly programs also. There are more than 1lakh books in a library. so we got from there. Some classrooms have ac and good facilities. Sports facilities are mediocre. Other than cricket and football there is no good sports facility. There is a good society for dance and E- cell for startups.

I always wanted to pursue Btech in CSE so that's why I chose it. There's 1 Faculty course subject per 80 students but you will get a mentor separately who will help you out in many ways. The faculties are very qualified. Most of them have experience in teaching at IIT and NIT. Each of them is really helpful and will give an effort in teaching you. You will have mid-sem and endsem. Basically, a semester last 5 months and the questions are mediocre.The annual fest and Tech Fest of CSE were conducted in Feb. 
You will get your semester books for free and the availability of books in the library will be too. 
The classroom is very good. You can't complain about it. 
Sports and extracurricular activities are the main limelight of the college. 
There are a lot of social groups run by students like there's coding club and many more.

Campus life is happening as we have a fully residential campus. There are two major fests every year - BREEZE and SURGE and a lot of events all around the year. BREEZE is conducted in the month of March after mid sem exams. 
The library is very big and has thousands of books. 
We have smart classrooms that are equipped with the latest technologies and air conditioners and heaters function in the summers and winters. 
Attention to sports is the thing that I love about SNU. It hosts a lot of sports opportunities including training. Competitions are conducted regularly.
There is a lot of cultural and technical club. I am a part of GDSC.I will become eligible for campus placements from the 5th semester onward. Tons of good companies visit our campus every year. To name a few, we have Amazon, Amex, Dell, Goldmann Sachs, HP, etc. 
The highest package this year is about 55 lpa and the average package is 12 LPA and the median is 11 LPA. 
I guess over 90% of students got placed. 
I am hoping to get a good placement after getting the degree.

college_name: IIT Gandhinagar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text:
The annual tech fest of IIT GANDHINAGAR "Amalthea" is conducted generally in January, and the annual cultural fest "Blithchron" is conducted in February. Both the fest are completely run by students, mostly first-year and second-year undergraduate students. All books which are needed for your course curriculum can be availed in the library. You can also find many tech magazines and other books too in the library.
All classrooms are air-conditioned and equipped with high-speed Wi-Fi. The library too is air-conditioned and is the go-to place to prepare for exams or to have a casual read. All hostel rooms are air-conditioned. Each hostel has a TV room, a table tennis room, medical facilities, etc. Rooms are very spacious and are cleaned once a week by the maintenance staff. The sports facilities are one of the best across all IITs and other colleges across India. We have an Olympic size swimming pool, a cricket ground with floodlights, a football ground, etc.I chose this course as I was interested in Materials Engineering and how the properties of materials can be altered to build better materials. 
The faculty: student ratio stands at 1:10, for every 10 students there is one faculty member.
All the faculties here have done their Ph.D. in top universities of the world, and have a lot of experience as well, they are highly qualified. Each faculty have its teaching method, but mostly all of them prefer an interactive session among the students.
We have exams almost every month and if the student pays attention to all lectures and solves all the tutorials it is very easy to pass the exams.

CS Course is in trends. Also, I have a lot of interest in it. There are more than 100+ faculty, and students in the college are more than 5000, on faculty opinion is mixed some faculty is exceptionally good some are difficult to understand. but they also have LMS online learning platform which will help you understand more, minor happens in around 35-40 days.From 5th-semester students get eligible for placements, placements are one the best reason to join this college, Top companies like Sony, AWS, Mercedes, TCS, Amagi, Twilio, Amazon and more, Highest package is 43Lacs, 30+ students got 20+ lacs package last year, almost all students get placed. I am planning to do job after getting my degree.

The annual fest's name is Amrit Mahotsav it happens in January, also many other programs happen from time to time like PUPA, and the Motorsports Association also conduct fest. Also, the library is good classrooms are good, and the sports facilities are also good there is a separate gymkhana for professional athletes also clubs like GDSC, Bits and bytes(coding clubs), GFG campus chapter, Codechef Campus chapter and KLE Motorsports, KLE Drama club are there, English club, Dance music etc are there.Students become eligible from the 6th sem for campus placements. Currently, placements are going on but companies like Amagi, Amazon, Twilio, and Juspay, have visited the campus and recruited. The highest package till now is from Twilio 43 lpa I think, the average must be around 10-11lpa. Almost all students are placed in product based.

TATA Consultancy service, Tech Mahindra, etc. more companies choose our college. 
There will be several tests based on a company like the Written test,  Oral test and then Interviews, Aptitude test, etc. 
Based on these tests they will choose a candidate.Btech CSE is a future growing branch, in my opinion, there is Coeducation in it also.
All teaching faculty are also good and trained.
There are 4years and 8 semesters in between we have seminars presentations mid exams also.

Cse is the favourite for many who are decided to work as computer software engineers so I opted for this subject there is one teacher for 10 students on the campus and the faculty is highly educated and they had very good knowledge of the subjects and the teaching is good, especially some professors. There will be two mid-exams and final exams these will be easy if you study. It will average the exam.The percentage of students getting placed is growing as compared to the previous year and the student placed is around 80 per cent there will be many companies coming to the college for campus interviews. And many will join in campus placements. But few of them do ms in the USA after the BTech for better opportunities.

The campus is small, with not too much space to roam around but still campus life is good.
Annual fest is 8th mile but it won't happen annually, Esummit happens almost after every year which is also a good fest.
Fests are conducted after every 2-3 months, small or big doesn't matter.
Enough books are available in the library to make use of it.
The classroom contains a smart board and is good for a student kind of environment.
The sports culture is pretty good as uk Anil Kumble and lots of other sports personalities are from RVCE.
Ecell, RAAG, Rotaract, coding club, Alap etc. are some of the clubs.7th sem students are eligible for campus placement. Placements are better than lots of NITs and almost all the government colleges.
Average placement is >14 LPA this year for the ISC department and >8 LPA for IEM 
Deloitte, Capgemini, Mercedes Benz, Microsoft, Volvo etc. are some companies visit our college.
No. Of offers are maximum >250 for ECE department.
The highest Package is nearly 88 LPA this year
Almost all the students are placed in IEM.
I may prefer MBA as I'm already studying management at IEM.

I chose this course as the placements in the college are very good.
the faculty to student ratio is around one teacher per 60 students.
Maximum teachers are PhD and they are very qualified.
Internals are very regular, they happen every month and the term exams are 1 per 6 months.Students are eligible for placements from their 3rd year ending.
Previously lots of companies used to our campus. But during the recession, the no of companies coming to our college has decreased.
The highest placement this year is Rs 88 lakhs.
Placement is around 60%

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Annual tech fest occurs in a very short interval of time during the month of February and March, TechNiti is the tech fest of our college which is organized during the month of February and Utkansh is organized during the month of March, most of the books that we need for the study we can easily get from the library, we need to show library card to book issuers and we can issue one book for 15 days, our classroom is very well organized where we get the AC facility good sitting benches, and plane benches for the writing work, sports activities organized in very discipline way in our college we can play any game in our college, students play volleyball, badminton even in the night time as well, apart from the sports activities our college also have several social groups and clubs these clubs organized all the events in our colleges, each club and society having their different websites, some clubs and society of our college are TTA, GDSC, Zeal Society, SOME society, etc.The fee structure is different for different category students, the fee of any student depends on the category and the Income certificate, If a student belongs to SC and ST category then he/she don't need to pay only the hostel fee they don't need to pay tuition fee and If a student belongs to the general category then they need to pay full college fee, If the Income certified is less than 1 lakh then only hostel and other charges need to pay no tuition fee for these students, There is no scholarship for general and OBC students in our college but You can get the loan from bank easily without any extra charges, In our branch, more than 80 % students easily get placement in tech and core companies with an average stipend of 8 lakhs per annum.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
I am a student of textile engineering in nit Jalandhar. To be honest I didn't choose this branch. I got it by jee cut-off. but at this time I know the importance of this branch. And you also get many job opportunities and placement facilities. The faculty to students ratio is 1:20. The qualifications of faculty are PhD or m.tech. and all teachers are experienced in their area.
