You can pass them easily by sincerely daily revising the things that are discussed in the classes.The NITJ provide good placement every year this year Highest placement got 1.2 crores and average placement every year was approx 14LPA and the lowest placement is approx 5LPA, 3rd year and 4th-year student are eligible for applying for the placement interview, many companies visited every year Microsoft Flipkart Google olx Amazon byjus Samsung Tesco cdot American express Goldman synopsys Paytm Honda Airtel and Reliance strand Vardhman and so may approx 250+ companies visited and provide 700+offices
Every year almost 90%student got placed in my plan after completing b.tech work in an MNC company

Because in this Kumaraswamy layout campus, cse students will get every year good placements, and computer is my favorite so I took cse, faculty of cse is around 50 professors, and students are around 250 most of the faculty are ph.d people. exams are a little hard to pass as the faculty is a little strict about exams.In this college fees structure is around 440000  It depends on the students' recommendation process, for kcet cse people it is around 150000, they will create a WhatsApp group and they will send various scholarships through that group, every year above 95 percent of people are getting placed on this campus.

Every month every club held many technical and annual fest 
Every academic block has their own library 
There is Every type of sport and every sport has its own couch that is well trained 
There is an IEEE website which is based on women's empowermentPlacement regarding this course is great so I chose this.
Faculty resolve the problem of children efficiently, 
The teaching method is good every student feels comfortable 
The exam ratio is very good every student attempt their exam

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
Life at our college campus is very good, there are two fests that happen in our college one is the technical fest techNITI which is held during the month of February and another is the cultural fest which is celebrated during the month of march, and in the library of our college most of the books are easily available you can issue your book by using the library card and library time is 9 am to 5 pm.
The classroom is well furnished, the smart board is present in all the classrooms and AC is already fitted in each classroom, Sitting benches are very comfortable, a total of 80 to 90 seats are present in our classroom and the ventilation system of our classroom is very good.
Regular sports activities occur in our college, there is a separate club of sports there in our college, all types of games are played in our college like cricket, badminton, hockey, volleyball etc, we can play these sports in our college at any time.
Several social and technical clubs are there in our college like GDSC, Robotic clubs.Most of the companies visit during the 6th semester for the Internship program for the 2 months and during the 6th month, companies like BPCL  reliance, amazon, google, Veolia etc visit our college and provide paid Internships to students, Before the Internships students need to give Interview for the Internships, For the Internship Students of the 3 rd year need to build at least one project so that they have the sufficient knowledge of the project, If the project of the students is good then they easily get the Internship and they don`t need to do worry about It, Companie In Interview asked the question form the projects of the students. If the Interviews and the project of the students are well and good then they easily get paid for Internships.

My annual fees are around 1 lack 60 thousand and there is little bit fees hike every year I don't have any scholarship but apply for an education loan to lighten the fees burden from my parents.  Right now I'm in second and going to apply for an internship.I choose this course for exploring the tech domain in detail and get the opportunity to do research in a foreign country. Now the faculty Is nice. And student to faculty ratio is 4:1. The qualification of faculty is a min Ph.D.

The course curriculum is quite good. In the first year, you will study the extended version of the course you have studied in earlier classes, in your school. Later on, in the next 3 years, you will get knowledge in the field of your selected branch.
Here at AKGEC faculty to student ratio is quite good. The number of students in each section is approximately 60 and there is 18 section for the first year. Lecture time for each class is 50 min with a well-educated (but boring) faculty. Qualification of maximum faculty is a master's degree in their respective field for having Ph.D.
If we talk about exams in this college, they organize exams very frequently. There are 4 to 5 exams you have to give before the end sem exam. One good thing is that you can pass it very easily by doing last night's study. They mostly ask selected questions from the previous year.The course fee is 1,44,000 for 1st year, 1,20,000 for 2nd year, and 1,10.000 for 3rd and 4th year. After this Hostel fee is 1,20,000 per year. You have to give the full fee at the start of the year. 
College does not provide any scholarship or financial assistance but you can apply for state govt. scholarship. 
College does not provide any internship you have to find it on your own. But college surely gives campus placement opportunities.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
The annual fest Rendezvous is held in our college which is Asia's largest cultural fest and is conducted in March-April
Our Annual tech fest is Tryst which takes place in March-April and a lot of tech companies visit our campus during the fest
The college ensures that sports and extracurricular activities take place such as the Group Dance by the IITD dance club, Stalwarts by debating Society, and many more
Clubs run by students include the BRCA which conducts all the events of our college related to Cultural Activities4th-year students are eligible to sit for placements 
Many companies visit during the placement season, including BCG and Big 4 for Consult and Amazing, Google for Tech, and many more
The highest package of the Institute is around 1.5Cr while the average package differs from branch to branch. My branch gives an average package of 12 Lakhs
Nearly all people who sit in placement get a job 
My plans after getting a degree are to do MBA from IIM Bangalore

For every year one internship is compulsory that can be done outside also or under the guidance of the teachers within the department can be done and proper evaluation is done so that students take it seriously. There is no restriction for the subject of interest.For 2020 the fees were 2 lac per year but for 2021 it git increased to 2 lac 20 k per year. However several scholarship schemes like nsp and ssp are valid here also through kcet exam the fees are near to 50k per year but it is only for residents of Karnataka.

The fee's structure seems to be a little expensive as expected from a  government funded college. And the graduale hike in the fees of the college is also a downside, though availing for education loan is really easy and scholarship are also available for certain categories.The course curriculum is really strict and totally based on the fact that you have to get maximum output from it. The faculty members are very punctual and strict though sometimes the 75% attendance criteria can be a pain for us.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
I choose this course because I am deeply interested in forming designs and someday, I want to establish my own brand 
the faculty is interactive but strict and they don't accept any excuse at the student's end to maintain a good reputation and maintain the 75% attendance.
the professors are fairly qualified and have very interesting teaching methods but they require some contribution at the student's end
the examination marks distribution criteria are fairly simple. Firstly you have 2 minors (each of 20 marks)(happens every 40 days or so), then you have an end sem (50 marks) and the rest 10 marks are in the hands of the professor tutoring the course.life at the campus is pretty lively and very happening, there are lesser restrictions as you progress into next semester 
the infrastructure of the campus is above average but the classrooms and study rooms are well furnished and have an all-around nice vibe.
there are a variety of sports and clubs but the infrastructure for open sports is poor.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Students here also get the benefits of excellent internship opportunities both paid and unpaid or research interns both in Indian and foreign colleges. Min. stipend for paid internships is 30k per month which goes to a maximum of about 17 lakhs offered by Optiver for the year 2023. Although the CGPA required for such highly paying internships is quite high (Obviously).Rendezvous is an annual cultural fest and Tryst is an annual tech fest that occurs at the start of the year. Variety of books and journals available at 24*7 open library. Fully air-conditioned classes. Sports facilities are also good as compared to other colleges. A lot of clubs are run by student bodies such as Music clubs, sports clubs, dance clubs, etc.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
I first got admission to the textile technology branch but as I wanted to experience electrical branches I applied for branch change after the completion of the first year, where I got promoted to the ICE branch. 
The faculty-to-student ratio is about 1:20 at NIT Jalandhar though faculty is unable to pay attention to every student but they entertain their every doubt. 
Every faculty is highly qualified to educate students as they are PhDs. 
Every semester we attempt 2 minors and 1 major exam, at intervals of a month to precious.Mains on major cultural fest "Utkansh" And one major technical fest "technity" Is celebrated on large scale. 
Classrooms are newly built with high-tech facilities and even security measures are taken into consideration during building them. 
Annual sports day of 2 days is celebrated every year and many sports like running, long jump, etc. are performed. 
The Institute has many clubs like TCA for cultural events, TTA for technical events, criminals and dance club, spice club for students of ICE, various sports clubs, etc..

The fee for one student is about 125000 as a tuition fee and we need to pay other fees also such as the hostel fee, mess fee and a few maintenance charges which include wi-fi and other few charges.
Scholarships are given to the top performer of the year and discounts on tuition fees and the central government provides NSP scholarships to SC-ST students.The campus life is quite beautiful at the National Institute of Technology Warangal, we have a good environment and surroundings on the campus. We have majorly 2 main fests they are tech fest and cultural fest. we also have different types of clubs in which students can participate.

Fees are quite less if compared to the other engg colleges/university.
As it's government engineering college fees is not that much. 
There is a scholarship scheme that provides scholarships on the basis of the tax slab their family comes in.Faculty is quite educated in their respective domains but they lack a way of delivering their knowledge. The faculty is pretty good in nature. Exams aren't that tough if you study even a day before you will easily pass through.

The internship is offered in the 5th semester by various companies on as well as off-campus. Companies like Accenture, Microsoft, and Uber take interns by providing high stipends of 1lacs. Non-tech companies like Sprinkler also provide a similar stipend. The project for the interview should be made using web development knowledge and any other so skill. Every skill matter for the CV for interview cracking.The electrical engineering course is a mathematical course that is getting to realize in the subsequent semester. Subjects get tougher and tenacious. Faculty are greatly experienced in their field of teaching. Every subject has likely to have different professors in their field. In terms of exams, they are conducted every semester (normally 4 months) in between regular class tests and mid-semester exams.

My course is electronics and communication engineering with Artificial intelligence and machine learning (ECAM).
In my course, we learned about Ai, Ml, microprocessors, communication engineering, and electronics. The faculty of this course is very nice and the way they teach is also good and they use real-life problems or situations to solve problem.The total fees for this course are 894000 and we have to pay it in 4 installments(After every 2 semesters). Some scholarships are given by the government and universities. We have to apply for scholarships after serval verification then the scholarship amount is credited to our bank

Breeze is the annual cultural and technical event of SNU which happen in March every year. Surge is the sports event of SNU which happen in November. The library is the key location of SNU. The library is very well designed and you will find almost every book in there. The classroom is similar to any other university.
As for sports, you will find almost every sport at SNU like squash, indoor badminton, football, horse riding, etc.
University have all kinds of clubs 
A few names are: Nexus - gaming club, Celestia - astronomy club, and Inferno -  dance club.From the 6th semester, students are eligible for placements. All big companies come to SNU for placements few big names are Goldman Sachs, Dell, and HCL .95% of students are placed rest go for higher studies. 8 plus cgpa is recommended for placements. The highest package offered was 48 LPA and the average package varies from branch to branch for CSE it's 16lpa and for ECE it's 14 LPA.
My plan after getting a degree is to pursue MBA.

I have a deep interest in electronics so I opted for this course also the course curriculum here will not at all help you in gaining knowledge for core subjects so mostly you have to study on your own. There are 6 subjects per semester of ECE up to the 6th sem and there is only one faculty allotted for each subject. There is a tutorial for each subject which is mostly taken by TAs. All the faculties are highly educated Ph.D. in their respective specialization. Most of the faculties are graduated from NITs/IITs. Their teaching method is quite orthodox  They will provide you with notes from which the maximum number of questions were asked in the exams. Blackboard /Whiteboard / Projector is the mode of teaching. There are 2 main term exams - Midsem and Endsem. These exams are conducted every 3 months. There are minor tests and IAT (Internal Assessment Test) which are taken in between the term exams. The exams are quite easy to pass if you follow class notes and attend every lecture.The annual cultural fest of NIT Silchar is Incandescence and the annual technical fest of NIT Silchar is Tecnoesis.
Incandescence is conducted in March and Tecnoesis is conducted in October. NIT Silchar has the privilege of having Asia's Largest Digital Library Bharat Ratna Dr. A.P.J. Abdul Kalam Learning Resource Centre. Every sort of book be it academic or miscellaneous we will find here. There is a dedicated sports complex on the campus, a football ground, a cricket ground [Nets for practice], a basketball court, volleyball court. There is a club for each sport both indoor and Outdoor. Athletics club, Cricket club, football club, chess club, adventure club, skating club, etc.
There are numerous other clubs and societies at NIT Silchar.
Coding Club, GDSC, ML Club, Robotics Club. E-Cell, Illuminits (The literary society), Trading Club, NITS MUN.
There is a respective society for each branch.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
I don't know much about fests, not bothered about them, but there are like 2-3 of them held regularly every year. 
They introduce new books as per demands and inform the students or staff via mail. So yeah, the library is one of the best things in our college. 
We have online attendance for which a tablet has been installed. Also, we have a projector using which the professor teaches us most of the time as it's rather hard to see what's written on boards most of the time.
We have inter-hostel and inter-IITD sports meets every year. And there are many clubs in which students take part along with their academic studies. 
BSW, Board of Student Welfare, is one such run by students group. It's for first-year students to help them familiarize themselves with the new environment of the institute.I wanted to get into IITD so I had to compromise with the course so ended up with chemical engineering. 
The professors are pretty knowledgeable. And they would suggest books through which we could develop our ideas and thoughts. 
Exams are held over a regular period of nearly 40 days and they are pretty easy to pass as long as you regularly attend class and do assignments.

NIT warangal is one of the top NITs in India and it comes under tire 1 college. There is a ratio of 1:20 between the faculty and the students. All faculties are the best faculty in India. National Institute of Technology Warangal is the most strict college compared to other NITs and IITs getting good grades is little difficult than other colleges.There are many fests all over the year but two big fests are cultural and technical fests. these are the main fests of the college other than a few common fests such as poojas. one can say that all over the month at least one feast will be done in college.
there is a big library sports grounds and a good atmosphere.

The campus life is very great in LPU. Everyday you can find different events happening in LPU. One INDIA, one world and SPECTRA are three annual events of LPU. Coke Studio, Techfest,
You get tons of organizations to join, large number of events to participate, organist and enjoy. Large number of leisurely places, be it central vista or Mall or playgrounds or gyms or swimming pools or indoor stadium or skating rink or nets to practice. You will find a full packed library of 5 floors and can find books of all interest.If you are a engineering student you will never be happy with your curriculum but yes the course curriculum is quite average here. Practical knowledge lacks in their curriculum. The teacher student ratio is about 1: 80. The faculties are well qualified. You will find many faculties as Ph.D. holder. LPU has hell lot of timely exams. They do have three to four continuous assessment test in a semester, assignments, project, mids and end-semester. The exams are moderate. You can easily get good grades if you study.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Fees structure here is very good. I also received a scholarship for tuition fees. Every student gets some kind of scholarship. Fees are paid on the basis of category and family income. Those under-scheduled castes and tribes get scholarships from schools.The course faculty here are very good. They teach in such a way that we understand in one go. They are amazing, and their style of teaching is very good. Proff teaches very nicely such that all students get to know every chapter clearly.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
The annual cultural fest is Rendezvous which is a four-day event. The annual tech fest is 'Tryst'.
These two fests are generally held in continuation,i.e. one after the other.
IIT Delhi has a huge and 'rich-in-literature'  library, where books on various engineering and non-engineering topics can be found.The course curriculum is very interesting and that too being taught by highly-qualified faculty members is an awesome experience.
There is approximately one professor per 100 students.
The exams were held two times in the semester, one in the mid-semester and one as the final exam.

The campus is okay. They have an auditorium, ground, canteens and everything. 
They have all the social groups from arts to drama. You can find all your interests. 
Sports teams are also good here. 
The library is also there. 
No wifi on the whole campus you have to have your own internetFees are 135000 per year. 
You can apply for an up scholarship if you fulfil the given criteria. There are no on-campus jobs. 
You have to pay your fees on yearly basis. 
If you apply for a scholarship, the administration is quite helpful.

The computer Science course is in one of the most demanded and versatile courses available in the university. This course appeals to my area of interest and job scope.
The faculty-to-student ratio is about 1:70 depending upon the branch.
All the faculty are Ph.D. and teach with years of experience and in a way that is easy and comprehensible for students.
The Mid sem and End-Sem exams have a gap of 1-1.5 months. The semester feels short and so do the holidays.The two main cultural fests of NSUT are Moksha and Resonance which are held in summer and winter respectively.
The library has a plethora of books on all subjects. However, issuing books is cumbersome. The library guard does not allow one to enter with their own books.
The APJ lecture halls have smart boards, AC, good lighting, and foldable seats. There are also separate charging sockets available at each seat.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Rendezvous is the Cultural feet and Tyrst is the tech feat. The library is pretty big. The classroom is very active. People are very active in sports. I am an active member of the debating club. There are all kinds of clubs here.The placements are in the 7th semester. The companies include Quadeye, Samsung Korea, Mc Kinsy, and Texas instrument. The highest package was around 1.4 CR. The average placements are around 14 lakhs. I plan to be a professor.

The campus is around 300 acres and it's fulfilled with greenery. We have a private beach it's very nice. The area around the college is very peaceful.
Our college conducts an annual fest incident and one tech fest Engineer. Our library is very big so many books are there. The classroom of our college is very big and well furnished. In college, we have 2 basketball courts 2 volleyball courts 1 cricket ground 1 football ground.4 badminton court 2 kabaddi court and sports faculties is very nice 
Our college websites IRIS is run by our college Seniors and many clubs are there whose run by studentsCollege Fees are 157000 per year college management takes fees per sem. Our college fees are not high compared to other IITs. The college provides financial assistance scholarships for sc, st students and some OBC and EWS students took benefit from college fees 
National scholarship program NSP is a central government scholarship scheme there are many types of scholarships we can take

Here at HIT, campus life is decent. 
It has a good infrastructure, laboratories are great and well-maintained.
The library has plenty of books and students can issue them. Sports facilities are also good.
The college has many social groups and clubs, some are Rotaract for rotary, ghungroo ( dance club),  and many more.The course curriculum is good. 75 %Attendance is mandatory. The end semester exam has 70% weightage and the rest  30% comprises the class test, attendance, viva, and assignment.
The faculty members are great and helpful. The professor is very knowledgeable.

The course curriculum is completed autonomously. I choose CSE because I want to become a coder. Student to faculty ratio is a single teacher in 50- 60 students class. The quality of teaching is very good. Qualification of the faculty is also great.
All the exams are comparatively harder gate-level questions are given in the semester papers.No annual fest in the last 2 years. Tech fest also doesn't take place. The library is the best rather than any other college in Kolkata. Amenities in the classroom are up to the mark. Sports and extracurricular activities take place on a regular basis. There are many clubs which you join and show your skills.

From the 5th semester college will provide internships and we are eligible for internships when we don't have any backlog. There are many it and core companies which provide internships in our college campus interview and the stipend will be 10k to 50k.There is no such kind of fee hike. The fee hike will be directed by the ministry of education west Bengal, then our fees will hike. There is many types of scholarship is provided by the government to the girls or the topper of the class.

college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]
review_text:
Due to covid conduction of the fest is declined, but it's rising slowly as students are actively participating in college events. There is medium size Library, AC classrooms an LRC(learning resource Centre), Sports Complex.
Taking about clubs there is the IEEE club, AASF club, Manchtantra, Ecell, Uthaan, and Rotaract. All clubs have much importance in their own way.From Computer Science, branch students get Pre Placement offer from the third year and Placement in 4th year. From Mtech and MBA branch, students get PPO in 4th year and a Placement opportunity in the fifth year. Around 90-100 per cent of students get placements from our college.

my course is computer science engineering and according to its placement I selected this course because I am interested too much in it
main faculty and guest faculty both are there.
hod and top faculty are best in their qualification and also guest faculty are passed from IIT and have mtech and phdSo about the fee, I would like to say it's a bit costly as other govt colleges are much more cost-effective but this JAC Delhi college is not.
college + hostel+mess for my course is 12-13 lakh for 4 years which is costly and for scholarships, there is one merit-based that is available for everyone

So I got rank 78000. And in this rank mechanical seemed the best choice as I wasn't passionate about coding. After coming to college I came to know that mechanical is best in terms of placement infrastructure faculties. All faculties are Ph.D. from IITs. Labs are not that standard but management is working on improvement. 
There are two mid-semester and one end-semester exam each semester. The semester starts from December after covid till April ( 4 months).1st year exams are easy to pass but from 2nd-year the difficulty increases. Professors also don't give numbers in exams.Adhyay which is a cultural fest has a history of celebrities like Rahul Jain, Yasser Desai, Rahul Dua, Kumar Vishwas, Gajendra Purohit, etc. Also last year Ojha sir came here. Sports fest Aagaz, technical fest technicka, and also E cell conducts e summit which invites various entrepreneurs and alumni of this college.
Clubs like ASCPC, PSMSC, Aeroclub, Auto rob and singing and dancing clubs conduct various events each month. So great campus life. There is also a gym here in the west campus.

Overall course Fees are 8.94 Lakhs for the 2021 batch and every year it increases by some amount for a new batch. To those who can't afford this much fee, there is also a scholarship called CVSPK which is given to some specific students who full fills the required conditions or you can also avail of an education loan from SBI or UNION bank both banks have their branches in NSUT campus because of which it becomes easy for students to get a loan. If you are going to pay your college fee on a loan then must check the loan amount bracket  (up to which you can't give security ) by SBI ( only if you gonna take the loan from SBI ). 
Campus Jobs is good You can check the previous year's placement record.From the past 2-3 years, the internships opportunity decrease in NSUT. Right now I am in 2nd year and don't know very much about internship records but for step interns, some companies came in NSUT like UBER, GOOGLE, and many more. But many companies specifically allow step interns or internships only for girls and Pwd students.

The fee for one year is 2.2lac and it increases by 20 -25 thousand per year, and scholarship is also available there are two types of scholarship one is cvpsk which is provided by the college for economically weaker students and the second is e-district which provides by Delhi government.Campus life is very nice campus size is also good and around 40+ society that covers all types of extracurricular activities from technology to dramatic, and the largest fest moksha is the annual cultural fest which organizes in the end of March, and sports facilities are also well good.

i chose mechanical as my goal was always to be a civl servant and not a mnc puppy 
mechanical engineering was not my desired branch i wanted ece so badly but being a general candidate in this country is as tough as being a boy in a female parlour but never the less whatever happens its for the good
engineering me ratio bhi badhiya hai inka 1:5 i.e. one girl to 5 boys 
faculty and teachers of this college are highly qualified individuals and they are very polite in every aspect of teaching.
exams are mains mid sems and endsems along with 2 class tests held in every semester and as far as difficulty is concerned it always depends on the examinee and how much he/she had studied for it but as far as as i am concerned exams are mostly based on what the teachers are teaching during the class so if one is attentive in the class during lectures he/she will be good to score decent marks in exams.fees is acceptable considering a college of this level and its definitely worth it including the infrastructure and teaching style labs and all o ther co curricular things taken inti cinsideration>
in order to educate economically weaker section as well as other under privileged kids college provides different types of scholarships and all completely merit based .

There are two cultural fests Adhyaay and aarambh, sports fest Aagaz, technical fests Technica, and e summits, that are conducted here. Last year celebrities Yasser Desai, Rahul Jain, Rahul dua, ojha sir, Asim Azhar, and dz bands have performed here. The library is one of the oldest in India. You will all the necessary books here. For sports, there is great scope in cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, kabaddi, chess, etc.
There are clubs like aeroclub as CPC (which connects alumni) psmsc auto rob gruv(dancing) and octave (singing). There is also a good coding community.From the 7th semester, students are eligible for placements. Companies like Reliance, Samsung, cognizant, Paytm, Airtel, lnt, Asian paints, aarti industries, and also some multinational companies visit here. Last year's highest package of 55/pa and the lowest was 3.5lpa. was given. The highest was from CSE. In CSE and IT all eligible students get placed. For chemical technology from 70 to 90 percent of students gets placed. In electrical most students opt for higher studies. In mechanical 95 percentage students got placed.
I am planning to give a gate as well as a cat.

I chose this course coz this college provides great placements for chemical technology. The course curriculum is not so updated but it is satisfying as it offers a wide carrier scope for the future. The faculty-student ratio is quite good. Most faculties are PhD holders from Iits and some are from reputed institutes in India. There are two mid-semester and one end-semester examination that happen in the interval of one month each. First-year exams are easy to pass but from next year difficulty increases in terms of hard checking as well as questions.Campus life is very good and there is a great atmosphere among the students of our college. It involves several sports club cultural activities for exp. adhyaay, aarambh, aagaz, technika etc. Last year celebrities like yasser desai, Rahul dua, and Rahul dua have come here along with poets including aseem Azhar.
There are various clubs like ASCPC, PSMSC, Aeroclub, Autorob, gruv(dancing) and octave(singing). There is also a coding community here.

There are many fests like Adhyaay is a cultural fest technika is a technical fest and again is a sports fest which was held in every year in the month of December and January.  There is a college library for the books there you can get all the books related to your course or running in your branch. And you can rent the books from the library.  There are 2 boards in the class with luxury seats and fans there is also a smart board in the class for better studies and presentation and AC is also there are some new classes. There Is a badminton court, ground,  and Volleyball court basketball court in the college and trainers are also there to teach you. In every week there are programs or competitions held for the students in the college and programs related to the festivals are also held. Callidus, ascpc,  autorob,  Aero club, dramatic club,  octave,  psmsc,  kalakash, and many other clubs are also there. Which was run by the students or members of the clubs.I had chosen this course to get better knowledge about computers and coding and there are 40:75 BTW faculty and students ratio. Faculty must be graduated from B. Tech and from respective branches. They teach us perfectly and deeply with all clear basics knowledge he teaches us with theirs know note and they also share notes of every topic relates to the syllabus .exams are held in every one or 2 months. There are 2 mid sems and 1 end sem in one semester. The Mid-semester is about 1hrs and 2:30 hrs for the end sem.  There is a moderate paper for students it depends upon the faculty member how he wants to make a paper. The end sem is of 50 marks which are divided into 6 sections 5 sections contain 8 marks for each and 10 marks for other sections.  Mostly you have to solve the previous year's papers.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
College life is extremely chill. You will hardly study for the course you have chosen.
Annual and Tech Fest are usually conducted during Springs. They provide you with a lot of activities to deal with.

The campus life at Delhi Technological University (DTU) is a mix of academic rigor and extracurricular activities. 
- Social Life: DTU has a vibrant social life with a diverse student population from all over India. The university has a number of student-run clubs and organizations that offer opportunities for students to socialize and get involved in various activities. There are also several events and festivals(ENGIFEST, YUVAAN, etc) that are organized throughout the year, providing students with the opportunity to have fun and make new friends.
- Clubs: DTU has a variety of clubs, including technical clubs, cultural clubs, and sports clubs. These clubs provide students with the opportunity to pursue their interests and develop new skills. Some of the popular clubs at DTU include the Robotics Club, Music Club(Madhurima)a , Dramatics Club(Pratibimb), Dance club (VIBE and Nrityangana), and Sports Club. There are Tech and Business oriented clubs at DTU with huge number of accomplishments.The placements at Delhi Technological University (DTU) are quite good. DTU has a dedicated placement cell that works closely with companies to provide students with opportunities for internships and full-time employment. The placement percentage at DTU is consistently high, with a large number of students securing job offers from leading companies each year. In recent years, the placement percentage has been over 85% for most programs. DTU has a strong industry network and attracts a large number of recruiters from reputed companies across a variety of sectors, including IT, finance, consulting, and manufacturing. Some of the top recruiters include Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Flipkart, and McKinsey. In the 2022 session, 795 offers were made with packages between INR 10 LPA and INR 20 LPA. Highest package INR 1.2 Cr p.a.

pravaah
prarambha
convocar.

Campus life is satisfying not so exciting and not so boring. Adequate campus space for chilling and time passing. The ground is medium type. Cricket grounds and football all are on one pitch. college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text:
In BTech mechanical, we have a lot of things to do and better placement opportunities.
And our student ratio is quite good.
And our faculty is well educated and highly qualified and teaching methods are very good.
Our term exams were a little bit tricky but If you regularly attend a class you definitely maintain a good cgpa.In our university institute of Engineering and Technology Kurukshetra, our fee criteria are term-wise.
And our total cost of the study is near about 87000 per year.
There are a lot of scholarships in our college and we also organized some events where we help with financial issues.

The campus life is great as the infrastructure of the college is among the top NITs.There is one cultural fest Blitzschlag and one tech fest Sphinx organized annually in college. There are various clubs that engage the students in their free time helping them in their development.I was keenly interested in Electronics and thought, I can make a career through it. Faculties are well qualified and had done their M.Tech Ph.D. from foreign and IITs.The course is divided into semesters, each semester is of 6 months.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text: College provides on-campus internships for 3rd-year students. Companies like Google, and Microsoft hire students for software development and give a stipend of 100000/month. For frontend and backend development internships learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript is necessary and also you have to make a project in it.The campus is 100 Acres in area. The campus life is nice. The campus has a library which more than 1000 books of different genres. The college has different clubs for different activities like art, coding, and sports club. The classrooms are big. The college has a website also.

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text:
The faculty in the college is well qualified. Most of the teachers have a doctorate in their respective fields. The mechanical engineering department has a total of 60 seats in the first year and the Computer Science Electronics and Communication engineering departments have 120 seats each.Placements usually start from the 6 semesters and go on till the 8th semester.
Various companies such as Bajaj, Tata, and Infosys visit the campus during the placements.
Average packages for mechanical engineering Rain from 6 to 8 lacs per annum.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: The placements of this college are impressive. This college is known for its placement with an average package of around 12 to 13 lakhs and the highest package of 1.15 Cr. Around the top 20 % of students get placed in top MNCs such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon, De-Shaw, Intuit, Jio, etc. with high packages ( of above 15 LPA ). Rest get placed in mass recruiters and other service-based companies like Wipro, TCS, etc.I chose this course because I was interested in this course. There is a good faculty: student ratio of about 1:15. There are some very good teachers while some are average, overall, there is a good study environment. There are two unit tests, mid-term vivas, and labs, and end-term exams. Exams can be very well aced in one month of preparation.

The annual fest will be conducted in march (vitopia). Due to covid, it was not conducted for the past two years. The technical fests will be conducted frequently. we can have a peaceful atmosphere in the library we can find any book regarding the subject. Their social life of vit was quite nice.I have opted for the option of computer science and engineering. The student and faculty ratio is about 30:1 .all faculties are with high experience and well-trained faculties. The teaching way is unique which makes students listen. Three Exams were conducted during a semester.

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text:
Life on Campus is unforgettable and most enjoyable.
Many fests happen in the college and the biggest fest is IGNUS, the socio-cultural fest of the college. Prometeo is the technical fest of the college.
all the fest generally starts in September and ends in March.
Many books and journals are available in the library. 
Lots of clubs and societies are there to showcase your nonacademic talent.Students are eligible for placement from sem 7 i.e. from the 4th year. 
Companies like PayPal, Deutsche Bank, Microsoft, De Shaw, etc. have visited the campus and have made many offers.
The highest package was 44 LPA while the average package was about 29 LPA.
generally, all students get placement and rarely very few are eliminated.

The nascent fest happens every year for freshers & tech fests also happen every year in tech fest students get to know and participate in technical challenges like hackathons AR/VR challenges etc.
All books available in the library whatever you need for study library is very well established and very large the environment of the library is very silent and there is a facility of digital library in which students can do the PC related work their Own Laptop isn't allowed in the library also no lab work can be done inside library no assignments can be written in library.
Well-established amenities are provided in the classroom like there is a projector through which the teacher teaches well lightening is provided and seating arrangements are also provided as per the student count.
Sports and extracurricular activity can be expected at the time of the fest there is competition among inter-departments students, a basketball ground is provided two football ground is provided one main canteen situated in the college.I wanted to do Engineering after my diploma and after the diploma, very few good colleges take lateral entry. As heritage is a very reputed college in West Bengal that's why I decided to choose this college because here coding culture is very fantastic.
Around 90-95% faculties are Ph.D. holder so you can expect good faculty for your depart mostly in IT, and CSE and the student ratio is also good every student attend classes every day.
As most of the faculty members are Ph.D. Holders that's why they have very good teaching experience and they teach very nicely our IT department's HOD is very kind I can not explain her kindness in words she supports her department students in every possible way 
Term papers are not very tough if you give a little effort every day in your semesters then easily you can have an 8-9 CGPA in the semester.
Heritage also gives awards(medals) to the topper students at the time of the 8th semester result declaration time, the farewell ceremony can be expected at college.

I chose the course because CSE is in high demand in today's world and BMSCE is very well known for its CSE Department the placements of the CSE Department are the highest here and are very good compared to other colleges. The faculty-student ratio is also very good. Each and every faculty has done MTech or Ph.D. Many of them are from NITs, IIITs, and IISC. Exams are easy to pass and they are conducted as frequently as twice a monthPlacements are excellent at BMSCE students get placed from the 5th semester onwards. Some companies which visit include Infosys, Intel, hp, Cisco, etc. The highest package will be 40 to 50 LPA each year and the average package is around 9lpa.
In CSE the placements are almost 100% but considering all branches it's around 80%.
I am planning to do a job after my degree.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
IIT Roorkee has one of the most beautiful campuses.
There are various cultural as well as technical groups.
Thomson is the annual cultural fest and Cognizance is the technical fest of IIT Roorkee.
For sports, there is a ground for each sport like hockey, football, volleyball, badminton, squash, and cricket.One can choose the course based on their JEE Advanced rank.
The faculty: student ratio varies for different courses.
Professors are well-qualified.
Term exams are moderately to tough depending on the courses and professors. If someone is consistent enough he/she can easily score good grades.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: The tuition Fee structure at our college is around 1.4 lakh per for general/obc students whose family income is greater than 5 lacks and for students whose family income is between 1 to 5 lakh, two third of the total tuition fee is required and no tuition fees are required for students whose family income is less than 1 lakh or sc/st students. The scholarship is also provided by the college for poor people and good in academics.Yes, in our college every year a fest is organized, branch wise fest is also organized every year. In the library, you find lots of books. The classroom is also well maintained and in our college two big grounds, a gym, a swimming pool, a basketball court, and a volleyball court as well. In our college, different clubs are also there you find different clubs like music for the euphony club, dance for reflex beta club.

The fee hike policy is constant for 4-5 years depending upon the government rules and regulations and the cost of study here for the 4 years B.Tech is around 8 Lakhs 
Students get UP scholarships from here and 
Simens scholarship for economically weaker students  and students get jobs on campus with campus placement with a stipend of 25000 to 30000 per monthFrom the 7th semester, students are eligible for campus placement
There are 60 students eligible for placement in the class of 70
Most of the companies visited for campus placement Like tata motors, hero, reliance, Exxon Mobil, etc
The placement ratio is good 80% of students got placement here 
My plans after getting the degree is to higher studies

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: IIT Delhi attracts the attention of the best recruiter in town! You'll get enough opportunities to get into any field you like it may or may not be the field your major is in. All tech and non-tech companies that have their operations in India do visit the campus plus quite a few international companies also seek to take the unparalleled talent of IIT Delhi to their headquarters! Almost everyone who sits in the placement bags an offer. The highest package goes to 2cr+ while the average being around 15-20lacIIT Delhi boasts to host the best fest in north India which is Rendezvous. The social life however depends more on the individual but still, it is the most exciting and vibrant experience one could have in his lifetime. Classrooms, libraries, sports ground, and cultural and technical clubs are well equipped with all the facilities and provides a host of opportunities for every student on campus.

In year 2021 fee structure is :
1st year  206000,
2nd year  219000,
3rd  year 229000,
4th year   235000,
and every year for new students fees constantly increase.
there are many scholarships provided by colleges as well as government:
1. merit cum scholarship,
2. category wise scholarship.
you can apply to any company like tech, nontech, core, etc depending on your skills. the stipend is different for each company there are 300+ companies who come for fresh requirements during 3 and 4 years.Engifest is the biggest annual fest of DTU and there is more fest like aarambh and homecoming for freshers. most of the society of college organize their separate fest also.
a huge central library within the campus with more than 100000+ books.
every year there is an annual sports competition between branches.
Cultural counselling(management society), lets talk DTU ,vibes(western dance society), prathibimb(drama society),
madurima( music society) and more society.

I had chosen Btech in Information Technology because I had a deep interest in coding, software related stuff and wanted to make my career in that field.
Faculty to student ratio is not up to the mark there is always a shortage of teachers and a lack of good-quality teachers. Most of the faculty are Guest Faculty. Permanent Faculty and Good Quality faculties are limited.
Some faculties are highly qualified which reflects in their teaching method also but there are some faculties also who are highly knowledgeable but they are not able to deliver it to the students. There are some of those faculties also who just teach to fulfilling their job, they don't have deep knowledge.
Till last year there used to be 1 mid-semester exam per semester but to make the examination process more disciplinary from session 2022, there are 2 mid-semester exams which means 4 mid sem exams and 2 end-semester exams in a year.
Scoring marks here in hbtu is quite easy with a decent level of study. Marking is student-friendly.Our technical fest is Technika, our Sports fest Is Aagaaz and our biggest fest is our cultural fest Adhyaay. Before Covid, they were organized in the months of November and December but post covid they were organized in the months of May and June.
The library of Hbtu is the biggest with 10000 books of all genres. All subject books are available in our library.
Our classrooms are provided with smart boards some classes have air conditioners also but they don't work( government college for a reason).
Sports activities are not up to the mark on campus but extracurricular activities are top notch including poetry, drama, quizzes, art, etc.
There are various clubs being run by students for each domain be it coding, robotics, dance, singing, etc. There are councils and societies which are managed by students. Their respective social media handles are there on Instagram, Twitter, etc.

Electronics and communication provide good opportunities in software industries as well as hardware industries and there are recent investments in chip building and the semiconductor sector which will increase job opportunities in this sector. Government services are also there to cater to the needs of Electronics engineers. Approximately 1:60 is faculty: student ratio in most departments Faculties are well experienced and educated have high qualifications, and mostly have post-doctoral fellowships and 5+ years of experience in research. Teachers are really supportive and whatever they teach in class is very relevant for end-semester examinations. One can ace the examination if he/she is really attentive in class. Exams are held timely, mainly two types of exams happen in one semester i.e. mid-sem and end-sem. End-semester examinations have high weightage than mid-sem exams.Fests occur in January. The Central Library is available on the campus and will be fully air-conditioned all the Books are available in the library. Sports and events occur frequently. Many clubs are available on campus.

The Annual Technical and Cultural Fest of the college, Corona, and Melange are held in the months of January- February. Besides, Eccentric is a cultural event hosted in the month of November. Other than these, numerous other events are organized in the college from time to time. Libraries are well-maintained and furnished with all course content and more. Classrooms are well-maintained too. There are various clubs and societies in the college catering to all sorts of technical and cultural feeds. Students in these clubs actively organize numerous events in the college from time to time too.Electronics and Communication Engineering is one of the most dynamic departments of the institute with excellent faculty and ample resources and opportunities accessible to students. The faculty is highly trained and the student: faculty ratio is also commendable. Term exams take place per the academic calendar and for every semester we have a Mid-Sem exam of 30 marks and an End-Sem, including Viva and practicals of 70 marks. There is a total of 8 semesters as per the curricula of any engineering college.

Our campus is more about academics not so much enjoyable. There are two fests in my college Corona and Millenge, which are very intersting.
There is a Central library in my college which contains about 75000 thousand books in different fields. It contains general and different categories of books.
There is a big Ground which is helpful for Cricket, Football, and Volleyball. There is a badminton court and music clubs.
There is a different clubs in my college including Technical (Hackslash, GDSC, Coding Club), Nontechnical clubs like saptak, IEEE, Robotics club, etc.There are nice placement and internship oppurnuties in my college after 2020. Companies come from the third year so you can apply from the third year onwards.
Big companies like Google, Amazon, Atlassian, Microsoft, Facebook, Goldman Sachs, Infosys, L&T, and lots (approx more than 100 companies ), and especially for CSE students get 100 % placement. another branch student also gets a good offer.

The highest package is 160 crore CTC Facebook 
Average package 16LPA.
I will Join an SDE role in a product-based company.

Tech Fest in recent months: ECCENTRICA 2022,
In Library almost all the books are available that are required for our courses and can take 4 books at one time and the max day to return is 14 days.
The classrooms are good but look old, giving school vibes.
There are often sports activities and other activities conducted, like cricket, badminton, chess, online games, kabaddi, etc.
Some clubs are Natvansh, Hackslash, GDE, etc.Students are eligible for campus placement after 6 sem. There are many companies that come for placement like Goldman Sach, Facebook, Infosys, L&T, etc. This year there are 110% offers that will come to our college campus. In the CSE dept, all students were placed and overall 70% of students were placed. I want to Take a placement in the IT sector.

The CSE curriculum at the Malaviya National Institute of Technology(MNIT) Jaipur generally includes courses in the following areas:
1 Programming and Data Structures
2 Computer Organization and Architecture
3 Algorithms
4 Theory of Computation
5 Data Structures and Algorithms and many more
I chose this course because I liked computers from my childhood and that's why I opted for science
 The student ratio is about 1:15 which is good (For one semester)
The teaching method at MNIT Jaipur is a combination of lectures, tutorials, lab sessions and practicals. The faculty members use various teaching aids such as slides, videos and demonstration experiments to explain the concepts to the students. In addition to regular classroom teaching, the faculty members also provide additional help to the students through office hours and special classes.
The term exams are usually held at the end of each semester and cover the syllabus taught during that semesterThe annual fest at MNIT Jaipur is called "Pragyaan" and is usually held in the month of February. The tech fest at MNIT Jaipur is called "Techspardha" and is usually held in the month of March. Life at MNIT Jaipur is generally quite vibrant and active
There are several clubs at the institute including the Music Club, Photography club, Literature Club and sports Club. These clubs organize various activities and events throughout the year
The social life is quite active, with students participating in a variety of cultural and sporting events.
The infrastructure is modern and well-maintained. The institute has a number of well-equipped classrooms, labs and workshops that cater to the student's academic needs. The campus also has a well-stocked library, a sports complex and a number of hostels for students who live on campus

They were all amazing. Campus life has been great with 2 big canteens along with many other canteens. Very spacious for students to hang out and roam, and huge gardens are also there. All the cultural festivals are held and conducted on campus. The name of the fest is blitzslag held around Jan or Feb. The library is very well maintained with a large database. Sports events are conducted occasionally. Sports like football, basketball, volleyball, and cricket are played. Many workshops are conducted. And different social-related activities are also done.The placements are good and it is getting better as every company like amazon, divert, oracle, and adobe visits for placement opportunities. Talking of internships the College provides an opportunity to get into different companies and also they provide industrial visits. In sum, the experience is more than satisfactory. Placement and job opportunities are 80 % among the students who clear all the subjects without any back. More than 600 companies are coming every year which includes subject core, IT , sales and marketing, and product companies.

review_text:
Three fests

Almost 100 per cent of students are placed.

fests over here are very beautiful and posh we can enjoy to full of us. almost every book needed by students would be available in the library. classrooms are very huge and wide but they are old
sports are also very much encouraged over here
There are many clubs like AI AND RIG and also cultural clubsgenerally, fees here for general students are 67500 per semester. sc/st/pwd/below 1 lakh would be getting exemption from it and have to pay 4500. many students apply for various kinds of scholarships like nsp,fee

The campus is very good we all enjoy the campus and our entertainment they organise every type of performance and celebrities come to Galgotia University.

The qualification of a faculty teacher is also very good.
In one semester there is three exams conducted at the university. In my view not very tough to pass the examination.

I have to complete my dream.
student:teacher ratio is 10:12
Qualifications are in 12th you need to score 85% and all general knowledge like current affairs and teaching methods is overall excellent all classes are computerised classes.
There are semester-wise exams and not any type of exams.Yes fees are to high every course has its own fees but according to fees you have got facilities if you got placement in any company you earn a minimum of 2 to 3 lakh in a month so fees are good.
Many scholarships are allotted in college but I don't get any so I don't know about it.

Life is pretty fun at Dtu you get to be whatever you want to be. From artists, singers, musicians, dancers, or whatever. The most famous place in Dtu is the oat (open-air theatre.  All small fests are organized here all the singing performance, dancing, and comedy shows are performed here , engifest the most awaited fest of Delhi it's is the largest fest in Delhi very famous singers, comedians come in engifest there are around 150000 people who come in engifest.I choose this course because I have interest in production engineering since school time, the faculty in this course is good and bad also some teachers are very rude and don't teach anything about the subject and on the other hand, most teachers are good and highly qualified and they teach very effectively and nicely that the student can understand easily what they are teaching, there are two exams taken the first midterm and second end term exam.

I was fascinated and focused on technology, coding, and development, so decided to pursue engineering in a computer-related stream to work and learn under my dream companies. In NUST, all the staff are properly managed and very good at working and interacting giving a good healthy environment for us. A 10:2 student ratio (approx.) is there. All the faculty members are highly qualified and have expertise in their respective subjects. All the classes are equipped with smartboards with a big lecture hall for the 1st years with all kinds of labs for research and experiment purposes. All the classes help with a practical class in a week giving us some practical knowledge of what we have learned till now. Some subjects are a little harder than other subjects and extensive reading of books and practice will be required to score good marks.We got a very large campus of 145 acres with trees, a park, a gym, grounds, and more with exciting events happening all year around. Big events consisting of games, coding competitions, concerts, and more are held, a recent event was our MOKSHA. We go to a very big library consisting of every book you want and can be issued with your ID card. The classroom is equipped with proper wide desks and chairs with projectors and a smartboard for effective learning. Various sports events with proper equipment are available. There are several societies run by students like GDSC (google developer student club), DebSoC (for debating),IEEE , ARES (robotics society),Nakshatra (astronomy club),and many more

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
Cse is the best course at this college.
Having separate labs with fully functional computers for different courses.
All faculty members are highly qualified with M.Sc from IITs or other renowned universities.
Faculty members take tests and term exams periodically to test the ability of students and exams are of good difficulty.There are 3  fest in college named - Vivacity (Cultural), Plinth (Tech fest), and Desportivos (Sports fest).
All these fests happen in Jan and Feb.
There is a big library in the college for students.
We have all types of sports in our college from kabbadi to squash.
there are more than 30 social and sports clubs.

The course curriculum is pretty good. I chose the computer science course because I have an interest in computer science. The faculty-student ration is good,  there are enough professors. Most of the teachers are PhDs. Their teaching methods are pretty good. The exam level is good but you can pass easily If you study well.College placement is good. We become eligible for placements in 3rd year. Famous companies visit colleges like Microsoft, Google, Samsung, etc. The highlights package is more than 1cr and the average package is 15 lakhs. Almost 60 % of students get placed. I will prepare for placements after getting a degree

Life of NSUT is really chill the whole Semester, It just becomes a little hectic when exam comes that too for those students who have not studied for the whole Semester.
There are many societies to enhance your likings and make new friends. Societies like Shakesjear, Kalpana, FES, FINSOC etc
Most of the books are available in the library
Every sports ground is there in the college and college will provide you the equipments for the same

Curriculum is not that good, 75% attendance is must otherwise you won't be allowed to sit in the exams.
Some faculties are good, some are very bad.
It totally depends on your luck.
I have chosen this course because it has the best placement stats
Most of the teachers have done their PhDs
They all are highly educated.
There are only two main exams..mid term and end term exam....it is really easy to pass the exams not a big deal in it

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text: The course curriculum is up-to-date and industry-required. We also have the flexibility to choose minors, specializations, electives, etc, in domains in which we are interested. Almost all the faculty of the department have Ph.Ds. from IITs or some foreign universities.  The teaching method is alright. Some teach from slides, while most of them use to project the tab/iPad screen and write on it(well, class strength demands a projector). For each semester, we have 2 minor tests (1 hour duration) and a major test at the end(2 hours), Quizzes, assignments, and tutorials happen from time to time. As it is relative grading,  the chances of failing a course are very less(sometimes, maybe more, depending on the professor).  Some may feel the academic load is more, but it isn't anything unbearable, just keep yourself up to date in all courses.Well, The Tuition fee for each semester is 1,00,000. We have to pay around 27k as a semester fee which includes room rent, laundry, and medical. etc. charges, aside from that we have to pay a mess fee, which is around 128 per day (around 3840 per month).  Almost all IITs have similar fee structures. SC /ST and also students whose family income is less than 1,00,00 per year don't need to pay the tuition fee and students having family income between 1 lakh and 5 lacs have to pay only 33% of the tuition fee. We have Canara Bank and SBI branches inside the campus itself where we can avail of the education(scholar) loan.

college_name: IIT Goa - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text:
I am from the sc category and due to this I did not pay tuition fees but it is good because my family is not able to pay my fees.I took an education loan for my fees submitted.
There are scholarship like NSP which is good and internship is also very good the company gives a maximum of 50000 stipends .and if they like your work they offer you do the direct job in their company.This is the most important course I think because it is a mixture of maths and computers and I love maths but I also like to study computer science that's why I chose this branch. all the faculty is very good they have done their Ph.D. with a very prestigious institute. there is 6 month semester which is not so much difficult to pass out.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: SPARSH is the annual fest of Saradar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology. The name of the tech fest is MINDBEND. They are conducted around March or April. SVNIT has an awesome library with a good number and quality of books. Students prefer a library for study purposes as the environment is good and silent enough to study. You will get a beautiful experience while studying there. The college has nice classrooms with proper facilities except for the old departments of the college. As it is a national institute the sports facilities are amazing. There are basketball courts, Tennis Court, a Badminton court, a gym, etc. for sports and healthcare. There are many clubs in the college such as the literary club named LAC, the Hindi cell, the fun club named CHRD, the technical club ACM and many more. You can find more about the clubs on the official website of SVNIT. svnit.ac.inAfter completing the third year of the course i.e the 6th semester, the student is eligible for sitting in the placement-related exams and the interviews. Awesome companies like Maruti Suzuki, Microsoft, airtel, Bharat Petroleum, J.P.morgan, TATA, Intel, Godrej and many big companies visited the campus for the placements. The highest package of SVNIT is 49.5 LPA and the average package is 8 to 10 LPA. 79% of students are placed in SVNIT. My plans after completing my degree are to take placement.

VIT Chennai is best when it comes to events, and clubs and the infrastructure is also quite good.  There will be a club for each and everything, like a dance club, photography club, trekking club, designers club, music club, dramatics club, event managers club, and many more. In fact, if there isn't one for something you can make one! The library is the one that everyone will like at first sight XD.From 3rd year, students will be eligible to apply for internships and jobs. Many top companies visit every year.  And the placements for Vellore and Chennai campuses are centralized. So, when it comes to placements both Vellore campus students and Chennai campus students will be having equal opportunities.

I have chosen this course because this was the best option I was getting from my rank.
Faculty to student ratio is approx  120/30, which is equal to 4.
The qualifications of faculty members are quite good, as they all have done their Ph.D.
But some of the tutorials which are not taken by the professors are taken by the ph.D. Scholars of mnit Jaipur, who are completing their degree. The style of teaching or we can say the teaching methods are not up to the mark.

For 0-1 lakh income-100%fee waiver on institute fee.

I have chosen this course because it has more scope for the future.

After the completion of the semester, the college collects feedback from the students about the faculty and their performance. 
For a semester we have two internal exams namely CAT-1, and CAT-2 and one final exam FAT. 
CAT(continuous assessment test) for 50 marks and FAT(final assessment test) for 60 marks. These are descriptive examinations and each question carries 8-12 marks. Subjects may be open or closed-book exams. 
To pass a subject we need to score a minimum of 40% of marks combining CAT-1 CAT-2 and FAT.Life on campus is very interesting. We have vast exposure to students. As we have different classmates in a different subjects, we can make many friends.
Our college has 100+ active clubs and a total of 600+ clubs & chapters. We have technical, non-technical, cultural, and different clubs. 
The library is fully air-conditioned with a capacity of 300+ students. We can find more books related to the subject then journals. 
Every classroom has a whiteboard and projector. You cannot find blackboard and chalk anywhere. 
Labs have 32GB RAM-supported desktops. 
The college focuses less on sports than academics. 
Clubs are generally organised by students. Some of them are CSI, ACM, Rotract club, Machine Learning Club, and Data Analytics Club.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text:

33.33 % Off on tuition fees if your family income is less than 6 lakh.

IIT Dhanbad offers you great opportunities via intern, hackathons, events by which if you work hard you will earn a lot and cover your fees in one month.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
IIT Bombay organizes Asia's largest college cultural fest, MOOD INDIGO, and it also organizes Asia's largest college technical festival, the TECHFEST in the month of December. People from all across the world come here to perform in Mood Indigo and showcase their talent like Foreign singers and dancers.

There are various scholarship programs provided by the institution for the weaker section like those whose income is less than 1 lakhs per annum their whole tuition and mess fee are dismissed, and those who have income between 1 lakhs and 5 lakhs have to pay 30% of the total tuition fees. There are various named scholarships also. 
For getting a stipend on campus, you can apply for the job of Teaching Assistant whose work is to help the prof in conducting the exams, helping in experiments, and teaching the batch if the prof is unavailable.

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text:
There are many different kinds of fests held: PROMETEO (Entrepreneurial and Technical fest) conducted in January, VARCHAS (Inter-college sports fest), KRIDAANSH (Inter-hostel sports fest), Aaftaab (Literary fest), conducted in the winter this year, INGUS (Cultural fest), etc.
Yes, books and journals are available in the library and can be issued whenever required.
Classrooms are well-air-conditioned and have projectors and speakers.
Almost every time sports are played on the campus (excluding some, like swimming, polo, etc.). The campus has a large football ground surrounded by a race track, 3 basketball courts, lawn tennis courts, hockey and cricket grounds, and a nice indoor sports complex for badminton, table tennis, and squash. But you are supposed to bring your own sports equipment.
There are various clubs run by students: LITERATURE SOCIETY, THE GROOVE THEORY (Dance), SANGAM (Music), Programming Club, RAID (A.I. Society), FRAMES (Film Making), Game Development Club, Drama Club, etc.The choice of this course was primarily based on ranking in JEE Advanced exam and interest in computer science-based fields. Faculty: student ratio was around 1:200 in the first year. But from the second year onwards, the ratio drastically to 1:120 or 1:60 depending on the common courses. The professors are highly qualified and have done their master's/research / PhDs from renowned institutes in India and around the world.
Teaching method varies from professor to professor. Some have a straightforward way of teaching, while some go rather deep into the subject. It is subjective. The lectures are taught mostly on a projector, where the professors use slides or a writing pad, depending on their choice.
Quizzes are conducted from time to time. And there are 2 minor exams and 1 major exam each semester, an almost duration of time. The exams have sets of both easy and difficult questions. In some courses, there is negative marking also in the exam. There is relative grading.

review_text: I have chosen this course because I want to serve myself as a software engineer in big IT companies and this course is best for it as well as this college provides good placements to students. review_text: we are talking about IIT Delhi here, so there is no doubt that the life at the campus, social life, clubs, and all other co-curricular activities are one of the best in the country. college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
There are various fests organized here. The annual fest is Asia's largest cultural fest called Mood Indigo and we also have TechFest, both of which are conducted in December.
IIT Bombay boasts a great library, you can get almost all course books plus various fiction novels. The library is pretty spacious making it ideal for studying.
The classrooms are really good and there are no such big amenities except their maintenance.
Here you will get a lot of exposure to extracurricular activities and sports since there are many events and fests around the year and we have hostel general championships which encourage students to participate in sports and other events like coding, creative writing, drama, etc.
There are various clubs like Analytics club, and Aeromodelling club, and websites like www.ecell.in.I chose this course since this was the most interesting course I could get at this college at my rank.
The faculty: student ratio does vary but it can go from 1:200 to 1:90 easily. However, the faculty are pretty qualified since many of them have completed their Ph.D. and most of them have great accomplishments being from colleges ranging from Caltech to Harvard.
There is enough gap between the end semesters and mid-semesters but mostly there is no gap between the exams that is the exams happen on consecutive days mostly. Although many of my friends have not just passed but received good grades by just studying a day before the exam. So, if you are consistent from the start it is very easy to pass the exams.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: I chose this course because it was the best that was available at my JEE rank. The student-faculty ratio is quite high due to the recent increment in IIT seats. All the professors are highly trained and hold various degrees like MTech, PhD etc.The annual cultural fest Antaragni is one of the largest fests in Asia. So, is Techkriti, the annual technical festival at IITK. Both Antaragini and Techkriti are conducted in May or April of the year. PK Kelkar library has tons of books with new books getting added every week. Classrooms are equipped with projectors and sound systems.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Campus life is just so great here at IIT Delhi. You make great friends here with whom you spend nights on campus. The campus itself is so beautiful and colorful. IIT Delhi also conducts its Cultural /Annual Fest called Rendezvous and Tech fest called Tryst. Both fests take place around the Feb-Mar-Apr period. The Central Library of IIT Delhi is one of the biggest libraries in India which contains almost any book you need. It also has the Thesis of many students and old texts of great writers. Our classes take place in the Lecture Hall Complex (LHC) which was built only 10 years ago. It has one the most modern classrooms with Charging points, Projectors, a centralized AC system, Speakers, etc. IITD focuses not only on Academics but on Co-curricular activities too. Talking about sports, we have a Hockey ground, Basketball, Volleyball ground, Tennis Lawn, Football Ground, Cricket Ground, SAC (Student Activity Center) which has TT, Chess, etc. We also have Dance, Music, Photography, and DebatingI choose this course because I have a great interest in Electrical Engineering. Faculty: The student ratio is pretty good. You have a great interaction with the professor under the condition that you participate well in the class discussions. Faculties are highly educated having degrees from Foreign Institutes. Some professors may not be able to convey what they want to teach. One of the reasons for this is that these professors are great at their knowledge but weak at conveying that knowledge. From the next semester, IIT Delhi would be starting 2 Minor exam patterns, wherein we would have 2 Mid-semester exams and 1 End Semester Exam along with quizzes and assignments. Passing courses can be difficult if you don't pay attention to Classes carefully.

review_text:

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: There are different criteria for fees based on income. If anyone belongs to a financially backward family, then even he/she can afford it easily. college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Rendezvous is the biggest cultural fest which is conducted in April.

 All Classrooms are decorated with Smart Boards and Projectors.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
