The sports culture is also very strong.Students become eligible for internships in their 5th semester. The OCS conducts all internship activities in the college. The internships can be at home or abroad and also in a variety of domains. The Students work very hard for interns as they are more difficult to crack.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: I choose this course because civil branch at IIT Delhi is no 1 in the country. Faculty: student is about 1:9.all faculty have done Ph.D. from top colleges and come from top universities of us .acedemics of IIT Delhi review the teaching method by floating forms Teo times a semester. term exams are done once or twice a semester. It's difficult to score but due to relative grading, one can get good grades scoring average.Annual and tech fests are conducted generally in April and October months respectively. About one lakhs books and journals are available in the Central Library and a few in respective hostel libraries. the classroom is full ac with sound absorbing materials used .almost all sports facilities are present on the campus. robotics club and Hindi club are some important clubs. BSW is a website for students run by students.

The annual cultural fest is named Blitzslag and it 2nd the largest fest in Rajasthan, top 5 fests in the North after IIT Delhi and BITS GOA. It is organized in the month of Feb but for the past 3 years, it has not been organized due to corona, in 2023, it will be a grand 4-day fest.
There is a central library which consists of almost all books of every kind.
Classrooms are well equipped with full centralized AC and good benches, furniture, soundproof environment.
The playground consists of a football field, a cricket field, and an athletics ground. Opposite sports complex which has 5 badminton courts, 3 basketball courts, 3 volleyball courts, and a shot put field.I chose this course as there are good placements and the course curriculum is way too interactive. There are good internship opportunities also.

All the faculty members are well educated from NIT/IIT and most of them completed their Ph.D. from foreign universities. 
Teaching includes smart board presentations.
You need to study about 7 days prior to mid-term and 15 days prior to end term examination.
Passing exams is not that difficult and you can score really well if you pay attention in class.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text:

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:

The faculty is quite good with the best teachers and a student ration of 65:7. I am not enough qualified to judge the faculty teachers but the teachers are highly educated and mostly all have a Phd degree with great teaching methods. Semester exams were all on time but the difficulty level of the exams are moderate, if one studies through the year it will be easier for him/her.From the 6th semester students become eligible for the campus placements. Mostly all the popular companies visit our campus and offer many packages. Companies like google, microsoft and amazon are popular during the placement season. According to the previous year data almost 100% students were placed in the previous placement session.  My future plan is to join a MNC after completing my B.E. degree at SDE post.

In the first year, students are taught the basics of almost all branches. Special focus is given to programming. Faculty focuses on building the core of the student. I chose this course solely due to the high rising demands of software engineers. A class consists of 65 students on average and there are approx 8 subjects taught in each class. Each subject has a different faculty assigned. Each faculty deals with approx 100 students. Almost all of the faculties assigned are achievers or passed out from high institutions. The teaching method of teachers is refined and particularly focuses on the weak points of the particular student. There are two exams taken in a semester namely Mis Semester Exams and End Semesters Exams. The facility for writing Back Papers is also available. The exams were neither too easy nor too hard.The college tries to offer a decent environment for every type of student. Many facilities are also provided by the college to enhance the skills of the students in the particular sector. An annual fest like Gra Fests or Mini Gra fests is organized by the college. The fests are usually organized in August and September. The college provides a large library of books consisting of books on almost all the topics of Engineering. The classrooms are large and wide. Each classroom is provided with projectors and speakers. The college also has multiple courts: Basketball, Badminton.
The college also has a Cafeteria consisting of multiple games: Table Tennis, Carrom, Snooker, etc. The college also organizes multiple sports events and annual meets. Many clubs offer students a wide variety of exposure apart from studies.

The reason I choose this course is because of the growing IT industry scope.
Faculty-student ratio is very good, faculty details are available on the website of the Jadavpur University Department of Information Technology
The exam system is the same as in all engineering Colleges, There are two semesters in a year, and class tests and internal in each semester.From the 6th sem student are eligible for an internship in various MNCs, and from the 7th sem, place session started, companies like Amazon Flipkart optimum slice, and many other MNCs visits to recruit students, 100% student placed, my plan is becoming an SDE in an MNC.

I choose this course because I love technological things. And the teaching staff of the branch because I heard that the B.Tech teaching staff is so loyal and respectful and livelihoods. There are a minimum of 150 students in our batch. The quality of teaching is highly perfect. And the teachers are so humble and kind-hearted. The exam is so tight but not tough enough because they covered the syllabus timely.All the students work so actively even in real. There are many occasion that happens in a year like freshers, annual function etc. The book is available in the library. The amenities of the classroom are so good. Everybody maintains cleanliness and is well dressed every time. There is a big playground and so many games are present. There are so many clubs like students union club etc.

This course is one of the good courses in university.
There are sufficient faculties present.
All faculties are Ph.D. holders and have good knowledge. Most teachers are good and student-friendly.
Exams happen two times per semester midterm and end term but many quizzes happen during lectures which seems a good thing as this carries a good weightage and makes it easy for students to get good cgpa.For internship from 2 years onwards, you will be eligible and for placement around 6-7 semesters, you will be eligible.
There are many good companies who come for placement in our college and with a good no of job offers.
The highest package is 57 lakh latest and the average is around 10 lakhs.
Around 90 percent placement.

For internships one must have to maintain a good Cgpa for the first 3semester like if have an 8.5+cgpa then one will be eligible to sit for internships.
Most of the tech companies come for sde interns but there are some consultancy and finance-based companies too.
The highest stipend is around 1lakh/month for three months by uber, code nation.
For interns, you must have completed your dsa, oops cn, etc. With your projects, and the project is as important as dsa because they are looking for a developer you have to show your skills there are many different ideas available on the web but you don't have to exactly copy them and improvise them.College life is best than other colleges except for IIT Delhi. Dtu has many societies like Cc which organize Engifest and then there is yuan, assets, and others in a year you will experience 4 to 6 fests in DTU. 
There are around lakhs of books available in our library with good Wi-Fi connectivity and it closed at around 10 pm.
There is an indoor sports hall where you get 2 badminton courts and four table tennis tables. Cricket ground, football ground, kabaddi court, basketball and volleyball court, and lawn tennis. There is a small gym if you are into fitness you have to wake up early because there is a crowd after 7 am.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The ceramic engineering course is very interesting and mostly related to the chemical properties of minerals. It also has a nanotechnology part in the course which has various research-related opportunities.
Faculty here at IIT BHU ceramic engineering are all highly educated Ph.D. holders and very student-friendly.
The term exams are generally very tough in courses like the thermodynamics of ceramics. Relative grading is a boon here.Fees of IIT BHU are divided into 3 sections according to the student's parental income. Parental income Under 1lpa, between 1 and 5 LPA, and at least above 5lpa.
There is no tuition fee for those under the 1LPA section while 1/3 of the tuition fee is for the 2nd section and full for the 3rd section.
IITs are famous for their very good on-campus placement and internships. IIT BHU is among one the top in terms of good placements.

Companies like siemens, google uber and many startups are providing internships to students and the roles are software developers stipend varies from 10 k to 60 k depending on the companies and they get direct PPO if work is good.Placement in my college is very good around 20 lakh per annum is the average salary of tech branches, almost 95% of students are placed every year they get a big companies like amazon, google, sprinkler, ola, uber, and Zomato.

It has a better future in the industry.
Faculty: student ratio is 1:10.
All Faculty are Ph.D. qualified and they are very helpful.
There is theory part is maximum, and practical is also their w.r.t. subjects.
Exams happen in the 1st week of December and 1st week of May.There are so many clubs for helping young people, like Dancing clubs, music clubs, etc.
There is also a department library and a central library on campus.
There is a big playground on the campus where football, cricket, badminton, and basketball players are.

I choose B.tech in civil engineering because I want to be an IES officer and this branch has the highest opportunity for being an IES and its subjects are also very interesting.
The faculties are just OK but some of them are very helpful and talented. Faculties are highly qualified but the method of teaching of mostly all the faculties is not up to the mark . The ratio of faculty to students is 1:70.
There are two mid sems and one end-sem exam and these exams are very easy to pass because the pattern is totally based on past year papers.The campus is good clean and green, but as the college is very old, the buildings are old but there are also many newly developed areas and renovation is in progress.
There are many college fests like Arambh,Adhyay, etc, and many clubs and councils like auto rob, ascpc, etc. also startup cells.
College does not provide any extra effort towards sports, you have to do it yourself but yeah there is a sports fest named Adhyay where you have the chance to prove your skills.

The annual fee is approximately 220000. But there are lots of scholarship opportunities in college. If you belong to SC/ST you can apply for NSP(National Scholarship Portal). There are also Fee waiver scholarships provided by DTU guided by a serious interview by faculties along with your parents based on your financial status. The most common scholarship is the E-District scholarship by Delhi Government which gives a 100 percent waiver on tuition fees if you have a ration card. The loan facility is also available easily by SBI bank in dtu campus.DTU is well known for its chill life, less academic load, and healthy culture. There are lots of clubs like CC(Cultural Council), Innova, and Yuvaan. The main fest is Engifest( one of the largest gathering fest in north India). A lot of revised books are available in the library ranging from various fields and interests. All the academic books you need you will find there. The sports facility is awesome. There is a sports complex with a badminton court, Football court, Basketball court, Cricket ground, Indoor badminton, table tennis, and gym.

I have chosen electrical engineering because of my interest
Faculty and student ratio is approx 1: 30
Maximum faculty members are PhD holders 
And their teaching methods are very good. 
Exams are conducted by the examination committee very timely as per the Academic calendar. 
And exams are always moderately level.The name of the annual fest is - Upadhyay 
And the name of the technical fest is - Technica and aarambh is also a startup-based fest
Classrooms are average, sports and extracurricular activities are also fine 
There are more clubs like e-cell, ASCPC, technical Subcouncil, hobby Subcouncil, etc

There are a lot of fests that are conducted each year in the months of November December February April like the ADHYAAY cultural fest which has a lot of fun singing, dancing, comedy nights, and stand-up comedy. Hige availability of books in the Central Library which is fully air-conditioned and will make you experience Kashmir in summer 4 books are issued to each one.From the 8th semester, a lot of companies visited Oracle, Microsoft, Amazon, Flipkart, SBI, L and T, Tata, Havells, Asian paints, and a lot more. 61.7 lakhs is the highest offer given. 87% of students get placed. To become an IAS officer.

There is also an annual college fest named " Pyrokinesis" which is conducted in the year of April- June. We must have to issue a Library card during the admission period by providing 300 rupees and it is used to issue all the class requirements books from the Library for the whole of the B.Tech course. Classrooms are modern. Sports are held in college week and there are intercollege games. There are so many clubs available, Coding, Robotic, Literature, Art, and many more, etc.Assam Engineering College, Guwahati is famous for its Electrical Engineering Course which provides a minimum placement of 800000 per annum. The Electrical Department faculty is excellent. This branch provides the highest package. Electrical Department we have, 78 faculty, and the teaching mode is modern having a projector and Touchpad TV in every class, with smart classes. There will be taken every class test after every module completion.

The course is boring and the curriculum is out of date. They are teaching us useless subjects in which we don't need any expertise. Teachers are not at all supportive and very aggressive. They will take out personal anxiety on your exam sheets. We have to beg them for a 1-1 mark so that we at least pass. I got decent marks in my external exams but they gave me fewer marks in internals and so I failed.Fees are very affordable for students and ews students can avail the facility of scholarship. Besides that, we receive free medical insurance from the college. Campus jobs are excellent but personally, I don't feel that BTech is worth it after this lot of pressure and tension. I have been diagnosed with depression and this is taking over my mind. Should go for BBA instead.

I chose this course as this was the best course I got according to my WBJEE rank. 
The faculty-student ratio is approximately 1:20
The faculty are highly qualified and most of them have a bachelor's degree from the same college and a master's from IIT. They have immense knowledge in them. Exams here are not that time and the difficulty is easy.The annual fest is Sanskriti and Tech Fest is Srijan.
You can get all types of books in the library. We have a digital library where there are computers.
The classroom condition is really bad with no AC. The sports faculty is also good. There are various types of clubs anime club, mountain club, finance club, dance club, etc

This course is for three years and the course curriculum is very simple and straight. All the facilities are very good and well educated and qualified. It consists of six-semester exams. And four internal in each semesterCampus life is very enjoyable and friendly as the college is anti-ragging. As the college organizes fests like technovation and many more in the mid of the semester. And all the classroom in the college is very beautiful

Since computer science has the most scope and as IT is an ever-growing field, I chose this branch. There is a proctor allotted for each student to supervise their activities. Faculty are highly qualified as most of the senior faculty are Ph.D. holders and highly experienced. The term exams are once in 4-5 months and if studied it will be easy to pass.From about 6th semester students become eligible for placements. Many IT companies such as Microsoft, Infosys, etc. visit our college and offer job opportunities. Most students are placed in our college so maybe about 98% of students get placed and some of the remaining students go for higher studies. I will most probably work in an IT company.

The placement is exceptional at Jadavpur University. From starting of 4th yr students are eligible for placement .there are 150 plus companies coming to Jadavpur University. In technical placement Google,Amazon, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, big four companies, come in ju .in core companies like Airbus, Texas instruments, mobile etc
90 percentage students placed at the average package of 12.83 LPA .highest package is 1.8 crore per year from Facebook.There is a projector in every classroom, and there is the seminal hall where some teachers can take classes which is fully ac .in college there is 24??7 internet.
Yes in college fest there is a sports fest-like arena and there is a cab(cricket association of Bengal) which is big.
There are many clubs in Ju like e-cell, dance society, singing society, hiking club and etc

The courses and curriculum are pretty relevant and good. Professors are also good at teaching except for some, teaching methods are mediocre here neither too advanced nor too backward. Some professors are really helpful and good some are just rude. One can easily qualify for exams easily by studying one week before the exam. Attendance is not that important, but it depends on prof.Fees hike is very much here, every year fee is increasing exponentially and my 4-year fees are as( 2.06, 2.19, 2.36, 2.45 lacs). 
There is financial assistance from the college itself for low-background students and there is some govt scholarships too. If you want you can take loans at low interest from banks too.

college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
There will be a technical fest every year in the college and many programs in the college students will enjoy college life. The library contains so many interesting books and all branches of books and there are so many sports in the college like Volleyball,  Football,  Basketball, 
Cricket, Athletics, Chess, etc. and so many clubs like Renaissance,  beehackers,  Nirvana, etc.In the 4th year 1st semester, the students get placed and many companies will come, the highest package is 36 lakh per annum. We have to maintain at least 7.5 CGP but placements do not depend on grades. If we present well in the interview and exams we will get good packages.

I want to pursue a course that gives me exposure to the core as well as to the IT sector. Core especially Electronics and Instrumentation. And it gives a very good placement as well.
There the faculty and students ratio will be 10:1. That means for every 10 students there is a teacher for guiding them.
Most of the teachers are done with P.HD. Some of the teachers are from IISc Bangalore, IIT KHARAGPUR, and various other reputed institutes. They coordinate with students very well. 
The semester exam will be conducted twice a year. If you follow the teacher's guidelines then you can easily pass the exam.Sanskriti, Srijan, U-turn, and CIIPPUS are the fest that happens at Jadavpur university. U-turn is conducted in January. CIPPUS is the fresher's fest of Jadavpur University Salt Lake campus it will also be conducted in January. Srijan is conducted in April. Sanskrit happens in August.
There are two libraries one on the Jadavpur campus and one on the Salt campus. All books and journals are easily available. 
Football, Cricket, Table tennis, Badminton, Chess and many other sports are happening.
There are many club CODE club, SCIENCE club, and DRAMA club.

Almost all the engineering courses at Jadavpur university have the same fee except IT ( information technology) because it is not yet government-sponsored, for the first year and second year you have to pay 5500 rupees, which includes everything like lab fee, library fee, tuition fee etc,  then in 3rd year, you have to pay approx 2500  and in 4th year also approx 2500, so total four year cost is approx 11k. You can also apply for many govt and private scholarships, the university will fully help with that.  govt scholarship like swami Vivekananda merit cum means scholarship (svmcm )  which will give the student  60k per  year  during the course but score at least 60% in every year  of the  college and 75% in 10+2 board exam, you can also apply  for a university  scholarship which will return your tuition  feeCampus life is one of the best things about this university,  it has 2 campuses, one at Jadavpur and the other at salt lake,  salt lake campus has 5 engineering department construction engineering, information technology,  instrumentation and electronics engineering, power engineering, and printing engineering. Jadavpur  campus  has all the other 11 engineering departments  and all  arts and all science departments,
Both campuses have canteens, a library, big playground. On the campus, we also have a basketball court, mountain climbing spot etc.  Many fests take place in our university like campus, turn,   Sanskriti etc.

There are broadly two important fests namely Srijan (annual tech fest) and Sanskriti (annual cultural fest) which are generally conducted in April and May respectively. There is also the fresher's event which consists of a series of games and cultural events.
The library of Jadavpur university on both campuses is filled with adequate resources both offline and online. Departmental books, research articles, topic wise books not related to the curriculum but academic. Also a wide variety of storybooks and magazines. JU has its own magazine (Indie publishing). 
Classrooms don't have AC but fans are present, and there are basic benches for students to sit and attend classes with projectors. 
There are numerous social groups made and run by students ranging from science to sports to literature and films. Chess, debate, dance, music, science, anime, fashion, cricket, football, coding, entrepreneurial, drama, literature, film, photography, Motosport and many more clubs. All of them are highly active.Over 95% of students get placed in my department of Instrumentation and electronics engineering (IEE). From starting of 4th year, students can seat for interviews and grab placements in top firms. All companies visit here with offers like Amazon, Flipkart, Microsoft, Adobe, ZS associates, DE Shaw, IDFC first bank, wells Fargo, Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, Texas instruments, micron, Samsung, tata, PWC, airtel, reliance, ONGC, Gail, bpcl, hpcl, Nvidia, Intel and many more. Opportunities are endless. The average package is 15lpa and the highest is 42lpa. 95% of students got placed in IEE. 
After getting the degree, I will continue with the placement that I will get(hopefully a tech company) and after a few years of the experience I want to do MBA and hope for a promotion.

Srijan is the tech fest of Jadavpur University, where we have various competitions ranging from coding to mystery.
Sanskriti is the annual fest of our college, and we have a very elaborate itinerary, though, without any very big names, the fun is unparalleled.
Classrooms are very spacious, and as per current developments, all classrooms in the BCSE department are going to get air conditioners. 
There is hardly any sport you can't play here in Jadavpur, from football grounds to cricket pitches, you have it all. 
We also have a mountaineering club, a photography club, a coding club, music and dance clubs, and a lot more.Students are usually allowed to sit for placements from the beginning of the 7th semester. 
Students from CSE bag placements at almost all of the FAANG companies, while some even get placed in very high-paying startups. International offers, too, are very common.
The highest packages, when converted into Indian currency and the PPP not accounted for, are usually in the range of 1 to 1.25crs per annum. The average placement is around 20 to 25 LPA.
Everyone who decides to sit for placements gets placed here in JU BCSE, though a sizeable number decide to opt for higher studies and/or research.

There are two annual fests. 
Sanskriti and Srijan,
Sanskriti is the cultural fest, and Srijan is the techno-management fest. 
There are ample books in Central and the library for you to get knowledge. 
Clubs are ECell, Motorsports club, mountaineering club, fan club, photography club, debating society, IEEE society, coding club, etc.
Classrooms and labs are well furnished but you have to compromise with the infrastructure due to a funding crunch.The fee is the best, 11700 for just 4 years. In this capitalist world, this is the best fee structure you can get in India.
The administration wants to hike fees but we students are working on it not hike fees. 
You can get SVCM, FFE Oasis, ONGC, Siemens, etc scholarships.

Our campus life is pretty much happening. Our annual cultural fest is called Sanskriti. It happens in March. Our annual tech-fest is called Srijan. It happens during May. 
There are sufficient books in the library and our central library is very big. Our classrooms have both digital and traditional facilities. We have projectors as well as blackboards. However, we don't have ACs in all classrooms, only in Seminar Room.
We have CAB (Cricket Association of Bengal) Ground for playing Cricket. We also have a Football ground, Basketball ground, and Badminton court.
We have many clubs and student branches in our university, namely Music Club, Dance Club, Debating Club, and Film Society as well as IEEE JUSB, IET Student Branch, etc.Our placements are pretty good. Many from our college have been placed in FAANG companies in the past years. Our average package for the CSE dept is 25LPA.
The companies visiting our campus include Apple, Accenture, Byju's, Deloitte, De-Shaw, Wells Fargo, Google, Facebook, Microsoft, Amazon, etc.
Our highest domestic average package was 65LPA given by Apple, and the highest international package was 2Cr+ given by Google.
Around 90% of students from each branch get placed, and it varies from department to department. For our department, we had a placement percentage of 97%.
I want to get a high-paying SDE job in my fourth year.

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text:

There is an okay amount of teachers, which can be improved.
there are around 70 students in one sect of cse and in total there are 2 sections.

70% of students get placement.
will try to have an off-campus placement.
in some good ut sector companies.
placements are not that good if you are average in your studies.

Also, NSUT is known for its placement. So, no doubt placement and internships in this college are very good.

I was having a keen interest in physics from class 11 which led me to choose mechanical engineering. If we talk about the qualifications of faculty they have completed their MTech and PhD from IITs.there are two exams held per semester the first is mid sem and the other is end sem and it's not that difficult to pass.The placements at our college are satisfactory. most of the students get placed with a median package of 6.5 LPA. the package of CSE and IT is much higher than the package of other branches. The placements in core branches are very low

I am a second-year BTech student but as far as I know at the end of third-year students get to sit for on-campus placements and they get PPO and most of them are placed. Some big companies like Microsoft, google, amazon, and Adobe visit campus every year for tech placements. Core companies like PWC, Tata etc also visit the campus every year. A lot of consulting companies also visit campus. The highest package this year was 85lpa and the avg is somewhere around 10-12lpa. Around 80-85 per cent of students get placed every yearThe annual fest of BIT is called BITOTSAV which is around Feb and the tech fest PANTHEON which takes place around September. Classrooms are also great and well equipped with projectors and all. The college has a huge amount of clubs you can be a part of and learn from a different groups of people.

From the seventh semester, the students become eligible for campus placements. A lot of companies come for hiring, The highest package offered was by MOTORQ in 2022 and the CTC was more than 1crore .then the highest package was CTC of 45 lakhs by Microsoft, and the 1 companies like Goldman Sachs, De Shaw, Amazon, Morgan Stanley provide a package of more than 25 lakhs. There are also companies like Deloitte that offer 9-10 lakhs of CTC. Then comes the mass recruiter like TCS, Wipro, and cognizant which offer a CTC of 5 - 6 lakhs. I would mostly prefer to do a job after obtaining the degreeThere are 2 fests namely Riveria and Gravitas.
Rivera is the annual fest conducted in Feb, whereas Gravitas is the tech. fest conducted in September.
They is a more than 1500 books and magazines in the library, you will most probably get all the books related to academics in the library 
There are a lot of sports and extracurricular activities and clubs you can join for exposure except for your academics. You have a lot of sports infrastructure like basketball courts, Gyms, Squash courts, badminton courts, Swimming pool.
VIT MUNSOC is one such club run by students to help improve their communication skills

I choose this course because of mint Jaipur i want to study in this blog and i thought chemical engineering is also a good option for me to pursue my BTech 
Teachers are not so good their experience is also less 
Mid-term and end-term exams are normal and students can easily pass them if they studyCampus Placements of unit Jaipur are very good you get decent packages from this college from the 7th semester you got Placements and there are many good companies come here like amazon Flipkart and many others highest package is 48 lacks in chemical engineering 100 percent Placements.

Scholarships offered to the students are again based on the entrance examination rankings.Their are different categories of scholarship(Category 1 to Category 5):A rough estimate is:

Category 1:195000/yr
Category 2:295000/yr
Category 3:395000/yr
Category 4:495000/yr
Category 5:595000/yrAs for this, whatever we know is based on what others say(100% placement). But still, we know that a lot of companies do visit the campus, and the fact that the placement procedure is a joint one,i.e, all the students attend the placement procedure jointly in Vellore.

Campus life of NSUT is very good apart from academics. There are very good environment around students. There are total of 3 fests happens in our college every year. One is Moksha which happens in the month of march for 3 days. Socond one is technical fest inovision in the month of may and the third one is resonaz(cultural fest) in the month of october for 3 days. Fests days are the best days of our college. I have chosen Electronics Engineering because I felt like it gives me something to be proud of rather than sitting in front of a computer my whole life. This course gives us field experience and makes us feel like real engineers.

The University has a Student activity center.

INSIDERS is the official western dance club.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:

college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI]
review_text: Life is pretty good and at my college, there is a nice crowd which comes from all over the country so we have good exposure to interact with every community person in the country which helps in personality development there are many clubs in our college such as gaming club acting club music club as well as tech club such as robotics Synaptics CFA CAE, etc.placement in our College is tremendously improving each and every year with 24.75 lakh as the average package in the class of 2022 mechanical engineering has an average package of 17 lakh while the computer science engineering department has an average package of 38 lakhs and the electronics department has an average package of 27 lacs.

When it comes to campus life it was an amazing minimum of 2 fests were conducted every month. And there is a separate student welfare division where you can enjoy every day in a wide range of different activities.
A library is available which is on 5 floors where you can find every book and journal related to your course. And the library is so comfortable and gives the perfect vibe to study.
Amenities in classrooms are fine when compared to other universities but should be improved.
They were many clubs for students that are run by students like Dance clubs, Coding clubs, and more in different fields.
We can all kinds of sports here from chess to hockey, football, and from kabaddi to boxing.I want to pursue cyber security as the main field so to have the basics of computers I've chosen computer science engineering.
Faculty to student ratio is  1: 80  where each classroom has a minimum of 70 students. Qualification of the faculty is compromising, every lecturer here has a Ph.D. And when it comes to exams you'll have 1 midterm in the middle of the semester and 1 end-term examination at the end. And course assignments are conducted once every 2 to 3 weeks. And they are not difficult except Mid terms .you have to do some work to get good marks on those exams

CSE is the trend and the future. Anybody who wishes to choose to engineer should opt for CSE according to me for a smoother future with a lot more benefits.
For every 80 students, there is one faculty and there are more than 5 teachers for every subject.
The teachers are all Ph.D. with more than 6 years of experience in teaching. With this much experience the methods are good but a little orthodox for older teachers too.
Every month we have to give a sessional test and after every 6 months, we are required to give the end terms. The papers aren't much difficult and are set pretty nicely.Algorithm and techlon are the annual fests and tech fest respectively and they are conducted in November and December respectively.
All books are available in our library including e-books.
There are extracurricular activities happening almost every day along with sports contests. And, all sports are played at the university.
There IEEE and ACM student chapters, CEED, toastmasters international CSI, GFG Student chapter, and GDSC, and all these are run by students.

review_text:

college_name: IIT Ropar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
Course Fees for the B.Tech program are 8Lpa. 
Merit-Cum-Means Scholarship:-
The institute offers Merit - Cum_Means scholarships to undergraduate students. these are permissible for 25% of the students. The present value of Merit-Cum-Means scholarship is Rs.1000 per month and the recipient is exempted from paying tuition fees.My decision to opt for this course was merely based on the college placement record compared to other new IITs. The faculty at IIT Ropar is great. The majority of the professors hold a Ph.D. degree. They are cooperative and provide proper guidance in projects as well as examination preparations.

The first-semester fee was 80000 when I joined the college and the rest of the semester fee is 70000. And apart from this, if you choose a hostel. The hostel fee is 33400 per semester, including mess. So you'll have to pay around a lakh, every semester.
Talking about campus jobs, you'll not get any opportunity for a part-time job anywhere around here, because the college is in a village where you have to go 3 km to reach the highway and then travel for about 30 to 40 minutes to reach the town.The campus life is good. But DJ nights and many other events keep happening every two or three weeks for student entertainment. 
There are many clubs here that keep doing events. Many sports and cultural events happen here. 
The college's fest, ALGORITHM happens every year. But it has not happened since I joined the college. I also have only heard about it. So anyways
The minus point of the campus is that it is very small. Otherwise, if you have friends every place is nice.

review_text:

secondly, the top 5 students from the second year to the final year in every branch received 20k per year as a Merit scholarship reward.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: LIFE ON CAMPUS IS A LOT MORE LIVELY AND CHILL. YOU CAN FIND PEOPLE LITERALLY  ANYWHERE AS THE CAMPUS CROWD IS A LOT AND THE MESSY FOOD IS NOT THAT GREAT. THERE ARE A LOT OF CLUBS WHICH ANYONE CAN JOIN TO EXPLORE  THROUGHOUT AND GET ENGAGED  IN VARIOUS  ACTIVITIES  HAPPENING  ON THE CAMPUS.THE COURSE  CURRICULUM  IS A BIT OBSOLETE AND OUTDATED. THINGS TAUGHT ARE MOSTLY OF NOT THAT TECHNOLOGICAL ADVANTAGE  TO THE STUDENTS. THE QUALITY OF THE FACULTY IS GOOD THEY ARE MOSTLY PH.D. HOLDERS ALONG WITH FOREIGN  RESEARCH.

The annual fest is VIBRANCE it happens every year in February and is one of the coolest college fest and tech fest is TECHNOVIT it organise in September. Both are just fabulous but in vibrance girls and boys are sepererate from each others by baricating. 
Library is soo big .we have 3 floored library with awesome environment but wifi sucks.
Classrooms are not as cool as other colleges but labs are cool.
No sports life in campus its very less. Vit do not encourage sport culture . It is extend to somewhat extend.
There are more than 200+ societies clubs and group . You will surely get into one of your choice and hobbies.i am currently in dance club.Fees vary from 1.75 lakhs to 5 lakhs per year according to your category. There are total five categories. First cat fee is 1.75 lakhs per year likewise increasing . The higher you score in viteee exam the lower your fee . If you score rank below 20000 you can quickly get cat 2 .
Scholarship is just for the name sake. Their are three categories of scholarship. First is your rank should be under 4000 in jee mains you get 50% concession on fees . Like why a person with 4k status will come to vit. Second is you should be your state topper in boards  third is score 1-100. Rank in viteee.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: The fee structure is different for different categories. If you belong to an unreserved category and your family income is greater than 6 lakhs you will be charged a total tuition fee that is 110000. But if you belong to the ews or OBC category and your family is between 100000 and 500000 then you will have a waiver of two-thirds of your tuition fees. For students with a family income of less than 100000, the tuition fee is fully waived.From 6th semester, students become eligible for campus placements. Several companies visit the college every year. The highest package for my branch went as high as 1crore while the average package is 33 lakhs. Almost 95% of students get placed. I plan to work in an MNC after the placements.

I chose this course because it is a circirutary branch and has a good possible opening in its sector as well as PSU.

2 main professors, 4 assistant professors, and some Ph.D. scholars in every department, the subject are taken by the respective department, and students per class are around 100 for each batch except civil which has 180.

Every faculty is highly qualified and many have ongoing research as NIT is a government college, teaching methods of many are excellent but some are a little bookish and hard to understand.

One mid-semester exam (30% weightage) and one end-semester (60% weightage) along with internal assessment (10% weightage) is the standard pattern, exam standard entirely depends on the teacher but the thumb of rule is that at least two subjects are going to be either tricky or very difficult (generally math).No annual fest, fests are mostly not conducted and events are generally just done by sitting in classrooms, tech-fest is done similarly by club semicolon.

The library has all course-related books and a few additional ones related to competitive exams like gates and periodicals.

Classrooms have elevated seats, smart boards, air conditioning, and proper lighting system.

Sports and extracurricular activities are at a bare minimum but will change after the completion of the construction of multiple basketball, tennis, cricket grounds.

Groups can't be formed without faculty approval but are still run by students, some groups include, the sargam club (music), semicolon (coding), rhetoric club (debate), etc.

It is one of the most placement promising courses in this college, and that's why I opted for it. Also, the faculty of IT is most supportive in college in all aspects.
Each class comprises about 60 students which is just enough for one teacher in the classroom and a pretty common teacher-to-student ratio per classroom.
Faculty is well educated but they lack in the skill of teaching. Sometimes they don't get what the student is trying to imply.
There are 3 sessional tests throughout a semester, which is pretty easy to pass, then comes a pre-university test just before the final sem exams, which, if have studied well, are a piece of cake.In the library, only educational, coursebooks are available. You can't read anything if it's not in your syllabus.
Classrooms are equipped with smart boards. I think they should be air-conditioned as well, given their enormous fee structure.
You can't play sports there if classes are going on in the meantime. You will have to wait till 4 pm.
Colleges have many cultural societies and many tech societies. Only tech societies are beneficial.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
Yaa Fest also is the main thing in college life. So every year Fest is organized About Library. It is very huge and mostly all types of books are present. No Such sports are there in this college basically this is a study college.  All the extracurricular activities are available in the college with the best infrastructure in the college.Yes In placement, students can sit in placement for 6 semesters.
So I cannot explain the name of the companies because there are too many companies coming every year. mostly around 400 tech company visits And the percentage of placement is very high 99%. Mostly all the students are placed in good companies.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text: There are many social groups/ clubs that IIT Bombay have, including E-cell, mood Indigo (the largest college cultural fest of Asia ), provides( a program run by students to organise concerts ), Sarc, IITBSSP ( student satellite program), rocket team and many more. The sports culture of IIT Bombay is the best among all IITs as it includes indoor and outdoor basketball courts, volleyball courts and cricket nets. Almost all the clubs I mentioned above have their own websites. You can search them directly by their name in chrome.Mainly 4th year students are eligible for placements and thirties do their internships. Many companies visit iitb including Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Tata motors, Bajfinace, BCG etc. The highest package varies every year with 1.5 cr + definitely placed from past 3 years. The average package is 22.7 lpa Around 70% of students get placement while others start their own startups. I will choose to get into the consulting field after getting my degree

Campus life is good at mnit . There is annual cultural fest called "blitzslag". Many colleges take part in this fest . 
There is central library which is quite huge .
There you will get almost every book of your course ,novels of your choice and also the journals. Library is fully air conditioned. 
All the lectures are conducted in vivekanand lecture theatre complex (vltc), which is Asia's largest lecture hall. It is very very huge with large sized classrooms , fully air conditioned. There are total 4 floors in this building. There is student activity centre in this vltc where there are many indoor sports for students. Also it have gym with all necessary machinery but not trainer.
There are  various dedicated grounds for various sports like volleyball, basketball, cricket ,lawn tennis , badminton ,etc.
There are many students runned clubs like dance , singing, photography,  coding, nss, world heritage ,etc.I am pursuing metallurgical and materials engineering as I am interested in manufacturing industries like TATA ,reliance and many other , so I found this branch appropriate for me.

In our department there are highly qualified professors and assistant professors with minimum qualification of phd in many domains of research. All of them have worked  and gained experience in major companies of steel making , materials processing , metal extraction etc.
Teaching method is quite good. All of them are very responsible for taking their classes and also quite flexible if students appeal them to cancel the  class for an appropriate reason. 
Term exams are conducted in proper time with complete submission of syllabus in class. If someone has attended majority of classes then it is not difficulty to get passing marks.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
bharat dhwani is the main annual fest  and utkarsh is the technical fest of this college. bharat dhwani is the faondation day programm celebrated on the month of october. there are also small and big events organized in the college according to branch and year.
in our central library there are almost all the books are available related to course and many other novels, jouranls, newspapers , encyclopedia, thessis are there in library.
all classrooms has projectores and smart boards. and also all the lecture halls are airconditioned.
atheletics, football , volleyball, cricket are the main sports club of the college which take the inductions to join them.
you can also join prayas society and become a volunteer where you can  teach the local students.
zeal society , debate society are also the mains clubs which are run by the students of the college
there are criminals and si clubs where you join and do dance in the feststhis course I get by my rank in the jee mains so there is no choice although I want to do preparation for the CDS exam so there is a lot of time I get for preparation so this course suits me 
there are two teachers for every subject one take the classes and other take the tutorial classes to solve the doubt. it is basically a doubt session.
all teachers completed their phd and the teacher for tutorial classes are doing their phd from this college.
there are 2 minor exams and one end sem exam and almost a half-month gap between exams. the difficulty level depends on the subject and students there are many students which got 90% marks and there are students also which don't even get the grades to pass the exam.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text:
I choose this course as it fits best for me according to my rank in jee advance. And the faculty here is too helpful and promoting but don't forget to give time to your studies too don't fully depend upon the professor's teachings.
Here every subject has 2 quizzes 1 mid sem and 1 endsem.IIT Guwahati conducts a fest every year some of which are alcheringa, technique, spirit, and much more, and they are conducted only when students are somewhat free from academics.
Many clubs like robotics, electronics, expression, cadence, and much more clubs are run by students.

Campus Life at our college is good because the infrastructure of the campus is very good. The College campus is on a hill so it is looking very good. Talking about college fest there are 2 fests in our college one is furor which is an intercollege fest and one of the biggest and finest fests in Bangalore other one is vibration which is Intra college. The library of our college is very big and you can get a very silent and for study environment so if you want you can go library for studying my friend is also going to the library for studying but if you want to issue a book from the library then you need to pay for that. 
The sports environment is also good our college has a football ground, basketball, volleyball, badminton and table tennis, but here for cricket lovers very bad news because our college does not have a cricket ground. 
There are many club culture clubs, sports clubs, entrepreneurship clubs, coding clubs, developer clubs, etc and all are in active mode.First of all, I am not eligible for computer science and engineering so I choose computer science and design but it is also good you can get some extra knowledge about some design parts along with computer science like AR/VR, UI/UX, etc.
The faculty and student ratio is good approx 1:30.
Faculty are well qualified like Ph.D. holder, iitian and their teaching method is also good. 
Exams are in proper time in 1 sem there 3 three internals of 50 marks along with assignment and quiz and one 1external/end sem. One can easily pass if he/she can average in study or study before 1-2 days of the exam.

The course curriculum is vast and quite challenging which makes it better as the crop of students and competitiveness is also high. The course of CSE is on par with some of the top institutions in the country and the faculty is highly established and provides high research output which provides students an opportunity to expand and aim beyond their prescribed course.The campus is still undergoing a lot of construction but the lifestyle is really good and there are a lot of clubs that provide the students an opportunity to learn through experiences that help them better themselves and push through the normally prescribed books. There are a lot of sports competitions held on campus which is a plus.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
there is an annual fest called Bharat dhwani celebrated in October. and also there is a technical fest called Ukash. there are also some big and small events. there is a decent library in nit Jalandhar you can find many books related to your course and there are also many journals and novels in the library. all the class is a centralized air conditioning system, and there are also one projector and one smart board in every lecture hall. there are many sports clubs like athletics, football, volleyball, etc they also organize inter-branch, inter-hostel tournaments. NCC, NSO, AND NSS are also the main clubs there.
there is a debate club where you can enhance your communication skills. there is also a Prayaas society where students volunteer and teach the local kids. zeal society is also one of the famous societies.I got this course due to my rank in jee mains exam. and specifically choose this branch because it is a circuital branch and also there is programming culture involved in the curriculum like c language, dsa, etc.
in the main subject we got 2 teachers, one is the main teacher who takes classes and the other takes the tutorial sessions for doubt solving.
in lab classes, we get again 2 teachers. all the faculty is Ph.D. qualified and also involved in many types of research.
there are 3 exams in a semester 2 minors and one end sem there is almost 1.5 months gap in each exam.

Placement is quite awesome and it's increasing day by day with top MNCs company like Amazon, Atlassian, TCS, Paytm, parth universal, and phone pe acquiring students from every branch of the college, and core and PSU companies like bpcl, hp, iocl visit the college with a good package, tata motors, Let also come over here, 69 students got to intern in Amazon this year and some went to Atlassian and other companies, this year parth universal offered 6 students a package of 45lpa.and off campus placement of 3 students above 1 cr in Amazon, Facebook etcFresher party every year for fresher, then the annual fest corona every year, and many other club events from time to time in college, every year there is intramural sports event between departments, in the library u can access and issue every book of ur choice, u will get access of most of the extracurricular activities, many clubs are present like dance, music, drama, debate, expresso, and tech club as gods, backslash, ASME, IEEE and many more

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
This course is really good for further learning and growth. Faculties are really nice and friendly. Faculties are highly qualified with most of them having their own Wikipedia pages. Exams are held at regular intervals and some of them are pretty difficult to passThere are so many things going around it is sometimes difficult to decide where to go.
You have so many clubs that I can guarantee you, you will find something that interests you.
Mood Indigo is a cultural fest which is famous worldwide

The college is best known for its fests and cultural activities. The Annual fest Ecletia is considered to be the best Annual fest in Kolkata. Also, the fresher is a 3-day long fest which is called Nascent. It is also the best freshers in Kolkata. But I am in my second year and I have seen none of them. Usually, Ecletia happens around April-May.
The college has 3 buildings. All the buildings have proper elevator systems and are connected via a bridge. Toilets and drinking water facilities are available.
The library is a big 20000 sq. feet air-conditioned with books from all genres. The semester books and previous year's exam papers are provided by the library and you can borrow any book you like.
The campus has a good canteen where you can spend time and munch something, however, there are very less places to chill. The campus has a cricket ground and the basketball infrastructure is the best.
There are several clubs like the photography club, Rotaract, Google developer societies, Literature club, etc.I chose this college as this college is one of the best in Kolkata and probably the best autonomous college based on ROI.
In my branch, the faculty members are awesome. All the faculty members are very supportive and helpful but strict at the same time. All the classes and labs are proper and there is a GATE-oriented environment in the college. Most of the faculty members have PHDs and if any student is preparing for GATE then I don't think he/she will have to take any coaching classes.
The exams are the USP of Heritage. It is popularly said that if any student is scoring a 9 CGPA in any other college then it is equal to a 7 CGPA of Heritage.
The exams are conducted in a very strict manner and are tough to crack. Even during the online exams, the average class CGPA was 7-8.
But, the environment is very hectic. You will have to maintain 75 percent attendance and the curriculum is loaded with assignments. So don't imagine some movie-like chill College life.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text: Every year there is a 10 percent hike in the college fee. It will cost around 20 lakhs for the complete BTech. I have not got any scholarship but if you are eligible you will definitely get it. It has a procedure which you can see on the college website.Life is very good here. There is one cultural, tech, and sports fest held every year. There is a gym on campus and SAC for table tennis and badminton. There are many clubs like imagination, insignia, eminence, astronomy, cybros, etc.

There is a total of approx 10 distinct programs offered by Accenture. You can select your preferred subject to get an internship with Accenture.??Software engineering, information security, human resource, and operations??are some of the top-notch fields for getting the internship. If you have the desired set of skills then you can make it through the internship program with Accenture.The average cost of a B.Tech from Graphic Era University is approx. Rs.1200000.
Students having a percentage of more than 80% in 12th get scholarships on the criteria of percentage. 
10% additional discount for girls candidates. 
5% additional discount for the defense personnel.
5% additional discount for National sports players.

The course curriculum is great well mine is computer science and engineering (Ai and Ml) ,the faculty for the 1 semester was good and 2 sem faculty was ok ,all the teachers that taught was most of them were PhD and were good ,the term exam were always on time except when the college was online ,well the exams are pretty easy to pass and its not much hard to score 9 pointers also if you study.The fees according to me is very high but we do get scholarship according to merit so it does take a lot of burden ,the fees per semester on average is 1,30,000 ,well you are given your own personal fees chart for the whole four year and it doesn't change.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
after the 4th-semester internship opportunities are there and after the 7th semester students are eligible for the placements. the placement record of the college is amazing. there are so many companies that provide placements you can find all the names of the companies and their packages offered on the official website of amazon, google, Microsoft, Nvidia, texas instruments, and engineering India limited these are some famous companies that visit for placements.
the highest package is 1.2cr per annum CTC by amazon to an ECE student and the average package is 10LPA 
percentage of students getting placed is different according to the branch. The overall 80percent is the placement percentage, it is more or less according to the branch 
I plan to join the civil services.there is an annual fest called Bharat dhwani which is conducted in October month. there is a tech fest called the annual techno-managerial fest. utkarsh is the famous one fest of this college. the library has a huge no. of books you can find every book related to your course. and novels, journals, and other books are also there in the library.

all classrooms have air conditioning. there is a projector and digital board is also there in every classroom.
there are so many sports clubs related to cricket, football, volleyball, athletics, etc.
there is a prayer as a society which is run by the students where they provide tuition classes for free of cost to the nearby students.
zeal society and some more societies are famous in college.

I chose Computer Science Engineering because I am enthusiastic and wanted to explore the multitude of fields of Computer Science and its applications in multiple industries. The faculty-to-student ratio is very balanced, which ensures enough attention and consideration for each student. The faculty here is competent and ensures their teaching methods are compatible and optimal. The exams were held timely, and students need to have a complete understanding of all of the concepts taught in the course to ensure good marks.Students become eligible for campus placements in the sixth semester. There are 1000+ offers and opportunities coming from a variety of companies including Amazon, Adobe, Microsoft, Accenture, Capgemini, TCS, Infosys, and more. The highest package offered to a student was 54 LPA, with an average package lasting around 5.5 LPA. Around 90 to 95% of students do get placed during campus placements. I, personally, am still exploring this field and exploring the vast multitude of career paths and opportunities to come.

The annual fest happens in the graphic era. It is a very grand fest called GRAFEST, many celebrities are also invited like previously Arijit Singh came. Many other fests like the summer fest held in June, the winter carnival held in December, etc are also organized. In these types of fests, celebrities are invited like in the summer fest this year Urvashi rautela and Chetan bhagat came.
The college has two big libraries. One library is dedicated to CSE students.
Many clubs are there in college like a literary club, ACM student chapter, IEEE club, singing club, and dancing club which from time to time held many events in which students take part and also get rewarded.I was more interested in computer science and the college has a very good placement panel and placement record. It has a very good alumni network.
The faculty-student ratio is also good. The teacher takes the timely tests and checks the progress of the student and also one thing I like the most is the teachers are very humble and they always clear your doubts no matter how you ask.
There are mainly two major term exams apart from internal class tests and sessional tests. they are mid-term and end-term which are held after three months and at the end semester respectively.

I love coding that's why it can be called the hub for the computer science engineering of Uttarakhand. Major of the faculties are also highly qualified. Exams are not difficult to pass if you study properly in the classroom. The teachers are really nice and understanding.I am currently part of two organizations. The first one is called National Service Scheme or NSS which is a non-profit organization that works for social services and the second is IEEE an international organization for technical exposure.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: The annual cultural fest in my college is KASHIYATRA. while the annual technical fest is TECHNEX. Kashiyatra is held usually in January while technex is held in February. The library has many books and journals, one can easily find books of any genre in the library. Some classrooms are AC equipped while some are not. The bigger classroom has speakers. But talking about classrooms the amenities provided are rudimentary. We have the facility of playing almost every sport we can think of. We have got a newly constructed student activity center for all the indoor games and the gym. There are many clubs in the college, and I am active in the biz, scs, and sochem.Students studying in the 8th or last semester are eligible to sit for the placements if they don't have any backlogs. Around 200 companies last time to the campus to recruit students. They offered jobs to around 800 or more undergraduate students. The highest international package is 2 cr while the highest domestic package is around 1 cr for the sde profile. The average package is around 18 LPA. Most students are easily placed in those who sit for the placements. I will work as a data scientist after my graduation.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: There is a library with all books available. There are a lot of clubs like Business club, E-cell, Photography club, Astronomy club, Chess Club, SCS, etc. There are various websites for departments like udyam, and Cops. Social life is good at IIT BHU like other IITs.Fees are 2.16 lac for people above 5 lakh income and from general and obc categories for SC/ST fee is 16k per year fee waivers are available for income between 5 to 1 lakh, 66% waiver and for income less than 1 lakh 100 % tuition fee waiver.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: students become eligible after the 7th sem for placements and the 4th sem for internships a lot of big Tech companies like Google, Graviton, Microsoft, Adobe etc come to our college for recruitment and also hire for international offers.There are a lot of clubs which you could explore like E-cell, Business Club, COPS, Cycling Club, Astronomy Club etc you can join whichever interests you and enjoy your campus life. Classrooms don't have ACs that's a Big Con over here.

I have a good interest in electronics so I chose this course and it has higher chances to get a student placed in good comapanies. faculty members are friendly but all you have to do on your own. most of the faculties are Ph.D. and exams are conducted every semester.cost to study is not much if you have an income certificate of less than 5 lac income. You get 2/3 of the fee remission. Many scholarships are also there you get emails whenever a new scholarship comes. One of the scholarships known to me is from the state govt.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: So we have here 10 technical club which deals in every current technical field and 10 cultural club which take care of every aspect of cultural side. They work very efficiently. Plus we have tech board and sport boards which provides World class facilities in the same . Classes are fully central air conditioned with projectors and sound system.Fees of IITs are same everywhere and is decided by central government considering inflation and World class facilities they provide here so it's not a issue besides financial assistance of every kind in form of scholarship of various ministries and institute scholarship for top performers are available here.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: I chose this course as it provides us with so many opportunities. Faculty depends on the department as I am from computer science and technology. Here, faculty members are very helpful and polite. Some faculty members are alumni of this college. Teaching method varies from teacher to teacher, some teach from the slides, and some on the board as well as from slides. There will be mid-semester and end-semester exams in one semester. The teacher's notes are sufficient to pass the exams.Kolkata's largest tech fest- Instruo is organized by this college annually in November. Clubs are very active and they also help to build confidence in oneself. Ramanujan Central Library of this college is one of the top libraries in Kolkata. Sports facilities are there. College ranked 7 in the Sparda event which was organized by the IIT bhu.

I chose this course as I have a passion for computer science. GLA is adding faculty from time to time to comply with the student ratio which is increasing per year. Faculty is well qualified to give you knowledge about his/her subject in depth. The teaching method is OK.
There are 2 exams per semester, Midterm and End term. In the midterm, you hear around 30-50% of the syllabus and in the End term, you get the next 50% including the previous one (however the percentage of midterm topics is less as compared to end-term topics).We have fests organized by clubs and festive fests in corresponding months.
Maximum books are available in Library, they have an online system to search for available books.
You will have AC classrooms and halls with good lighting.
There are many sports activities organized by the sports club of gla.
Many clubs are run by students, E-cell, Abacus, Gla Voice, Nature Club, etc.

Life at the campus is very good you get a lot of exposure, and interaction with a variety of people.
Many fests are conducted but there are not at all that good but it's fine.
The library is also good. The classrooms are fine.
Sports and extracurricular activities are also there.The placement is good for BTech cs students they can easily get a package of 6-8 lakh which is also the average package. If you put a little more effort you can easily get 10+ LPA. Companies visiting the campus are well known like Microsoft, Amazon, and tech Mahindra.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text:
Campus life is far better than private colleges in India as there are no PDA charges. 
There are many 2 important fests on our campus. The technical fest is INSTRUO and the annual fest is REBECA.
There is also a library that holds over 100000 journals and books and is one of the oldest in India.
The classroom is quite good and there are fans for 1st-year students. But don't worry you will get AC from the Second year if you don't belong to the civil or mechanical branch.
There are 2 grounds Oval and the Lords. One is football and the other is cricket ground.
There are many clubs and one may join accordingly to their interests. There is an EDC club that is run by students and funded by the institute.The course curriculum is similar to that of other NITs and nothing much difference. The faculty members are highly knowledgeable and approachable. They clear your doubts from time to time.
I chose civil engineering because I wanted to serve my nation in its building and infrastructure.
There is a good amount of faculty: student ratio. Some professors are old and hence they still have old-school style methods of teaching but new teachers and professors make teaching interactive by introducing smart boards and learning with AI.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: The cost to study is quite good if you are in a category (st, sc). But if you are not then might feel it to be quite expensive. Job offers are good in my department as it is a very good department for IT jobs. Students also get government scholarships.I choose computer science and technology because I just love to spend time with electronic equipment. Many faculties are very friendly and very supportive.  Exams come twice in one semester one in the mid-semester and the other at the end semester.

The campus is impressive and attractive. Some proctors are always there to feel safe. There are libraries in each block where you can have max to max for every book. There are hundreds of clubs that conduct multiple events with the collaboration of other colleges like IIT NITs.I think some fewer people get placed off campus. The placement criteria of my college are nearly 75 to 85 percent. I wish to get the on-campus placement and then off off-campus one. There are many great companies like Amazon, and Google that comes to the campus for placement.

I choose B.Tech (CSE) because I had a slight interest in programming languages and computers. And I can say without any risk that this course is at the top in terms of no. of students pursuing it. GLA University gives quite good placements in CS brach compared to other private colleges in UP or nearby states. There are more than 12000 students in the university and more than 2000 faculty. So roughly the ratio of faculty students is 1:6. Almost every teacher is helpful and literally has good knowledge and you'll find a few outstanding faculty but sometimes if a teacher is not delivering their knowledge well and if you complain about that teacher, they will listen to you. 
In a year you have to give exams 4 times in a gap of around 1.5-2 months. If you studied well and attend classes properly you can easily score good marks but if you are the one who studies the night before the exam then it won't be easy for you to pass the exam.There are a lot of fests conducted by different types of clubs. E-cell club organizes tech fests like inviting some intellectuals and they conversate with students. Cultural fests like DJ night and dandiya night happen. The library has a lot of books you can get any book you want. And I should mention GLA's library is something to talk about. Every sport is available and sports tournaments are conducted annually. Clubs are run by students and mentored by one particular faculty.

Placement is most important for students. Mnit is a government college mainly it is NIT so placements are pretty good. every major visit in mnit and average package is also good and percentage of the job given to students by companies are sufficient.Mnit is known as Nit Jaipur so fees are decided by government bodies. The total fee is approx 2 lakh per anum but the college gives benefits to caste or Economic backward students as per their requirements. many students get scholarships also.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text:
The annual fest is Alcheringa and which is conducted every year in March the tech fest is Techniche held every year in September. there are more than 4lac books and journals available in the library. Every facility is available in academic complexes.
The campus consists of various sports including every ground reserved for a specific sport
Also, various clubs are run by students bodies in every fieldI had a keen interest in mechanical engineering and also wanted to pursue automobile sort of engineering so it was best for faculties are very interactive and communicative faculty: students ratio is 1:40
Generally, faculty members are postgraduate and have unique teaching methods generally, exams are in two weeks' time and they are Moderate to pass

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text:
After the 6th sem placement drive starts and you are eligible for campus placement.
Google, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, and many more with packages of avg of 10+lakh.
in the current year, 81Lakh offered were made by Atlassian and avg package is 18+lakh.
The percentage of students getting placement is above 90+.
Get Good Placement in Good Company.Because DAIICT has a very good placement.
You did not need to see the faculty: student ratio because it's not needed.
all professor is having P.hD from good universities and has 10+ years of experience.
we have three exams each semester and exams are really tough but we have relative grading.

1.20lac+ for annual income greater than 5lac,

%placements
