college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text:
Campus life at PEC is very good. Social life is great as people here are quite open and broad-minded, and seniors are also quite involved and helpful. There are more than 20 active clubs and cells run by the students. The infrastructure at PEC is nicely built and well maintained, high-quality labs are available and also a very good library is there.
Annual fest and tech fest - PEC FEST conducted in the month of November.
In the Library, most of the books and journals required are easily available, and more than 1.5 lakhs volumes are available. Although Library is small compared to that of an IIT.
Classrooms are of sufficient size and have a capacity of 120 to 140 students. Classroom equipped with smart boards and a projector. Wi-Fi connectivity in every classroom is available and is also equipped with air conditioners.
Most of the sports are available here and extracurricular activities such as summits, surveys, and street play are held here. Every club or social group here is run by the students.The course curriculum is nicely designed and well structured to keenly implement the outcomes of courses. Faculty members are highly educated and well-trained and are also quite involved in research with students.
I chose this particular course as I had a good interest in mechanical engineering and also the college was able to provide ample opportunities in this particular field. College is able to bring good companies for placements.
Faculty to student ratio is 1:20 (counting only full-time faculty) excluding part-time faculty.
Every faculty is required to have a PhD MTech BTech in the specified courses. Additionally for Professor 10 years of experience and 6 research papers published are a must, for an associate professor 8 years of experience and 6 research papers are a must,  for an Assistant Professor these are not required. The teaching methodology is completely dependent on the professor.
Exams are held as per the Academic calendar and no delay occurs.

DTU is known for its amazing campus life and fests. Dtu has about a 170 acres campus area, consisting of A library, two auditoriums an OAT, A huge sports complex, and many other things. Social life in this college is really good, seniors interact a lot with the freshers and also guide them in their career paths. Clubs and societies are very active here and take auditions or interviews every year to take in interested candidates. The best thing that happens in DTU is THE ENGIFEST and which occurs around April or May. It's the largest cultural fest in Northern India. Many celebrities come to perform on our campus, and the cultural clubs also play their role in making this event a huge success. In the end, I would like to say that, if you're looking for a great campus life along with a good placement, you should opt for DTU.I chose this course because at my rank this was the best possible option. Also, DTU ( formerly known as DCE) is an esteemed institution with good faculties and other facilities. The teaching faculties are quite experienced here, for example, you might have heard of Professor Dr. HC Taneja. He's the HOD of the mathematics dept. at DTU. We only get a month to prepare for our end-term examination after giving our midterm exams, and the syllabus is also completed by the teachers. But studying efficiently and solving past year papers, helps a ton and one can get good marks in the exams.

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text:
The annual fest here is RATNAVALI which is the biggest college fest ever organized in Haryana and the annual tech fest here is EXCELSIOR which is also a tech fest of the international level.
All the Books you need to study or your course books are available in the library.
Classrooms are like normal classrooms like benches, smart boards, projectors, etc.
If you play sports there are many sports clubs here that you can join according to your preference and sports competition are also organized here every 4-5 months.
Not many social websites and all are run by students but there are many social groups and clubs here that are run by students for the students.The students of the 6th semester become eligible for placement.
Wipro, TCS, honda, Infosys, etc are the main companies that visit for placements. Not much idea about the no of offers made but about 70% of the students get placed.
The highest package offered here was about 1400000 that was from TCS and the average package here is about 4.5 Lakhs 
About 70% of students get placed.
My plan after the degree is to peruse a master's in my field.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
A well-maintained library is constructed, where all types of books are available for reading as well as issuing. 
Lecture halls are big enough to accommodate 200 students at once, there are 10-12 as such. Seminars and sessions are held there as we lack an auditorium.
There are decent sports facilities, one SAC includes 3 badminton courts, one squash room, and one dance room. Then the outside area has a volleyball court, lawn tennis court, basketball court, football ground, and a cricket ground.
Campus life on normal days is not so good, we have a canteen area where one general Store, one stationary, one salon, one snacks corner, one ice cream corner, one laundry shop, and 2 restaurants are there. 
There are many clubs and societies one can be a part of and develop its personality. We have 3 fests cultural/technical/sports namely, Vivacity/Plinth/Desportivos.Companies visit the college for an internship, take coding round, and interviews. During the recession period as well companies are visiting for internships, it's a big thing in itself, and in coming years these stats will go up. Students apply for campus internships as well in summer break after the 2nd and 3rd semesters.
Some companies provide internships and then recruitment in 4th year, for that we have the 8th semester also known as SLI (semester-long Internship).

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: The annual fest name is THOMPSON, which is conducted in the month of October. Every book is available in the central library. Sports and other extracurricular activities are conducted from time to time. The best part of the fest is DJ nightMainly this depends on your computer skills like ( coding, and editing ). And the companies are Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Hcl etc and some others are start-ups companies. After interns, you may get a job as well after good references.

Some of the new faculty (mostly phd students) are not very good, but all other professors are excellent in there respective field and are very intelligent , well qualified, very helpful and also support students in research field. We have regular classes, premium quality labs ensure amazing educational experience. The course curriculum is relevent and useful.There are two fest one is Engineer's ,a technical fest and incident a cultural fest ,which is one of the largest fest in south india . Libary is very good, excellent infrastructure. There are many special clubs.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
I chose this course because I am interested in mechanicals.
There are few courses in which more than one branch studies so more number of students otherwise 70-80 students per faculty.
Faculties are well qualified done masters from foreign universities. Some faculties use slides others use chalk and board. 
There are minors(mid-sem) and majors(end-sem).  Other than that there are quizzes ~4 per semester per course.Life in this campus is very amazing, Cultural fest Rendezvous in April, and Tech fest TRYST in January.
There are approximately 2 lakhs of books and plenty of space to study in the library.
There are lots of cultural and technical clubs like AXLR8R, EDC, and DEVCLUB.

The yearly fee the college is affordable for middle-class families. If someone is not able to afford it, then there are many scholarships given by the Government of the state to their respective students of the state, so that their studies can go smoothly.Since the scope of Artificial Intelligence in nearer future is high, so I opted for this Branch. Teachers are highly qualified and they do their best in clarifying the doubts of students. Teachers are very polite to students in clarifying doubts.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: first of all, every department gets placement. most of the students get a place in 8 semesters but some of them get PPO during internships. Microsoft, Google, Wipro, TCS, etc. Microsoft gives the highest package and mass recruitment give the normal package. from my dep. 100 percentplacement.There are three fests which are the most important in our college. I am giving the name of the three fests. name of the intro, Rebecca, music event. our college has a central library with thousands of books available. name of the library is Ramanujan.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: Internship opportunities are provided. every department has the opportunity for an internship. Students from cs and IT branches got internships at Microsoft, google, and amazon. we get to learn different things from internships and get more and more experience.The campus life is very good. There are many clubs and various societies in my college. Students can easily find their niche and their interest and can follow their hobbies and got their dream. the library facilities are also good.

Choosing the computer science because the cse course is demand and need in future must. The facultys are very friendly type an solving us doubts weekly ina brief context. The qualification of faculty are dr, graduated with a 1 st tier college s the  exams are taken monthly basisThe college doesn't provide any type of internship we have to do internship us only with the help of YouTube, google etc. But the university provide the chance of project s this is the best option provided by the university

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text: The campus area is small but includes everything that is needed. One bad thing is that hostel time is 10:45 pm and you cannot roam on campus after that. There is the cultural fest Vivacity, sports fest Deportivos and science fest Plinth that occur annually. Fests are held between the months of December to February. Many books are present in the library, containing all course-related books and many novels and literature. Classrooms are well structured with proper lighting, a big blackboard, a projector and proper ventilation and ducts. The sports part of college is also good with courts for basketball, table tennis, volleyball, badminton, and cricket ground and tt table and coordinators for each sport. Clubs are there for almost all extracurricular activities such as dancing, singing, acting, nukkad, robotics, astronomy, etc.As this college comes under private colleges, fees here are too high compared to other colleges. Fees per semester can be divided into 165000 tuition fees and 50000 mess, electricity and hostel charges. Fees hike is 10% of tuition every year. Scholarships include merit-based that is based on jee advanced rank and jee mains score. Further, if your parent's income is less than 500000 and you score good marks in a particular subject, you can become a teaching assistant in that subject in college. College also helps you with internships.

The course is quite decent and good. I chose the Undergraduate degree of B.Tech in the Civil Engineering Department as it was my dream to study at this Institute since I was in school. All the faculties are well educated and qualified to the best of their knowledge and the teaching is average. It depends on the student how well he interacts with the teachers and grasps knowledge.Life on campus depends from student to student. The campus is very beautiful including lakes, roads covered with trees, ponds etc. The sunsets here are so beautiful and aesthetic. There are many clubs including literature, poetry, music, dance, spiritual, trading, machine learning, coding, GDSC and many more.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text: The fests are one of the best things about the college. The cultural fest of LNMIIT, vivacity, is 2nd largest cultural fest conducted in central India. Artists like Jubin Nautiyal, kk, Kailash Kher, Gajendra Verma and many more come to perform in this fest. Companies like Redbull, Nestle, and Gaana are a few of the many sponsors of the fest. The vibe of the fest is just amazing. The tech fest of LNMIIT, plinth, is also quite good but is not of vivacity level. The fests are conducted between mid-January to starting February. The sports fest, Desportivo, is also amazing. Colleges from all over India participate in it. Lnmiit is a student-driven college which means all major decisions are taken by the student committee. Gymkhana elections are conducted every year and the election time is something everyone must experience. There are clubs for almost every possible interest and all are run by students.I actually wanted to take the CSE branch but with my score in jee, I could only get ECE. But I would say the branch really doesn't matter once you get into a good college as almost all companies are open to almost all branches. All faculty members are well qualified either from IITs or other reputed institutes. Faculty to student ratio is good. Faculty members of ECE specifically are very strict. A semester runs for 4 months which at the end of the first 2 months mid-terms are conducted and end the term by the end of the semester. Academics are tough I have to say. And the major reason behind this is that the marking scheme is very strict.

The course of the college is very good. I choose this course because I liked this course and I know a senior from before who said that the faculty members of this course are very good. The faculty: student ratio is 15:1.The semester exams are taken every six months and one class test exam is one or two months before the semester.The fees structure of the college is very convenient it is around four to four and a half lakhs for a complete course and this fee is very nicely structured in eight semesters, there are many scholarships also that the college approves.

Campus life is pretty awesome. The library is available from 8 am to 8 pm and there you can find almost a variety of books and journals. Computers are also accessible in the library. Apart from this, there are mandatory extra curriculum activities from different clubs in college like sports, coding, online gaming etcThe fee structure is pretty average. It is about 4 lakh for four years excluding hostel and mess fees. For West Bengal students there are TFW(Tuition Fees Waiver) seats in every branch. Apart from that svmcm and other scholarships are also available for WB students.

My course is electronics and instrumentation. I chose this course because I always had an interest in electronics, my rank fetched me this branch and I really have no regrets. The course curriculum includes mainly core electronics, a few subjects of core instrumentation, few information technology-related subjects. Moreover, the college also focuses on developing the soft skills of a student. The faculty members are highly qualified with each one of them holding a Ph.D. degree from a reputed institution. The teaching methods are amazing and they are always ready to help. S semester three internal exams(this comprises of a 50 marks written test and 10 marks quiz) are conducted out of which the best of two is considered for final marks and one semester-end exam of 100 marks is conducted. It is not at all difficult to pass the examinations. In fact, with a little sincere afford and hard work, one can get a very good cgpa.Rvce is a bit hectic academically but there are a lot of ways to chill as well. There are a lot of clubs be they cultural, technical social clubs where one can engage in different activities. The main social clubs are the NSS and the Rotaract club, cultural clubs are carv, alaap, and footprints, and technical clubs comprise the coding club, robotics, car racing clubs,antariksh, etc. The crowd here is good. Sports facilities are good as well. There's an indoor stadium where one can play table tennis or badminton. There are many playgrounds where one can play football, cricket, kabaddi, Kho-Kho, etc. Overall campus life is amazing.

Our fee structure is very easy . And it is a very low dee of Btech and we have to pay 75000 for the first time and after that, you have to pay around approx 45000 before the start of the semester and after completing the degree you will get back 10000 as a security deposit. And scholarship is also available.There is an opportunity for an internship in a company like Microsoft, TCS, and many other companies after the 2nd year completion. Microsoft provides a stipend of around 80K and other companies around 5k. Any projects you made in college you can write in your resume.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text:
The course curriculum is designed in such a way that it trains students and makes them ready for industry jobs. I was interested in the IT Sector job.
The faculty-student ratio is 1 to 17. Every professor holds a Ph.D. degree. Each semester has two exams one is mid-term and the other is the final sem exam. It is easy to pass.As compared to other colleges, this college lags in this arena. There are very few cultural fests. There is a very good library which contains 1.8 lakh books. The classroom is fully air-conditioned. I'm not much interested in sports so I don't have much knowledge about them.
EDC is one of the groups run by students.

Social life here is very nice. Lovely Professional University is famous for its sports complex but mostly of them are paid(You have a take a subscription fee for playing in stadiums). The library is good most of the books are present there without any fees for a maximum of 7 days.Personally, according to me faculty here is average. In some sections, you might get some good teachers but not all teachers are cooperative. Teachers here are mostly have completed their master's and doing their PhD There is an average of 75 students in each section.

A tech branch is what I always wanted. It provides the best opportunities in every imaginable career aspect. 
Faculty: student ratio is well maintained. Every faculty has certainly been provided with some load but overall it does not hamper the quality of teaching.
The faculty is very very highly qualified. Most of my faculty have done their master's or phd degrees abroad. However, their phd scholars or MTech scholars lack similar qualities, unlike the faculty.
2 exams: midsemester exam and end-semester exam. The difficulty was moderate. Not difficult to pass. Not easy to get a full score.The biggest fest is Engifest, conducted every year by the cultural society of the college. Generally conducted in the month of Feb-April.
Super large library with every kind of educational book available. The librarian's behaviour is very warm and polite.
Sports and extracurricular events occur very very frequently. Students interested in them participate and win various trophies and medals.
Website: resources. tech run by students for students

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
6th semester onwards
Microsoft, Google, Goldman Sachs, Sprinklr, Trilogy, Procter and Gamble, De Shaw, Salesforce,

         30% concession for income (100000,500000)

Family income doesn't matter for sc and st students

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: With a huge campus, the social life of students at Thapar is very good. There are more than 50 societies to join and many societies organize numerous events on campus which include standup comedy shows, concerts, and fairs. They also organize hackathons and other tech fests. Usually, all the events start early in a semester but they happen till the end of semester exams. One of the best features on campus is its library. It's a 5 floored building filled with books and chairs and tables for students to sit and do their work. Every type of book is available there and even the library hosts some literary events. The classrooms are well equipped with projectors and speakers so that the faculty could be understood by everyone listening. Thapar has many sports and equipment along with the courts and fields and annually organizes events like inter-year competitions and triathlons.One can sit for the college placements in any year provided they have the necessary skills for that but most of the students apply for them after their 6th semester though many apply in the 5th semester too as they get an internship as well as a job offer. Recently Microsoft offered a package of? 52,00,000 per annum in the college and many more like Maruti Suzuki, Amazon, MakeMyTrip, etc upto 200 companies offer seats for students here. With the highest package of Microsoft of? 52,00,000, the average package is? 10,00,000. My plan after getting my degree is the get into a good company and do the job I took

As vit is one of the fewer universities that provide the course in artificial intelligence and machine learning I preferred it over other courses which are regular, faculty to student ratio is a bit on the heavier side, vit is often described as a crowded University, faculty in vit are extremely qualified, teaching methods are great, the examinations in vit are of high standard a bit difficult to passPlacements in vit are pretty good, students are eligible for placements right after the 3rd semester, the top companies like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, cognizant, and Wipro visit the campus for recruitment. the highest package is around 45lakhs. The placement percentage is around 92% past year, many students aim to crack placements right after the degree

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
The placements at Thapar are good. The average packages go around 9.75 lakh CTC and it increases every year. Around 200 companies visit Thapar. If you are not able to get placed during your graduation year then Thapar gives you one more chance to get placed. From the 6th semester, they start your placement training. And by the end of the 7th semester, 75% of students are placed. 
If you don't want to go for placements then Thapar also provides an option for startups. They give a grant to startups and also you are guided by their special team for the same.Many companies like JP Morgan, TATA, Mahindra, Maruti Suzuki, TCS, amazon and many others visit Thapar with internship and job offers. This year Microsoft recruited students with a stipend of Rs 125000 per month and a job offer with Rs 51 lakh CTC. They also help students if they want to go for projects. This year a group of students and faculty made a satellite for the PUNJAB government to monitor wheater for Earth's low orbit.

The course curriculum is hard but chill also when you compare it with nsut and iiit-D. The faculty and teaching are nice. The schedule is somehow hectic, u need to give mid-term and end-term without any break . But it also gives tech opportunities to the lower branches, hence it's worthy to do.Internships are unbelievable here. You'll get a stipend of lakhs. Even in data analytics or management jobs, you can get a stipend of 70-80K. Hence these numbers are just unmatchable. Even if you are from a lower branch, you can get good opportunities if you are skilled enough.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: Campus life is the most memorable life of a person. IITM makes it more memorable by adding clubs as an ingredient. There are a lot of clubs in iitm. Every club has their own profile and project which are completely different from each other. There's also a library for us which is open 18 hrs daily and free to everyone. There's a sports club also here for all types of sports. The best social page of iitm is saarang.iitm on instagram. Must check.The course curriculum is so nice and lite. Everything here is well structured. The faculty-student ratio is also high. You get time in this course to explore other fields also. So the course is obviously not hectic in my opinion. Faculties are from IITs and also from international colleges. They have done phD. Term exams are held monthly here. It's a little difficult to score high in these exams.

after the end of the third year, the students will get a chance to attend the interviews. There are many reputed companies such as TCS, tata, etc are the main recruiters of the college. almost 80 percent of students get their placement in the college itself, some may go for further higher studies.The fee structure is not that high in the institutes like NITs or IITs. If you are of the general category then you have to pay the tuition fee of 78000 per semester or else for other categories fee waiver will be provided. Those who are not able to buy immediately can take loans from banks.

Due to my inclination towards Aviation and aircraft, I thought it was best to opt for the Engineering Physics branch at DTU so even if I can't get into aviation I would still have the option of making it into the IT sector through on-campus placements a students from engineering physics are allowed to sit for almost 80% tech companies' placement.Getting an internship at DTU is very difficult, companies arrive in the last week of July or early August and all have a similar procedure with the first round being an online coding round the second round being a technical interview and the third being an HR round, it may vary but this is the general operating procedure

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The life inside of the campus is unique, with a lush free environment and mild weather; the students at IIT BHU are beneficial and social with each other and always ready to help; there are clubs for almost everything in the college, from Photography, dance, cinema, drama, athletics to fine arts we have it all.
The annual cultural feat is Kashiyatra, held in January, and the technical accomplishment is Technex in march.
You can find books on almost all topics and subjects in the central library of IIT BHU.
Classrooms are equipped with a green board and a projector but do not have an air conditioner facility.
Sports of IIT BHU is one of the best in the country, with squash courts, tennis courts, volleyball ground, Kho-Kho field, and whatnot.
Social groups are SCS which is entirely run by students also. COPS is a website built fully by the students of IIT BHUPlacements are something IIT BHU is famous for, students get placed in the seventh semester itself, and they then complete the 8th semester before joining the job.
A lot of companies visit our campus for placements with big names like Uber, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Swiggy,
Around 1300 people were successfully placed through campus placements itself.
The highest package was 2.05 Crore by Uber to 5 students, and the average box is 19-20 LPA and. Profiles for which companies recruit are SDE, Product Manager, Data Scientist, Consultant, and Analyst.
95% of students get placed each year with great salary packages.
I plan to do MBA after graduation from IIM Ahmedabad

I chose this course because it provides an opportunity to take a placement in core and tech companies both. As EEE includes DSA in the curriculum which provides us an edge over other branch students during placement. Faculty are highly qualified from different renowned colleges across the globe.
End sem exams are conducted based on the academic calender released after every semester.This year till now 200+ companies had offered internships to students. The stipends provided were very good. In some profiles, projects are required during an internship interview. But mostly they don't ask for it. DSA is sufficient to take good internships.

There are 3 fests held on a national level. Pantheon is the technical fest, the vajra is the sports meet and Bitotsav is the cultural fest. There are numerous clubs including technical and cultural clubs. Depending on your interest you can join any. Excellent sports facilities are provided.I choose this course because it is a circuital branch and all tier 1 companies allow to participate in placements. The faculty ratio is excellent in college and all facilities are IIT pass-outs and have immense knowledge and experience. Exams are easy to crack and not frequent.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: The fee is about 2 lakhs per year and with a hostel fee of 35000 rupees per semester including mess accommodation and everything there is no fee hike policy or none that I know of there are many opportunities.The annual tech fest of IIT madras is shastra and the cultural fest is Sarang There are many books and journals in the library. There are various clubs in the institute for all various kinds of co-curricular.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: We have around 1,00,000 per semester tution fees and around 35,000 for hostel and mess fees. We can apply for mcm scolarships. There is no hike policy or none that i know of.we can do some part time invigilating during placements and we get paid by that. Course curriculum is very good we have labs and all. Professors are also good, they are very experienced and knowledgeable. They are also very dependable. Courses are scheduled properly for every semester.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: Placements are very good here as we are moving to a high marketing company. If your speaking skills and your CGPA is strong then you can get a high-package job in your 4th year ending. You can go to campus placement or any other placement.We have many clubs like sports, NSS, NCA, and some other clubs to refresh our minds Our infrastructure is good as our students are giving their opinions to make them strong as well as beautiful. We have a great library to study a lot.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: The course has a total duration of 4 years with 8 semesters in total. The frequency of exams is usually twice or thrice a  Semester. The faculty are extremely skilled and helpful and the resources available for studying are really. Great help.The fee structure for a semester is approximately 1 lakh per semester. But for SC ST and low-income students, the college will provide a full fee waiver. And for medium-income students, there will be a waiver of 1/3rdS of the total fee.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]

review_text:

Exams level is not that much easy and we have certain other things to be completed which carry credits like EL (Experiential learning) of every subject which carry 40 marks.The first-year fee is 2.5 lakhs after that I think it will come down to 2kakhs since I am in the first year only so I don't have confirmation about it. The cost to study is high for outside Karnataka students but it is not so for locals. I didn't get any scholarships because I am not from a government school. Placements are really good in our college but I don't have that much idea because I am in the 1st year only.

Supposedly the best placements in Karnataka after IIIT and NITK, but looking at the placements, they seem like peanuts, taking a job after B.E for chemical engineering at least is an absolute waste since the average package is 7-8 lakh p.a and the highest is 14-16 lakh p.a. Percentage for chem is less since most students do Masters or M.Tech after Chemical engineering. But yes it's the best u can get in Karnataka. The companies that come are actually good. The number too.The term exams are known as SEE or semester-end exams, the papers were fairly moderate but electrical was insanely hard for all students combined. This curriculum is good and the faculty members are also very good and supportive. They help a lot with different things. RV college has an annual fest named 8th Mile. And there will be some technical fest happening in college over the months(not that much fest here).

8th Mile is one of the biggest fests in Southern India organized by our college having a lot of celebrities in attendance. It is organised probably in the month of September. E Summit is another fest here at RVCE its magnitude is not as large when compared to 8th Mile but still, it's a sight to watch and be a part of, Aman Dhattarwal and Raj Shamani were a few celebs this time at the e-summit We have a good library which opens from 9 AM to 9 PM. The library holds a vast collection of books related to different subjects and also many other reading materials for research purposes too. There is a Digital library as well and the library has good seating for the students to sit and study. We have a gym, playground, badminton court, and table tennis court. college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: You have to pay fees for every semester; for one semester, fees are around 130000. In the college, there is a scholarship for those students whose parent's income is less than 5lakhs for sc/st/PWD, three is no tuition fees, and for those students whose parent's income is less than 1lakhs, there are no tuition fees, and whose parents' income is less than 5lakhs they have to pay 1/3rd of tuition fees.Our annual fest is Thomas, and this is conducted in October. In college, there is a Central library whose Name is Mahatma Gandhi central library almost every branch books are available, all sports facilities are available there are many clubs in the college, for example, UBA, NSS, NCC, CIVIL CONCLAVE, ROBOCORN, PAG, etc.

students become eligible of appearing for placements after the end of the third year which is the 6th semester numerous companies visit the college campus for placement among the top including Qualcomm, Atlassian, Samsung, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Walmart, etc. Overall companies visited the campus and made more than 1400 offers. The highest package this year was 58 lpaLPAd the average was around 15 lpa. Many students received PPOs which brought the placement percentage to a very high number of 85. As far as I am concerned intend to pursue higher courses.RVCE is the best college in Bangalore. I have got ELECTRONICS AND COMMUNICATION here and it's the best course of RVCE in Banglore. People use to compare RVCE with top NITs. Faculty: student ratio is 1:30. All professors are Ph.D. holders and have published many research papers. Every teacher has a different teaching style. Some use animations, some use real-life examples. We have 3 internals almost every month and later 1 external exam. If you are giving your best and concentrating in class then it will be very easy to pass exams.

I chose the course because I wanted to work in the pharmaceutical, industry, and cosmetic industries. It is a very vast course and you can work in any industry after studying chemical engineering. I have an interest in the chemical field, and that's the only reason I chose this course. The faculty-to-student ratio is decent for our course. Some of the teachers are good and some are okayish. CSE and ISE have around 18lpa+. Almost everyone gets placed in college. I've different plans after getting my degree. Either I'll go for a master's or MBA.

We have wonderful fests at RVCE. All of them have a very high budget and are usually very successful. There are loads of clubs as well, both technical and nontechnical clubs. There is a club for everyone and I highly recommend everyone to join a club. The campus is very big (Approx-52 Acres) one of the biggest in Bangalore. The Metro station is attached right next to the college.The fee is based on how you obtain admission to the college. If you are obtaining admission through KCET your fee irrespective of the course is 1L/year. If you obtain admission through COMEDK your fee irrespective of the course is 2.25L/ year. Financial assistance is provided if you are eligible and is based on the reservation system.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: I chose BE in Computer Engineering at Thapar University because I always saw myself as a software engineer and have worked on some projects while was in school, the faculties are helpful and for every lecturer 35 students are there. The classrooms are fine the teachers use Projectors, Edu Pen, and presentations to teach the classrooms are fully air-conditioned. We have 5 Basketball Courts,1 indoor badminton court, several outdoor badminton court,s and a well-maintained football field encircled by an Athletic Track. We have many societies some are tech some are cultural Thapar has room for students with all kinds of interests.

some of the companies that  are visiting regularly are Google, Microsoft, Apple, tower, Deloitte, bane, amazon, texas instruments etc

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text: I choose this course because I wanted to get in this line. The faculty and student ratio is more than enough there are so many teachers for different works and classes. Exams are not too tough not too easyIn the 4th year or the 7th 8th semester, students become eligible for campus placements. Companies visits in our college are Infosys TLC Mahindra etc. The highest package on campus is 12lac and the average package is4-6 lac. Almost 90% of students get placed in CS and 70% of the ece got selected

ECLECIA is the cultural fest of this college. DAKSH is the technical fest of this college. The annual fest happens in February. Different singers came here like Sonali Thakur, kk, Benny Dayal, etc Students of this college enjoy so much infested. It has a big central Library on the college campus where you will get several types of books apart from your curriculum. It has big classrooms. It has a different lab for different branches with modern equipment. Students play cricket, volleyball, basketball, football, table tennis, etc. It has a separate ground for all games. There are various clubs in this college like NSS and Rotract for social service, Resonance for singing, Ghungroo for dance, GDSC and Geeks United for coding, etc. All the clubs are run by the students of the college.8/10
I choose this course because I have an interest in programming. I have already little knowledge of programming. The faculty of our course is very good. They are always ready to help students. They clear all the doubts of the student. All faculties have their Ph.D. in their respective fields. They have also so much experience in teaching in their respective field. They have their teaching assistant who had done master's in their respective course. The faculty-student ratio is 1:30. There are two class tests and one semester in one semester. That means you have to give four class tests and two-semester examinations in one year. If you read all the notes provided by your faculties then you will easily get an 8 cgpa.

The campus life is ready great. There are all kinds of societies a person can join according to their interest. The cultural fest called 'Engifest' is held usually in February and the tech fest 'Invictus' is usually held in April. Other than these, many other fests are also conducted throughout the year. All classrooms are equipped with AC and wi-fi. The library has all the books we need in ample quantity. All kinds of sports facilities are provided in the college.The placement here is great. Students can start applying for projects or internships from the 4th semester. Campus internships are provided in the third year. Texas Instruments, Qualcomm, and NXP semiconductor are the major recruiter for ECE. They offer about 18-35 LPA CTC. Many students apply for an internship in the third year and get a PPO in the company. There are international offers also which cross a CTC of 1 cr.

There are a lot of placement opportunities for good students, and the college itself helps the students with good placements. There is a placement cell too. The college also provides special aptitude classes for good placement. In the current year, 2022 almost 1400 offers were given, and many students got double triple quadruple offers. The Top companies are TCS, Capgemini, Oracle, Adobe, Ola electric, Accenture, Infosys, and Cognizant. The highest on-campus offer was  16.64 LPA given to 15 students by Oracle, Adobe with  11 lap to 1 student, EQ technologies with 11.76 to 6 students, Lexmark with 10.5 LPA to 8 students, Ola electric with 9 LPA to 9 students and many more. There are also gate opportunities in the college, the college itself provides GATE classes. I will prefer to take a placement.The course basically teaches everything you can think about engineering. The faculty members are very helpful, particularly the ECE branch faculties are very good and the HOD is the best of all. I chose this branch because I think ECE is a branch in which you can go toward GATE preparation or you can also choose placement for IT industries, so basically you have lot more options. The faculty-to-student ratio is 1:20. All faculties are phd qualified and have doctorate degrees. Almost every faculty use ppt to make us understand every topic which is further shared with us. There are 4 mid sem exams and 2 end sem exams in a year. Mid sems are held every 2 months and end sems every 5-6 months, which are moderately tough.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: An annual fest is organized on the campus named Utkarsh during the spring season. The library is full of engineering books it's hard to find any literature in the library. Talking about sports there are two basketball courts, a cricket ground, and many badminton courts. There is a room for table tennis and a separate room for chess and carrom. There are many clubs like perianth racing which is known for designing and fabricating racing cars for formula Bharat. The clubs like Bhangra club, photography, coding, and many more.Fees are divided into three categories for students having a family income less than 1lpa there is a full tuition fee waiver and for those between 1 to 5 LPA, there is around a 50% tuition fee waiver. SC and ST students are also given a tuition fee waiver. The full tuition fee for 1 year is around 150000 which you have to pay if your family income is above 500000. There are many scholarship schemes.

I opt for this course because I have a great interest in machines and their working, Design of automobiles. faculty to student ratio can be estimated to 1:10.Faculties are well qualified with Ph.D. degrees. four exams are conducted every year that is two mid sems and two end sems and the schedule is followed quite strictly.Total fees in the academic are 125000 and hostel and mess fees are 48000 per annum. There is a hike in the fee structure from the year 2018. Scholarships are not there but a fee waiver can be guaranteed on the basis of family income less than 500000 for all candidates can be assured.

DTU college life is pretty liberal to students and allows us to spare time to pursue out interests. The annual fest conducted here is engifest, students generally get plenty of life to explore different opportunities here in fests like Yuvaan and esummit. engifest is conducted in February. 
The University library is well maintained with proper availability of books and journals needed for an undergraduate/postgraduate student/research scholar.
All classrooms are decently managed with ample space along with a centralized air-conditioning system, though you may sometimes face problems in locating the classes.
There is a separate sports complex made available for students 
There are many societies you can take part in, in fact, you can find and organize your society, and peer group here is very helpful.I chose Mechanical Engineering because DTU  is well renowned for its excellence in Mechanical Engineering for the past few decades, also the faculty to student ratio here is pretty good, there are 70 students per faculty member, and almost all of the faculty members are Ph.D. holders, and the term exams are conducted twice a semester and it was easy to pass them if you have a basic grasp of engineering concepts.

DTU is famous for its "chill" college life. Students get plenty of time to discover themselves and have fun.
There are many large-scale events and fests almost every month. Engifest is the largest cultural fest in north India, E-summit, Yuvaan is the biggest literary fest, and many more.
The library is well maintained and there are plenty of books from every field you would want to study.
All the classrooms are decently maintained and have centralized air conditioning. Though, you might face some difficulties in classrooms in the old departments because sometimes the ACs do not seem to work there. 
There are many societies that you can take part in. In fact, you can even form your own society with like-minded people.I have always been interested in Software Engineering as a subject. Moreover, the branch offers great packages. The average CTC for the Software branch is about 21 LPA. 
There are 99 students in one batch.
The faculty members are highly qualified but the teaching methods are quite old-fashioned as one expects from a government college.
Exams for the first year are difficult to pass for some students but they could be passed if you study regularly. From the second year, it depends upon the branch you are in for example software engineering is an easier branch and Electrical is a difficult one. There's 1 midterm exam and 1 end sem exam per semester.

Placements are okay if you see 4lac per annum as the average placement which is 6 lac per annum for tech branches on average.
You can do your own hustle and hit offline placement.
All big tech firms are recruiters like Amazon, Accenture, HCL, IBM, Infosys, etc.
Mostly all the Upper Branch students get placement offers and most of the lower branch students who did amazing in coding or studies also get the offers.
There are no exact figures but it's 96-98% placement.
Placements are not the problem for this college. But it's up to your side hustle to get placed above average.Course Curriculum is almost the same as most universities.
Faculty members are good and you'll find some more supportive teachers in your 1st yr. The only thing is you need to ask for the things you want, nobody will come and serve.
Well Qualified teachers and disciplined.

One class has 60 students.

More than 50 campiness came to campus during placement. students are getting placed in many MNCs.I cannot share many things about internships but summer internships are provided to students of 3rd and final years. there are many projects going through college in which students can participate and get a good amount paid for it.

For internships companies visit the college in the third year. There they recruit the students and give them a good stipend. Faculties are good at teaching and every group of about 10 students is given a mentor.

Almost every faculty member done PhD and some are PhD scholars.
Exams are average not difficult. So pass the exam not so much hard for students.
And I don't choose this course,i get this course according to my jee rank.Internships are offered to every student at the end of second year, but very few students grab paid internship in MNC.
But most students doing free internships.

review_text:

review_text: The options I was getting were mechanical, chemical, and all the branches after it, and from these branches I chose biotechnology, as I had a slight inclination towards the subjects of this course as compared to the mechanical ones. There are approximately 200-250 girls and 500-600 boys in one academic year. The faculty is quite good. The theory subjects are usually taught by the professor with a PhD degree, and the lab classes are mostly taken by the PhD scholars under the guidance of the professor. The professors have good knowledge of their subjects and usually provide practical knowledge, so you can ask for their help whenever you need it. There are basically 3 exams held in a semester. Two minor exams of 20 marks each and an end of semester exam for 40 marks. The rest of the marks are based on internal checking and assignments.As this college comes under the central government, there is no hike in fees every year. The hostel and mess fees are around 25000 and the semester fee is around 80000. So the total fee for one semester is approximately 100,000. Many merit-based programs are there to help these students who are unable to pay their fees and whose family's annual income is less than a specified amount. One of the scholarships is the Siemens India Scholarship, which is for first-year students. The college offers a variety of internships with stipends ranging from 20,000 to 15,000, though only 2-3 students receive a 15,000 stipend. But the stipend varies from company to company, but many offer a 50k stipend as well.

Nature and eco-club and many more clubs are available.My goal was to do chemical engineering, so I chose a sub-branch, there are sufficient enough faculty members, and overall faculty is good but some are not helping in nature, most of the faculty members are PhD holders. Term exams were taken after every two months and their difficulty was good enough.

I will say it was moderate To Get Pass but more often every Student gets a pass.They have many fests organized by the college like farewells, fresher day, independence day, flashmobs etc. they have a huge library section where you can get no. For books, they have one specified building for the library. The classroom is very good with a smart board projector and a comfortable bench. Sports is the best they always organize some tournament like Reva cricket, football etc as well they have a gym. Social group run by students Swach Bharat, e-waste and counselling.

if your income is more than 8 lac per annum then 100% tuition fee you have to pay if your income is between 1-5 lac per annum then 2/3 of your tuition fee will deduct and if your income is below 1 lac then you have to pay zero tuition fee.

review_text:
I wanted to pursue robotics as a career since high school, so I chose mechatronics as my course. This course includes subjects from electronics, mechanical, and computer engineering. The faculty-to-student ratio is about 1 professor for 60-90 students in a class and for tutorial classes 30:1.
All the professors here have a minimum qualification of PhD in their subject and tutorial teachers, they are the people who are currently pursuing their PhD at Thapar. The examination system here is pretty easy to understand.  We have a mid-semester exam and an end-semester exam both with a 10-15 minute MCQ quiz before the exam. Other than that, we have lab evaluations for our practical subjects. Exams are very easy to pass and are all based on a similar patterns every year.we are eligible for campus placements from the 6th semester. Internships for the mechanical branch are held in their 6th semester whereas, for COE and electronics, it is in their 8th semester. Companies like JP Morgan, American Express, D Shaw, and Cisco visit the campus for campus placements every year offering both technical and non-technical jobs to the students. A very high percentage of students get placed every year from all branches, 90% from mechanical and almost 100% from COE. My plan after getting the degree is to pursue my research goals in robotics.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The fees structure of the college is poor as they continue to increase the fees day by day not only the study fees but also the hostel/mess fees so it is becoming really difficult for the middle class and the lower class to pay for the everyday increasing fees. the fees for 4 years is around 22 lakhsplacements of Thapar are pretty good and have an average salary of around 10 lakhs per year. the highest CTC ever to be offered is 43 lakhs. companies like American Express, amazon, oracle, etc. visit the campus for job offers every year. many companies visit both tech and nontech for job placements

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The annual fest of Thapar is Saturnalia and it usually happens from September to October. The Library of Thapar is the best thing on campus. It is a huge library and all types of books are available there. The library is fully air-conditioned. There are AC as well as NON AC classrooms. Usually, all the classes for the first year are AC. All types of sports faculty are available on campus like lawn tennis, cricket, basketball, badminton, swimming pool etc. Sports are extracurricular activities are quite good. There are also merit cum means scholarships for financial support. There are also other scholarships provided by the university.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: My Branch is ENC ie Electronics and Computer. This is a new branch and aanuup growingone. This course provides both knowledge and skill in electronics as well as computer fields. The faculty here are Ok. but still, in the end, you have to work out yourself. Quality teachers are there but only oneper branch. Teaching methods need improvement. Due to covid Mid term exams were held within one and a half months after arrival. This course is very expensive though, At least you need to have 30 lakhs in your pocket to complete 4 yeats least east in renowned branches.Campus life is good. There are various societies in Thapar. Various events happen every year. Sports facilities are not up to that level, Only one badminton court, one main basketball court, swimming pool is also there. The lecture classes are having AC but only that which are built recently  There are many societies both tech as well as nontech. such as MLSC ( Microsoft learning society ), MUDRA  EDC( Internship Development Cell ), etc.  Tech fests are held every one and a half months.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Computer science is the stream that is in the boom in today's time. The faculty to students ratio is 100:1. All the teachers are minimum PhD which is why all the teachers are very knowledgeable. term exams were taken almost every 1.5 months and it was difficult to pass at times.College life is pretty good, the fest is done from time to time but only when the semester is of full size. They are conducted in month of september. classrooms are spacious and AC work just fine. extra curicular are there sports like cricket and football and many more.

There are mainly two exams held each semester which have called the mid-term examination and the end-term examination which is not much more difficult if your basic is good then you definitely pass. I choose this course mathematics and computing because it is demanding in the industry and provide a good placement, also this branch is great if you want to do artificial intelligence and machine learning course which is more demanding in the tech industry. All about faculty is good and friendly for students and student ratio is approx 20:80 and the teaching methods are great.Many companies are visited for internships like apple, and sprinkler. Apple gives a stipend to students approx 150000 per month and this year sprinkler gives the highest stipend of 200000 per month for an internship. And also you need to make a 2 project minor project in the 7th semester and a major project in the 8th semester which is compulsory for placement.

The reason behind choosing this course is that this course benefits you if you are interested in artificial intelligence or data science. A lot of maths you have to study in this course. It has also a good scope in cryptocurrency. The faculty is also good .some of the faculty are very senior and experienced like H.C Taneja, JP Kesari,, and many more. Their teaching method is very good, they tried to make students comfortable. Term exams are very frequent this year due to make up for the loss due to Covid-19. But the term exam is of moderate level.There are many Fest in the Dtu. Some are annual fests and some are organized by the societies of Dtu. Some of the famous national-level fests are Homecoming and Engifest. There are an adequate amount of amenities in the classroom to help students like projectors for presentation,a.c rooms, etc. Dtu has their sports team to represent them in different competitions. There are various societies run by students in Dtu.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Campus life is also pretty decent. Hostels are nice enough . There are multiple canteens , night canteens . Sports facilities are also pretty nice . The campus consist of various outdoor grounds as well as indoor places . Further the main annual fest include Utkansh , Techfest . Further various clubs are also here for fun .The couse curriculum is nice. The branch is a bit related to robotics . Its main base is semiconductors . One of the major reasons for choosing this branch was that I was only getting alloted this branch as per my rank . And further my research also proved that among all the branches that I can get , this ones the best .

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Basically, I have enrolled in college last year, and it's my 1st year in the college I have given all exams online and the frequency of exams is like there are multiple quizzes on the short part of the syllabus and end sem at the end, the difficulty of exams is normal and it's very easy to pass. the teachers teaching us online are very well qualified and they know how to teach online so that we understand everything. they also take query classes.Currently, I am a guy in 1st year so I have less experience but yes I have seen my seniors in college getting very good placements and internship opportunities, it only depends on your skills and somewhat luck too, the rest of college provides a good scope in jobs and placement.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
There are many societies like - Virsa, Frosh, and ASAIC  which mostly conducts fests, mela, proms, ramp walk, etc

college_name: IIT Ropar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: Fee is quite less than private colleges as IITs are government colleges but placements are too high than private colleges. And for scholarships I have somewhat less knowledge as I am a first year undergraduate. But placements is quite high. As average package for CSE branch is 26lpa and highest domestic package is 82 lpa.About faculty, faculty is highly experienced and extra-ordinary in their respective fields. You can ask your doubts in class and via email. In case if respective professor is not there then you can ask their TAs doubt anytime they will help you. Each TA have around 30 students.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: The college hosts two annual fests cultural fest Utkansh and tech fest TechNiti both are quite good many celebrities like Hardy Sandhu , Gajendra Verma has performed in these fests in past years.There is a huge central library where everything is available for the students there's also an online portal for the Library from where students can access everything they require. The college infrastructure is quite good. All the classrooms are air conditioned and are equipped with digital boards projectors. Most of the buildings are newly constructed. Wi-Fi is available throughout the campus.The sports infrastructure is also very good at NIT Jalandhar. It has a football ground, Cricket field, 2 basketball courts , 3 volleyball courts , 3 indoor tennis court, badminton court and all the equipments are available and everything is in sound condition apart from this every hostel also has a badminton or volleyball court of their own. There are many clubs like Dance club , singing club etc All the teachers are well qualified and are knowledgeable but yet like every other government college the professors don't teach quite well except few good ones. The faculty: student ratio is nearly 1:19 as there are 208 teaching staffs for 4000 students. The course is relevant to the industry's demand. The course at ICE is updated timely and it make the students ready for industrial works. There's a criteria of minimum 75% attendance to appear for the End semester examination.The end semester examination are moderate in difficulty level if one studies just a bit he/she can get a descent GPA. Very few students get backlogs around 95% of the students who appear for the end semester examinations pass without any backlogs

The reason to choose this course is as I'm not getting higher branches and I also like mathematics and faculty here is quite okay and many faculty have written their own book which is great and our sem was online and which not that difficultThe placement of DTU is really great this year as many big companies come to give good placements and the number of offers is more than 1500 and the highest package is more than 1 cr and more than 90% of students get placed.

I choose this course because this was the best course I was getting based on my rank.
in the first year, each batch has 75-80 students and every subject has one teacher.
and in the 2nd year, there are department wise batches and each batch has 50-60 students based on branch.
The examination is centralised and of the medium level, there is not very much academic pressure in the collegeThe fee structure is given on the DTU website it follows the same for the year but it is revised every year so that the right service can be given to the students but at the time of admission the fees for 4 years are already told and that is fixed. My course is 214000rs.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The faculty is well educated. Some of the teachers are very good and friendly but apart from them most of the teachers only teach through ppt, and slides and only focus on completing the syllabus rather than making the students understand the syllabus. Most of the teachers are unapproachable outside the teaching hours and do not clarify their doubts. Apart from that the teachers are good and can make the students understand the concept if studied hard. We have to study on our own and cannot only rely only on the faculty.  One can score very good marks if he/she started preparing a month prior to the exams.The campus is very big and beautiful. There are all the facilities in the college from sports grounds for different sports to shopping complexes and other stuff. The buildings are fully air-conditioned and well furnished. There are many events that take part frequently along with many societies. There are also food shops/stalls on almost every corner of the campus. The campus is also very green and pleasant to look at. The hostels are very well maintained and the rooms and mess are very neat and clean. Proper hygiene is maintained.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Summer Internship- June, July 
6 Month Internship at the end of the fourth Year

Library is pretty huge and has almost all academic material one can every need.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The course curriculum for computer engineering is core-subject-based. The student-faculty ratio is good for my branch (approximately 100:1). Almost all the faculty members have completed their PhD. The mid term exams were easy enough if one studies regularly. The grading system is relative so for tough exams passing marks would be lower compared to an easy one.Life at the campus is pretty chill and fun. There is a techno culture fest called Saturnalia which is usually held in the month of November. The library of our college is amazing with thousands of books and ebooks. Classrooms are air-conditioned and well maintained. There are a lot of tech and nontech societies and a student can join multiple societies.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Our annual fest is Rendezvous, which is Asia's largest cultural fest. It generally held in April every year. Almost every book is available in college's library. Classrooms are fully air conditioned, fire proof, with 2-3 projectors(in every class room) and each of them is capable of holding 300 students at a time. Any sport can be played here, all facilities are available.Placements are really good here. IIT grads are highly demanded in multinational tech giants. Most of the students who apply for placements get placed in first 1-2 days of placement season. Highest packages go as high as 2 crores and lowest can be mere 10 lakhs. This year, 60 students got packages over 1 crore.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: campus infrastructure is outstanding. wifi connection is somewhat weak. hostels are well maintained and neat & clean. .quality of mess food is poor. students use to order their food through Zomato or Swiggy. all sports from swimming to football, tennis, cricket, etc are available inside the campus. huge library with all kinds of study facilities like discussion room, projector room, the reading room is available for students.fees are very high. approx 25-28 lakh is the amount a student will spend for the 4 years of engineering. Thapar is increasing its fees every year.  most of the students move toward PGs due to the high amount of fees charged for hostels and mess fees. financial assistance is not provided in Thapar but other colleges like NITs and IIITs do provide their students with financial aid.

I choose this course because my rank somewhat landed me in this branch simple. 
The faculty-student ratio is good in the university than in other institutes.  
Qualifications of the professors are the level best all are post-doc or post-graduate some are doing parallel their post and teaching us others senior professors are having a work experience more than my age seriously. 
Our JU belief is that the quality of the examination and evaluation process doesn't get diluted our Ju was among the first and the few who should spine of conducting offline exams because it may be bad for the students in short term but was good for the long horizon. 
Seriously they believe for the betterment of its students it's upon students what they understand. 
Exams are not easy at all they are tough but can be easy if you attend classes of the professors.Sanskriti is the annual fest it occurs after the 1st sem or between May and June and srijan is tech fest it occurs before Sanskriti and there are sports tournaments also occurring.  We have one of the largest collections of books and articles in eastern India in our central library. 
Most of the classrooms are well ventilated few are air-conditioned.  All have projectors equipped in each classes. 
For sports we have tennis ground, basketball, volleyball courts,  a big playground in the heart of JU where it can be seen folks having criket, football and rugby.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
Hostel life at IIT is quite Integral part of life. There are many events and cultural activity every year like mood indigo, Techfest, Inter IIT,Ahvan and in campus there is central library  where you can find all books you want.there are big classrooms and presentation screen + big board  is also there.At campus, almost all sport ground and board games available and also happen competition in each sports.Tere is many websites which is made by student only like in each techfest and event websites.(ex. ahvan iitb,techfest iitb,mood indigo iitb etc..)The cource curriculum is one of the best things in IITs. There are many opportunities in many field.The teaching quality is best.faculty to student ratio in our branch is approximately 1:20  and all faculties are done world-class Ph.D. from international callege and have a lot of experience too.
Every week there is a quiz and assignment in  most of the cource and there are two exams per semister (midsem & endsem) and the question paper was average.

Life is too good at BIT MESRA. There is a huge scope of development in every field here. The annual fest of BIT is Bitotsav which is conducted every year in February. Apart from it, we have a technical fest called Pantheon and a sports fest called Vajra. The Central Library of the college has a huge amount of books. Students can issue the books as per their needs. BIT has a big sports complex with various facilities including a gymnasium. Lecture Halls are quite good in size with audio video facility provided. There are a good number of cultural and technical clubs present in BIT MESRA. These clubs conduct recruitment drives every year for the students enthusiastic about the field the respective club is associated with. BIT also has an NGO named Drishti. NCC and NSS are also there in the college.Students become eligible for placement after the completion of their third year. Many renowned companies visit the campus. Generally, Computer Science students receive quite a hefty package with lots of opportunities for other branch students too. There have been offering up to 44 Lakhs from Microsoft. The percentage of students getting placed is quite high.

Campus life is very good. And you feel relaxed, peace everywhere like a library, sports complex, OAT etc. The college life includes improving behaviour manners, and speaking skills and when we see around we get inspired by different students. All are talented students some in the study some. In sports and some dancing. Annual fest is held every year many stars celebrity come. We enjoy ourselves a lot there. It helps by e summit, manifest Or others. The availability of books is good here every semester books are there and every branch. The classroom is fully ac and with projectors, lights, and big desks.Many students take internships here and get help in their placement. The internship is around 3-4 months it's taken 3 years. Companies are Samsung, Microsoft, Paytm, airtel etc provide internship and after job opportunities. We have to make different innovative projects to get better placement. The package of tech placement is a very good average package is 8-9 lakhs per annum.

The fee for my course is Rs.1,82,000.
Excluding the hostel and transport fees.
There are 2 scholarships. One for Scoring 85 above percentile in JEE MAINS and 85 above in PCM in class 12th.Both are of Rs 25k annually. The scholarships can be canceled if the student fails to maintain more than 8 CPI in the coming years.In the first year,there are 2 semesters,In the 2 semester all the branches have to study some basic subjects of all the branches.The faculty here is excellent clearing the basic concepts of class 12th topics and giving attention to all the students.For every subject,there is around 13-15 faculty

On the college campus lot of tech,annual, sports fest is conducting every year. Here two major library complexes where you can find textbooks and reference books to prepare for exams and for research purposes Classroom has average facilities.Here so many social group like people group and so many club is available and they are run by students.Our college provides placements for students from the 6th semester and they send Google form links which we have to register first. Company like Amazon, TCS,  Microsoft, Apple, Infosys and many such companies comes here for students.Average package is around 4.5 to 6.5 lpa. More than 95 percent students placed from the college every year.

B Tech is what I was planning to do but can't get to a good govt College so came here as it was near my home and had a decent placement record for b tech cse.
The faculty-student ratio would be probably around 1:200.
The faculty is well educated and some teachers are really good with their teaching methods.
2 exams per semester (2 semesters a year) exams are easy to pass but difficult to get good marksthe campus is great. students are helpful and here they have made an environment where you have to study the whole day if you are in the college team then you can practice. Overall if you are too serious about sports this campus isn't recommended.
There are several clubs and societies in the college... Yea they work decently and have their own fest kind of things every year

Many companies offer internships like Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, and IBM with stipends of around 45000 per month on average. 
Projects always differ, generally based on Android development, machine learning, and on specific topics and areas that the company wants first the company engineers train you in initials days and later on you can work with them on specific projects, Internship period is generally June - August, College gives 2.5 months holidays for internships for 2nd, 3rd and 4th-year studentsFrom 6th-semester students start getting placed, internships you can get from 3rd sem itself. 
Almost all types of companies come from tier 1 to tier 3, eg. Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, Flipkart, Adobe, etc to all tier 2 and tier 3 companies, highest package varies but is around 50 lakh, avg package for CSE is 10lpa+. For CSE almost all get placed, I have thought of joining a good company

Campus Life here is top notch because in our campus we got many clubs like e-cell and et.c and all of them are run by students of our college. We have separate clubs for each sports and we organise sports event within 2 weeks .we have very decent classes our classes are mounted with projector and a.c. and each class have personal p.c. in it. We have 10 department library and 1 Central library so that make students to easily avial the books without any problem . Our campus organises a Annual fest in December which is massiveCourse and curriculum here is easy and that's why I opted to study here . We have 1:20 faculty ratio here so it is easier for faculty to look out for students most of the faculty here have completed there ph.d and they opt for classic method of teaching. Usually exams here are of moderate difficulty level . Mid terms are easy and end term exams are little harder to crack.

Our college hosts multiple fests throughout the year. From E-Summit to Bitotsav there is no dearth of fests. And each fest opens up more opportunities for students to explore and to grow. Our cultural fest Bitotsav is a grand fest and is in its own feat. 
Regarding the library, we have a fully air-conditioned multi-storey building dedicated to the library only, It has all the course books. There is also a book bank for SC/ST students. Also, the library has multiple newspapers and journals.
The classrooms are very well built. They are fully air-conditioned and spacious, and also have projectors for better teaching.
Our college has a very good sports complex having all kinds of sports amenities from a badminton court to a shot-put ground. Also, it has a very well equipped gym (one for boys and one for girls).
There are multiple clubs for students to participate in and grow from Leo club to EDC.All professors are PhD qualified and are knowledgable. The course curriculum is not relevant to today's world, but then the curriculum is the same for all engineering colleges (as AICTE gives the curriculum). Professors are friendly, but some are exceptions too. They provide extra time for doubt solving and can go the extra mile to help the students. We have three quizzes, one mid-sem and one end-sem in a semester. One needs to score 5 SGPA to pass a semester.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: The total fees for the course are 555000. But if your income is less than 1L, you can get a full tuition fee waiver, and if under 5 L, you can get a 2/3rd tuition fee waiver. No tuition fees for SC/ST students. Students can also apply for government scholarships.I chose CSE because I liked computers & technology. This college gives qualified faculties, all have PhD in their respective branches. After the COVID period, teaching is an online-offline hybrid, which is better. Term exams were average on level of hardness.

The teachers are highly qualified either in masters or Ph.D. the ratio of the student is approx 1:60 , the teaching is based on Lec and tutorial hence covers both the theory and practical form and touch the aspects of lifeThe fee structure is either to be paid in one full go or semester-wise, there are multiple scholarships based on the class 12 the result or how your performance in academic scholarship is also given based on the sports.

Bitotsav and pantheon are the biggest events in the college. All course books are available in librsry plus a lot more. Classes have very comfy seats with good ambience and centralized AC. Sports complex is huge and provides all ammenties. All possible clubs are present at college. You are a literature fan, got you covered. Oh you like Space oddyesses, still covered.The fees for Mesra is moderated by the admission department and a general pattern suggests that the fee increases 1.2 lakh(for four years) in a year. It costs approximately 15 lakhs for btech in 2021 batch. G.P. birla scholorship is given to students who perform well. However you can also get student loans very easily since it's a reputed institute.

Students become eligible to sit on the campus from the 7th semester. Students get internships from as early as fourth sem. 120+ recruiters with 746 job offers are there in the placement season. Companies like DShaw, Goldman Sachs, Microsoft, Amazon Flipkart come to select students in campus.Bitotsav and pantheon are the biggest events in the college. All course books are available in library plus a lot more. Classes have very comfy seats with good ambience and centralized AC. Sports complex is huge and provides all ammenties. All possible clubs are present at college.

Faculty to student ratio is about 1:12
All the faculties are very well qalified the professors have doen their PHD from IITs .
They teach application based only.
Each module has a quiz .rigorous study is needed to pass.

BIT Mesra conducts one of the largest annual fests known as 'Bitotsav'. The fest is fully organized and controlled by student body. There are many tech fest, hackathon etc conducted by the tech clubs like IET,IEEE etc. Library is decent with most of the textbooks available with help books, magazines,journals. You get to use full sports complex with gym,badminton court, track etc. There are many clubs run by student body like IET, Rotaract etc there are more groups of students for various purposes just not official though.The placement for cse/it branch in BIT Mesra is very good the reason to choose these branches. Thanks
