I chose IT bcoz well I was getting it...I didn't have anything planned before and my brother who did too is passed out from this college referred me here. college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text:
Genral fee college including hostel and mess fee is about rupees 152000 per semester but it varies with family income of candidate
For candidates with family income Campuses placement of IIT Kharagpur is probably one of the best in India with over 300 companies visit campus last year and over 1600 student places in 1st phase of placement with average pacage was 16 lac and highest offer given was 2.5 crore

-- The fees were taken as it is what they said while applying to college..and they were
Mess - 3360 per month
1st year - 55000 per sem and hostel 55000
2nd year onwards it varies in sem fee about 5k greater and hostel fee was same.
-- The scholarship criteria were there ..which were given to some students who got 93% above in 12th or 95% in mains if existAnnual fests and tech fests were good and were celebrated every month.
The classrooms were also good having a Central AC system in classes.
Sports and extracurricular activities were also included and a good time also provided for the students to play ..and the ground is also nice 
There are so..many clubs run by students in the college.

I have a keen interest in robotics and programming so I decided to persue my BTech in Electronics and communication engineering. Faculty members are good.They are highly educated and teach the concept very well. Exams held on time and are not so difficult to pass if you have studied earlier.North india's one of the largest and popular fest named Engifest is held by Delhi Technological University. Unfortunately it is cancelled this year due to covid. We have a huge library with loads of books. Classrooms are clean well equiped. We have a huge sports complex also.

each year college increases its fees by 10%, currently fee for 1 year is 2,06,000 rupees which is 9 lakh rupees approximately for a four-year BTech, college also provides fee concessions to needy students of 100%, 50% or 25% of tuition fee, accordingly to students need.Mathematics and computing are one of the most underrated branches of engineering, faculties in dtu are very interactive and always ready to help students, they use technology and classical board for better visualisation and better understanding.

Delhi Technological University was one of the colleges that was on top of my priority list. faculty are highly qualified in their respective curriculum, and exams are held in the midterm semester and end-term semester patterns.campus life at Delhi technological university is amazing but organizes 2 events/fests almost every month, the library has numerous books on each course, and first-year students can also join societies to more explore and learn.

The campus life is very very very good. The campus is 625 acres. Fests are held regularly. Some big fests are POSUA, and TECNOESIS(it is our college's tech fest). There is a special building for sports(indoor games). There is a cricket ground football ground and everything else. There are many clubs like a coding club, dancing club and all.In JOSAA last round I was allocated this college with an electrical branch so I choose this branch. In our batch, there are 85 students with 16 teachers. So, faculty students ratio is quite good. Some faculty are excellent but some are not up to that mark. Exams are taken after every 15 days.

The campus life is amazing and KIIT organizes the KIIT fest every year this fest is the biggest cultural fest in Kit. The newly built central library is like an office of an IT company and comprises more than 30k books. You will find almost all the sports complexes. There are many clubs and societies for extra skills and co-curricular activities. The kit has almost all the sports complexes available for the students. KSAC- KIIT Student Activity Centre is for all co-curricular activities. The kit has also coding societies.In the present world, everything depends on technology so taking computer science as my career is the best option for me. The faculties are very friendly with the students. They will try their best to explain the concepts except for some of the professors. The student-faculty ratio is 12:1. Two exams are held every semester - mid sem and end sem and quiz tests are also taken.

the fest is quite good, one of the best in Delhi. the annual fest is moksha,its amazing even the tech fest or hacknsut organised by the clg was India's second student organized hackathon. we have big library with lots and lots of textbooks, if you want to read fiction books you can find them in the hostel library for that you have been a hostler. sports and extracurricular activities and competitions are quite often but if becomes bit hectic to manage and begin a Covid batch our course started late ,so to keep up the load adds on. classes are form 8am to 6pm . but if you mange well you do get enough time chill outcs is the most booming therefore I choose it, the facility are Ph.D.'s and most of them have great knowledge and some are passionate about teaching while some just come and go. the bad part is 75 percent attendance criteria, we have classes from 8 am to 6 pm and most of the time is gone in clg. one thing is for sure in most colleges that you have to study yourself whether for exams or placement and the attendance criteria suck. exams are twice every semester and classiest too. if you study a week before exams you are good to go.

The annual fest conducted is BLITZLAG. It is conducted in February every year. The library here is top-notch and with the availability of plenty of books for current Engineering classes. Classrooms are pretty good and ambient for students with full AC'S wifi and comfortable seating. Sports & extracurricular activities are good. There is a variety of clubs here and you can participate in any type of club you want and pursue your interests.Students become eligible for placements after 3rd semester. Placements here are top-notch. In the year 2021 highest package offered was 64 LPA with an average of 12 LPA. Major recruiters here include apple, adobe, Amazon, Texas Instruments, Deshaw, and Deloitte.Nearly 85-90% of eligible students get placed every year. My plan as of now are to grab placements after getting a degree

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
IIT Guwahati placements 2022 concluded recently with an overall 73.20% placement rate. As per the latest report, over 180 recruiters participated in the placement process and successfully placed 910 students in various new-age job profiles. Further, the highest domestic and international packages offered to BTech and BDes students were INR 1.2 crore per annum and INR 72 LPA, respectively. Not only this, over 28 international offers and 179 PPOs were also offered to the students of BTech and BDes programs during the 2022 placement season. The top recruiters of IIT Guwahati in 2022 included Uber, JP Morgan, Microsoft, Apple, etc.The teaching faculty members in our college are highly qualified and educated. 
The course curriculum is updated recently, and students are benefited from it as it provides good marks to only lengthy and tough topics. 
The course is relevant which makes students eligible for various jobs like PSU jobs or makes them ready to appear for the Indian engineering services exam.
They provide the best teaching methods with proper guidance and help to the students.

The annual fest is KIIT FEST organized every year during Sep or Oct and there are many tech fests organized from time to time every year.
KIIT has a central library, an 8 storied building spread over an area of 10,000 sq km.
It contains approx 10,18,434 books, 28,078+ E-Journals, Newspapers in over 27 languages, E-thesis & Dissertations - 4 Million from 1700 top universities of the world.
KIIT has wi-fi multimedia air-conditioned classrooms, virtual classrooms, video conferencing, state-of-the-art laboratories, and many more other facilities.
KIIT has 16 sports complexes sprawling over an area of 29 acres which include BCCI Approved Cricket Stadium, Internation Archery Stadium, Chess stadium, and many other sports facilities.

KIIT a dedicated center for student activities that is KSAC in which there are many societies span across many domains like KORUS, KALAKAAR, QUTOPIA, KZARSHION, KIIT FILM SOCIETY, TEDX-KU, MUN-SOC, KRAFTOVITY, KIMAYA, KRAYA & KUBER, and the list continues.I am pursuing B. Tech CSE at Kalinga Institute Of Industrial Technology as its CSE branch is very demanding and the college achieved NIRF Ranking under 50 for many years and continues to do so in the field of engineering.
Faculty Student Ratio is good at about 1:30.
Yes, faculties are qualified but some teachers' teaching methods can be better.

The University strictly follows Academic Calender, the Mid Sem and End Sem Exams were timely conducted and the level was moderate.
Anyone can get a good SGPA if attends the classes sincerely and regularly.

It's not really hard to score well in the LPUNEST exam if you do you can avail of the scholarship, which also adds up your 12th Marks to it. 
Fees are not really an issue, My fees for SEM 1 were 1,12,500 INR after the scholarship and 3000 INR additional exam fees, Which any engineering student can afford to pay for such best education and college.
Additionally, you can also do any part-time jobs which are available near the college area.
Also, Internships are provided to students by visiting several companies, and the college also removes the attendance restriction for interns as they genuinely support a lot and put their hard work into making projects a part of the curriculum.
Placement cell helps you for trainings and the LPU campus had 42 lakhs was the highest package and also this year 1 student also got 59 lakhs package from google California almost in CSE.I chose this course keeping in mind Future demands and developments related to the computer and science Industry.
Saying about Faculty:student ratio is 1:30, as there is much care taken about by the college.
Also, the faculty is good and highly educated in the teaching field, and have a Ph.D. in their subjects, which brings the best teaching methods to the professors.
SEM 1 AND 1 MIDTERM EXAM, the exams are really important and given much importance.
Additionally, there is no difficulty in passing the Exam as you study hard and give your best.

Campus Life at PSG is awesome, they provide enough freedom for students in academics as well as in dress code and socialism. There is a huge library on this campus called GRD memorial library, in this library, there are more books such as books related to academics, novels, journals, nontechnical books, etc. Classrooms are well equipped with smart projectors. Many clubs are available in this college students can express their talents in that clubsThe fee structure for this course is reasonable when compared to other engineering colleges. The fee structure per year for this course is 83000. There are n number of scholarships available in this college such as Central government scholarships, state government scholarships, and private scholarships., The campus jobs are average and good when compared to other colleges.

My course duration is 4 years so during the seventh-semester students start applying for companies and the placement season last till mid of the eighth semester. Google, Microsoft, Amazon India
DeShaw, Deloitte, Wipro, Mckinsey, and Atlassian are some of the top recruiting companies more than 300 offers were made last year and a lot of preplacement offers were also made. Software engineer and web developer are the top roles offered. The highest package made was 1.25 crore and the lowest was 9 Lacs in our course with an average of about 18-20 LPA. Almost all the students are placed in our course with a decent package. As my course is a specialized one either I will continue it further and get an MS degree in it or do a job for 3-4 years in a tech company and then do MBA or I can do something else as well if I will develop an interest in doing that thing.I have chosen this course because it includes knowledge of computer science along with artificial intelligence and the future is all about AI and this field excites me. We have around 5 main courses in a semester and separate faculty for each and on an average of 75 students in each class. All teachers are well qualified and some of them have done their Ph.D. from IITs. Teaching is done mostly through smart TV in form of ppt but some 
teachers also provide handwritten notes. Mainly two exams are taken that are mindset and endsem, besides class tests are also there for internal grading. One needs to have all of the basic concepts clear and good practice to score well in exams and pass with a good GPA.

Udbhav is the biggest fest in our college it conducts around July or August but due to COVID the months have been changed and many more tech fests occur frequently in the college. Library is the biggest and you can't any kind of books I think we have the best library than other colleges and we get a digital library also which helps so many students. In classroom we have projector and fans and good ventilation. Sports is the major part of the college it conducts regularly in the campus. There are many social groups and clubs in the college few are NSS, ECELL, NAKAMA, and STARDUST. etc.I chose this course because I was very much interested in that field and the faculty is very good and our branch is divided into two sections and teachers are good for teaching. The teaching method is good but it should be advanced more to get most of the students and for every semester there will be 2 tests and 1 semester-end exam passing is easy in the tests.

Actually our first semester was expected to be online so the curriculum was made keeping the online point of view.Since,the covid cases reduced and the colleges were ordered to get reopened.But the curriculum didn't change which was right.So we were having continuous evaluation in our curriculum. Semester length was reduced to 4 months from 6 months due to covid.So we were having three class tests and two surprise tests out of which best two CTs and one best ST were supposed to be considered.Moreover there were mid term evaluation through projects and innovation. Practical and workshop classes are there and continuous viva was also there.So we were busy throughout the semester from the academics point of view.You will automatically get the environment which will push you to pass rather than failing.I chose ECE as it is an evergreen branch,you can go to any field be it govt, be it private,be it public,be it startups.Proper and spacious labs are there ,one student can perform easily.College life is too awesome . Being a day scholar is painful.If u come DTU then try your level best to get campus life.In first semester I enjoyed three fests already.So the biggest fest of North India,the Engifest is held on 29-30 April.I am excited, 'crowd dekhne layak hoti h'.After engifest there will be yuvaan film and literature fest.Library is too big and designed very nicely,You will get everything there.Classrooms are large and spacious with proper sitting arrangements and WIFI.The college is full of extra curricular activities.There are hundreds and thousands of societies for that. Sports complex is there .You will find every game except swimming.A huge cricket ground is there, volleyball, basketball, tt and badminton courts are there. Few weeks back DTU TEAM went out to IIT kanpur for sports meet. And our volleyball team got gold medal beating all other IITs team, NSIT team.There are many websites run by out college students.

The fees of nut are 2 lakh 12 thousand for the first year after this it increases every consecutive year. The living expense outside the university like pgs are expensive even the hostel is expensive. In the third year, we get a chance to work in companies but that is also by cracking their interview.The fest in the nut is moksha. The library has lots of books in it but you can only borrow them but having a nut student id which they give you at the end of the first year so for the first-year library is just a spot for reading. The infrastructure is good for first-year students only

As DSCE is an autonomous college the Exam Scene is relatively easier when compared to other VTU Affiliated colleges.
There are 3 Internal and one-semester end every Semester. Exams are checked on the campus itself. In other non-autonomous colleges, the Semester-end exam is prepared by VTU and is checked externally which makes it difficult to score. It's better to be in an autonomous institution.There are few Technical, Entrepreneurship, and Cultural Clubs on the campus.
The Technical clubs like Point Blank and GDSC provide a good environment for students interested in Coding and Development Skills.
The IEDC Club is for Entrepreneurship regarding Finance, Management, and Social media advertisements.

My course is MCE (mathematics and computing engineering). This course is relatively new compared to others. I have choosen this course as it is first of all a tech branch and has a lot of applications in ML and AI. Students can also go to the finance field through this branch. Faculty at DTU are highly qualified and some had completed their PhD in USA, Ireland and UK.  Mostly in every class faculty v/s students ratio is 1:70. Exams will be conducted as per the faculty and administration of DTU only. Some teachers do postpone exams at the request of students.The campus life of DTU is quite good. You have full freedom to do anything inside the campus just be in a disciplined manner. There are many societies in the college where you can pursue and enhance your talent and capabilities. The annual fest of DTU i.e Engifest is the 2nd biggest fest in north India after IIT-D. many students come here to participate in this 2-day event. The library is equipped with every book that you will require in engineering or for other fields as well. the infrastructure of DTU Library is of world-class level.

The annual fest of DTU - manifest is quite popular and among the best in north india. Many big artists come and perform and it is altogether a memorable experience. Besides that there are many events which happen regularly. There are many societies and teams of various disciplines which helps student grow. The infrastructure and campus itself is very big around 170 acres. Talking about library, classroom, labs, medical centre, sports complex everything is really nice.Talking about placements in DTU, it is getting higher and higher after every year. The average package is about 12-13 LPA. Highest domestic package goes up to 64 lakhs (offered by amazon) and 1.04 CR as international one. More than 300 companies visit campus for recruitment. And everyone gets placed in best of the companies.

Fee Structure is yearly, you have to pay tution fee one's in a year and you can get hostel and you have to pay hostel fee each sem. Tution fee per year is 206000 and  52000 per sem. You can get scholarship on your performance in class.Campus Life is really great you gonna enjoy a lot in college there is lot of fun in college special during feast . You can play any sports what ever you want there are many inter college sports event in college.

Almost 100% of the students get placed in top MNCs. Campus Placement starts after 3rd year and the internship season starts on starting of 3rd year. Most of the top MNCs visits for campus placement and this year 2022 highest package is RS. 52 lpa.Campus life is amazing KIIT. KIIT also organises KIIT fest every year which is the biggest cultural fest in the eastern Indian college. The newly built central library is like an office of an IT company and comprises more than 20k books.

Our placement is very good there is average package of 15 lakhs and in our college international package also comes many companies come on campus like uber, google, facebook, amazon , Adobe Netflix, etc. Start up also come to take students percentage wise our college placement is 92% in tech branch and all over branches including core and non tech branches placement is 85%.
My plans after getting degree to join any reputated company with good placement.
This year our highest package is 64 lakh in apple domestic and last year is 1.05 crores in international company.Due to covid, there is change in exam pattern we have to give 3 class test , 2 minor test and teacher select best 2 class test marks and 1 minor test marks and there is one innovative project for each subject and assignments also there for each subjects. The semester size is 3 months due to covid .The paper level is decent if you study 2 or 3 days before you will get good marks.

Basically due to my rank in jee mains i preferred the mathematics and computing branch because it is a technical branch and has a good placement records in the past placements, there are 67 boys and 7 girls in my class and total 175 students in mathematics and computing the teachers are highly qualified but not good in teaching, exam's were easy this time due to less syllabus.A fee is 890000 for 4 years and there are many scholarship programs in the university, very less students avail these scholarships whose family income is around 1lakh they will get a scholarship of 50% in their tution fee and who have a ration card will get 100% fee waiver and between 1-2 lakh income students will get a scholarship of 25%

Campus placements start after the end of the 3rd year and the internship season starts on starting of the 3rd year. Most of the top MNCs visit for campus drives. In this year's 2022 session, the highest package is 52 LPA offered by Yugabytes.Nowadays, everything depends on technology so taking computer science as my career is the best option for me. The faculties are very friendly with the students. They will try their best to explain concepts except for some professors.

Kiit organises many fests every year and the KIIT fest is the biggest cultural fest in eastern Indian college. the newly build central library is like an office of an IT company with a capacity of more than 20k books and articles covering all the subjects. All sports facilities are available.The fees structure is 1.75 lacs per annum(tuition fees) and hostel fees depend on the type of room one takes.3 seater non  Ac 28k per sem and 2 seaters non-ac 40k, 3 seater ac 50k and 2 seater ac 60k. If your rank is under 1000 in the KIITEE scholarship of Rs. 10kper month is provided.

There are a large number of fests organized on the college campus.
There is a multi-storied library available on the campus available for 7days a week.
The classrooms are also very nice.
There are many clubs like the nature club, a dancing club run by students.The fess of this course is quite large. 
The total fee for the compilation of this course is around 1500000Rs for four years.
You can get a scholarship on the basis of LPUNEST.
You can also get scholarship on the basis of 12th marks.

Yes, there are many different types of activities, and fests going on during every semester, where you can participate and learn something new. sports facilities are also really well, like we have a ground for each and every sport, like cricket, badminton, Kabaddi and also we have an indoor sports ground for sports like table tennis.In the 3rd year companies for internships are getting started coming and companies like a sprinkler, Twitter, Adobe, de Shaw and many other big tech giants come that offer a very good amount of stipend, and internship is generally around 2-3 months depending upon the company.

I don't know much about the fest because I joined the college during the COVID period.  The campus is WIFi enabled with a digital library, the library is huge, and labs with all the necessary equipment. In which you can find any book or journals which you want. There are many clubs in our college that are managed by the CPA(Centre of performing Arts) team. Students get various opportunities to showcase their talent here. Every Saturday, Cultura is organised which is filled with loads of entertainment.The total fee for engineering is around 10,00,000 for the whole four yrs for COMEDK students and for KCET students it is around 5,00,000. For the management quota, I don't have any idea. For scholarship, I don't have any idea because scholarships is mainly available for KCET students.

There is no certain free hike policy available in this institution but the fee will be raised after the second year.
There is only a slight increase in fees. no further scholarship is available but if you score well in a final exam you will get a few concessions.life is campus is very beautiful campuses spread around 200 acres a huge campus there is also lots and lots of flour social club sports club and various interaction club such as NCC NSS. The facility for all the students is very efficient.

There are different kinds of fests conducted in Our college like projects fests, annual fests, Cultural fests like Furore Square, and ethnic day fests and tech fests. And every Saturday one Cultura is held in our college. There is a specific building for Library where you can find any journals and books which you want. You can find a basketball court, squash court, volleyball court, football court, Tennis court, and other indoor courts inside the campus. Many social groups and clubs are there inside the campus like IEDC, LENZ club, Pitchers desk, etc.I choose the EEE branch because DSCE gives more opportunities to their students for research and development and the placement for EEE is also good. The course syllabus will be updated as per the schedule. And university provides enough industrial exposure. Faculty members are experienced and one more thing if you will argue with any teacher they will become too bad for you. And the student ratio is 40:1. There will be three internal before every semester end exam and if will study before the exam you can pass with a good number of marks.

It's a private engineering college so of course, the fees are high, but if your rank is good then the tuition fees are pretty much affordable. hostel fee is a bit on the higher side. The scholarship is on basis of your rank and if your topper in the 12th Boards then you can avail 100% tuition fee waiverThere are mainly 2 types of packages offered in vit, i) Dream Offer (5 LPA+), ii) Super Dream Offer (10 LPA+) . There are many companies that visit VIT and u get an ample amount of opportunity so, it all depends on the student and his ability. the highest package was 75 LPA.

Fees are divided category wise Category 1 to Category 5. Cat 1 and Cat 2 fees are less, but fees are very high from Cat 3 to Cat 5. Scholarships are available for the top 100 rankers in VITEEE. You can get financial assistance like education loans from banks - the university can help you apply for loans. I think it's not worth it if you are taking admission in Cat 4 or Cat 5 because fees are too high - in that case either take a drop and try for tier 1 college or take admission in an affordable tier 3 college.CSE is the most sort out branch here. Faculty: The student ratio is 1:80. Almost all faculties have done PhD - but most of them are average. Some faculties are the best in their branch and some are blacklisted. Faculties are chosen here by the FFCS system. So you can avoid selecting blacklisted faculties here. There are 3 exams conducted CAT 1, CAT 2, and FAT each semester - with quizzes in each subject. So it is pretty much hectic. Also, we have to submit many assignments for each subject.

The internships come from all the companies which recruit from India. From Google to Microsoft to Adobe, IBM, and many more top-notch companies, we also get to intern at startups that originate from VIT. The campus is decent 400 acres and it has the amenities of a good College not like IIT NIT and other govt colleges

Computer Science and Engineering is the best course to opt for at VIT. Most of the faculties are PhDs and highly qualified. In VIT the student gets to select their faculty, time slot and subjects, this system is called the Fully Flexible Credits System.The campus placements are one of the best among all the colleges. If you work hard you can grab a super dream offer. The fee is distributed among different categories: 1,2, 3, 4, 5 which have different fees according to the rank.

The fees depend upon your rank or the category you fall into. Category 1 has the minimum fees structure while category 5 has the maximum. Fees range from 2lakh to 5 lakh per annum. Scholarships are given to exceptional and intelligent students who are amongst the top 1000 rank in VITEEE, also financial assistance to those who are economically backward.I chose the computer science course as I was keen to learn about it.
The course curriculum is very good, the college provides experienced faculty with a 1:60 ratio of faculty to students. The term exams are a bit lengthy but easy to pass with constant learning throughout the semester.
All the faculty have completed their Ph.D.

The curriculum is pretty good and we as students must give our best efforts as teachers alone cant help us understand everything. There is a huge range of scholarship opportunities available for university students across the world, ranging from partial scholarships which cover some tuition fees, to full scholarships which cover the duration of your studies. The majority of scholarships cover tuition fees only, leaving students to cover their living costs.

There are also many different types of scholarships for international students from certain regions, or even students studying in a certain field. For instance, many students from developing countries, including parts of Africa, Asia, Latin America, and Eastern Europe, can apply for specific scholarships, as can many students from all over the world looking to study in a STEM-related field (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics)For this internship, I recommend being organized and writing a list of goals. It is amazing to look back at the end of your internship to see how much you have learned and achieved. Building a strong connection with your supervisor will make the internship experience a lot more fulfilling. If there is something that you wish to learn or get out of the internship, ask your supervisor, as they will love to share knowledge. I hope you all enjoy your internship experiences as well and get as much out of it as I have!

college_name: IIT Hyderabad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITH]
review_text: The placement season for 2020-2021 was great despite the covid pandemic. 466 offers were made to 427 students which was 100% more than the last year. college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
Fee structure is very good it is divided in three categories in terms of your family annual income. If you belong to sc/st category or your annual family income is below 1 lakh than you only have to pay other college charges no tution fee is charged from you . And if your annual family income is in between 1lakh to 5 lakh than you only have to pay 1/3 of the tution fee and other charges . And if your annual family income is above 5 lakh than you will be charged complete fees i.e 164000 for day scholars. If you want to stay in hostels then you will be charged with few more amounts but as it is a government college so they do not charge much for hostels.
There are various scholarships that are given to students. Scholarships are given on the basis of your tuition fees.The course curriculum for first year is somewhat familiar and is common for all branches. Faculties are also good except for one or two you will get enough free time as well. All the faculties have done PhD from the top universities. There are a lot of exams and assignments you have to do so time management will play a key role in your college life. Exams are taken at a continuous interval end term exams are not so tough if you have studied well during your semester it is not necessary to study for 4-5 instead you should listen to your class lectures sincerely and keep on revising them after every class that will take you to score good marks in your exams.

The library is stocked with books, journals, magazines, thesis too. 

All the classrooms are equipped with a smart board. 

We have sports teams for cricket, football etc. The teams also take part in many inter-college competitions. 

Many clubs are there like An events socity, a fashion club, sports club etc and also there are societies for students from different branches too. The college fees is ?1,41,756 which includes tuition fees, Books fees for both semesters, Medical charges and Wi-Fi charges too. 

The hostel fees is ?1,20,000 which includes other charges such as mess charges, electricity, Wi-Fi too. 

Government Scholarships can be availed easily as the college also informs about it and helps you with the forms too.

I chose Electronics and communication as it was a great stream, The faculty is good and they are very knowledgeable, most of them have done PhD and MTech. The Institute take exams frequently and it's getting hectic for students to manage everything.The campus life is fun, you get to know a lot of people from a lot of different cities, everyone has a good nature over here leaving few, it didn't take a lot of time for me to make friends over here. There are a lot of societies over here.

Our college offers a variety of graduate and postgraduate courses. It offers more than 10 branches in Engineering. I've chosen the Computer Science branch because I always wanted to work in software companies and job opportunities are also more in this sector. Faculties are highly educated and highly qualified. They have done their Mtech or PhD from renowned universities. They are very polite and supportive in nature. Classrooms are well equipped and they are big with good infrastructure.Our college provides best placements. More than 200 companies visits in our college fro campus recruitment. Students get eligible for placement from 7th semester. Average package offered is 5 lpa while highest package offered is 48 lpa. Companies like Google, Microsoft, Flipkart do visit for campus recruitment.

College life is very hectic. Though there are many co-curricular activities yet the students do not get proper time to participate in them. 
The infrastructure of a University is far better than any university in India we have different facilities in a university such as a library which is of two floors, we have great experimental labs for each field. Our classes are well occupied with modern gadgets Wi-Fi enabled and a condition classes the quality of hostel life is very good the food which we get in mess and canteen are extremely great quality he has sports ground but it is of knowing you since due to pressure you will not get time to play anything?We do have a great faculty in our university we have more than 50 teachers for each registered course with high teaching qualification
In our university, we have three exams in one semester that is cat 1 cat 2 cat 3 which consist of 30 marks for cat 1 and cat 2 and 50 marks for fat 1. Out of 50 marks, you have to score 50% in your final semester exam and in continuous assessment test, 1 that is cat 1 and cat to you have to score 40%. The difficulty level of semester exam is to high to score.

the term exams are not very difficult.There is a lack of fests as the college is focused more on the academic performance of students. There are plenty of books available in the library.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: The course curriculum is upto the mark  and the faculty is cooperative and techno friendly with a few exceptions at it. I chose chemical engineering since it was my passion . I could have opted for a higher branch with my  jee score being 96 percentile however I wish to lead the world to a greener and healthier environment with my improvisation of chemical processes . Our batch had  8 teachers for the 1st semester and each teacher taught almost 3 such classes of 90 students approx . So the ratio would work out to be approximately 1 faculty member per 34 students irrespective of the subject. Each faculty member is a PHD degree holder with specifications in their subjects . There were continuous evaluation tests each week and finally the end semester examination with difficulty levels ranging from moderate to difficultThe fees structure is divided into 3 categories for students with family income below 1 lakh , between 1 lakh and 5 lakh and above 5 lakhs. There is provision for the national scholarship policy nsp for almost all the students however the institution's lack of due attention towards the distribution of Bonafide certificate was a really big nuisance. Still the fees for an average general category student with family income between 1 to 5 lakhs would cost around 93 thousands which is much more affordable compared to the fees of private colleges . Not to mention the value of facilities provided exceed the fees paid

Our college keeps on organising many fests and events every year like dance events, sports events, technical events which helps students to increase there skill and knowledge. Classrooms are big and with good infrastructure.RVCE offers variety of courses. Our college offers more than 10 branches in BE/B.Tech. I have chosen Computer Science branch because I always wanted to work in software companies and job opportunities are also more in this branch. Faculties are highly educated and highly qualified. They have done Mtech or Phd from renowned universities. They are very supportive and helpful. The course is of 4 years.

AI is the future so there are a lot of scope in this field.

The curriculum is properly managed and exams are not that easy to score good marks u have to study actually.There are many fests that are organized in our college but the most prominent is the udbhav fest which is held every year in our college. The campus is not that big it is only around 23 acres but you will find everything inside the college. The college won't disappoint you in terms of infrastructure.

There are various clubs in this college like NSS and Rotract for social service, Resonance for singing, Ghungroo for dance, GDSC and Geeks United for coding etc. All the clubs are run by the students of the college.I choose this course because I have an interest in programming. college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text:
For 1st year Education Fees is 150000 and hostel fees is 50000.
Then for next three years, education/ tuition fee is 125000 and hostel fees is 50000.
As far as I know, the fees hike is not done in our batch (2021-2022) than last year (2020-2021) and for later years I can't say anything   .
If someone is can't able to give fees then they can take an education loan and there are many scholarships available.
Fee remission
General / Gen-ews / OBC category: family income less than 1 lac then you will get 100% refund of tuition fees
General / Gen-ews / OBC category: family income is between 1 lac to 5 lacs then you will get 2/3 refund of tuition  fees 
SC / ST / PWD category: you will get a 100% refund of tuition fees
there is no fee remission for MSC maths, physics, chemistry branches.
One scholarship name is FFE, in this scholarship Who have secured a rank under 75,000 (CRL) will get scholarship of 40,000 rupees every year.
there are many scholarships available.
In chemical 67% of students got a job (2021).The cultural fest of SVNIT is Sparsh and the tech fest is mindbend which is conducted every year in the month of February. This fest is one of the largest fests of west India.
The Library of the campus is super amazing with world-class infrastructure and around 2 lacs books, and many e-books. If you want any book and not available then there will be a high chance that you get e-books for sure. 
Classrooms are Air Conditioner, one projector in every classroom and benches are new and comfortable, classroom building is very clean.
All sports ground available, here in college we have full opportunity for extra-curricular activities.
There are many clubs for following passion which are all run by students that were an amazing experience.

The best part about this college is you get a quality education at a very minimal cost. Overall fees for BTech for 4 years is approx 390000.
There are no scholarships available. 
On-campus there are many companies who visit for placement offers like Microsoft, Amazon, Wipro, etc. The packages start from 3-4 lakhs and end a maximum upto 50-55 lakhs. The avg package is 6-7 lakhs.I am new student but what I have heard about fests is just great . We get freshers and annual fest. 
All sorta of books which you require to gain knowledge is available in library. Library is one of the beat place in the campus . 
Classrooms are very organized and clean.
We have inter departmental sports event which is great.

Our course will cost 4.2 lakh including exam fees, semester fees library fees, and others. In my opinion, this amount is fair because there are lots of facilities provided to students. The classrooms are good, labs are well maintained, and full of modern equipment for a better understanding of students.Unlike the other colleges of Kolkata, The Heritage Institute of Technology have its own campus and playing grounds. Unfortunately, there are no hostel options for boys inside the campus. Only girl's hostels are available inside the campus.

The annual fest is held for freshers. Every year when freshers came. We have a library which is called the central library and it is quite good and I think have all kind of books needed for all stream students also there are books for enhancement of the knowledge and experience of all things beyond the studies. We have many sports activities like cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, etc college has its own team in all sports and they even present our college in different tournaments. We have many clubs like for dance, music drama, fashion and also Rotaract for Social.I wanted to take CSE but because of my rank and counseling, I got IT. I want to become a software engineer and entrepreneur. I think 1/60 is the ratio. I think all are qualified teachers and many are done with their Ph.D. Exams are held on time to time 2 internal tests before every semester papers are held.  Nd if you are attended the classes then it is easier to get a pass with decent marks.

The course of IT at Heritage Institute of Technology has a really good curriculum and is valuable for the future.
The college has really good faculties who encourage you and are extremely knowledgeable.
The faculty: student ratio of the course is also good approx(1:30).The fest is one of the best college fests in Kolkata.
The library is big enough and is well maintained with varieties of books.
The classrooms are big enough and the labs have all basic amenities.
The college has its own football/cricket grounds and basketball court.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: In the pre-final year of their course, College intership season starts. Each students applies for various companies internships. In the final year, in pre-final semester, college placements start. Various companies like Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Dream11, JPMC and Co. and many more multinational companies visit in Campus for placements. Highest package is around INR 2 Crore and average package is around 18 Lakhs. Profiles offered for the job are Software Developer, Data Analyst, Data scientist, Quantative Researcher, Quantitative Analyst, Product Manager, etc. More than 60% of students sitting in placements get placed.y plan after getting the degree is to continue the job in the company in which I have been offered the job.Life at the college and is full of fun and education. We learn so much new things and technologies. We develop day by day in the campus. Annual fests of IIT BHU are Kashiyatra (January), Spardha (October) and Technex (March). Each type of book is available in the library for the students. There are various clubs to join like Dance, Fine arts, Sports, Programming, etc. Students can join the clubs accourding to their interests. Sports activities are done regularly on campus. Each sports club member is trained properly. Kashiyatra website is run by college students only.

Fees like I haven't seen yet the cost of the study is good if we consider hostel fees.
All scholarships are provided here. 
The campus interview is pretty good here.
Sastra even provides students with welfare funds to make students into normal life.I choose this course because we can go for both IT as well as a core extra advantage.
The student ratio is good I study in a class of 51 students.
Ph.D. is min quality.
Monthly once CIAs are conducted it is quite tougher to get a good mark.

From the sixth semester, students are eligible for internship and students can sit for the campus in their fourth year. For internship and placement, many companies visited our campus like amazon, google, reliance jio, Mercedes, CRED, BPCL, godrej, federal bank, Infosys, texas instrument, oracle, Samsung, Hyundai, tcs and many startups.
For the session 2019-23,636 were registered for placement and all were placed and they have offered 717 jobs.
The highest package is 52 LPA and the average is 14.14 LPA  this year.
percentage placement is 100%.
My plan after getting my degree is to sit in campus placement.Campus life is fine here there are many clubs in different areas and incubation centers. The techno-Culture fest of NIT PATNA is corona and Melange which is held in January every year many activities are organized. Hackslash and gdsc is a tech club of NIT PATNA, a month coding competition is organized and a learning session is taken by seniors and given guidelines. There is a dance club, photography club, ecell and many more clubs.
the classroom is good, some of the classrooms have also ac.
In sports, many sports-related events are organized frequently and inter-NIT sports are also organized.

One India is celebrated as the annual fest of LPU,  a four-day event that is usually in the month of March. 
The library of LPU has a great collection of over 3 lac books with a seating capacity of over 3.5k students.
Classrooms are equipped with comfortable chairs and desks to ensure that the students can sit comfortably for four long hours without any discomfort.
It also had a wide range of sports facilities with extra curriculum activities.This course (BTech CSE) offers a better placement and also has a great scope in future with the increasing technology. 
Genuinely the faculty of LPU is highly qualified and friendly in nature with each student and even they treat students with love and care.
I love their way of teaching with fun which helps us to learn easily without any boringness.
To pass term exams was quite difficult which used to hold each semester.

The infrastructure is nice. It has all campuses surrounded by CCTV and the environment is green and healthy. 
The building is nice. The washroom is hygienic and clean. The drinking water facility is there. There are many clubs run by the student such as humans of galgotias.Fees structure is quite reasonable compared to any other university. Apart from regular fees, there are no extra fees that are to be taken by students. The cafeteria is there for refreshments and stationery shops were also there to help students.

all types of books are available in the libraries.

college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology - [Vel Tech]
review_text: I chose cse because I am interested and have good placements as of now. faculty are good they are a friendly maximum of 1:50 ratio there. minimum qualification for faculty is mtech .they explain in new teaching techniques the term was monthly once and passing the exams is very easy.According to student interests, placements are started in 3 Rd year with no standing arrears. nearly more than 100 companies can visit the campus last year highest package was 44 lakh and the least package was 3 lakh per annum 90 percentage students are placed. maybe ms if possible.

I chose the engineering course here, firstly, because yeah, my parents wanted me to be an engineer and mainly because the placement of this course here in LPU is wow. As the strength of the students is around 7-8 k, the strength of the faculties is also Good enough, around 3-4k. And every one of them is well qualified. Although we engineers don't need that type of class study, we just study 2-4 nights before the exams and also we score well. Well yeah, it's possible because of the quality of education, faculties are providing us in the class. We just have to see once the terms and the score are in our hands. Per semester is of 14 weeks, and after 7 weeks you have to give the mid-term, which is tbh not that tough. If u are just regular in the class and just revise the course once before the exam, it'll be much easier to score well. And after another 7 weeks, there will be the end term, the final exam, which is a bit tough than the midterm exam.The life on campus, wow. As u can imagine, this University has an area of around 600 acre, every type of activities u can imagine, is present here, like swimming, mall,and a lot . Talking about the library, the biggest Library in the north zone of India is here, At LPU. U can get every type of book here, novels, and books of every course. Coming to the extracurricular activities, u know, there is a separate block, just for these extra activities like dancing (every type of dance traditional, Bhangra, folk and bla bla bla) singing, acting, reading, infact Coding club is also there. If u are interested in any of these activities, LPU is a good platform. Also, this University don't just focus on your study , they suggest, infact force u to go, explore yourself.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Placement for our college is good, placement percentages for the different departments are different for example for the cse department it is approx 95%, for ece is also 90% and other branches also get good placement whose percentage is greater than 70%. After the 6th or 7th semester students become Eligible for the placement. All the great companies like amazon, google, meta, TCS, Flipkart, l and t, and many more. A good number of offers were made to the students by these companies. The highest package for the cse is 65 lakhs which is offered by Amazon and the average is 15 lakhs, the highest package for ece is more than 1cr and the Average package is also 14 lakhs which are offered by google. Usually, 70 to 80% get placed. I am interested in getting the job.I want to work in the construction department so luckily based on my rank. I got into the civil department in the college. The faculty of this college is fairly good. They are cooperative with the students. As we know about the faculty of the engineering colleges they are a little different so students don't rely on them they do self-study and get passed. The student ratio in my college is approximately 1:4. All the faculty members have done their PhD in their subject. Teaching methods are both modern and traditional. All the things in college are done timely, they release time table for the semester at the start of the semester which includes classes, holidays, exams, etc everything, so everything is on time.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: Generally, the fee is constant but there is a hike in this year's price of 25000 from the year 2022. I got a scholarship by applying for the ITR certificate of both my parents. And also I got a 10000 stipend yearly for being maintaining CGPA above 7.5.Annual tech-fest is cognizance and fest is Thomson having a footfall of 15000. there is a library named MGCL in which there are 1.5 lakh books available online and offline. Sports are top-notch level. There are 50 clubs including tech plus non-tech.

The annual cultural fest of JU is Sanskriti and Srijan is our annual tech fest. They are conducted in the first quarter of the year. 
We have a big building as our central Library and many books and journals are available here. 
The classrooms are not so bad-no so good kind of. They have projectors and ACs. 
Sports like cricket, football, basketball, long tennis, table tennis etc are hugely Encouraged by the college and separate fields are there for each sport. 
Many clubs are there like Mountains, photography, Debating, drama, science, coding etc are here on campusStudents can apply for on-campus internships from the 5th semester and for placements from the 7th semester. 
Many companies from Google, Amazon, and apple to Deloitte, Razorpay, slice, grab more than 150 companies visit campus and make offers of more than 1.9k
Last year's highest package was 1.8 cr per annum from google London for the SDE role. 
Around 80-90% of students get placed in JU and  ETCE has a 100% placement record. 
Though college offers great placements, I'm willing to do an MBA after my graduation.

I chose the engineering course here, firstly, because yeah, my parents wanted me to be an engineer and mainly because the placement of this course here in LPU is wow. As the strength of the students is around 7-8 k, the strength of the faculties is also Good enough, around 3-4k. And every one of them is well qualified. Although we engineers don't need that type of class study, we just study 2-4 nights before the exams and also we score well. Well yeah, it's possible because of the quality of education, faculties are providing us in the class. We just have to see once the terms and the score are in our hands. Per semester is of 14 weeks, and after 7 weeks you have to give the mid-term, which is tbh not that tough. If u are just regular in the class and just revise the course once before the exam, it'll be much easier to score well. And after another 7 weeks, there will be the end term, the final exam, which is a bit tough than the midterm exam.The life on campus, wow. As u can imagine, this University has an area of around 600 acre, every type of activities u can imagine, is present here, like swimming, mall,and a lot . Talking about the library, the biggest Library in the north zone of India is here, At LPU. U can get every type of book here, novels, and books of every course. Coming to the extracurricular activities, u know, there is a separate block, just for these extra activities like dancing (every type of dance traditional, Bhangra, folk and bla bla bla) singing, acting, reading, infact Coding club is also there. If u are interested in any of these activities, LPU is a good platform. Also, this University don't just focus on your study , they suggest, infact force u to go, explore yourself.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Placement for our college is good, placement percentages for the different departments are different for example for the cse department it is approx 95%, for ece is also 90% and other branches also get good placement whose percentage is greater than 70%. After the 6th or 7th semester students become Eligible for the placement. All the great companies like amazon, google, meta, TCS, Flipkart, l and t, and many more. A good number of offers were made to the students by these companies. The highest package for the cse is 65 lakhs which is offered by Amazon and the average is 15 lakhs, the highest package for ece is more than 1cr and the Average package is also 14 lakhs which are offered by google. Usually, 70 to 80% get placed. I am interested in getting the job.I want to work in the construction department so luckily based on my rank. I got into the civil department in the college. The faculty of this college is fairly good. They are cooperative with the students. As we know about the faculty of the engineering colleges they are a little different so students don't rely on them they do self-study and get passed. The student ratio in my college is approximately 1:4. All the faculty members have done their PhD in their subject. Teaching methods are both modern and traditional. All the things in college are done timely, they release time table for the semester at the start of the semester which includes classes, holidays, exams, etc everything, so everything is on time.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: Generally, the fee is constant but there is a hike in this year's price of 25000 from the year 2022. I got a scholarship by applying for the ITR certificate of both my parents. And also I got a 10000 stipend yearly for being maintaining CGPA above 7.5.Annual tech-fest is cognizance and fest is Thomson having a footfall of 15000. there is a library named MGCL in which there are 1.5 lakh books available online and offline. Sports are top-notch level. There are 50 clubs including tech plus non-tech.

The annual cultural fest of JU is Sanskriti and Srijan is our annual tech fest. They are conducted in the first quarter of the year. 
We have a big building as our central Library and many books and journals are available here. 
The classrooms are not so bad-no so good kind of. They have projectors and ACs. 
Sports like cricket, football, basketball, long tennis, table tennis etc are hugely Encouraged by the college and separate fields are there for each sport. 
Many clubs are there like Mountains, photography, Debating, drama, science, coding etc are here on campusStudents can apply for on-campus internships from the 5th semester and for placements from the 7th semester. 
Many companies from Google, Amazon, and apple to Deloitte, Razorpay, slice, grab more than 150 companies visit campus and make offers of more than 1.9k
Last year's highest package was 1.8 cr per annum from google London for the SDE role. 
Around 80-90% of students get placed in JU and  ETCE has a 100% placement record. 
Though college offers great placements, I'm willing to do an MBA after my graduation.

review_text:
I choose this course because my course is an integrated M-tech course. In this course, I directly get an M-tech degree also from such kind of prestigious college
Here faculty and students ratio is too much good
And every faculty of this college are doctorate. This is the only college in India with all the faculty members who are PhD Qualified from India's top colleges such as IIT and NIT.

you must have a cycle or vehicle to roam around the full campus.
Greenery is everywhere on the campus. NO Pollution inside the campus. The infrastructure of our college is quite good but is it also improving day by day. Recently NAAC comes to our college for the College infra is quite improved a lot within some days and They accredited this time an A GRADE from a B grade in past.

For Quizzes and other stuff just before night study is enough.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
Fest-Major ones are techniti(technical fest) ,Utkansh (cultural fest) also festivals like Lohri, janmashtami,kite festival, etc celebrated here. This year techniti was organized in Feb month. First day Italian Dj Olly esse came and on the third day Punjabi singer and actor Ninja came.
library-library has a good no.of books but it lacks in quantity. But novels are not enough there . Current Library will be replaced by a new one in some months.
You can study in the library till 9:30 and can get issue book till 5:30.Overall my experience is good.
classroom-it is well equipped with modern equipment like smart boards along with two blackboards, a projector, fully ac classroom with the proper seating arrangement.
sports-U can enjoy cricket, football, volleyball, basketball, badminton, carrom, and chess here. sports facilities are good here. The ground is spacious and of standard dimensions.
Clubs-There are many clubs like prayers for teaching students who can't afford to study Other clubs are music, robotics,ldc,tca,tta.many companies visit the college. Most of them I could remember now are Amazon, Samsung, coca cola, etc. But most companies come for the IT section. But Coca-Cola comes to campus for industrial engineering and recruits interns about 30+ students each year. If you belong to any other branch like mechanical or civil then few companies provide internships. Also, you could get local industries for internships in Jalandhar Or nearby. So, it's not tough to get an intern at NIT Jalandhar.

Because I am interested in this course and the student's ratio is high here but the education quality is best teacher support students with a lot of exams held every one to two months and it is easy to pass if you pay attention in classesThe campus is good it looks good every two or three months fest is going on and students enjoyed lot them facilities of the classroom are high tech full ac and seats are good and projectors in every class social media handles available

Annual fest unifest conducted in April every year
Tech fest like hackathon
Books are available in library
Ac, projector are available in classroom
Good sport facility is available in campus
Clubs like management culb tech club are thereCollege placement is around 90 percent with 250 companies appponded the big ones are Microsoft Cisco and many other like this . Package is quite low because of mass placement company take students in bulk and do not give high package

Unifest is conducted in April,
All books are available in the library, 
AC classrooms, projectors are also present in classrooms good seating system is also there.
Our university conducts many sports events like ICSL. and takes players to other college events also. 
There are many clubs like coding clubs sports clubs dance clubs etc.I choose this course because I am interested in this course and the student ratio is too high but the education is too good and their teaching methods are unique exams occur every one to two months and are easy to pass if you pay attention in classes only.

college_name: IIT Hyderabad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITH]
review_text:
We have an annual fest called elan & nvision which is conducted in February.
There's no as such any tech fest but we have Milan (inter hostel competitions) and Diesta (inter-departmental competitions).
There are quite a number of books available at the library but it's a bit small because IIT Hyderabad Hyderabad being 2nd generation IIT is currently under construction so the new library is still in the making.
Classrooms are small sized but the facilities are good.
A new sports complex is being constructed and thus for now we have to play sports in a small area which is not up to the mark.
There are many clubs such as Aero, robotics, ACM, etc run by the students.This course is the second best chemical engineering course in IITs after IIT Bombay. That's why I chose this course.
We have a 1:2 faculty to student ratio. It's quite good actually.
All the faculties are post graduate or PhD holders.
The exams were like 2-3 in a semester per subject. So it was not that hectic because IIT Hyderabad has segment divisons in semester so we have exams at different times for different subjects.

The college is well known for its placements and its college life. DTU has many societies with different choices like dance singing racing sports and some coding societies.
Dtu has a very big library which has books for studies and some great novels.
engifest is the annual cultural festival of dtu which is one of the biggest and oldest colleges in North India It helps in February every year and attracts thousands of participants and spectators from all over Delhi across every college.
some other fests are aahvaan innovation and even small fests at dtu are bigger than the best of nsut's moksha.The Total cost for 4 years of study is around 9 lakhs and it increases every year. Like for me my first-year fee was 212000 but for my junior, it is 219000.
but there are some good scholarships provided by DTU as well as the Delhi government for DTU there is an interview of the student and his parents together and based upon that one can get the scholarship similarly for e-distict Delhi there are multiple. scholarships based on merit and annual income by using these scholarships ones can get tuition fees refunded which is approximately 65 percent of the total fess.

Unifest is conducted in April,
All books are available in the library, 
AC classrooms, projectors are also present in classrooms good seating system is also there.
Our university conducts many sports events like ICSL. and takes players to other college events also. 
There are many clubs like coding clubs sports clubs dance clubs etc.I choose this course because I am interested in this course and the student ratio is too high but the education is too good and their teaching methods are unique exams occur every one to two months and are easy to pass if you pay attention in classes only.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text: Placement opportunities are very good in the college. Students go for internships in the 6th semester and are eligible for placements from the 7th semester. A lot of companies from a variety of fields visit campus and provide ample opportunities. The average placement percentage is around 80%. My plans are to get a good campus placement.Faculty to student ratio is 1:60. All of the professors are well-qualified and do their duty well. They teach good and are dedicated. Exams are conducted twice every semester and the difficulty level remains moderate to difficult and its rather easy to pass the course because of due weightage given to assignments and other stuff.

A student has to conduct majorly three internships. A social internship (end of 2nd Semester), an academic internship (end of 4th semester), and an industrial internship (end of 6th semester). The first two internships are not provided by the college. It is the responsibility of the student to do the same whereas the training and placement cell provides the Industrial Internships to the students in some good companies.Placements are an important part of any Engineering College. In our college, placements are quite decent. Some PSUs like NRL, Oil India, Indian Oil etc visit on a regular basis. Mass recruiters like Cognizant and Capgemini visit almost every year. The training and placement cell of AEC does a good job of providing opportunities for the students.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text:
The internship opportunity is not good for an internship you have to apply by yourself to another college for any industry. for research internships, You can apply to your faculties members and you can take help from your faculties for internships.
But you have so many opportunities for projects in college.Campus life is excellent there are so many fests happening in your college. At even semester time events are conducted.
Freely you can use the library and the infrastructure of the library is very nice and full ac library, so the opportunity for sport and extra curriculum.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text:
The internship opportunity is not good for an internship you have to apply by yourself to another college for any industry. for research internships, You can apply to your faculties members and you can take help from your faculties for internships.
But you have so many opportunities for projects in college.Campus life is excellent there are so many fests happening in your college. At even semester time events are conducted.
Freely you can use the library and the infrastructure of the library is very nice and full ac library, so the opportunity for sport and extra curriculum.

Choice: Due to the brand name and alumni network. Also, product design placements and opportunities are better and more versatile. Also, intake is low (although intake competition is high) and opportunities are better.
Faculty: The student ratio is very nice. 30:1 and there is much attention on all.
As the course is new so there is no such permanent faculty. Although there are guest faculties from IITs, NIDs, and NIFTs with versatile qualifications.There are multiple technical and cultural fests throughout the year that you can enjoy.
Books are ordered according to authorities and no one knows when they will arrive design books are not in libraries.
ACs are installed in all classrooms.
There are various societies and sports you can join and they are very active and have connections with other branch peeps.

Basically, at this percentile, I am getting this course and also I don't have much interest in coding. the faculty members are very helpful and friendly the student ratio is quite good compared to other colleges. the exams are conducted twice a semester and if you study at least one week before exams you will nail it. the level is moderate.From 7 semester onwards student becomes eglibile for placements. The highest package was 36 lpa whereas the average is 11lpa. here come big companies like amazon, google and other big MNCs. The percentage of students getting selected is 93 %.

college_name: IIT Jammu - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text: IIT JAMMU has very good placements stats as compared to all other 3rd gen IITs. The highest package Offered in the year 2021 is 52LPA for BTech and Median Package for CSE is 21LPA. Almost 83% of students get placed. The Overall median package of Btech is 16LPA. In MTech Highest package is  45LPA and the median is 25.5 LPA. The top companies visiting are amazon, Microsoft, sprinklr, indeed, Samsung, and Qualcomm.There are basically two Fest of IIT Jammu: RENAO id the Name of our cultural fest and Technuctus is the Tech fest of IIT Jammu. The infrastructure Here is good because it is newly constructed and all the classrooms are centralized AC. Almost all the major sports facilities are here. Also, there are various Clubs Run by students eg- Coding club, astronomy club, Beat Street etc.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: The fees have been the same since. The beginning and mostly no hike was there. There are several scholarships for students as well as relief for the economically unstable. There are a lot of opportunities as a grad from IITD.There are a lot of internship opportunities at the internet of things, designing, coding, robotics, manufacturing as well as tech jobs. Indian institute of technology Delhi is also known for jobs in consultancy and finances

The course curriculum is good, for CSE students the courses are perfectly designed and the code practice is from the beginning of the year you join the course.
The faculties are also qualified and try to make the student understand the topic and also they are friendly towards the students. Here, in CSE the ratio of faculty and the student is about 5:6. The faculties are qualified professors holding doctorate-level degrees and have good teaching experience. Here, the faculties are friendly towards the students, they help the student to pursue the knowledge as better he can. The students are provided with a mentor who guides them in curriculum activities. Here, 3 Semester tests are performed before the final end-term exam where the student needs to perform well in at least 2 STs out of 3. Exams are performed every 1.5 months gap.Chitkara conducts several programs among which 'Algorithm' is one of the biggest program fests in Chitkara University, HP. The students are offered different societies and clubs here. Every Student gets the opportunity to perform. University has a library which has good availability of books and journals and if you need any other you can inform the librarian about the book or journal you want. As well as sports are conducted by Chitkara and also it participates in different sports programs.
The infrastructure of this University is good and clean.

So people who are interested in all the domains are internships inside the college.Average fees in college are 4-5 lakhs for the course and differ when the branches are changed they also provide government scholarships so that non-financial students can also study and build there bright future.

Grafest is the annual fest that is organised on our campus every year during winter in the month of November or December.
all the books related to the course are available in our library.
the classroom contains projectors .mike for faculty, and some classrooms even have A.C.
every now and then there is one or another activity going on in the college. there are many sports and extra curriculum clubs in our college.The fees are not very low for this course. if we sum up all the expenditures of the hostel, fees, and food then it may even exceed 1700000 in amount.
There is one girls' scholarship which provides a 10% discount for every girl child.
jobs are pretty well with the highest package of 80lacs per annum

I have chosen this course because of the placement and the brand value of Delhi Technological University. The faculty is well experienced and helps in any way. There are almost 2500 students in our batch. Exams are held per semester wise and you can pass them easily by studying 2-3 days before the examThere are mainly 4 fests at Delhi Technological University. Firstly, 'Yuvaan ' is the literature and film festival fest. Second, ' INVICTUS' is the tech-fest of our college. Third, 'ENGIFEST' This is the cultural fest and last 'AAHAVAN' is the sports fest.

I have the curiosity of doing the coding.
Good faculty and good students.
The qualification of faculty is PHD, MTECH.
terms or exams are taken in 2 months' time and the next final terms are a month. which is of 100 marks and we get the choices in question.Fees are based on the number of marks a student carries and there is no need to give any donation you will get a scholarship according to the marks a student has obtained. The scholarship is on the basis of GEU. The fee is 1 lakh rs.

I choose Computer science engineering with artificial intelligence as my specialization because I wanted to pursue my career in an IT company. The student in my whole course is about 500. With the girl-boy ratio as 40 and 60 respectively. All the faculty members are either retired IIT professors or Ph.D. professors. The faculty is very good and adaptive and very helpful. They want to give you marks unless you don't want to earn they can't help. There are 2 types of exams throughout the semester. One is the mid sem exam and the other is end sem exam. The assignments and quizzes along with the projects and hackathons go along the whole semester.The placements at the university start for 3-2 years. The average package is about 8 lakhs and the highest varies. There are about 100 companies that visit. And you have a lot of opportunities to apply and make it to your dream company. The placement cell of the university is very active and very helpful. You can contact them any time of the day and ask for their opinions. The percentage of the students getting placed is about 80-85 percent.

The faculty is at the top level, there are professors from IIT and all are at the doctorate level. the campus is also good, the ratio is 1:1 and the qualifications of the teachers are also nice if you are thinking of a next-level experience you can join Bennett.Fees as compared to other colleges are slightly high but if it comes to the opportunity it gives you ample things like supercomputers are there with various labs built. It is a nice place where your child can grow. The fee is 2 lakh 40 thousand.

I opted for this course as I found this college good in terms of ROI in cat 1 and also it is a brand-value college. Faculties are good if you follow their instructions they would be nice to you. The student ratio is good around 1:4
Every faculty is a doctorate here And the teaching method is good as one of their campuses follows Caltech's method of teaching
Generally, they have two processes one cat1 cat2 exams and term end and the second midterms and term-end. Depends on the semester.From the end of 3rd year or I can say the 6th-semester students are eligible for on-campus placements. Approx 900+ companies visit vit for placement which includes Microsoft, IBM, Tata, Wipro, Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Amazon, PayPal etc.
The highest package is 1 cr+ got in 2022 and the average package is around 7-8 LPA.
Around 85-90 per cent of students get their placements.
My plan is to take a job in an MNC from placements or I can seek for MBA.

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text: motilal nehru national institute of technology popularly known as mnnit Allahabad is quite famous for its placements. Students are eligible for internships from the fifth semester. and hundreds of companies visit this college and almost all students get a good placement depending on his/her skillalthough the fee is very high you can avail of a lot of scholarships here depending on your academics and family income as well. if your family income is less than 8LAKHS but greater than 5LAKHS you will get two third waiver on your fee and if it is less than 1LAKHS you will get a 100% fee waiver

All of our professors are Ph.D. graduates and are acquainted with the latest technology. They always find paths to make us familiar with the latest technology. They encourage us in each step of ours. The curriculum is industrial-related and mostly focuses on the subjects that are required during the time of placements. The semester exams are quite good if we are well planned. They aim to test our basic understanding about the subject and test our understanding capability. The pass percentage is nearly 80 as far as I am concerned.We have well-sized classrooms with very high SEED Wi-Fi of min 200 Mbps and A good library with good number of books. Hostels looks so good with a well-maintained room of 4,5,3,2 shared rooms according to our budget and food is like okay but compared to other colleges it's better, we have a Hospital in hostel with a doctor and there 5 to 6 canteens, then coming to sports and games we have cricket, basketball, Football, badminton with some indoor games and we have a gym for both girls and boys separately.
