The B.Tech CSE fees without scholarship are 120000 per semester. But I got a 38% scholarship and my fee is 74400 per semester. The placement is very good. The name of the Scholarship test is LPUNEST Exam. 
The cost of Studying with a Scholarship is not expensive as compared to another college.I Choose B.Tech CSE at LPU, Punjab. The curriculum is very good. All the faculty members are very supportive and caring and help students enhance their carrier. The faculties are Phdian. Exams are conducted in a regular manner. 
Test, mid-term, project, and term.

Algo is the biggest fest of the university. It basically occurs in March-August. For a library, Anyone can issue a book and even all study material is available in the library, you just need to have your Id for it. Classrooms are well furnished with AC and WIFI facilities. There are many groups for co-curricular activities and even for coding.There is no hike policy in the college. For the first semester, we need to pay 80000 then the rest is 70000 semester and the hostel including the mess is about 35000 per semester for 4 seater boys. There is no such scholarship but even it provides loans for students who want to have one.

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text: There are about three courses for btech. My seniors told me that this course has low effort I think most of that I have taken this course the faculties are very good here there are very supportive. Faculties: student ratio is 1:10.The fees are very decent compared to all other institutions private my college is very good and there is a hostel facility also available in my college they provide decent playground services with the campus is also very good.

As a mechanical enthusiast, I chose mechanical engineering as my branch.
We have lecture and tutorial classes, lecture class strength is 72 and these 72 students are divided into groups of 36- 36 each.
Faculties are knowledgeable and experienced.
Mainly we have three Continuous assessments and one midterm and in the last end term.There is a number of events organized at the university, and every day you will get a chance to participate in events.
They have separate libraries for most of the branches and one central library.
Indoor stadium, Swimming pool, two large-size groups.

The course curriculum is better than most other colleges offering this course. Faculty members are well experienced but I personally did not like them. I chose this course because I wanted to study CS and the practical applications which this course fairly offers. There are multiple batches. Each batch consists of nearly 55 students. There are 2 term exams: CAT 1, and CAT 2. And 1 final exam. It's quite easy to pass if you are just thorough with your course subjects.Compared to other private colleges, this college is a bit strict. There are hostels at times. It varies for boys and girls. Overall, campus life is good. The infrastructure is nice and pleasant to move around. There are many clubs and chapters in which you can take part. On the campus, you can find 2 to 3 basketball, volleyball, tennis courts etc.

Recently we celebrated a fest named Riviera as the college's Annual fest and also there will be many named Gravitas. There's a big library named Periyar Library. About classrooms, there are different blocks for different branches. There are many social groups and clubs like Dance clubs, Music clubs and many others.In general computer science engineering is the best among all the branches of engineering. As looking into faculty and student ratio the exact answer can't be provided but yeah it's better. The qualifications of the faculties are good. Term exams are good and not much difficult to pass the exam.

I opted for the mathematics and computer branches, although I was getting CSAI too, it's because I'm interested in maths more. Here on mac, you study 60% of maths and 40% of computers. You'll read all the subjects of cse and higher mathematics. The teachers are good here, I mean they teach well but I won't say that they are really good. I mean just okay but this is the case for every college even IITs. In NSUT you get a good student ratio in our class it is 1:5 boys to girl ratio. But don't worry in the whole college it is quite good around 1:3 (because of BBA). The qualification of teachers is really great, all of them are either pursuing PhD or they've qualified, but it's about the way of teaching, only 2 out of 6 professors are good rest just come as time pass. Here comes exams, exams are really a problem in nsut. Exams are strict. I mean no way of cheating. And the monthly test is there. It is quite easy to pass the exams. You'll have to study since starting.The campus of nsut is 150 acres in area. Which is boring, I mean there are two fountains, and it's pretty but you will find it pretty for only a few days. After that, you'll feel bored. The best fest of nsut is MOKSHA and it's one of the biggest fests in northern India. You must come to enjoy this fest. Moksha is organised in April. There are all kinds of books in the library. So you're not going to be disappointed if you're a bookworm. Classrooms in nsut are the best, even better than IIT's. They are known as APJ Halls, but the sad part is that these classrooms are for only 1st years.
There are lots of societies in Nsut, whatever you can think, and there shall be a society for that. And the budget of each society is amazing too.

Fees hike is not that much. Scholarships are provided by various alumni. An internship for 3 months is provided. Also, the placement is really good here. The cost to study is not much compared to other iits and nits. The fee is also not so much, it is just 59000 per annum which is really okay as compare to other private colleges.We have a compulsory three months internship program as a part of our curriculum. Various companies like reliance tata godrej croda honeywell jubilant asian paints come here to take students from the Institute of chemical technology.

The curriculum is vast and provides good information regarding the scope of this field. They provide good lectures and seminars. 
The student ratio is 75:1 and I think it is a bit bigger ratio and can have different sections and not such crowded spaces. The faculty is qualified and have great years of experience. They provide good opportunities and exposure for the same.The fee structure I think is a bit expensive. But the department provides scholarship facilities for the top scorers. They consider delays in finances and payments. 
Different timelines are set for those who cannot afford exact-date payments.

I choose this course due to my passion to enter in business industry. I took HR and IT specialization because my bachelor degree is in computer applications. In distance education mode, university provided study centers in our city where the class length was 10-50 students in one classroom.The lecturars were recruited from university and their teaching techniques was excellent.

The exam session was semester wise and we had exams in feb and sept.The examination system was good and was not too difficult to pass.The tuition fee I paid was 22000 rs per year in which the university provided us with books and learning material from campus.
There was not any scholarship scheme in distance education mode. But the university organised campus placement events for us.
But I found a job outside of campus.

As a current student at ICT, Mumbai, the fee structure is affordable for a government college, with my fees being around 80,000 rupees per year as a general category student. I am currently availing the Maha government scholarship and the merit-cum-means scholarship, but I am not aware of any on-campus jobs with stipends. There are several scholarship opportunities available, including state and central government scholarships and private scholarships like the Asian Paints scholarship. The fee hike policy is nominal, and overall, the cost to study is reasonable.Life at the campus is lively with a good social life and many clubs to join. The infrastructure is good with well-equipped classrooms and a great library with many books and journals. We have an annual chem fest called Vortex and an annual cultural fest called Manzar conducted in February and March respectively. The campus has many sports facilities, and there are many extracurricular activities to take part in, including sports, music, arts, and literary clubs, all run by students.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: There are a lot of clubs at IIT. Students can do any extra curriculum activities in which they are interested. One of the largest fests is conducted in IITD called RDV every year. There are many fests conducted the whole year i.e. Tryst, Pravriti, Becon, Kaizen, I-connect, etc. Rendevous is a cultural fest and Tryst is a technical fest both conducted in the month of Feb-march. There is the central library which is 24x7 open and there is a huge collection of books where we can get all books, there is another library also in SAC where we can get books other than academics like manga and comics and others. Lecture halls are too big where at a time 300+ can sit and there are tut classes also for tutorial classes. There is a couch for every sport. There are grounds for every sport and even every hostel has a separate sports room and ground. There are many social groups run by students of NSS. Clubs also run by students.I choose Electrical Engineering because I'm interested in Electric Department and it is a circuital branch of one of the top most IIT. At IIT, we get the best faculty from all over the world. There is TA for every group of 30 students and a personal acad mentor. Profs are too good and very experienced most of them had a lot of research in their life. As they are experienced their teaching method is better than any other college. We have 2 minors and one major per sem. They are quite tough one should have to study regularly to pass the exam. Discipline in IIT is too strict no one can practice unfair means in exams otherwise they got failed that course. Here things are more practical than theory because they believe in making us used to the real world. We have labs regularly and we have to do experiments properly.

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text: We have three main fests called Concours, I.Fest and Synapse in which concours is a sports festival, I. Fest is Gujarat's largest technical fest and Synapse is our techno-cultural fest. Concours, I.Fest and Synapse were conducted respectively in November, December and March. Very good availability of books in Library. There are 23+ clubs here most of which are run by students i.e. IEEE student branch, cultural committee, student body government called SBG, Drama Theatre Group called DTG, DADC for dance, music club, sports committee etc.In the 4th year, students become eligible for placement but we have got facilities for internship eligibility from the 5th sem and if possible companies give us pre-placement offer also. We recorded 100% placement here there are many companies visiting here i.e. Google, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, Sprinkler, Atlassian etc. The highest package we got was 82 LPA for two students placed in Atlassian Australia-based company and the average package is around 20 LPA.

Total fees are 2,19,000 rupees in total including tuition fees which is 1,44,000 and various other parts like medical assistance, minority section relief donation and such. There are various scholarship opportunities if you are in need of it with enough low income. DTU concession (tuition fees return ), merit cum means (100%/50%/25% tuition fees according to condition ), post  matric scholarship ( for  SC/ST students only & 100 per cent fees return )Except for one or two professors most of the staff is top-notch and have a lot of knowledge. There are different experts in different fields. So they cover a vast area of knowledge.  Most assistant professors are relatively young from their late 20s to early 30s and others are above 40-50. Lectures are relevant to the placement but not enough practice. You have to learn everything and apply it in real on your own.

Fests in GLA are average 
From January to April, you would enjoy number of functions in gla 
All books you need are available in the library if there was not a book that you want you can apply for that book and that book easily be available in 10 to Many cricket matches, baseball, football matches are organized from time to time and also students selected for university level tournaments 
Many clubs are there at GLA university 
Yantriki society 
ASME 
Literario club
Math Club
Poetry club
Droid
Speed Club
Analysis club 
Laughter club
And many moreI choose this course because of my interest in cars and bikes engines, and designs from starting
Faculty is well qualified for their subjects 
The method of their teaching is a practical basis
Term exams was not difficult even if you study 5 days before exams you can easily pass the exam

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text:

All faculty member treats us like our own children they always help us if we have any doubt at any time
Qualification of most of the faculty are p.hd to M.tech

college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI]
review_text:
I have chosen this course because I am interested in physics subject and I like solving physics problems.
The civil branch has this. 
For the whole branch, there will be one main course coordinator and 1-3 supporting teaching assistants for any course.
The faculty teaching us here are the ones who have done their mtech, pg, Ph.D. with a decent percentage.
They teach very well. The classroom atmosphere is very interactive too.
Basically for a semester, there will be one mid-semester exam held within one and half months followed by mid sem break of one week. Then within one and a half months, there will be an end-semester exam.
Difficulty to pass depends on the course and the strictness of the course coordinator.
Most of them were easy to pass. Very rarely in one or two subjects, there will be difficulty mainly in civil, that too can be passed with enough hard work and even there are chances of getting good grades if we work consistently.FLUXUS is the annual techno-cultural fest at our IIT. It is basically conducted in March.
IIT INDORE's library has a vast collection of books, newspapers, and journals. We have to take a library membership card to take the book along with us for a few days.
The library, classrooms, lecture hall, seminar hall complex, and auditorium are well-equipped and they look awesome.
Classes will be held in lecture hall complexes. Exams, seminars, and webinars will be held in the seminar hall complex and big events takes place in our Nalanda Auditorium.
Our college gives scope for taking part in extracurricular activities and sports.
There is a huge sports complex, volleyball and football courts on our campus.
There are lots of cultural clubs for promoting extracurricular activities like dance, singing, etc,, and also activate students towards social activities through social welfare club(Avana), EBSB club, etc.

I am currently studying Btech in AI ML. As the name suggests it's about artificial intelligence that's why I choose this course. The faculty is good and experienced. Also, their teaching method is quite good. But some faculties are not so good when it comes to teaching. All faculty have PhD degrees in their respective fields. Midterm exams are conducted after 2 months after the semester's start and the end term is conducted after 2 months of the midterm exams. Exams are not so difficult if you study little daily you can easily score good marks.Social life is quite good recently the abacus society organise IT mela fest where there were tech games, interesting workshops, etc. There are around 30 active clubs in college. 
Sports tournaments are regularly organized by a sports club on campus. Also, our teams go to different colleges to perform. 
Datum club, dedsec club etc are clubs organised by students for the students.

Students from the 8th semester are eligible for placements.
The placements in the college are good, and they make sure to place every student by providing ample opportunities. Top recruiting companies like Infosys, Wipro, TCS, Accenture, Coforge Ltd, HCL Technologies, Byju's, Huawei Technologies, etc.
The average package is 6 LPA and the highest package is 44 LPA.Fees for engineering are 159000 per year.
You can avail of scholarships like the up government scholarship and Bihar government scholarship and many more after paying the fees.
Once you pay the fee, you can apply for your state government scholarship and then submit a form to the registrar's office.

The faculty is very good as they ask for doubts and also the teacher in a very good way I am very satisfied with them.
Students are very more as it is a university in each semester, cat 1 and cat 2 exams are there and then ET (final exams happens ).
One thing that is bad is that 75 percent is a must otherwise uhh have to pay 5500 for one subject debarred.The fees of my course BCA are not as much as it is affordable and the job opportunities are also there.
But there is also a very much crowd as you know the competition is also very tough in a computer science course.
Scholarships are there in our college for reserved categories but I don't have any.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Campus life in college is an exciting experience filled with opportunities for personal growth, academic advancement, and social connections. Students can participate in a wide range of activities, including clubs, sports teams, and events, while also taking advantage of resources like libraries, study centres, and career services. Living in dorms or off-campus housing allows for independence and the chance to forge meaningful friendships with peers. The college environment provides a unique and memorable period of life that helps shape students into the individuals they will become.The internships can range from short-term projects to longer-term assignments that last for several months. Some popular companies that offer internships include Google, Amazon, Deloitte, Goldman Sachs, and Microsoft. The roles offered under internships can vary based on the company's requirements and the student's skills and interests. They can range from software development, business analysis, financial analysis, marketing, and more

The campus of BIT Mesra is one of the best campuses in India. It's spread over 780+ acres with forest cover all around it. It's really a treat to walk around the campus on holidays. Having a cycle is a must in our college because of the distance between hostels and main buildings and food joints (ofc girls hostel too. Our annual cultural fest is BITOTSAV held in February this time BITOTSAV 23 was held after 3 years and it was worth the hype with Papon's performance in Pro night. The annual tech fest is known as Pantheon, it has a huge scope impact me. We have a big library with almost all kinds of academic books available. The classrooms are all air-conditioned. The sports culture is pretty average. The sports complex is big and also with a gym. There are many cultural and tech clubs all organizing some events throughout the year. But our administration is very student unfriendly and struct that's a big con!Students are eligible for placements from the 7th semester onwards 6th sem onwards for internships. Placement for circuital branches here is comparable to the top 5 NITs with CSE average package being 22 LPA and the highest being 58 LPA overall average package was 13.5 LPA. Core companies also visit our campus and offer jobs to core branch students like mechanical, chemical, civil etc. but they are fewer in number. Top recruiters are Atlassian, Microsoft, Tata steel, Hero motor corp, Hyundai, Genpact etc

Placements are good at the National Institute of Technology Warangal, this campus is named for its placements every year and maintains good records every year with high and good packages and placements.
Companies such as Microsoft, IBM, Uber, Oracle, TCS, Deloitte, Capgemini, KPMG, Infosys and a few more top companies visit and offer placements, highest package last year was 88 LPA and the average was 19.9 LPA, about 95 per cent of students get placed last year.The mechanical department has good faculties and all are highly qualified they have years of experience in teaching and they teaching can be understood by the students easily. Faculty to the student ratio is about 4:20. We have two exams in a semester they mid-semester exam and the end-semester exam, and they are a bit difficult to pass with out studying.

From 2nd year you can sit for internships and from 3rd year you can sit for placements. The average package of our college is 16 - 20 Lakh per annum and the Highest Package is 1.2 Cr per annum. Most students get placed. After getting my degree I will peruse MBA.NSUT is known for its cultural fest Moksha which is one of the biggest fests in North India. There is a big library in our college, you will get all the books here. Sports and extracurricular activities are also there and many societies.

There were many social clubs and technical clubs on the college campus. The library is on 3 floors with abundant books. There were many fests which were organised like bitotsav or rostra. Good amenities in the classroom like a projector, and fan.Faculty were in the ratio of 1:10 and they helped us in every bit. The course was not very difficult to pass but the tussle for a higher GPA was surely there. Practical exams were again the tough part where we had to toil hard.

I have chosen BBA as a course because it is a professional course and learning opportunities were good at Delhi technological University.
The class size in my college is 60 students, the faculty is okayish and not so good some teachers are really good some are not so good.
I think the faculty should be improved in our college, faculty in our college is highly qualified and have done PHD in their respective fields, but the problem arises because of the teaching method and language barrier of teachers. Some teachers have excellent teaching methods and they go in-depth to teach that particular topic so that students can understand. In our college, the first mid-semester examination is conducted which is for 25 marks, and the level is moderate and not too difficult to pass the exam it is timely conducted by the college and the college is really very particular about exams. At last end, semester examination is conducted each semester and the level is moderate to a little bit difficult, and not too very easy to pasI am in my first semester at my college and have not experienced a fee hike as of now but yes I have heard from seniors that a fees hike is there in our college, for example, if the current fee is 98750 then in the next sem fee shall be raised to 100000. Scholarship is provided to a few students who have done excptionally well in their examinations. Financial assistance is provided to defence personnel children and to central reserve police force children and they are given fess concessions every year. Our college internship fair is conducted every year which provides internships to students to upskill them, and some students are given stipends according to their performance they have done. Placement cell in college is highly active and gives opportunity to students to learn new things and they also invite companies to internship fair. Also, the placement cell helps in recruitment process and providing placements to students and the average package of our college is around 400000 which is respected

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: The curriculum of IIT Kanpur is a bit tough. A semester usually ends in 4 months and has 5-6 subjects. Most of the subjects in 1st year are Institute Compulsory courses which are taught to students of every batch. Department course starts from 2nd year.The internship season starts in the 5th semester and the second phase starts in the 6th semester, where you can apply for an internship through SPO. The internship is also provided in 2nd year by E-cell. The internship is less for some core branches.

Every Year Amity Organises AURA where there are numerous events for days and there are many fests for technology, fashion, management etc quarterly. Our library has 13000 approx Hard copy books and Many soft copies. The library is made of two floors where a student can study with peace of mind. Each classroom has a mic with a speaker for faculties with comfortable chairs for the student where a student can seat in class with relaxation. the classroom contains a whiteboard with a projector, AC, Fan, Lights and internet Wifi for the students. There are numerous extracurricular activities which are organised every 15 days and there is an annual Sport named Sangathan where Every player from each campus of Amity comes to the Noida campus for the match. and there are sports competitions which is organised quaterly.
When we talk about social groups. clubs. there are many clubs for dance, music, coding, robotics etc. made by students.I have an interest in coding that why I have taken admitted to btech CSE In Amity they offer a variety of Courses for Every degree. for example, I am learning french for the past 2 years. The course provides us best knowledge for the industry fit level.
Faculties are highly qualified and they provide us best Knowledge according to the student.
Btech Students Are taught by the faculties who have completed their PHD and the methodology of the faculties o teach the students are best as they understand the student thoughts.
Exams are conducted at the end of the Semester, faculties also take tests once in a month. The level of the Exams is normal not very easy not hard, if the student had attended the class they can clear it.

The annual fest is named youth-pia the conducted ones in a year.
And the other fest is spurti.
They have a general library and lots of books in the library.
The sports activity is good they conducted a sports program name spurti for all Dehradun colleges.
The lots of groups and clubs.
For it Microsoft club
Google student club and many more.The total fees of the B. Tech programme at DIT University are Rs 8.51 Lakhs - Rs 12.51 Lakhs. Candidates need to pass 10+2 and appear for JEE Main entrance examination.
The median salary is 4.8 in present days and the highest is 58 lakh in palo alto
The placement is good 90 per cent ratio in it placed

Life at LPU is very Nice, coz There are so many fest running. There are 355 Student Organizations is there in LPU. LPUNSS and NCC are also Here, There are so many Societies like the Dance music society and many more. Fest includes One world, Coke Studio, Sukoon Youthfest, and may Techfest.The course Curriculum is very Systematic and well Designed In the First Year They Provide a Web Development course along with this C programming and Python Programming DBMS, and Software Engineering. After Faculty is very supportive

The campus of our college is extremely small in an area with almost very less places to hangout and next to no playground.
Besides this infrastructure of our college is well maintained with lifts in every building.
There are plenty of activities and annual fest like youthopia.
Our college has a well-maintained library.
There are also several technical and social clubs but not up to the mark.The fee structure for btech cse is 2.4 lpa
There are several scholarships provided at the time of addmission based on the merit of jee/12th marks.
You would also get a scholarship if you perform well in exams and several other awards are also there
There is no fee hike throughout your btech.
Companies like adobe, fiserv gives internship opportunity to 3rd year students with stipend of 40k

The infrastructure is pretty good here. In a short period of time, they gave developed it decently. In the coming years, this will be even bigger and better. There are various clubs here through which every Saturday we'll be having various events which will be fun. Over here is Non-ac the classrooms and all I mean. Something everyone should rememberThere's no fee hike policy. The fees division comes under category depending on the VITEE Exam rank. The highest fee is around 600000 per year. In Vit-ap students can get into the university through EAPCET rank too. The cut-off is around 5k-3k for CSE.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
There is a 10% hike in fees every year. and fees are above 2 lakhs where 1.5 lakhs are tuition fees and 50k is the hostel and mess charges.
The scholarship is based on your academic performance and the average package is around 11 lakh, many know companies come to campus for placements like google, Oyo, olx, Barclays, BYN, MakeMyTrip, etc.The student becomes eligible for campus placements in 3rd year 2nd semester.
The companies visited are BYN, Deloitte, OYO, OLX, MakeMyTrip, Barclays Google, Tekion, Samsung, media.net, conde nation, Mittal, etc.
The percentage of students who get placed is 98%.
and the highest package is 30 lakh and the average is around 11 lakh.

It's okayish. I am part of 2-3 clubs. It's fun to organize large events even college. You make friends and network a lot. Handling all the responsibilities great. Recommended to join clubs and work, it's very useful. The sports facility is also good here in our colleges like volleyball, cricket, and basketball ground all are available here.The curriculum is a bit hectic. You don't get a lot of time. Attendance is something they emphasize a lot although no one is interested in whatever is being taught in the college. Everyone just studies on Youtube 2-3 days prior to exams. The faculty is also not very excited to teach.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
I was interested in the ECE branch. 
The faculties are quite in large numbers and the students for each batch is around 600 students as our college only has 4 branches. All the professors are IITians. Some of them have researched from foreign universities and have worked on different projects.  It is not difficult to pass your exam if you study well. You just have to study a day before the exam and that's enough.Fees policy is very sorted and you'll be properly understanding the fee structure easily without any disturbance. You can also take an education loan here don't worry you'll have a great time in this college because everything's too good here and your parents will also feel safe and relaxed.

Exposure is very good at Amity University and there are a lot of clubs like the data science club, artificial technology club, dance and singing club, grooming club and many more. The library is also good most of the reference books are available there but the only issue is with the due date as books are issued for only 2 weeks and you have to get them reissued after 2 weeks and there's a 5rupees/day fine after the due date. The sports club is also good like kabaddi, cricket, basketball and table tennis. Debating committees are also available.The course curriculum is very good and there's one foreign language is also provided as a mandatory subject. There are many foreign languages like german, french, Arabic, Spanish and Sanskrit. 
Most of the faculty members are PhD holders. There are midterm exams after 2 months of the semester starting and end-term exams are conducted after 4 months of the semester. The difficulty level of exams was a bit average but most of my classmates passed exams without having any back log.

Campus life is really good at VIT and I m sure would really be happy about it we have numerous fests happening and you can really enjoy fests and on every Saturday there will be time allotted for fests and you can really feel good as they burst your stress out and make you happy and I really like dj nights in VIT where students can dance and enjoy you will definitely like them too. And VIT has the best library with the best books which are useful to students and we have various sports competitions happening and there are numerous extra circular activities too. We have a telegram group for VIT AP too. get latest updatesI have chosen ECE because I was interested in electronics and in VIT you can find students from all states students' strength was more in VIT and equally we have good strength in faculty too and they divide slots for each subject and faculty will be allocated according to slots so that they can cover all students. Most of our teachers have done specializations in their respective fields and they have good knowledge about the subject. Exams in VIT are a bit tough as they were asked application oriented you have to understand in depth about the concept and if u understand that you can definitely score well.

The course curriculum includes all the basic subjects like english, engineering mathematics, engineering physics , basic electrcial engineering, basic electronics, python programming , c programming , special subjects like php and ai based on your stream. These will be provided subjects for you in your first year.
I chose AI as my course to pursue because i am really interested in this field and wanted to learn more here.

The faculy student ratio here is quite good. I would say there would be 1:70 ratio and there isn't any issue for teachers. Yes sometimes in first year , there are issues when new teachers join and they cant match up with students properly.
Most of the faculty in my first year. has done phd. Or mtech. From good colleges like NIT or IITS. There are lectures and tutorials to solve problems as well. You will be taught in lectures and you will be given sheets which you can solve and ask doubts in tutorials.

The exams here are semester wise and it wasnt very hard to pass.The placement provided by college is basic and depends solely on your skills.
 Students can start looking for placement from 6th semester or third year.
There are many great companies visisted here like google , infosys , etc. Around more than 600 recruiters came this year.

The highest packagae was of 44lpa in Microsoft. The average packagae here woulf be around 5-7 lacs and it depends on your stream as well.

Mostly all students get placement though it may be of less amount but they get placed.

I am thinking of taking placement if its around 15-20lpa otherwise i will look into doing masters.

There are many annual fests such as Engifest, Yuvaan, and Aahvaan. Tech fests are very interesting and cool. Engifest in February, yuvaan in February before Engifest, and Aahvaan (sports fest) in March. Sports complex and a cool campus.
Many sports and too many (in a good way) societies for music, dance, poetry, and tech societies (robotics and stuff).
Freesources is a website run by students (seniors) and volunteers which provides notes and assignments, it is a very important thing to have as it helps everyone on their exams and academics.I can handle physics and you are allowed to appear in the placement of all kinds of firms such as tech companies, and non-tech companies, and core is also an option.
The faculty is fine, not too involved, and rather strict in terms of exams and marking, labs are cool. The student ratio is very few girls compared to guys.
The qualifications of the faculty are good.
Exams are held every two months and they are manageable if you study properly.

I have chosen this course because of my passion on CSE. Faculty are pretty good and well trained. For every 20 members there will be one faculty mentor. All the faculty are PhD qualified. There will be 2 mid exams ane one sem exam in each sem along with quizzes, assignments, labs, language, and extra skill section. If you prepare before the exam for one or two days we can pass 40% is the pass percentageThe infrastructure is good. Even though not a vast campus. It is pretty good. Surrounded with greenery. Some events will be conducted every weekend, Sunday, and Monday holiday. Every year there will be a tech and cultural fest. The library is small, there will be all sports and coaches available. There will be many clubs and workshops run by students under faculty visionary.

Life is really good here at LPU,  Social life is really amazing you can know people of different cultures and states.
The library consists of books in large amounts, you can issue them whenever needed. There is a huge craze for sports among students.As for the faculty members, the teachers are excellent and well-qualified.
Teachers are very friendly and you can ask your doubts whenever and wherever needed, teachers are always ready to help.
Teaching methods are excellent.

I want to know how big organization works and make trends in the market. There are many 80 to 90 students who pursuing BBA. There hire very good teachers and their teaching methods are also very good.
Recently in Nov -Dec, They took my 1st sem exam. No there is no difficulty to pass the exam.Recently in the first semester beginning they organize a fresher party for the new students their management is very good and the event is also awesome. They have many extra curricular activities and also many sports like cricket hockey football and many more.

Engineering students can do many things including designing, developing systems and working with AI. It covers multiple sectors like electronics and healthcare. This sector helps me to pursue my career in a good direction that's why I choose to start my career in engineering.
The faculty of my colleagues were very highly experienced, very skilled and very talented. They provided us best skills and knowledge that's why students got good placements in great companies. The ratio of students per faculty is not more it is about 30-35.
All the faculty are Doctorate or pursuing Doctorate except 2-3.
The entrance exam is basically conducted 2 times in an academic year and to passing in exam is not much difficult.AKTU provides the best placement in every branch of engineering. Every student can get placement in good IT companies. There are more than 150 companies visited every year. All high-class MNCs visited here for campus placement. Google, Microsoft, Jio, Capgemini, Wipro and all. 
The average package on the AKTU campus is 5 LPA and the highest package on the campus is 95 LPA.
Students become eligible for placement after completing the 5th semester of engineering. There are no percentage criteria for placement but some companies have their personal demand for placement.

The best thing about this college is the KIIT fest which is the annual fest of the college. This year's fest was held for 16 days and it was very awesome. Everyone enjoyed the fest. They conduct many other activities like sports and extracurricular activities.KIIT is better than any other private college and its placement is quite good. 
Faculty members are also good as they are very knowledgeable and they are friendly with students.
There are around 200 students in the civil engineering branch (one batch only).

On the other, there are a lot of scholarship programs also like the mark scholarship, ewl scholarship and there are many more scholarships in university.

Cultural tech-fest - VITOPIA and Technical - VITAPP  conducted around December and march.
The library is awesome the climate is so good and you can get whatever you want.
The classrooms are good and big enough.
Sports and co-curricular activities are conducted but the problem is native people are preferred and thats what hurts me a lot.
Clubs are some wonderful things they have especially cultural state clubs representing their cultures. Small events take place every Saturday. We have so many clubs. Social life to talk about is you can't come in physical contact with your opposite-gender friends. All the faculties are highly qualified, and most of them are IIT PHD scholars. Teaching depends on person to person, some still follow old methods of teaching on board and some follow teaching on the projector via pdfs. Exams are pretty often like every month, we have consecutive 3 exams in one semester and you have to pass every one of them. They are easy to pass if you study properly. You can even pass if you study the night before but you need your concepts clear.

From the 6th semester, the student is eligible for the placements they will give placement training for the students who have to crack the placement in a cool way
800+ companies visit the campus and the students apply and are placed in the top MNC companies
70 per cent is the minimum percentage for the required criteria for the placement and it is based on the companyWe have the international fest called VITOPIA every year, the life at the campus will be awesome
The books and journals in the library are too good for the students and the faculties too
We have large spacious grounds and game course too they will be conduct tournaments

VIT has a good social environment. Every month an event with regards to our carrier. Yearly once there will be a sports event and we will get cash prizes. Annually there will be an event called RIVERA in which celebrities participate. Our university has a huge library where everything is digital. Which includes sports, social work, and so many societies where you can apply and move on.

I am interested in both mechanical and electrical, so I chose mechatronics. our principal said (who is HOD of my DEPT, He taking the class for this sem) " our college only first launched a mechatronics course In India,  our college curriculum motto is to develop knowledge in both MECH AND ELECTRICAL. The faculty ratio is 1:60. All the faculties are Ph.D. holders.
In all debt except our dept, there are 20 to 30 faculty per dept, but our dept has 11, which is minus but we have a principal.
120 students per class,10 departments,120 *4=480 students per dept, 480* approx 4800 students in college.
Faculty 25*15 =375,  dept including math, Phy, chem so the ratio is  375/4800=0.078.
I checked my college website most of the faculty completed their Ph.D.
Teaching method is
1)  ppt presentation
2) using software
3) practical
4) sometimes oral
There are 3 internal exams namely the cat exam, and 1 external exam per sem above have difficult to pass.First of all, our college is old around 70 years, so you can not imagine advanced infra, but old is gold, a very peaceful environment.
There is n number of clubs.
No ragging.
I am not sure about fest because am a 1st-year student.

enough book is available, the sad thing is there is no storybook.
you can take a book for 2 weeks with you.
The classroom has a 2-seated bench.
have a projector and a peaceful environment.
IOT club.

I choose the course cause I will really passionate about coding and was also having a good experience in the same. The faculty: student ratio is 1:15 and the faculty members are well qualified and most of them are highly experienced.
Most of the faculty have either completed their Ph.D. or are currently pursuing it within the university, while others have done their MTech and are good at teaching. While you might find some teachers teaching methods to be a bit novice.
The exams are conducted timely like mid sem, ends, and the CAs the exam are of moderate level and the questions are mostly from the study material provided by teachers.The fees are a bit on the higher side and I pay 90000 per semester and 75000 for the hostel and 35000 for the mess which is pretty average.
The cost to study here while availing of all the facilities provided by the university is around 3.5 lac per year.
The scholarships available here are through LPUNEST, JEE entrance, and on basis of 12th percentage.
Financial aid is given to orphans, the specially-abled, and children of the military of defense personnel.
Currently, I am not eligible for internships and jobs but placements here are quite good.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
I had to go for UPSC civil services exam. So this course seemed to be the most appropriate one for it.
Faculty to student ratio was really great. It is not fixed. It rather depends upon the course you are pursuing. Could be anything like 30 to 600 students. Usually, 120-150 students are in a class. There are about 850 faculty members.
Most of the faculty have some higher education from renowned foreign institutes. About 40% teach really nicely. 30% acceptable. Rest is just to make you fall asleep in class.
Exams are held at regular intervals. Passing is not a big deal. 1 day before the exam is sufficient. However, getting a good grade is really hard.Rendezvous is the annual fest conducted on 9-12 March 2023. Tryst is the tech fest conducted on 6-9 March 2023. Dates vary every year. Usually, from Feb to April, these events are carried out.
Our library is one of the biggest in India. You can ask them to avail a book if it's not available. 
Classrooms are simply world-class. They have projectors, biometric attendance and everything you can ask for.
The sports facilities are really great. There is a sports complex and grounds for various sports.
Most of the work here is done by students. Both the fests are organised by students.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Life on campus is very happening. Yearly many fests are conducted by different student bodies on the campus. Among all Tryst (technical fest) and Rendezvous (Cultural fest) are the most famous and are Asia's largest fest. We have a central library which houses books of every kind. Amenities for sports and extracurriculars are provided and these activities are promoted through various competitions organised by respective clubsFrom the 7th semester, students become eligible for placements. Many companies in various domains like HFT, Finance, Consulting, core, analyst, management, and product management come to campus and offer a satisfactory amount of seats to students. In recent placements, the highest offer was made to a student of 4 crores pa by Jane street. Around 70-80% of students get placed.

I chose this course because of passion I chose btech cse over any other course.in lpu the ranking of computer science is far more than any other course so I preferred lpu. most of the Faculties are Ph.D. holders so they are understandable and exams will be conducted twice in a semester.the scholarship which I got is from the university itself of 48000/- deduction from the base fee of 120000/- for me annually 144000/- is the fee. the total course fee will be for btech cse 560000/- for maximum scholarship holders.

In 3 yrs students starts getting offers of intership

Top recurting companies are google, Microsoft , Amazon and many more companies
97 percent student get placementFees of dtu is too high 
It's about 9.3 lakh for btech course. 
DTU provided the scholarship for economically weaker student . 
Dtu campus placement is out standing
Highest international package is 1.8 cr 
Domestic package is above 60 lakh

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
The annual fest of IIT Kanpur is Antaragni which is also one of Asia's largest technical fest Techkriti and both are conducted in the month of march.
Basically in the library, we have academics-oriented books that are large in number.
Nearly 10k books are there which is also the largest among all IITs
We have AC in our classes and staircase seats are there which also makes the board seems visible
We have a  projector which projects on a large screen.
There are facilities for as many sports as possible. 
We have been provided with coaches for respective games. Seniors help alot which makes it easy for some people who are starting fresh. 
In extracurricular as well have had every thing possible and seniors teach u so well that if you are starting from scratch also, you won't face problems.
All the groups, clubs, website, ig as well as fest, everything is run by students.The faculty-student ratio depends on the department to department My department has a batch strength of 70 students whereas electrical peeps have a strength of 190  The faculty here are highly qualified.
Almost all have done PhD in respective fields that too from colleges around the world and are ready to help you out if u ask for it. We have our exams namely quizzes, mid sem and ends It all happens within 4 months Exams are not difficult to pass but its really difficult to get a good grade because the competition here is massive.  Also, you must be looking at your friends who are from let's say tier 2 college, they study just one or two days before the exams and gets result but there is nothing as such.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: Placement is conducted by our training and placement cells at Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of technology. we have to register on the portal of TNP and called for an interview accordingly so overall liberal placement.Fees structure is the same as for all nits colleges 1 lakh 25 thousand and scholarship are given to SC and ST students. there are also arrangements for loans per SBI bank already situated on the campus. So no financial crisis

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
The fee structure is based on your family income. For example, if your family income is below 1 lakh per year then you have to pay only 12000 rupees per semester.
And you will also get a scholarship of 1000 rupees per month if your cgpa will remain above 6.5.

Job opportunities at IIT Delhi are great.
If you are a little bit sincere then you definitely get a job.The cultural fest is known as rendezvous. Tech fest is TRYST. Generally, these fest organised in March or April month.

The library of IIT Delhi is world-class having more than 3 lakh books and journals.

The classroom looks like a 5-star hotel room.
There are many cultural clubs including music, drama, dance etc

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The Electronics branch and IIT BHU was a good balance of branch and reputed IIT. The branch is also considered a circuital branch which provides subsequently more opportunities during the placement season.
Most of the faculties are highly qualified and some are recognised researchers in their fields. 
Mid sem are held at the end of the second month of each sem while end sem is at the end of the fourth month. Assignments are given variably according to profs. Passing these exams is fairly easy but getting good grades is really really hard!In the 7th sem, the placement season starts and you are allowed to sit in those. Decimal Point Analytics, Microsoft, Sprinklr, ServiceNow, Uber, Scifom, Trilogy Innovations (CodeNation), Mentor Graphics (Sicmens), Texas Instruments, Oracle,
MyKaarma, Wells Fargo, American Express, INTUIT, UI Path, Standard Chartered
BNY Mellon, Digit Insurance, VISA, Axxela, Publicis Sapient, etc. A total of 474 offers on the first day.
I might pursue MBA after getting the degree

LPU offers a scholarship of up to approx.6 lakh per student. LPU also provide many facilities to underprivileged students the amount of the scholarship depends on the passing PHASE of the particular student.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The biggest annual fest is Kashi yatra held in Jan. Technex annual tech fest is in March. Sports fest is held in October. The library is not that big but would be sufficient to find the books for the courses taught. The classroom is basic with good benches and projectors but no air conditioning they are well equipped with a mic. Sports are well appreciated by each indoor and outdoor sport. Everything is unique from shotput to Javelin throw. Many clubs from business management to social service to robotics to sports car average to the aerodynamic club to music club.This was the best option I could find. Electronics classifies as a circuital branch. This would open the same opportunities the Computer science students would have. 
The faculty-student ratio is around 10 professors for a department of 400 students across all year. The teaching method is great with their high expertise in any topic they teach. Exams are timely.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: The campus first of all is very scenic and beautiful as it is located amidst Mountains and valleys. And the campus has all basic facilities like Wi-Fi, classrooms, library, labs, etc., and has very good infrastructure. In hostel rooms, rooms are compact but sufficiently enough, semi-furnished with beds, desks, almirahs, chairs, etc. And the mess food is good with variations. The campus has a badminton court, Table tennis, a ground for practicing cricket, etc.I am still studying here but I have heard from my seniors about placements So I may be wrong. It seems to be above average. Above 90% of students were placed in this course. The highest may be around 45 LPA and the lowest was 13 lpa while the average goes around 23lpa. Top companies include Oracle, TCS, etc. Regarding internship around 78% of the course strength got into. The top roles offered were as Data Scientists, Data analysts, etc.

I had chosen the course because I wanted to peruse the same. And lovely is only famous for engineering here we have students in almost every part of India and it has collaboration with many foreign countries. The faculty member is either pursuing Ph.D. or completed their Ph.D., they are very cooperative. There are two exams called mid and end term weightage of mid is 20 marks and 50 for end terms. 25 for class assessment and 5 for attendance.Here, we have a placement cell which is very active for students from the third year we have the placement classes but from the first semester only they teach us how to present ourselves. In the placement more than 500 companies take part. If you are in LPU, it's sure you will get a job but how much package you will get it's up to your performance.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
I have a passionate coder since 9th standard so B.Tech was the obvious choice for me. Another reason was that I always topped in PCM in school without taking any stress so I thought Engineering will be the perfect fit. IIT was my dream since 10th standard because my mother's bestfriends son got admitted in IIT Kanpur
Faculty to student ratio balanced. A single subject is divided between 3 faculty members alongwith Teaching assistants
Maximum faculty members are alumni of IITs themselves and have pursued Post Doc from foreign universities in USA, UK, Germany. Faculty is very learned and have immense knowldege surpassing even their domain. Teaching style differs from professor to professor but most of them are young so they understand and teach according to students.
Mid sems and End sems are conducted with 3 quizzed before mid and end sems. Reading PPTs and notes of the professor alongwith previous year question papereven just 3 days before exam are sufficient to get a good grade.The annual Cultural fest is Kashiyatra and conducted in January,
Annual Technical fest is Technex conducted in February,
The annual Sports fest is Spardha conducted in September/October.
IIT BHU has a big library with vast resources of books and journals. The library has an AC facility and 4 big large reading rooms.
Classrooms are soundproof and have a blackboard and smart boards. Depending on the department one may have AC too.
IIT BHU has 4 councils - Cultural, Film & Media, Sports, Science & Technology.
Each has different clubs and IIT BHU is the perfect place to hone your all-around skills. You name it and we have a club with experienced seniors and alumni to guide their juniors.
All the clubs and councils have their Instagram pages.
Some of the clubs are dance, quiz, literary, Indian & western music, aeromodelling, astronomy, coding, business, media, outreach, cine, badminton, aquatics, and volleyball. This is not even half the list.

An annual cultural fest named Blitzschlag is organised in our college during the month of February 
Technical fest named Sphinx is organised in our college during the month of January
Different types of books are available in our library and ebooks also available.Students become eligible for placement after 7th semester of their course.
Some of the companies that visited are Deloitte, JP Morgan, Expedia, TVS Motors, Reliance Industries, L&T. And different types of roles are offered in different companies.

I graduated with information technology as my major so Mtech in computational and data sciences was my first choice. The faculty-to-student ratio is the best in the country. Faculties have studied at top universities in the world like ASU, MIT etc. and their Research level is at par with any high-ranking researcher in the world. Exams were based more on practical knowledge than theoretical. We were given projects on completion we would get marked.different companies ranging from meta, google, pay u, Microsoft etc visit our campuses to offer internships with a handsome stipend ranging from 50k to 1 lakh depending upon the work and interview of the individuals. I interned at pay u as a data science and I currently working at pay u as a data engineer from 2021

Btech course is quite interesting and beneficial for the student's Carrier although after COVID the requirements of this course are increasing
The faculty is quite eligible and Qualified
They have full knowledge about the subject also experience holder more than 2 years 
Our semester is divided into 2 terms mid-term examination and end-term examinationFests are usually held on the campus at a weekly process which is interesting for the students
All the books which are important for the students are available in the library also you can take books from the library to study at your home
Sports facilities are very good on the campus
Whether it is football or handball or any other activities

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: There is a cultural fest Kashiyatra, a tech fest TechNex and a sports fest Spardha every year. All these events are very big and happening. There are many other small fests and events throughout the year. All course books and popular journals are available in the library either as e-books or as hard copies. The classrooms are very basic with a board, speaker system, projector system etc. Sports are very much respected and encouraged here. There is a new building with really good sports equipment and space at IIT BHU. Their many clubs run by students namely Cult, Science & Technology and Social Services.I choose ECE because it is very interesting and gets you good placements. Faculty is very strict about core studies so it is hard to manage side interests. The faculty is well-qualified for teaching and will help you with your curriculum always. Exams are hosted twice every semester and are easy to pass.

Course curriculum compared to another college, vit will be great. Faculties are skilled and knowledged persons, they will communicate with students very well. It will be better to clear your course on the time , later it will be difficult to you , because there is rules and courses are offered at the particular time, You need to pay 6000 to re register your single course.The Rivera is a famous event in our college, which is conducted every year, Celebrities and famous singers are participated , there is also different events conducted by various clubs in the college.
Especially sangamam and tamili are Tamil students

Life at the campus was definitely so good there are many clubs to join and explore and there is a tech fest called Gravitas that is conducted every year the library is very big and contains every essential book and the classroom facilities are neat and good and there are many sports competitions conducted throughout the year and there is also a club called TLA run by studentsPlacements are quite good, students are eligible for placements in their 3rd semester starting, companies like city union Bank, Federal Bank and BNP Paribas, etc came for placements and  12 students got selected per company, the highest package is 6LPA and the average is 3 LPA and 60 per cent of students getting placed and after degree, I plan to do MBA

The fee structure is a bit of a pinch on the pocket considering this is a government college. Nonetheless, the facility and the networking opportunities in my opinion give you a bang for the buck you put on the line. College is as much a space to study but is also a hub of networking with people who even 5 or 6 years down the lane would help you out giving a push to your career in the right direction.
Plus for many students who can't afford it right away, the college has provisions for many scholarships and schemes for relaxation and easy loan lending which is a great boon.Curriculum and Faculty here are a hit or miss. Some are way too good with students, others might have their walls up and be strict to the teeth with their methodology and teaching. But nonetheless, the faculty has always been made of qualified individuals with some of them being one of the best in their streams. Exams are straightforward if you're continuously revising and doing what the teachers are teaching.

The fee is an important talk in college because fee hike is a big issue. But when we see the maintenance and facilities of the college then we are satisfied with these fees also.
There are many scholarships comes in college, like government and private charity alsoAnnual fest in college is a much more awaited moment in college life because fests come and the pressure of the students is released.
Books are available in the college library, any books you want then surely you find in the library

I chose computer science and engineering as I wanted to become a software engineer. the faculty: student ratio is nearly 1:10 here. there are many faculties here in vit. faculties here are all doctors who have done many ph.d's in renowned universities. the teaching method is good, and they let you choose your own faculty under the system called ffcs. exams are mostly easy to medium and now the time between exams is too low. there are no holidays after a sem and they are continuously keeping sem after sem without any holidays. it is pretty easy to pass the courses here.The annual fest here is called riviera and it is conducted in February. tech fest is called gravitas and it is from September-October. the library here is very big and there are many books and journals available here. classrooms are good but they're not like auditorium ones. they're normal classrooms and don't have any AC. people participate in many sports from vit and they have 2 large stadiums for sports and fests. there are nearly 150 clubs and chapters which is pretty huge for a college.

College life entirely depends on what you do with your life in college. LPU will give you all you want for a successful career and the skills thereof. But won't give you enough time to waste on non-productive and unnecessary things. You get tons of organizations to join, and a large number of events to participate in, organist, and enjoy. A large number of leisurely places, be it the central vista, Mall or playgrounds or gyms or swimming pools or indoor stadiums or skating rinks, or nets to practice. But it does not compromise in any case with your safety, your studies, your skill attainment, your employability, and finally your placements. Whatever the duration of your program, your campus life will depend on what YOU do with it. If you are sincere about your studies or any other activity, it will be great. The university charges a fee from your parents to enable a great career for you, and that's what LPU does best.Usually, the university collaborates with various reputed companies to offer internships to its students. Some of the companies that have offered internships in the past include Amazon, Google, Microsoft, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, etc. The roles offered under internships may vary depending on the company's requirements and project needs. During the internships, students get to work on real-life projects that help them gain practical experience and industry exposure. The projects assigned to the students are carefully designed to match their skill set and learning objectives. The internships usually come with a stipend, and the amount may vary depending on the company and the duration of the internship. The internship opportunities provided by Lovely Professional University help students to develop their skills and get hands-on experience that can boost their career prospects.

88%-92.9%= 50% scholarship,
Above 93%= 100% scholarship,
No internship is provided by the college.I chose this course because of my interest in psychology. the faculty here was good in the beginning but now it is average. College life entirely depends on what you do with your life in college. LPU will give you all you want for a successful career and the skills thereof. But won't give you enough time to waste on non-productive and unnecessary things. You get tons of organizations to join, and a large number of events to participate in, organist, and enjoy. A large number of leisurely places, be it the central vista, Mall or playgrounds or gyms or swimming pools or indoor stadiums or skating rinks, or nets to practice. But it does not compromise in any case with your safety, your studies, your skill attainment, your employability, and finally your placements. Whatever the duration of your program, your campus life will depend on what YOU do with it. If you are sincere about your studies or any other activity, it will be great. The university charges a fee from your parents to enable a great career for you, and that's what LPU does best.Usually, the university collaborates with various reputed companies to offer internships to its students. Some of the companies that have offered internships in the past include Amazon, Google, Microsoft, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, etc. The roles offered under internships may vary depending on the company's requirements and project needs. During the internships, students get to work on real-life projects that help them gain practical experience and industry exposure. The projects assigned to the students are carefully designed to match their skill set and learning objectives. The internships usually come with a stipend, and the amount may vary depending on the company and the duration of the internship. The internship opportunities provided by Lovely Professional University help students to develop their skills and get hands-on experience that can boost their career prospects.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: The college has two main fests, "Techniti", the technical fest and "Utkansh", the cultural fest. This year the Techiniti was in February and Utkansh will be in March. However, these are not the right timings, before Covid these fests took place in other months. We have a decent library with all the required material. Classrooms depend basically on your branch. Most classrooms are good and fitted with the required amenities. There are multiple clubs and societies for all sorts of talents.IT and Electrical branches are new in the college, hence, faculty are still being hired in these departments. Other departments have a decent faculty and faculty: student ratio is about 1: 50. Most teachers are highly qualified with a PhD degree. As far as exams are concerned, minor exams are conducted twice each sem and the major exam is conducted at the end of each sem. The minions are comparatively tougher than the major exam.

The placements toatlly depend upon the capability of the student. KIIT brings various companies every year for placements. Every year more than 300 companies visit the campus and hire KIITIANS. The stipend is around 5 to 6 lacs and the roles offered are software developer trainee.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
Life on the campus is pretty chill. You have ample freedom. 
The crowd here is nice, and everyone is grounded and friendly. You will have a memorable social life here. 
The classrooms and labs are decent. The infrastructure is beautiful.
Jaipur is an amazing city with lots and lots of things, activities, and food, but the campus is 20km away from the city. 2 buses go from the college to the city but are always overcrowded, so you must come much in advance to get a seat.
The sports on the campus include cricket ground, football ground, basketball court, Lawn Tennis court, Badminton court, Squash court, Kabbadi ground, and Lawn Tennis. There is a Gym, but its quality is not that great.
There are many cultural and technical clubs in this college, entirely run and managed by students. Being part of them is always fun and you learn things and manage things and make friends along the way.The course curriculum is bery similar to IITs. Faculty student ratio could be better. Faculty is qualified in terms of knowledge but I believe teaching methods depend from teacher to teacher and some classes are good and some are a waste of time depending upon the teaching method.
Every semester has mid sem and ends sem exams and several quizzes along the way and all are evaluated for grading. 
Getting a good CGPA is very tough here as teachers are strict in giving marks.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology (TIET) Patiala is a private institution, and the fee structure varies depending on the program and specialization. Here is some information about the fee structure, financial assistance, and campus jobs at Thapar:
The fee structure at Thapar is on the higher side compared to other engineering colleges in India. The approximate fee structure for the CSE program is around Rs. 3-4 lakhs per year. The fee includes tuition fees, hostel fees, mess fees, and other miscellaneous charges.
Thapar provides various scholarships and financial assistance to eligible students. Some of the scholarships include merit-based scholarships, need-based scholarships, and scholarships for students from economically weaker sections. Students can apply for these scholarships during the admission process or after joining the institution.
Thapar has a dedicated Career Development Center that provides assistance and scholarships.TIET has a dedicated placement cell that works closely with the industry to provide internship and job opportunities to students. The placement process involves pre-placement talks, aptitude tests, group discussions, and personal interviews.
Students become eligible for campus placements from the seventh semester of their B.Tech program, and the placement cell provides ample training and guidance to prepare them for the recruitment process.
TIET has a robust placement network, with top companies from various sectors such as IT, consulting, manufacturing, and finance visiting the campus for recruitment. Some of the prominent recruiters at TIET include Amazon, Microsoft, Deloitte, Samsung, Oracle, L&T, and Mahindra. The highest package offered at TIET is around 43 lakhs per annum, and the average package offered is around 11 lakhs per annum.
TIET has an excellent placement record, with over 90%  of eligible students getting placed every year.

review_text: Elan & Nvision is the annual fest which is huge compared to other colleges in the city except for bits-h. The library currently is limited but will be expanded upon completion of the new building, classrooms are well equipped, and decent sports facilities are already available, when the sports complex is completed it will have the best sports facilities of all colleges in Telangana. Almost 20 clubs here are run by students and any interest you have, you can surely work on.From the 7th semester, students are eligible for placements. All the major companies visit IITH as it is an IIT, and if you have the skills you will almost certainly be placed.  The highest package is around 57L and the average package is around 27L per annum.  Around 70 percent of students get placed. And if you are in engineering physics the scope for research is very good.

this course was the one available for my rank at this college and I didn't want to miss such an opportunity to attend DTU. I could have gotten better courses at other colleges but none of them came close to DTU in package availability and the amount.

Enough that out of around 36 batches of the first year. No teacher has to teach their subjects to more than 2 batches.
They are well educated and got high degrees in their respective subject, no one being lower than M.Tech and their teaching is a bit difficult to understand if you don't have prerequisite knowledge of the topic and if not you have to study the topic at least once after they teach it..to fully understand it.
the term exams are timely with them being mid sem exactly in the middle of the semester after half the duration and the end-sem exam at the end of the semester. The difficulty level of passing is pretty high. But if you have studied with effort you can definitely pass.There are many fests organised in the college with very high attendance not only from the DTU but from other colleges like engifest, freshers, tech week in last 4 months I have been in dtu.
College facilities are good with a bit of lacking parts such as the maintenance of washroom but there is visible progress in it.
classroom fans and lights work well 
With there also being some classes with AC.
Labs are also well-maintained and clean.
There are various societies which organise their events and the application process
I also applied to one named DTU consultant society. The seniors taking the interview were nice and friendly.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Campus life is quite chill here. the hostel time is around 8-9 p.m. The campus time is around 7-8 p.m. 
Talking about the college fest - it is 'saturnalia' which occurs around November - December. It's not that great but also won't disappoint you.
The library at thapar is the best library you can get in India or maybe it has some ranking all over Asia too. There are tonnes of books here and a lot of aesthetic sitting areas.
There is an area for sports basically all sorts of games are there, even you can get the equipment issue for some hours without any cost.I wanted to pursue Computer engineering, it is a bit different from computer science engineering since Computer engineering is a bit more inclined towards hardware but most of the things are still the same.
In lectures, the ratio is 1:150-200
In labs, the ratio stands at 1:30
We have a semester of around 5 months, and two major exams each sem i.e. midsem and endsem.
Before both, we have sessionals and lab evaluations probably of around 10-20 marks.
They are quite easy to pass by studying just a week before exams

DTU is well known for its huge fest and very chill campus life. It has 5-6 really big fest per year. The cultural fest i.e. Engifest is the biggest fest in North India. Along with it, we also have technical, sports, literary, etc fest. Some fest is organised by different societies of the college. DTU library is really big, you can wide range of books in it. All classrooms are fully air-conditioned and have projectors and good-quality benches. College is very supportive of sports. The sports facilities are really good. There are many societies and  clubs in the college, you can choose based on your choiceCurrently, for a btech course, the total fee for four years is 9.31 lakhs. In the first year, 2.19 lakhs while in the last year, it is 2.5 lakhs. Students get scholarships via college also or through the Delhi government. The college also provides you with an education loan at a cheap rate of interest as dtu has SBI bank on its own campus. University provides you with good internship opportunities and on-campus placements
