I chose B.E IN CSE bcoz I was more interested in maths, so I took CSE.
There are many faculties in our college and almost all of them did M.TECH and few of them also Did Ph.D. from good colleges.
But when it comes to teaching it's not so great. they teach up to a certain extent you can't completely depend on them for exams.
The exams(internals) are conducted almost every month which sucks and sem exams will be conducted once in 5 months.The annual fest is conducted once every year in the month of April/May and they call it 'furore' .
There is a vast library where you can find almost every book which is related to the courses offered by the college.
The classrooms are fine, but not so great.
There are many clubs in the college like a center for performing arts, point blank-it is a club related to coding and staff.

Fest is conducted every month but someone the great feast is conducted in the week of February or March. Engi fest is one of the biggest fests in dtu. 
The library provides all kinds of books for courses and for reading. 
All kinds of sports are available and competitions are conducted frequently.For a middle-class family, fees are average and college provides student loan that can pay after getting a job giving them 2 years of time after the course end. The big company comes every year more than 80+ taking students from every branch.

The course curriculum is quiet good. It's a balanced curriculum. I chose this course because based on my rank I have got this course only in this college. so I opted for this one as I wanted to stay in my mother's state. Faculty are well qualified and have doctorates. All our faculty members have completed PhDs in IITs. Their teaching is very good and strict as we can see that factor in every college probably. Mostly all of them are punctual in their own way. Coming to faculty to student ratio cannot be predicted as it will be based on the branch to branch on respective departments. Exams are conducted twice a year in 2 semesters and each semester has 2 mids and respective labs.As I am from a general category background I'm not eligible for any kind of scholarship given by the government and by academics standards I don't have many scholarships. The fee structure is based semester-wise. Campus placements opportunities are excellent in every branch of the college .in chemical engineering the campus opportunities are high in level and the average to min package in our branch is 8lpa. Internship opportunities are also provided by calling up the companies to our college. definitely, one can be placed in the final year if we concentrate on studies without any barriers

Udbhav is the annual feast that happens in our college, and we also have our kannada rajyotsava fest at the end of the odd semester. Our college has a vast library facility, and most of the classrooms are well equipped with projectors and stuff. We have a deca office which looks after the cultural stuff. And for the sports and all, we have good playground facilities and also have annual sports. We have a lot of technical teams for race car building,  aircraft building and drones as well Names of them are quatlas,  velocita,  stire racing, Editha, and Volante. Ramaiah has good strength of faculty. All faculty are highly qualified but the teaching methodology is not good for all the faculty, most of them are good. Due to admission during covid we won't get enough time for our exam preparation and the difficulty level of the paper is medium it's not too easy nor too difficult.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Fees per semester are nearly 1.2 lakhs for the general category whose family income is more than 5 lakhs. And for general category students with a family income between 1 to 5 lakhs and all OBC students get a remission of 2/3 of tuition fees which are 1 lakh per semester. And whose family income is less than 1 lakh will get full remission of tuition fees and a 100 per cent tuition fee waiver for sc /st / pwd students.

Sports and other facilities are great and there are too many clubs, societies, and teams run by the student body to discover and live their passion.

The annual tech fest of KIIT is known as the KIIT FEST and is very popular. Every year big artists from all around the country visit our campus to perform. There are also many tech workshops held and extracurricular activities and cultural night programs during the fest. KIIT FEST is generally held from December to February. The KIIT Central Library has world-renowned collection of journals and books. It has more than 100000+ books and 40000+ journals. The classrooms are modern and equipped with all the required amenities to serve the students. Classrooms have screens, projectors, air conditioners, and whiteboards. KIIT is well known for its sports facilities and many renowned superstars are alumni of KIIT. KIIT spends a lot to improve the sports infrastructure. KIIT MUN is a famous club here at KIIT and very well-known all across the globe. Other clubs include Kaliope (Anchoring), Kreative Eye (Photography), and KFS (Film Society).The fees here at KIIT are divided into different parts. The institutional fee is 175000 per semester and the hostel fees vary from 28000 to 60000 per semester depending on the type of hostel opted. The mess fee is fixed at 22500 and there is some other cost like exam registration etc. KIIT also takes one-time money of 75000 for providing a laptop and blazer. The total cost to study at KIIT varies from 15 to 20 lakhs. There are many bank loans that can be availed to study here at KIIT and there are many scholarship awards. KIIT provides scholarships to the top 1000 rank holders in KIIT. They give 10000 rupees per month to the students. There are also stipends for securing top rank every year. I secured 3rd rank in the 3rd semester and received 5000 rupees per month for the next 6 months.

The faculty is really educated and professional. Though I would say they were a little cruel to us and didn't have much time for us. We were given a lot of exams projects and assignments and ppts. college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Placement season comes every year likely for last year's students. The company from various fields including cooperate and research-based companies are present. 
The average package is like 10 to 15 lakhs CTC and the highest fits cores.
Almost everybody gets placed though depending on the skill of the person also.Companies come to grant internship opportunities after every summer break.
Moreover, information about internships is also circulated via mail between semesters. 
Companies for both research and industrial sectors grant internship opportunities.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Two months internship is compulsory after the sixth semester. Companies do visit for two months of internships but the scenario is not that good companies don't prefer two months of interns. But the campus placements are good. All major tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and Amazon do visit our campus for the placement and they allow circuital branches to sit in their recruitment drive. Avg package is around 16 LPA with the highest package from the ECE branch in the IT company. Core placements are also improving. Around 90% of ECE students get placed every year.Two annual fests are organized here. TechNiti is the name of the Annual Technical Fest and Utkansh is the Annual Cultural fest of NIT Jalandhar. Every engineering branch has its own society and there are many clubs that cover vast activities like Kalakaar and Baware are the theatre clubs, Octaves is the music club, etc. The library is not that good but it has almost all the books needed for curriculum coverage. Classrooms are air-conditioned and have a blackboard, projector, and Samsung flip touch display board.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
There is numerous fest which happens here, the biggest one is MOOD INDIGO, TECHFEST, E-SUMMIT, SOCIAL FEST, KALA DARSHAN, PAF, DHAROHAR, ANNPROD, STYLE UP, etc. all are conducted in there fix time mostly MOOD INDIGO and TECHFEST happen in December, others in march or Feb. rest happen in march. 
The classroom has all the facilities from benches to projector including ACs.
Coming to sports activities you'll have to choose between NS0 (sports), NCC, and NSS (social work) first to do one of them and it is compulsory in NSO you can choose your sports and can go forward and do whatever you want in that sports, other than that you'll get a coach in all game with Inter IIT competition and other inter hostel GCs.
there are numerous social groups, and clubs like Abhyuday, MoodIndigi, Techfest, E-Cell, cultural club and departmental club.You may feel that the FEE amount is higher than expected but you don't have to worry about it. You'll get assistant from your mentor and the orientation related to fees, where you'll get to know about the available loan facility through SBI & CANARA bank under VIDYALAKSHMI SCHEME, where you'll get a loan at lower interest rate, and you'll also get to know about the best part of it, i.e. Scholarships like NAMED SCHOLARSHIP, MERIT CUM MEAN SCHOLARSHIP, FEE REMISSION. most people got this scholarship if they have financial issues and have an annual income of less than 5 lac. Also, you can apply for a TA job after 1st year for 1st-year courses if will secure good marks in that subject. and you'll be paid for that, other than that you can participate in different competitions where you can win cash prizes up to 5 lac.

You are in dtu, so your campus life will be excellent.
Cultural Council and LFC are the main cultural councils of dtu. And a lot of tech society's like IEEE, siam, Innova, e-cell etc present in college. 
Engifest, yuvaan, homecoming, aarambh, pehli mulakaat are some cultural fest of dtu. 
Invictus is the annual tech fest of dtu. 
Aahavan is the annual sports feat. 
So you can imagine your college life is going to be awesome.Yes, companies come to dtu for internships. 
But very less no. Of students get internship opportunities at the campus. If you are a tech student and you have mentioned well cgpa then you can get an internship at the campus. 
Sprinkler gives max 2,00,000 stipend for an internship. And the average stipend was approximately 55,000.

You can assume that every course fees are same
Of Rs 2.20 lakh for the first year.
Yes, University provides scholarships for students who had a family income less than from benchmark mark of the rupee just they have to show valid documents that show their eligibility to grant scholarships.
On campus, you could have lots of opportunities to grab internships and also gets a chance to work with startupsYes, you will experience a very good life on the campus as talking about social life you will interact with International students and also the student from the master's background and from the design background there are lots of students to talk to elaborate your skills. 
 one of the most famous  annual fests is engifest and recently we had a literature and film fest we called it YUVAAN23

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Fees per semester are nearly 1.2 lakhs for the general category whose family income is more than 5 lakhs. And for general category students with a family income between 1 to 5 lakhs and all OBC students get a remission of 2/3 of tuition fees which are 1 lakh per semester. And whose family income is less than 1 lakh will get full remission of tuition fees and a 100 per cent tuition fee waiver for sc /st / pwd students. 
And there are lots of scholarship here for students.Life on campus is fun but academics here at IITK are more difficult than at other IITs. Fests time is too much enjoyable there is a total of 3 fests but something keeps going on campus for a year. Lecture Hall has AC and a lot of places on campus to study. Sports and other facilities are great and there are too many clubs, societies, and teams run by the student body to discover and live their passion.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Annual fest - Antaragni in October, Tech fest - techkriti in March. 
Almost all curriculum books are available in the library.
All classrooms have AC and projectors
One of the best sports infrastructures in our college. Almost all sports are played

Student Gymkhana website of iitk and all other club websitesThere is an organisation called the student's placement office - which manages all placements and internships given to students. Amazon, Mahindra, Microsoft and many more companies recruit for internships
I haven't sit for an internship or placement yet so can't say much about it

The annual tech fest of KIIT is known as the KIIT FEST and is very popular. Every year big artists from all around the country visit our campus to perform. There are also many tech workshops held and extracurricular activities and cultural night programs during the fest. KIIT FEST is generally held from December to February. The KIIT Central Library has world-renowned collection of journals and books. It has more than 100000+ books and 40000+ journals. The classrooms are modern and equipped with all the required amenities to serve the students. Classrooms have screens, projectors, air conditioners, and whiteboards. KIIT is well known for its sports facilities and many renowned superstars are alumni of KIIT. KIIT spends a lot to improve the sports infrastructure. KIIT MUN is a famous club here at KIIT and very well-known all across the globe. Other clubs include Kaliope (Anchoring), Kreative Eye (Photography), and KFS (Film Society).The fees here at KIIT are divided into different parts. The institutional fee is 175000 per semester and the hostel fees vary from 28000 to 60000 per semester depending on the type of hostel opted. The mess fee is fixed at 22500 and there is some other cost like exam registration etc. KIIT also takes one-time money of 75000 for providing a laptop and blazer. The total cost to study at KIIT varies from 15 to 20 lakhs. There are many bank loans that can be availed to study here at KIIT and there are many scholarship awards. KIIT provides scholarships to the top 1000 rank holders in KIIT. They give 10000 rupees per month to the students. There are also stipends for securing top rank every year. I secured 3rd rank in the 3rd semester and received 5000 rupees per month for the next 6 months.

The faculty is really educated and professional. Though I would say they were a little cruel to us and didn't have much time for us. We were given a lot of exams projects and assignments and ppts. A lot for 1 sem but I guess that is what btech is.LPU is known for fests more than studies lol so u are going to have a great time here. There will be lots of fests with celebs coming. The infrastructure is great. Every facility is available like the library, courts, clubs, etc

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text:
I chose this course due to my rank and my interest in chemistry, If you want to research then this is the perfect course. The faculties are great, however, the curriculum needs a few modifications and better lab equipment is there.
the male-female ratio in my year was 27:4.
there was one mid sem and one end sem and the passing marks in total is 50.
in 100 marks 30 was for mid sem, 20 for attendance and assignments and class tests rest 50 end sem.From the 7th semester we are eligible for placements the highest package was around 40 lakhs( Amazon) and the lowest is the mass placements like cognizant and Wipro.
in my year around 80% of placements occurred with students getting multiple offers.
I am doing a job after placement.

The social life at Jadavpur university is the best part of the University.
There's almost at least one fest every month. 
Like U-turn, IBTIDA, Sanskriti which is the biggest cultural festival of JU which last for 9 days for the FET( Faculty of Engineering and technology)
Books and journals are abundant with lots of copies both in hard and soft copy.
There are also a lot of sporting events and clubs that are interesting as they're very active and vigorous.The placements in JU are very good. It happens throughout the whole year and you can sit for it if you have the required skills or experience. And if you didn't get placed on-campus you can always opt for off-campus and prepare yourself for further education which is also a very good option.
Students can sit from the 6th semester for the pre-placements. And the percentage of students getting placed is like 95/100.

The course curriculum is very nice and good there are two mid-semester exams of 15 marks conducted in two months before the end semester exam of 50 marks conducted after the exams and every class has 70 students which are goodIt has the best cricket pitch and grounds which are well-maintained and clean. It has many clubs which organize many functions in college and events also and the library is very big and contains many books which are useful

The course curriculum is very nice and good there are two mid-semester exams of 15 marks conducted in two months before the end semester exam of 50 marks conducted after the exams and every class has 70 students which are goodIt has the best cricket pitch and grounds which are well-maintained and clean. It has many clubs which organize many functions in college and events also and the library is very big and contains many books which are useful

Life at college is extremely good. The infrastructure is something I really like complimenting the greenery of the grass and various flowers. There are good lecture halls, libraries, canteens, hostels etc. The annual cultural and tech fest is held yearly in the month of march and is known by the name of MOKSHA Innovision. The library is well-equipped with books and journals that are very helpful to everyone. The classrooms are well equipped with Ac, smart teaching techniques, charging ports etc. The sports culture is also well promoted in our college, Agasty is the annual sports fest conducted and Winter Sports in which all the year students compete with each other. There are a lot of societies ranging from dramatics, debating, singing, tech, consulting, finance, photography, arts and a lot more to give students a detailed experience of a fun college life.The reason why I chose this course is that I preferred college over the stream. Since then has studied and completed 3 semesters in a hard deadline of 1 year, and given back-to-back exams because again we were/are the 'COVID batch'. Nevertheless, it has been a rewarding journey too. The faculty here as any other college would have is mixed. There are some extremely good professors who really persuade and like to help the kids in any way possible. Their qualifications are also commendable. The kind of support these teachers provide is really to be thankful for. Reviewing teaching methods is also seamless as we are supposed to fill out teaching feedback forms which help us to keep a check. Exams are held timely as per the length of a semester and the difficulty always varies.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The placements here are excellent, better than most IITs. Placements are held at the end of the 7th semester. Hundreds of companies visit the campus.
Microsoft, Google, Goldman Sachs, Sprinkler, Uber, Amazon, Oracle, Cisco, Flipkart, Samsung, Salesforce, and Adobe. A lot of software and banking MNCs hire from IITBHU, also companies hire in core subjects such as Electronics and Mechanical Mining too.
More than 300+ PPO offers were given, the Highest package -1,20,00,000
The average CTC of CSE is easily above 45 lakhs.

Similarly for lower branches. most companies are open to all branches. this any student can easily get a package above 20 even in lower branches.
All students are placed every year unless they want to go for higher studies.Kashi Yatra and Technex are IIT BHUs cultural and tech fest, conducted in January and march respectively. One can watch the official fest clips from the youtube channel. All sports are available here and we have a sports fest too called spardha and an Inter IIT sports competition every year.
There are a huge number of clubs from games, art, design music, journalism, and robotics to name a few.

- I choose this course because of I want to do engineering
- All faculty members are good they always support student 
Very good combination between teacher and student
- the qualifications of faculties all have a PhD degree
- Exam difficult or easy it depends on the student- The fees structure is very high but they provide all facilities according to they took fees it's ok
- Yes  they also provide scholarships for both girls and boys
- the also provides internships programs
University is also very good

The annual cultural fest is named Blitzchlag
Which is generally organised in the month of Feb.
The annual tech fest is named by Sphinx
Which is organised in the month of March.
There is a sports ground gym basketball court, badminton court, volleyball court, tennis court etc.
Social groups in this college are CACS, MC??, THINK INDIA, BLISS, F&P, UBA etc.Since this is a government engineering college so its fee is stable at approx 80000/- per semester for college.
Different state candidates can apply for their state government scholarship.
As well as those candidates whose family income is below 5 lac per annum they will get fee remission.

I choose this course because it has a better scope and there is a good ratio of teachers and students. 
Classes are also sufficient for all batches of all professional courses like MTech and B.Tech and Ph.D. and BDes etc. in one sem two exams happen. Mid sem and end-sem.I must say college fees are high energy by 10 to 12k. But my college is a government college so it's provided multiple scholarships for every category of students. It helps needy students who won't be able to pay the college fee. My college gave me the best placement.

The annual cultural fest of our college is the MOKSHA-INNOVISION which is held somewhere between march and April. Last year KK performed in our cultural fest and it was successfully completed with the footfall of 1lakh 50 thousand students. The tech fest of our college is RESONANZ and it is held in October every year. The course books are available in the library and you can take them whenever you want to. The classrooms are really big and spacious they're air conditioned and the washrooms are also really clean and hygienic. The college provides you with tons of opportunities and extracurricular activities to improve your overall development. There are many societies and clubs run by the students including the E-cell, enacts, venatus etc.The course curriculum is really good and they teach everything from the basic level. The student-to-faculty ratio is somewhere about 1:80 for my batch which is decent when compared to other colleges. Some of the faculty members are really qualified and have done their PHD'S from IIT while some of them just take classes for the sake of it. There are 4 exams in each semester, 2 class tests, mid-semester exams and end-semester exams. The difficulty level of the paper depends on the preparation of the student, some students score really well while some of them fail.

The course curriculum is current and the syllabus is continuously revised and made relevant according to the industrial skills needed. Some of the faculties are the best you can get and they motivate us to pursue and be deeply engrossed in the subject of their expertise. All faculties are PHD holders from reputed institutions. The exams are of moderate level and one could pass the exams with regular and sincere studies.The club's cultural and technical fest is spectacular and fun to be a part of. The opportunities one gets in pursuing extra activities and club work add to one's experience. The library is fully stocked with all the books. Classrooms are standard with a smart board and a projector. All kinds of sports facilities are available inside the campus. There are over 200 clubs and chapters run by students in VIT.

 And had good classrooms and there are regular sports and extracurricular activities. Had a great experience with thisI choose the course because I have a deep interest in it. 
There are good faculty members and the student's ratio is also very high teaching methods of the faculty members are very good and the setup of the exam is very good

I chose this course because of first thing interest in coding and second the coding culture of nsut and for last reason, the placements in CSE are mind-blowing, faculty is nice but as a government college some faculties are just doing their work and doesn't care about their teaching styles student ratio is fine. as a first-year student I have experienced they have well-planned exam schedule or class test schedule and practicals schedule and it's not hard to pass in them but you have to study consistently to score good GPAcollege life is awesome the campus is gorgeous itself greenery and natural beauty, fests are conducted in march month such as the annual fest moksha is going to be in march and our tech fest  was in February books are available in library classrooms are fully ac for the first years and annual sports day are also conducted in our college

Tech Fest name is TECHFEST conducted in 2nd week of Febraury. 3 days 
Annual fest- North India biggest fest -> ENGIFEST in February.
Ample amount of books are available in library. WIFI and big AC libraray.
Some classrooms are small and some lecture halls are big. Seats are okayish. Good lighting and ventilation but structure is old.
Versatile sport activities badminton , Table tennis , cricket , lawn tennis , basketball, athletics, gymkhana .
Various clubs for each activity. Debating, MUN, Drawing, Dancing, Finance, Business. Coding, Mechanics, Maths, Cultural Council.
I took DTU computer engineering because I had a prior experience in programming so I was looking for govt. college offering CS. DTU was the best option at my JEE rank(8064). Student ratio is neither great not bad 1:50 approximately. Teachers are highly quailified holidng Master and PHD degrees. They have good research experience. Term end sem exams were completed very fast in 1 week. so it was difficult to prepare as a day scholar. The exam level and moderate and mostly from pyq.

There are three fests held in dtu firstly 'YUVAAN' is the literature and the film festival fest, second there is a tech fest named 'INVICTUS'
and at the end, the cultural fest is named 'ENGIFEST'. There are almost all kinds of books in the library related to every branch.
there is a big sports complex in which you have every kind of sport like basketball, volleyball, lawn tennis, cricket, badminton etc.
and every year a sports fest is also organised named 'AAVAHAN'.I wanted to major in technology, but my rank wasn't high enough to qualify for cs, it or ece. Fortunately, engineering physics is a tech-related branch that you may choose from since you can acquire it with a low rank. There are 75 students in each batch. End-term exams are not too challenging, you can pass them by studying the night before or the night before the exam. Most of the students begin preparing a week before the exam.

The fees structure is sorted in every 6 months to pay the fees of your semester which depends on the course you have selected. Total cost of my course till graduation is about 2 lakh excluding security fees they charge 15000 for security purposes which will they return you once the course is completed.The course curriculum is very easy and since I love studying history the course seemed very easy for me and the faculty members are all very nice. They teach us well and also have nice behaviour towards the students.

As an effect of the corona lockdown, our first semester is of only 3.5 months every sem we have at least 2 ct, 1 mid sem,1 end sem, an internal practical exam, and a final practical exam. Teacher's are mostly teaching with pdf which is already shared in Google classroom, so no one shows interest in the class. Some teachers are cool, some are also very dedicated for study.We have one of the biggest fests in our college MOKSHA I can't brief much about it as I am fresher but as per our seniors it's totally worth it. People of college are very friendly and helpful but we have very pressure to study, assignments because the teachers are so strict.

When you first came to college you have to get this in your head that teacher to gonna care for you ,they not gonna care you understand the concept our not ,they gonna give you assignment and you have to complete and if you don't they cut your marks in cws (marks which are in hand teacher ) you have study hard not, it not fairy tail and rainbow ,senior say it easy ,don't take tension but in reality It gonna hard and eventually goes with a flow so you ,and most importantly don't cheat in exam using phone,chits if you got caught then punishment is to complete your 4 year degree in 5 year and we don't want do we Well fest is ongoing like month after month there always something going on . Dont even feel like you are studying engineering it's like you are in some delhi university college whose fest are always going on , for sports opportunity there is if you are good than you can get select in any sport like volleyball, 
badminton, cricket etc you gonna enjoy it but make sure you study too .

college_name: IIT Goa - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: The annual fest is cultural and technical fest is Cepheus. The library at IIT Goa is well-stocked with books, journals, and other resources. The library has a collection of over 10,000 books and subscribes to several online databases, providing access to thousands of research papers and journals. The classrooms at IIT Goa are well-equipped with modern amenities such as projectors, whiteboards, and comfortable seating arrangements. The institute also provides high-speed internet connectivity throughout the campus, enabling students to access online resources from anywhere on the campus. The institute encourages students to participate in sports and extracurricular activities. The campus has facilities for various sports such as cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, and table tennis. There are also several clubs on campus, including music, dance, drama, literary, and photography clubs, providing students with opportunities to pursue their interests. there are also social clubs on campusIIT Goa offers campus placements to its students. Students become eligible for campus placements in their final year of study, i.e., in the 7th or 8th semester. Several reputed companies visit IIT Goa for campus placements, including Amazon, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, IBM, Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro, Adani Power, etc.  Higher placements offer is of 1.2cr and average placement offers are16-25 lac per annum. The percentage of students getting placed during campus placements at IIT Goa is quite high, with around 90% of students getting placed every year. The remaining students generally opt for higher studies or pursue entrepreneurship. In case no campus placement is offered, students can explore other options for employability, such as applying for jobs through job portals, professional networking sites, or by directly contacting companies. They can also consider pursuing higher education or research in their field of interest or starting their own business.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
The course curriculum is tough compared to other colleges.
I had no other option left, So I chose this course.
Almost all the faculty are Ph.D. qualified. 
I don't know the exact student-to-teacher ratio but they teach approximately 30-60 students.
Exams are held every month and core subjects are really tough.The only good thing about this college is placements and besides that college life is basically zero.
The highest package is around 44lpa and the average package is around 10lpa.
99% of the students get placed in the CS branch and 95% get placed in ECE.

My course is computer science and Engineering. I chose this course because it will provide me very vast scope and opportunities. The faculties are good they explain things very easily. Student ratio: 65-70 students per class. Teachers are well qualified here and their teaching methods are good not but yes most of them teach well. Exams will be there every 2 and a half weeks. Examinations are not too tough but yes you should have to learn the chapters wisely so that you can get good marks.As my senior fellows said after 3rd-year companies use to come for placements or internships. Companies like Wipro, Infosys, Microsoft, Capgemini, etc came last year and they give good offers for students. The highest package of the last batch was 64 LPA and the average package is around 6lpa most of the profiles were full stack and product based. Around 81% of students got placed last year.

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text: I choose this course because I was interested in that field and also keen to learn new things. The ratio of faculty and students was 4:1 which is quiet good. The professors were quite well qualified and had good knowledge plus teaching methods. There were 2 mid-semester and 1 end-semester exam. Exams were moderate to difficult.Companies visit the campus in the 6th semester of B.Tech. and Many companies visited the campus for around 4 months like Google, Microsoft, Zomato, amazon, Capgemini, Unacademy, etc. The highest package was 55 Lakhs and the average package is 12 Lakhs. Around 92% of students got placed.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: This course intrigued me like no other. The faculty was very good according to the collegedunia website. The faculty members have Ph.D. in their respective subjects from the best institutions and teach in a manner that is very easy to understand. Term exams are every 4 months and passing is not very difficult.The campus is very beautiful and there is greenery everywhere. The social life too is very good. There are various clubs you can participate in. The annual cultural fest is impressions for 62 and Converge for 128. The classrooms are well equipped.

College life is very chill you can find people with a different attitudes towards college life some of them are very chill and some are studious too. DTU organizes the engifest which is the largest fest in north India. in that time it feels like you are in heaven no studies only fun for a week. the classrooms a spacious and good and air-conditioned. the sports complex is very big around 15 acres and has all types of spots you can imagine except swimming. there are various student clubs also like an inferno, Sahitya, cultural council, yuvaan, etc.the placements are the key highlighting factor for students to take admissions in DTU. the students get the opportunity of placement at the end of the 6th semester. the various tech giants visit our college and offer huge money like 2.1 cr was the highest this year. the average package of the college is about 14 lpa it varies through branches as tech branches have around 25lpa. there are various offers made like around 2000 were made this year. the percentage of getting placement is around 80%.

We have the biggest fest of north India called ENGIFEST and Yuvaan and Invictus and many more 
College life at DTU is one of the best in India, We have a chill environment at DTU and We have a Library and computer centre available to all dtu studentsTotal fees for 4 years btech : 930000
We have two kinds of scholarships one from DTU and the other from the Delhi government, 
Your annual income should be less than 4.5 lac for dtu scholarship and less than 6 lacs for delhi scholarship

From the 6th semester onwards, students can sit in placements of 400+ companies visiting our campus. To name Top IT giants like Google, Microsoft, and Apple visit the campus and core companies like Mahendra, NXP semiconductor, and Honda visit our campus. There are ample opportunities for students to get placed on campus. 
The highest package was 1.22 crore per annum as of 2022 stats which was given for the software engineer role..while the average being 15 lpa 
For the year 2022, 98.2 % of students got placed on campus . After getting the degree, I wish to work as a software engineer in some Top IT giantI chose EE at DTU due to the exposure and ample opportunities it provides. It is an IIT equivalent college and the DTU tag speaks for itself. Faculty: Student Ratio is brilliant:- We have 5 subjects in our 1 sem and for our 75 size batch..we have 2 teachers for each subject (Theory and tutorials teachers)
The faculty is highly qualified. Our Professors have taught in IITs and some of them have also taught at prestigious Foreign universities.
We have two exams each semester; Mid seems and End sem. Passing the exam is easy..but in order to score well one needs to grind hard.

The annual fest is engi fest is one the greatest fest that I saw tech fests like e summit and cultural fest are held by yuvaan and every month there is some event held.
Sports faculties are not that much good 
All the societies are run by the students like e-cell for entrepreneurshipCompanies mostly are tech-based so the role offered there is mostly based on coding and non-tech the companies majorly focus on getting industrial experience so that depends on company to company and project like.
 Problem-solving of a startup are generally in trend

I have seen an interest in electronics and communication thus I opted for this course.
The student-faculty ratio is good.
The faculties are highly qualified and experienced. They are masters in their field.
The whole year academy exams are divided into segments and they are done regularly.
If a student studies regularly during the semester it is not difficult at all to pass the exam.In the last year of study, the student gets placed.
Big companies like Microsoft, TCS, Infosys texas instruments etc come for on-campus placement.
The Lowest package was 6 lakhs and the highest package was of 1 CPA.
100% of students get placed in companies.

Computer Engineering is the best course to choose because of the boom in the IT sector all over the globe. The Netaji Subhas University of Technology has an ideal faculty-to-student ratio. The faculty is well-qualified and the teaching style is a great fusion of the traditional way of teaching and the new digital way of teaching. Exams in NSUT are held timely twice a year (mid-sem and endsem) and are not at all difficult to pass due to the diversification of marks between practicals, internals, mid sem exam and end sem exams.Moksha is the largest cultural annual fest at NSUT which is usually conducted in the month of March. Innovision is the annual inter-college, techno-managerial fest of NSUT.
NSUT has a huge library block. All Books required for studies are present in the library.
Classes for the first year are held at the apj complex. Apj complex is fully modern and air-conditioned.
NSUT has a huge sports complex with facilities for all sports such as cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, tennis, table tennis, basketball, gym etc.

I choose this course because here I got an opportunity for building up my career. Also, it is the best course and most interesting course for me. 
2:4. isTeachers: Student ratio and faculty are awesome. They treat well and are well-educated also. 
If a student is active then it's easy to pass.Engifest is the best fest in North India and tech fest is also a big fest. Books are available all the time and there is no shortage of books. Amenities are also in classrooms. Sports are well enough and huge area. 
Social group arena runs for students.

I chose my course because of my interest as well as the money, of course. But the course is not that relevant to what the market expects from us, but this is the case with all engineering colleges, even the best IITs, so won't say you should come expecting that.
The faculty-Student ratio is decent, but again not what the global standards are. But, as per an Indian engineering college, I would say it is decent. All I can say is if you really want to study you can easily get access to the teachers, because in any Indian Engineering college are really interested in learning much from the faculty, instead they learn from their friends and seniors. But if you are that rare one who wants to learn from the teachers you can easily do that.
Faculty are qualified, but it depends on your luck if you get a teacher who is chill & good teaching methods.
Our whole year is divided into 2 semesters, with Mid Term & End Term Exams in both exams.
You can score well even if you start the last week.DTU is known for its Campus Life. It has a big campus of about 170 acres, with great infrastructure & greenery. It is located in Delhi which makes it even better as Delhi is a city itself and its metropolitan culture is an experience to have, which you can't get anywhere else.
Its Cultural Fest "Engifest" is one of the Largest fest in India and probably the largest in North India, which happens in Feb every year. It is a 3-Day extravaganza and its night concerts are something which will make you addicted to concerts, thereafter. It is a great place to meet new and different people as many students from different colleges attend "Engifest".
Classes and the Library are good. New Classrooms are being built for CSE and IT students as well. 
Overall, College is a great place to be at. Culture is great. You can make new friends, and join clubs of almost any kind (even though some socities are better than others). Almost all people are friendly and they can help you if you need some.

I chose this course because i was always interested in computer science and it's been my strongest subject

A batch contains 30 students and there are enough faculty members that a student can get individual attention in classes

Almost every faculty member is PHD degree holder and their teaching method is very impressive they interact with students take every doubts however 1-2 teachers don't teach that good but despite that all teachers are really friendly they even connect with students personally we can even study individually with them

There are mid sem exams and end sem exams, mid sems are held after half of semester is done and end sem are held at end of the semester but throughout the semester we do get quizs and assignments which adds up in internals Uphoria our annual cultural fest is beautifully curated and creatively organised with student driver actives like stand up, project expo, movies,folk damcey, etc

Zenevia our annual tech fest where students from different colleges gets invited to explore various fields in tech domain

These are held in mid October
We have all the books and journals available in our library

We got almost every sports and you can even get in the college team in the tryouts

The course was chosen by my family members but I know that this branch has too much scope in the future.
Teacher: student ratio is pretty good in our branch.
The faculties are well qualified for their roles.
The exams are decent if u study well.The placements are good in our college.
We are eligible for placements from the 7th sem.
Top companies like Google and Amazon have visited our college.
The highest salary package I've heard is 35lpa.
90% of the students get placed every year.

Talking about the fest, we have one of the most famous fests of delhi-ncr, it's Engifest. There is no hustle in getting sponsors. It was backboned by our society, the Cultural council. It is responsible for every fest and program held at DTU. And it does its job quite well. For books, DTU has a big library with estimated 300k books and a lot more in e-book form. We are allowed to issue a set of 6 books for the whole semester. And we can issue some books for just a week other than those 6. Of course, being late invites penalties. Our classrooms are equipped with a whiteboard, markers brought by teachers and a duster is present all the time. We also have a projector in our class. It does not work in a few rooms. For sports, DTU has given a large piece of land to sports grounds. We have all types of sports grounds except for the swimming pool. I'm not a lot into sports so I cant tell you a lot about them. We hve website resources. the tech that has everything we need for our syllabus.I had an intrest in microcontrollers and semiconductors like raspberry pi. Also it is the second best branch after computer science. Also for a fact, we can also opt for coding jobs within campus. For our batch we have a faculty for each subject and faculties in each workshop and practical rooms. Most of the faculty hold P.Hd. degree and some have ongoing P.Hd. after their M.Tech. The exams happend preety soon than expected as we were short on time to complete first year, all credits to covid-19. I would rate the exams above average. They were not too difficult to cract but also not too easy to score high.

I chose this course because I have immense intrest for aircrafts.
The faculty-to-student ratio is a bit above average which means there are a decent amount of teachers.
Most of the teachers have a basic degree and some teachers also have a master's or Ph.D. in their respective subjects.
Exams are pretty frequent with one internal exam every month. The exams are not easy at all from my point of view which I blame slightly on the teaching methods of certain teachers.The fee is a bit too high according to me. I do not know about the fee hike policy but the cost to study is fairly high.
It has good scholarships through sports and other means, but the financial assistance in my experience is very bad.
I'm yet to get a job through the campus.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: I was good at drawing and had seen my uncles who are architects as well working and earning well. I used to have a passion for making models since childhood and this profession connected me to this interest which was also an escape from engineering if you are a non. med student at school.Spandan is a major fest where the whole college comes together as a student family where we make wall art, make installations, and prepare for music, and concerts, we have theatre societies as well. overall the event isn't big but yes creates great community bonding with seniors.

I chose btech cse as I am fond of coding and have some great ideas, the class ratio is 70:5 there are men boys and fewer girls the professors here are top-notch and very experienced there are 2 exams in a sem, one mid sem and another end sem and like this, we have 8 sem,2 sem a year for 4 years of college.this college is famous for its fests and all, it happens in Jan and Feb. there is a big library full of books and lots of amenities for the classes there is a huge sports complex which has everything one needs, the societies are also very famous here.

Almost every company visit dtu, and they offered various roles like apple offered both tech job and core jobs, google come for software engineer, Microsoft also come for both profile tech and core and also core companies come, and various roles offered by companies is software engineer, full stack developer, data analyst etc.. the stipend offered by the companies is very good like Adobe offered 1lakh in hand stipend, sprinkr offered 1.5 lahks in hand also the stipend is fluctuating between 80k to 1.5 lakh.The lush green campus is absolutely worth it, various fest is conducted by the dtu, the biggest fest is engifest(which is north India's largest fest) Then (Invictus) tech-fest also comes, and there is also yuvaan fest which is a film and festival fest.. sports are also Good here there is a big sports complex and all the games are here, our college seniors run a website that is a resource. tech which helps students to get all the study material, pyq, notes etc.

To be very honest as I'm in 1st-year 1st sem. our faculties members are not good as I supposed that they all are in a very hurry. They want to finish the syllabus as soon as possible may be the reason behind this is bcoz of COVID. The college wants its curriculum on its track But yeah some teachers are good in DTU faculty: the student ratio is very poor on average it is 1:37..which is very poor in comparison to other engineering colleges exams are conducted on time but this year they are very strict end sem and mid sem ends in just 5 days. It  becomes very hecticIn this field, DTU is very good. They have very good infrastructure and are spread in all around 190 acres area. They have a very big playground area. they have lots of clubs. I'm in like they have a club for every field. for example, inferno for making rovers and all, and UAS for drones which is very popular. Invictus for cultural activities and many more. Fest in DTU is zamm big. This year around 3 lakh feet fell on the concert ground. Fest is good in my opinion

I choose this course as I want to pursue my engineering in mechanical only and also my percentile was not very much high and only left with these choices. faculty to student ratio is 1:73 but there are many PhD scholars who help us in improving our subject. also don't expect very much from the faculty as it is a government college. exams are easily cracked and most of the questions come from the teacher notes only and only 2 exams are held in one semester.
I must say exams are not very difficult to pass and even if you study 1 week before or in some subject one night before you can achieve good gradesLife on the campus is very chill .your social life will depend upon you and which kind of friends you make. also on campus, you can talk to anyone .there is no ragging on campus.
There are many extracurricular activities held on campus also sports events are also organised and fests are just awesome. There are many societies in which you can take part which improves your all over behaviour.

I chose B.tech in Mechanical engineering because from starting I have interest in Robotics and I want to pursue higher studies in Mechatronics engineering. The faculty-student ratio is approximately 40:1 and it is much better than most colleges in India. Faculty are highly qualified all of them are PhD holders and yes last year 16 professors from our institute were listed in the top professor list by Stanford University.
Term exams ended just recently, the only thing to say for good marks you have to start studying at least 15 days before it's not like you will score well studying last night.In the 4th Year starting you become eligible for campus placement. Various top companies like Microsoft, tower research, Apple, Adobe, Google, Amazon etc. The highest package last year was
1.8 crore offered and the highest domestic package was 65 lakh offered by Apple. Mostly 90 per cent of student who wants placement gets placed before the last semester. I want to pursue higher studies in Robotics Engineering abroad.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
The course curriculum is very well made and learning capabilities and met.
Learning Resource Centre is filled with course-related and research-related material.
The faculty of every department is also very helpful and do not over clad students with assignments.Campus Life is very enriching at Jaypee Institute of Information Technology.
Many different hubs are present on the campus all run by students.
To make classroom learning easier in any weather Air Conditioners are employed in every class.

The annual fest is moksha which is one of the largest fests in Delhi apart from that there are many tech fests for instance the tech week conducted by IEEE ( A tech society ) and many more such events. generally, you can find a wide variety of books and journals at the college library however the timings of the library are a little off as it does not function after 8 pm and opens after 8 am. The classroom is inside the apj complex which has all the facilities from air conditioning to washrooms, and water filters, the classes have cameras Wi-Fi charging ports etc. There are many sports events that allow you to showcase your skills an example of the same is the annual winter sports which include Futsal, cricket, basketball, kabaddi, table tennis etc. In the college itself, you can find many societies which can interest you from photography to standup the societies almost include all the interests a person can have.The branch which I have is itns ( information technology with network security) the main reason why I chose this course is because of my interest in the coding world and to understand the proper working of this tech world and its wonderful workings . As of now during the first year each faculty teaches only your section(itns ) strength 70 and similarly, all other branches have strength around 100( faculty ratio. On average 1:90) the faculties are all PhDs and the hods have even are having proper research background in the 1 st semester one two exams are taken mid-sem ( generally 2/5 units) and end-sem (full syllabus). During the classes. Class tests are included. Your Jee preparation along with proper practice is enough to excel in the exams

The social and campus life is one of the best in India. There are loads of events and fests in our college. The annual cultural fest is Engifest, which is also believed to be one of the biggest North India cultural fests. The tech-fest is Invictus which includes loads of competitions, hackathons, fun events and games. Sports facilities are the very best. Huge grounds and courts for each individual sport and even a huge indoor complex are there.I chose this course over Software Engineering because I really wanted to study Maths (I'm good at it too ). Most of the faculties are PhD holders. Many are good with their subjects and teach well while some don't (exceptions are always there). There were only 2 exams Mid Sem and end sem in the whole semester.

From the 6th semester, students are eligible for placements. Companies like amazon, Flipkart, TCS, tesla, tech Mahindra, benz, bosh, and LinkedIn will come for hiring. 64 lakhs are the highest package in CSE.percentage won't matter ur talent matters in placementsStudying b tech for 4 years will cost around nearly 15 lakhs including all expenses. You can get a schloarship on basis of your class 12 marks if u have 95%above you will get 40 per cent scholarship or 30 per cents above 90% and 20 per cents above 85%

I Choose this hons course because it will give me practical knowledge of the subject not just about theories and numerical. I have learnt real-life problems in all subjects.
I don't have knowledge about the faculty: student ratio but I think it is good I don't feel a lack of faculties here.
All the facilities are highly qualified most are PhD holders and have more than 5 years of experience.
All the curriculum has been decided on starting off the year so every thing goes according to the curriculum of the University and for b tech hons it is little bit difficult exam.After 5th-semester students find mails related to campus placements.
I think college is famous for its placements and studies so if you are at GLa University you don't need to worry about your placement it will call you after the completion of your course so that you can join this company.
There are huge no of companies like TCS, Microsoft, Capgemini, etc 
The highest package is 44 lakhs and the average package is 5-7 lakh per annum in B tech computer science.

There are many fests which keep happening, like anadyanta, hackathons, flashmob and many more, most of the books are available in the library with ease, they're are many clubs, and lots of exposure to the outside world, like Ecell, flashmobs, music clubs, sports clubs, tech clubs and you can name it all. Amenities in classrooms are good too, have the basic stuff.I chose this course cause I'm really interested in it and hope to see a bright future in aviation and design, there are good enough faculties and many faculties in fact who'll be ready to help yall out anytime and very understanding.
Most of the facilities will be at least holding a PhD or master in it, exams are well planned and passing is a bit hard

The curriculum is between tech and design .it's more like computer science and design .faculty is good and the university is trying for the best. The electives are from the 6th semester and are great. From graphics to industrial all types of electives are available.Campus life is great. The campus is huge and NSUT has many events going on. There are many societies you can join . MOKSHA REZONANCE is a great fest all over north India. The sports facility is available at the hostel as well university sports Complex.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text:
The annual cultural fest is called PECFEST held in November and the now-discontinued annual technical fest was called Vyom. 
The PEC library is a 3 floor giant source of wisdom where various books on the most trivial of subjects or topics can be found easily. The digitisation of the data of books makes it way easier to find books. The classrooms are all well lit, Air conditioned to allow students to have an easy time while studying. 
The college campus has some or the other sports/extra curriculular activity going on all the time. Sports events like Atheletic meet, HPL(hostel premiere league) /FPL(football primere league) etc are held where more than 42 teams participate. Extra curriculular includes clubs like Dramatics, music, Saasc, PEB which work round the year to perform in various fests and In house events. Various meme pages, clubs, websites are run by students these days.Students get pre-placement training in the 4th sem, and the 5th sem is all about finding an intern. 6th sem is dedicated to doing the internship and 7-8th interns are dedicated to finding campus placements. 
The companies like JP Morgan and Co, mechanzie, Atlaccian, Microsoft, Maruti, and Uber visit PEC to pick PEC's brightest men and women of tomorrow. Various profiles like Software, Consultancy, data analysis, and core are open to students of various branches
Almost all the students from circuital and 80% of Non-circuital get placed. Dara science is the relatively newer branch and the exact package/stipend discussion is still underway and will only be found out after 2025.
My plan is to go on study for MTech after my degree is complete

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Course Curriculum is structured and well-defined as it is designed by experienced faculty members though it needs some improvements as it is very old school. Faculty members are very good in their field of research, almost all are phds though they might not be good at teaching. However, you can approach them anytime, they are open to all.
I chose this course as I love coding and computers!!
The faculty-student ratio is around 15:1.
Term exams were about after 1.5 months. It was a little difficult to pass the course.Life at the campus is very chill and enjoyable. You just chill with your friends, girlfriends /boyfriends too. You get a big library with a large variety of books to study. Classrooms are equipped with modern infrastructure. You get a lot of time to develop your personality. 
Talking about sports and extracurricular activities, there are a lot of competitions going on and club workshops are also ongoing. All clubs in IIT Delhi are for students and run by students for instance dramatics club, music club, etc.

The main fest is manifest and that's the best fest I have witnessed so far may artists came 3 days show day and night and what a vibe I am sure this college is will not disappoint you for fest books can be issued from lib for 6 months or 1 sem than you need to renew it by your I card all types of books are available whatever you'll need in 4-year sports I haven't witnessed anything special for it but events go on but on a smaller scale you can enjoy in dtu grounds badminton court and all the equipment are also availableFrom the begging of my life I know I have to do btech and having a craze for cars took me to choose mechanical engineering every year about 3000 students come into dtu and the girls ratio is high only in cs so don't come
With expectations, teachers are highly qualified but u have to study on your own and btech students study 1 day before mid sem 1 week before end sem exams are not that difficult

The curriculum is between tech and design .it's more like computer science and design .faculty is good and the university is trying for the best. The electives are from the 6th semester and are great. From graphics to industrial all types of electives are available.Campus life is great. The campus is huge and NSUT has many events going on. There are many societies you can join . MOKSHA REZONANCE is a great fest all over north India. The sports facility is available at the hostel as well university sports Complex.

The teachers and professors provided by the college are soo good and experienced. They are very educated and teach in a very good way. They provide us with notes, and tutorial sheets, and clear our doubts. The exams are tough but not enough, to clear. Follow the guidance of the teacher, and attend Every class, it helps very much during exams.The 4-year btech study program in dtu cost around 900000, along with other expenses. There are no fee hikes as such and the fee structure was mentioned in the prospectus at the time of admission. There are many scholarship programs provided by the college based on performance in exams and many other things.

Life on the campus is amazing ..as a hosteler, I can fully enjoy college life there r tons of time to do ..there is always something going on inside the college campus for students to participate like fest competitions interbranch matches clubs societies etc. Dtu 's main fest is engifest it is one of the largest fests in North India and it is usually held in the month of Feb followed by different tech fests and sports fest. The huge library in DTU provides every desired book classrooms are fully air-conditioned and have a beautiful infrastructure with important amenities like projector lecture stands etc .there r seniors and other students running website to help other students like resources for all the study material and pyq's. CC is the societies name that held all the fest and important programs in DTU.Dtu placement r just amazing . U can see every range of package. A lot of companies visit the campus students usually get placement from their fifth semester but u r always eligible for intership. More the 500 companies visit the campus including all major MNC's like Uber Google Microsoft Amazon etc the av package for CSE branch is about 24.5 for this year and highest offer was 1.85 crore .from this branch more than 90% students get placed and others opt for higher studies . I have planned to get a decent placement after my degree but I m also thinking of higher studies I haven't decided yet.

I think dtu has the best campus life in not only Delhi but in entire North India. In fact dtu conduct the biggest annual fest in India In the previous month it conducted three fests in which many famous artists like sunidhi chauhan, amit trivedi, nuclear, naseeb khan, rajat sood, and many others came.
Dtu has a very big library in which there are amole amount of books for all students.
In terms of sports, I love them.. Dtu has a very big sports Complex which contains separate ground and court for almost all sports like basketball, kabaddi, lawn Tennis, TT,  cricket, etc.
Almost all types of society are there like acting, music, robotics, automation, debating, etc.Because I have very much interest in computer science and its application and coding stuff.
There is an ample amount of faculty for students and we can also meet some of them after hours too.
I don't know about all but my chemistry professor is from iit jodhpur.
Terms exam comes very frequently and without any gap between them and it's moderately hard to get past them.

College life is very much good at DTU, there are, many societies in DTU that give the best opportunity and also a good environment for exploring many more things, seniors the cooperative, and the campus is always enjoyable.
with the greenery and positive vibes.from the last few years, the hike in fees can be seen throughout the B.tech students, but also college provides scholarships to the students who are needy for financial assistance also from the Delhi and state govt. financial assistance is given.

I chose this course because it gives the best opportunities. it has the best placement. the gender ratio is pretty poor. 10:1 or even worse sometimes. worst in the mechanical branch. The faculty is good but at the end of the day, it's you who have to study.The cost to study is around about 10 lac and getting a scholarship is pretty difficult. 10 thousand rupees are given to the top rankers at the end of the year. Books and hostel fees are extra expenses that one has to see.

The fees are quite high as it is a government college and if we compare the fees of this college to other government colleges you see a huge difference between them. The scholarship is given to students there are 3 scholarships there and you are only eligible to take one scholarship from a college or from the government.The course is good if you are selecting it for higher studies and research purposes and also my college gives great opportunity and priority to the EP branch for placements and internships. And faculties are good and bad both it only depends on which course and which faculty is allotted to you.

Life on campus is quite cheerful as there are many events and workshops conducted from time to time for refreshing+ there are many tech and nontech societies also Annual fest is ENGIFEST and Tech fest is INVICTUS. Books, Magazines and various kinds of journals are always available ranging on different topics in the CENTRAL LIBRARY. Our classroom is well maintained and fully air-conditioned and they have many charging ports also. There are many sports facilities also both indoor and outdoor sports. Resources is a website run by the students and for the studentsFees structure for the first year is around 2.2lac for the academic year 2022-23. Fee hike is around 5-10% for every academic year. There are many scholarships Offered to the students some are merit based while others are category based there are many private scholarships also including Reliance Foundation Scholarship and Fast retailing scholarship. There are many on campus internship and job offers which goes upto 1cr per annum (for placements) and 2lac/month( For internship at Sprinkler)

Exploring is the key at the age of 18-22 and DTU is the best place to get it. The social life in DTU is at its best with 2500 Btech students taking admission every year from all over India, bringing along different cultures. College life is just amazing with loads of fest being organized. Engifest the biggest cultural fest in North India organised in the month of February is always a memorable experience. Then every month or two Yuvaan fest and Tech fest by Innova Invictus Cell are organised. DTU has a huge library with every course book by several authors available. The classroom is well-designed with technical and digital assistance. Sports events are also organised at a large scale with every sport you could expect being competed. There are societies for music, different dance styles, dramatics, entrepreneurship etc.The course curriculum has been defined very efficiently. It enables students to explore the course along with their practicals with the best experiences. Considering all the subjects there are about 10 professors for a batch of 75 students. It has to be mentioned that the college faculty is highly qualified with PhD and Masters in their respective fields of subjects. This makes students understand the subject very clearly and practically and that's the reason why the term exams were quite a decent level but easily solvable if one has had a feel of the subject and studied efficiently enough.

DTU is famous for its college life and fests. there are many fests and events conducted here. The biggest fest here is Engifest, which is the biggest cultural fest in north India. Along with that other fests are YUVAAN  (literature and film fest)  
 ,Invictus(techfest).

there are many groups and clubs run by students such as 'fresources' run by second-year students where there are facilities for exam preparations and all college-related issues.The placements here are very good, every big company visits here for campus placements like google, apple, Microsoft, Atlassian, etc. with a very good package. last year the highest package was 1.84 crores. in computer science the average package is about 24.5 lpa like during 2021-22, the number of offers was 2459. given by a total of 403 companies .and highest domestic package was 64 lpa given by apple and the international highest package was 1.8 crores given by Palantir London.
my plans are of getting a good placement from the campus and to join the corporate world.

The course curriculum is one of the best here at my University most of the faculty are cooperative and good actually they support the students fully and provide the course material for the exams. I chose this course because I love to write code. You will get experience faculty in this college the faculty are Ph.D. holders with experience of more than 15 yrs or the term exam is about 2 months each. Seats up to a maximum of 5 per cent of sanctioned intake B. Tech course shall be available under Tuition Fee Waiver Scheme (TFW). These seats shall be supernumerary in nature. The waiver is limited to the tuition fee.
Pradyumna Bal Memorial Scholarship
Every year two poor and meritorious students are provided with completely free education under this scholarship scheme. The Scholarship covers college fees, hostel fees including mess, books and any other ancillary expenses.
KIIT Poverty Cum Merit Scholarship
Poverty-cum-Merit Scholarship for the poor and needy students. The amount and period of scholarship are decided based on the need of the selected candidates. KIIT Merit Scholarship
KIIT Merit Scholarship is provided to the UniversityToppers (1st, 2nd & 3rd ) of each semester for six months.

The social life is very good in college it does organize superb fests like Engifest, YUVAAN FEST, E SUMMIT, and many more technical and other fests, and its a lot of fun, and most of the fests are conducted in February itself.
The library is a plus point for DTU because the books suggested for us in the course are available in the library but students generally issue the books at the beginning so you need to issue them at the beginning of the sem.
Different kinds of the classrooms are there (SPS    Pragya Bhawan and Departments classrooms) but all have AC and are properly ventilated with good lighting conditions and enough space for students to sit.

This year the fees had a hike of around 10k for a year in first-year fees. 
apart from the fees I have rented a flat nearby dtu as my home is quite far and I dint get a hostel so my monthly expenses apart from studies are 15k including rent electricity water all time meals and some miscellaneous expenses which total around 1.8 lakhs a year. so my total expenses are approx. 4 lakhs per year.
it is quite high compared to many other courses and all but also low if compared to IIITD or BITS Pilani where per year fees are 4lakhs+.

It is one of the best planning schools running architecture since 1973 and has Masters's in Planning since 2012. Most of the faculties have graduated from IITs/NITs. Almost all faculties (Full time) have Ph.D. degrees. The student-teacher ratio is good. Exams are conducted as timely notified through academic calendar. An absolute system of marking is followed as the strength does not fit for relative grading. Exams are comparatively easy. More focus is on academic projects and portfolios.All the basic amenities are available. Hostels may or may not be available depending on vacancy. Clubs are available. Central as well as department library facilities are available. Access to almost all prominent databases is available. Sports, technical as well cultural clubs run on the campus.

Fests are the best part of DTU. You'll have Freshers, Engifest, Techfest, Yuvaan - literature fest, and many more. You'll witness the best concerts of your life here. The library is a bit off as you need to be fast as the books easily get out of stock. Classrooms are fully air-conditioned. Sports are damn good, a huge sports complex is right there with cricket, football, badminton, TT, kabaddi, Volleyball, basketball, and in short every sport.The faculty here is good, the teachers are enthusiastic about their field of expertise. They want to teach and so goes for the student also. Coming to the ratio it's around 50:1. Quality is topmost, the best you can expect. You'll have two exams one Mid sem and one end sem. They are easy to pass, just study exponentially, and don't pile up to the end.

The annual fest of DTU- Engifest (North India's biggest fest). 
Techfest, Esummit, and more fests are conducted all year.
Library: The library of DTU is decent enough and contains major books of the syllabus of every course going on here.
Amenities in classrooms: Classrooms are fully furnished with AC installed, whiteboard, Projector, and screen. 
Dtu contains almost every sport like cricket, football, table tennis, pool, kabaddi ground, lawn tennis, volleyball, basketball, etc.
In DTU teachers are also supportive of extracurricular activities and allow students in their period to work on that if possible.
Here societies are uncountable, you name it and dtu have a society for that, and if not then you can start yours as well for the same.Yes, of course, there is a fee hike for the last few years, and In fact, this hike got all over the world and not just in DTU.
It is difficult for one to pay fees on their own, so there is loan availability and a sbi branch is opened as well on the college campus itself.
The university offers merit scholarships for candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/Minority category and a post metric scheme for Sc candidates.
For the Btech continuous education program: The students whose family annual income is less than 4.5lakhs per annum are eligible for fee concession for students belonging to lean financial condition.
