5:1
The professors in the university were UGC approved, some were Ph.D.'s others persued Ph.D. They had good teaching skills and experience.
 Final exams would take half to one month to complete. The difficulty level of the exams was medium.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: I wanted to pursue some courses that had wide scope but that much core coding. So ECE was my good-to-go course. The faculty to students is such that 2 batches study in the same class throughout 1 and 2 years so it remains 40-50:1. Mostly they have faculty who have done MTech or PHD from IITs but not bachelor's, and some faculty are from international colleges also. The exams happen 3 times a semester. 1st after 1.5 months of start of semester, 2nd after 2.5 and 3rd after 3.5. the time after the start of the semester is the peaceful one other than its all hustle day and night. Too much academic pressure.Hardly 5-10 companies visited for internship opportunities and many or most students did not even get a chance to apply for the internship. Also, the college did not tell you that there is a specialization certificate in your own core field that you get after completing certain courses in a certain semester of your own domain.

Annual fest used to happen in the month of March. Tech fests for different departments used to take place throughout the year.
The library is one of the biggest that I have ever seen. A huge number of books with multiple copies are available in them. The accessibility is easy using the library card.
Fully A/C classrooms with projector services. Enough light and fans in the room for comfort. Blackboard/whiteboard with size bigger than usual.
Extracurricular activities were a little less due to the strictness in attendance. Sports was on high priority. There was an entire sports festival at the university for the same.
Math club, Chemistry club, AI club, Robotics club, cultural clubs, etc were all run by students with support from the faculty.Since I did my graduation in mathematical science, I wanted to pursue my higher education in the same field.
There were approximately 40 students in my course.
Each and every faculty was at least a PhD with a minimum of 5 years of experience.
The teaching method was according to all the student types considering that all might not be extraordinary.
Class tests are regular. For each subject, there was a monthly assignment or a class test. Then there were mid-semester exams and end-semester exams. Since the entire syllabus was already covered in the class, it was not much difficult to pass with good marks.

DTU has a huge campus spread over 190 acres. The infrastructure is old but top-class. The labs and library are huge. All kinds of books are available in the library.  DTU has a chill life... there are fests every month conducted by different societies. 
* YUVAAN, The annual film and literature festival 
* INVICTUS, the tech fest
* ENGIFEST, the cultural fest
They are conducted over a span of 10 days in February.  In 2023, the fest was conducted from 10th Feb to 19th Feb.  Social life is good here. There are cafes in the nearby place.  There are a lot of groups and societies here in DTU.. the UGV team, AUV team, cultural council, and so many more
There is a huge sports complex with cricket, football, basketball, lawn tennis, and volleyball fields. There is one huge indoor complex with a badminton court, table tennis and pool table Classrooms are well-ventilated and lit up, and there are projectors in every class.At my college, DTU, the annual fee for btech is approx 2.3 lakhs
1st year- 2.19 lakh 2nd year- 2.26 lakh
3rd year - 2.37 lakh 4th year - 2.46 lakh
There has been a fee hike. A few years back..the cost of the study was barely 50k per annum but now it's almost 5?? The on-campus internship season starts when you give the final exams of 2nd year(or of 4th semester). A lot of companies take interviews on the college campus and students get stipends as high as 2 lakhs per month. Most companies provide PPO. (pre-placement offer) to their high-performing interns. The student completed his btech degree and can directly work for the company at an earlier discussed package.

The placements here are very good and its the key feature of this college. If with so many students intake the avg package of this college is at par with many iits.All the companies that visit IIT DELHI come to this college and the avg package of cs branch is 25lpa+. companies like google, microsoft, amazon, Atlassian, apple, come here for placements, the highest package of year 2021-2022 was 1.8 cr and avg package of the college was 15.6 and avg package of the cs branch was 25lpa+. the placement % is also good in top branches like 95% students get placed in this college also 5% left dont get becase they go for higher studies.The college life here in DTU is the most interesting part here people here are very genuine they are very friendly. The college is very beautiful to roam around and there is no strict rules or curfew timing for boys ( for girls its 11 pm) 
 The annual fest of DTU is ENGIFEST its 3rd best fest after IIT DELHI and IIT BOMBAY.
The library here is very great and clubs and societies here are also good you get a lot of free time here. you hardly have 3 classes a day.
You can follow your passion easily here if you have any.
sports and extracurricular activities are good but the gym here is a bit small for so many students.

Campus life is awesome there are many fests or we can say DTU is full of fests in the month of Feb our annual fest manifest happens every year and our tech fest Innova is in the same week wow the vibe was awesome for a whole week. Dtu library is very good for the availability of books and journals because of the large fields of studies in DTU. The classrooms are fine. There are all the extracurricular activities and sports on campus wherever you go you'll find a person of your interest. There are many teams. Clubs that are run by students one of them is DTU super mileage which is a tech team for future cars.My interest in motorcars brings me here also DTU's mechanical branch is something else the faculties are quite good and experience the give required guidance for anything if you ask the student ratio is not good of course it is a mechanical branch so the ratio is something 1:16 something girls to boys. We have highly qualified faculty and experience they have exceptional methods for teaching. It was hard to maintain with pace of exams in the first 2 semesters but after that, it was not that hard because you will have enough time for all preparation.

Students get full time placement opportunities in fourth year. However internship opportunities come in the second and third year. Every big mnc like google,amazon, microsoft ,flipkart,apple come here for recruitment. Over 400 companies come for recruitment.
In 2022 the average placement was about 16 lpa for entire college and 25 lpa in cse branch. Record placements were seen in  2022. Number of offers were close to 2400 approx and almost all the students(about 90percent) were placed who appeared for placements in 2022. 
Highest international package was about 1.15 crore which was offered to 15 students form amazon dublin. Highest domestic package was 65 lpa given by apple. 

My plan is to get a tech( full stack devloper) job with a decent package in any of these companies.

However placement opportunities are really great, i feel that due to huge competition in dtu beacuse of large student strength, it becomes really competitive to get that dream package of above 40 lpa.College life at dtu is the best.
Our annual cultural fest is engifest. It is the biggest fest in north india. It is conducted in month of February. It is one of the best experience in our college to attend engifest . This time around sunidhi chouhan, amit trivedi and kaleshi chori guys came in our engifest and they just made the atmosphere crazy.

Library of DTU includes every book and journal which student needs. Students can go to library at any time during day and study in a peace environment. The infrastructure of the Library is very good.

The classrooms for first year students in the pragya bhawan are very good. But apart from that classrooms are not that up to the marks. They are older and don't have ac.

Sports facilities in the college are decent. There is gym , sports complex as well as big grounds for playing sports. Various inter and intra college sports competition are organised.

There are a lot of student societies in dtu for every damn interest.

college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]
review_text:
I chose Btech Mtech in IT because I am interested in a career in the computer science sector. The course curriculum is quite relaxed and to the point. In IIIT Gwalior, we don't have common subjects in the first year. They directly start with advantageous core subjects. DSA starts in the second semester itself. Faculty all have PhDs and are qualified. They teach using lecture slides and solving questions on the board. The quality of faculty and teaching method is on par with other colleges. The student ratio depends on batch, it's somewhere between 60-120.
Exams happen exactly as per the schedule and are fairly easy to pass. Studying one night before the exam is enough to pass, given that the student has attended some of the classes. There is a 75% attendance criteria that Some lectures strictly follow.Feb./march. There is a decent number of books available in the library. Classrooms are air-conditioned, and have one or two projectors. There is a sports complex with a swimming pool, 4 badminton courts, a few table tennis tables, 2 squash courts, 3 tennis courts, etc. There are many clubs run by the students such as the recreation/ journalism club(utthan), Rotaract, IEEEE student club, GDSC, AASF, etc. The only drawback is the limited student intake. This means everything here happens on a smaller scale.

All the teachers are professionals and experts in their respective subjects. Every subject teacher motivates students to ask questions and makes sure that they understood everything in the classroom. All of the teachers are good-natured, friendly people with whom you can interact freely and clear your doubts. The course curriculum is very good as we get to know about everything related to the field and gives knowledge about the industry. college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
The annual first name of the NIT Jalandhar is UTKANSH it will be organized in every year month in March it is the North East largest cultural fest and take festival name is technique it is the organized in February month, utkansh conducted by team cultures affair and tech festival organized by the team technit.
Most the books are present in the library. if any book or journal is not present then the library will provide e-books or journals,
Almost all games on campus like badminton, cricket, basketball, and table tennis sports fest also organized in January month every year.
Many clubs are also there like dance club criminal crew si crew Bhangra club natak club bawre and kalakar ladc club raj bhasha club robotics club prayers, and more than 50 + clubs are there.The fees structure is defined by the economical condition family of the student if the student's family income belongs to less than 1 lakh then the student should be paid 25500 per semester and if the student's family income is.
 belong to the greater than 1 lakh and less than 5 lahks then the student will be 46340 rupees per semester and if the student's family income will be greater than 5 lahks then should be paid 80000 per  semester,
 there are many scholarships available in the institute like SJE, NSP serman foundation Amazon girls scholarship, Tata scholarship, and many more, Bank also provides the lowest interest rate loan.

Fees are too much for the general category students. It is around 2,19,000 for all but other category students get a scholarship of 50% from the college side. There are various other scholarships provided by external firms and associations. For example:- Jio Foundation.I choose the information technology branch as I was really interested in artificial intelligence and courses related to computers. The faculty are really helpful to the students. Provide notes and are well qualified. First and second sem exams are a bit faster.

There are a lot of companies that offer campus placement from 3rd year onwards students are eligible to get hired. companies like Microsoft, Google, Paytm, and so many companies are interested in our college. there is an average package of 20 lac per annum and the highest is 1.2 crore per annum approximately.85% of students get placed. I want to get a job in a good MNC company.The fee structure is well-distributed for four years. it is approximately 10 lac which is not too much. there is a lot of opportunities for getting scholarship and internships in our college which has an average stipend of 50,000. and there are so many companies that hire direct from our college.

Our college has 3 fests, Pragyan which is a technical fest popular in South India, Festember a cultural fest and Nittfest a fest where competitions are conducted between branches. There is a very big central library available which has all the books and journals. Grounds for all sports are available and will be open all the time. There are around 20 clubs that are run only by students and include all the technical, cultural, etc.I have chosen this course because it is a circuital branch where all companies will be opened for circuital branches. There are a total of 125 students in the branch with 18 faculty members. All the faculty have a minimum qualification of a Ph.D. and have good knowledge of the subject. In a semester each subject has 3 major exams(including 2 minor exams and 1 end sem) and 2 assignments.

Social life is very good at this college Engifest provides all opportunities to the students conducted in February and Invictus tech fest is also conducted in February. The availability of books in the library is very good, amenities in the classroom were awesome sports complex is also very big, 400 m running track, 1 football court, one indoor auditorium, and two basketball courts, two badminton courts, and two tennis ball courts. Also, the college has various societies for participation in the robotic society debating society. Run by studentsThe total fees of the college are 2 lakhs19 thousand rupees. 1.5 lakhs is the tuition fees and the rest of the money is the University charge. For the general category, scholarship is the merit cum means scholarship, available on the e-district portal.

North India's largest fest for eg; Aarambh, Yuvaan, Engifest, and many more technical fests, is usually conducted from Dec to Feb month.
An amazing and large amount of books were issued. 
Large and big classrooms encoded with AC fans etc.
Large sports complexes apply for national international sports, Good societies.
Mainly students prefer free resource sites for their papers and assignments.219k for the first year and gradually increases to 5k for every year.
MCM scholarship and DTU Targetx scholarship for Delhi as well as the outside region.
And nsp scholarship for outside Delhi.
Amazon campus Easy to communicate and enchancs skills

Campus life is the best. DTU has one of the best cultures and societies. 
The Tech Fest (Invictus) and Cultural Fest ( Engifest) are one of the largest fests in North India. They were conducted in February. 
The library is really big and contains books for every subject and interest. 
Classrooms are nice and AC occupied, professors teach on the Whiteboard and the classes are clean too. 
There are a lot of societies run by students like Ecell DTU, Biosoc DTU, Cultural Council DTU, SCEE DTU, etc. 
There are a lot of startups and websites run by students like resources websites.The fees increase by 10 percent every year. 
The cost of study comes to around 9 lakh for me. 
You can get scholarships if you are from a reservation or your family income is less than 4.5 lakhs per annum. 
You can get student loans for cheap rates from the bank and DTU assists you with that. 
You can get internships on campus, ranging from 30,000 to 70,000. But, the competition is tough to get internships.

I am pursuing B.Tech (Computer science and engineering). For the general category, CSE closes at around 22-23k AIR in JEE Main. For me, the curriculum of this branch is slightly easier to understand and also, CSE has the highest placements with an average of about 12-14 LPA, and almost all students of CSE get placed. Overall, the average package ranges between 8-9 LPA. Faculties of every department are well qualified (almost all are Ph.D. holders) and they are experts in their fields, there are different teachers to teach different topics of the same sub. We have 2 mid-semester exams and 1 end-semester exam for each semester. Mid-semesters are held after every 3 months and the end semester is taken every 6 months. To pass the exams, you just need to focus on whatever is taught in the class, passing the exams is not a big deal but you have to maintain at least 75% attendance in the class to appear for the exams except that if you have a medical emergency, then min. 60% of attendance is required.Life at the campus is pretty decent. There is a central library on the main campus equipped with all the books that you will need for your program (there are no comics!). Classes are equipped with smart boards and fans, lecture halls are equipped with normal blackboards and air conditioners. Various sub-councils in HBTU take care of various clubs for your personality development. The sports sub-council frequently organizes various sports tournaments like cricket, badminton, volleyball, and many more in which anyone can participate after the screening. Then there is ASCPC (Alumni Student Connect Program Committee) which is dedicated to organizing different events like alumni meetings etc. in the college. The link for information regarding all the clubs is:-    https://hbtu.in/club
We have various events and fests like Aarambh, ChemCon, Adhyaay, etc. held every year, these fests are tech-oriented with some fun and cultural program.

I chose this course as I wanted to study computer engineering.
There are many faculties in the college but the biggest problem is that the student intake is very very large. 
Most of the teachers are having phd in the course they teach, and their teaching technique is good.The students are eligible to sit for on-campus placements from the third year or fourth year. 
The teachers can guide you pretty well for the placements. 
And there are many companies that come for the placements starting from good mnc to big startups.

You have a lot of opportunities here for enjoyment as well as developing your skills through clubs and events. But you have to manage academics. Since ECE is tough then you have to work hard as well as smart.
We have 2 main fests, a tech fest named TechnoZian and an annual fest as Spring Spree.
The library here is named Central Library and is enough big. All the academic books are available. Also, few fiction and novels are also available.
Coming to the classroom, for self-study, I choose the classroom. It is really a very peaceful place. I think better than a library.
The college provides a lot of sports and extracurricular activities. There is one Physical Education Department for gym and indoor games. For outdoor games, there is a separate playground for each game.
There are a lot of clubs are there in this college. You will get all types of clubs. Literature, tech, value education, music, dance, etc.Placement is pretty good in this college and since ECE is a circuital branch so I opted for this course.
Here student: The prof ratio is 50:1.
Prof is highly qualified but the teaching method is not satisfactory. In the end, we have to study on our own for getting better grades. Yaa if you are dependent on prof, u can pass the exams.
There are 2 main exams, mid-sem, and end-sem. Also 2 minor exams each of 10 marks in prof's hand. You will get assignments frequently. NITW ECE is one of the toughest branches.

I love finance that that the reason why I have chosen this course.
The faculty is very good cooperative and not irritating kind of. And also very helpful for us.
The teaching method is very good and also they are very much educated too.
Exams are not tough and easy to pass but there isn't any chance of cheating.Very loss or no fee hike policy is there and they have 3 kinds of scholarships.
1st 25 percent,
2nd 50.
3rd 100 
More than 93 percent marks will give you 100.
89 percent will give you 50.
And 73-75 will give you 25 percent for 2 starting semesters one sem is for 6 months.

Placements at our college are quite good compared to others from 7th-semester students who are eligible to sit in placements. The companies were Amazon, Goldman Sachs, BHEL, Atlassian, Accenture Tata, Adobe, Samsung, HLC, Mastercard, and Optum. and 514 offers were made.  The highest package was 44 lakhs from amazon. and the average package of the ECE branch was 14 lakh per annum.
In the ECE branch the percentage of people getting placed is 90% and in the CSE branch last year it is 100%. My plans are to get placed in a good company with a nice package and many people choose to go to higher studies or for UPSC.Technosis is the tech fest and incandescence is a cultural fest. Classrooms are well-designed and projectors are available for each classroom. And the library is Asia's second-largest library many books and journals are available.  And there's a cricket ground football ground and sports complex that have wooden badminton courts and table tennis tables. And many clubs dance clubs, music club machine learning clubs and chess clubs and cricket clubs, kabaddi and Kho-Kho club, and dramatics club like this many clubs are there.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Campus placements are very nice. After 6 semesters students sit in placements. Top multinational companies visits including Amazon, Flipkart, Jane Street, Adobe, Microsoft and Google, etc. Placement is about 90 percent in my stream.Fees structure have  tuition fees and facility charges provided in campus. Hostel accommodation and mess charges are also applied to hostlers. There are many scholarships opportunities. There is a decent campus placements.

the percentage of the students who were placed in this course is nearly 70%. 
the average package of 7 lpa is offered in the course companies like Amazon, Google, Cognizant, and Tata Consultant services. alphanumeric, knowledge academy visit for the campus drive around 50% of the students got an internship in companies like amazon, google, and Microsoft Top rules which are offered are web developer, data analyst, and other profile.The campus is very big nearly 55 buildings It very big university. So many fest and events every month. If you want to enjoy college life choose Lovely Professional University. There are so many clubs you can join and host events and also you got a certificate from the club. To help with your cv.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
Thomson and Cognizance. Cognizance is the second-largest tech fest in Asia. It's conducted in March. 
The library has most of the resources one needs.
Classrooms are nice and well maintained, especially the studios where we work, and are open at night also.
You name any sports, you'll find it here all with good equipment and a coach. 
Several clubs recruit students from 1st year and 2nd years. In the first year, the students have the golden opportunity to join clubs and explore their hidden talents.From 7th Semester.
Amazon, Google, Microsoft, etc.
2.9 Cr is the highest package and 18 LPA is the average. And for B.Arch. the highest package is 40LPA and the average is 13LPA. The companies which come are - Amazon, JP Morgan, Zomato, Mastercard, etc. 
90% placement in a year.
Will take a job for experience and start an architecture firm as I'll get a license after the completion of the degree by the Council of Architecture.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
Choosing Mechanical Engineering was out of interest since the topic of automobiles always fascinated me. I wanted to learn in-depth about their working and design, so opted for this course.
Well for my department, teacher to student ratio is pretty good, almost 1 teacher to 6-7 students easily.
All the faculty members of the Mechanical Dept are experienced and well qualified (strict also...) Their teaching methods are also conventional but some are transitioning to digital modes of teaching, which makes it all the more interactive.
The semester has 2 minor exams and one major end-semester examination, each in a gap of almost one month. Passing the exams was easy. We used to study the night before the exams and scored more than 50% easily on those exams.There are many clubs in the college. For example, we have clubs for theatrics, dance, music, debating, modeling, fine arts, and many more. We also have technical societies of each branch in the college, and also Robotics club, coding club, automobile club, etc.
Sports facilities include a football field, 2 volleyball courts, 2 basketball courts, 4 badminton courts, 2 tennis courts, table tennis tables in every hostel, and a multi-facility gym.
In a year, there is one technical fest, techNITI, and one cultural fest, UTKANSH.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Annual fest- Rendezvous with is going to be held in March this year.
Tech fest- Trust which is also going to happen in March just before the cultural fest Rendezvous
Mostly all famous books are there in Library and you can also demand books which you required in your courses from Library
In sports, every game's ground is there.
Indoor table tennis badminton tennis ground is there with Ac facility
Fine arts club, dance clubs, photography club, Hindi samiti and many clubs and societies are thereRipik technology, Uber, TATA consultancy, oracle, adobe, Qualcomm, exawizards India LLP, Sony group corporation, the world want, trilogy innovations and many more companies. You will get a stipend from 5000-200000 per month depending on company to company and type of internship

A total fee of 24 lakhs (as of 2023), 12 lakhs being the tuition fee and the rest 12 is the hostel and mess fee.
The scholarship is for merit students, and SAT Scholar and Sports scholarships are also there.
Someone coming from an underprivileged background or a financially unstable background will also get support from Shiv Nadar Foundation.SNU offers a rich campus life.
With best-in-class facilities, one can experience diversity and a great class of life on campus.
284-acre campus will have all the needed staff.
Fests and all are very enjoyable and good
You will get to learn many different things

I have chosen this course because I had dreamed to become an engineer since my childhood. The student-faculty ratio is 20:1. Every faculty is well-qualified and has a lot of knowledge. The exam happens from time to time and it is not that tough paper.34th-year students are eligible for the placemats.  The highest package which was given by the college was one crore twenty-five lacs last year and the average package is fifteen lacs per annum. Almost 100 per cent of students get placed.

There are 2 grand fests Incandesence and Tecnoesis and the other 2 regionals Posua and Oikyotan. Incandescence is conducted in February and tech noesis in October. Most books and journals are available in the library. The library at NIT Silchar is the largest digital library in Asia. The classrooms are provided desk benches and the class dimensions are gallery structured and provided with projectors for better visualization.  For sports, there is a sports complex that consists of a tennis court, badminton court, basketball court, and a gym. There are two grounds, one for football and another for cricket. There are numerous clubs in the college where you can show your talent. There is a singing club, dancing club, drama club, coding club, literary club, development club, eco club, etc. There are branch websites that are made by students for students and run by students.In the 7th semester, students become eligible for placements and can sit in placement rounds till the end of the 8th semester if they are not yet placed.
A lot of tech companies like Microsoft, amazon visit during campus placement and they recruit a lot of students. The highest placement is 44 LPA and the average package is 15 LPA . 74% of the students eligible for placement got placed. After getting the degree I would like to work at a tech company and grow along my career.

From the past few years, the placements are quite good. This year the maximum placement till now is 28LPA which is again a very good ROI as seen from tier 3 colleges. Most of the students get placed and there are mass recruiters to give placements to plenty of students.Fees are quite high and also there are scholarship opportunities for those who have secured more than 85% in class 12. They provide up to 100% scholarships to those who have secured 95% in 12th. Also, there are plenty of Campus opportunities.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text: The fee structure is available at the official college site also. Basically, 2.6 lakh fees have 2 lakh tuition fee components, you can avail of fee waiver benefits on that according to the parental income slab. There are many scholarships opportunity thereI choose Bachelor of Technology in Computer science and engineering because I have had an interest in CS before and CS placements are good in this college. Companies like Google and other tech giants used to visit campuses for placement & internship.

college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]
review_text:
Aurora: this is a cultural festival held over a period of three days in the month of February/ march. Aurora should not look upon as just a cultural event but as an attempt to promote the friendship, teamwork and alliances. 
our tech-fest name is Infotsav: our college organises events that acknowledge technological innovation and it is conducted every year. our library is the central biggest library in which over 30000+ books are available & our library boasts almost 80 journal titles both from India and abroad. Amenities in classrooms such as projector, laser light, chairs, fans, AC, black & whiteboard. we have a sports complex in which you can play all the sports such as badminton, table tennis, squash, one basketball court,  one cricket ground & one football ground. In IIITM gwalior there are various club such as Rotaract (which work for social welfare), Uthaan(recreational club), SGM(helps poor children by teaching them), E-cell  & IEEE.I have chosen this college because of my interest in CS/IT field and I want to pursue this interest. faculty: student ratio is 1: 20 due to the fewer number of branches in our college there is less intake of students and that's why our faculty: student ratio is well maintained. In IIITM Gwalior all the faculty members are phd holders & have a grip on their concepts and teach us in the best way. generally, minor exams are conducted in the span of 2 months and after that major exams are conducted after 2 months then the next semester will be started. And If you give attention towards the classes and solved reference books then scoring a good cgpa will be a piece of cake for you.

I'm interested in the mechanical course so I opted in.
The faculty are very supportive and helpful. faculty explains all the syllabi and covers them in time. faculty are well qualified in top IITs and nits also bits .completed mtech and PhDs in its. there are two-semester exams per year. if we concentrate well during lectures it's not a big deal to clear the examinationsFirstly I give my college a 10/10 rating regarding the fests. At top music concerts, musical directors were invited as guests. I enjoyed a lot in every single fest conducted by our college. I'm thankful for the management of its. Classrooms are also adjustable. Social groups, clubs, extra activities, sports, gym everything are available.

After the 6th semester, students are eligible for placement. In my department, there are several companies like maruti suzuki, hero motorcop, Reliance, valency international etc are coming for placement. Reliance gave 10 offers hero gave 8 offers Maruti gave 7 offers etc. The highest package is 22lpa in valency international. The average package is 7.5lpa.more than 80% of students got placed. My plan is to opt for placement and preparation for the gate.The annual festival is aadhay and the technical fest is technika. Aadhay was held in June and technika was held in May. There are some monthly programs also. There are more than 1lakh books in a library. so we got from there. Some classrooms have ac and good facilities. Sports facilities are mediocre. Other than cricket and football there is no good sports facility. There is a good society for dance and E- cell for startups.

college_name: IIT Bombay - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
The fee is modestly close to the? 2,00,000 per year for tuition fees, additionally you'll have to pay? 90,000 for the mess, hostel, sports, hospital and other facilities for an entire year. Hence, the total fee for a year is closer to the? 2,90,000. 
It might seem huge, but you'll not have to pay a penny outside this and you'll get all the services at IIT Bombay included in this itself. Normally free revision is done by a separate committee every year, and it is as per the policies which are quite legitimate. I did not receive any scholarships as they are only given to students from economically weaker sections and there are provisions for such scholarships. Students whose family income is less than ? 8,00,000 and whose CPI>6.0 are not required to pay tuition fees under Merit-Cum-Means Scholarship. The full fee is waived for students with less than ? 5,00,000 income. There are plenty of other named scholarships by our Alumni and believe me, funds won't be a problem if you want to study at IIT Bombay.Clubs: consulting, finance, tech, F1 racing, Mars rover, AUV, Cedrica
Asia's biggest cultural festival - Mood Indigo (Dec.), Asia's biggest technical festival - TechFest (Dec. or Jan.), Asia's biggest Entrepreneurial summit - The E-Summit (Feb.), Asia's biggest social festival - Abhyuday (Feb.)
 All kinds of books (digital and hard copy) are available at the central library of IIT Bombay. There is an online system as well to issue the books and find the exact location of those books on the shelves.
 Classrooms have cushioned seats, ACs, well lit, boards comparable to MIT in the US.  World-class Olympic standard sports facilities for every sport, basketball, swimming, football, hockey, squash, badminton, tennis, table tennis, gym, kho kho, etc.
 We have 3 big auditoriums at student dispense to be used for all kinds of events, and a separate student activity centre (SAC) where all the clubs and student bodies have dedicated rooms for an internal purpose, hence cultural exist.

review_text: In the Computer Science course's core curriculum, quite useful languages are taught. There are plenty of other named scholarships by our Alumni and believe me, funds won't be a problem if you want to study at IIT Bombay.Clubs: consulting, finance, tech, F1 racing, Mars rover, AUV, Cedrica
Asia's biggest cultural festival - Mood Indigo (Dec.), Asia's biggest technical festival - TechFest (Dec. or Jan.), Asia's biggest Entrepreneurial summit - The E-Summit (Feb.), Asia's biggest social festival - Abhyuday (Feb.)
 All kinds of books (digital and hard copy) are available at the central library of IIT Bombay. There is an online system as well to issue the books and find the exact location of those books on the shelves.
 Classrooms have cushioned seats, ACs, well lit, boards comparable to MIT in the US.  World-class Olympic standard sports facilities for every sport, basketball, swimming, football, hockey, squash, badminton, tennis, table tennis, gym, kho kho, etc.
 We have 3 big auditoriums at student dispense to be used for all kinds of events, and a separate student activity centre (SAC) where all the clubs and student bodies have dedicated rooms for an internal purpose, hence cultural exist.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: In the Computer Science course's core curriculum, quite useful languages are taught. The curriculum gives you a good start to the C language after that  C++ and OOPS and then DSA is also included in the curriculum. The web-development languages are also included and they are taught to a beginner's level. If one develops interests then he/she can dive deep into them.The average package of the college is around 13 Lakh per annum for the CSE branch. A lot of companies visit the campus for placements. However, due to the recession, the number of companies that visited the campus this year was reduced.

I choose this course because it was a good option for my rank in JEE and I don't regret it.
The faculty was awesome and they clear our doubts.
All teachers are experienced and completed their Ph.D. in recognized institutes.
We have to give time for studies and if we study with strategies it is easy to pass and get good scores in term exams. We have to spend some time for study in the hostels. Time is not sufficient in term exams we have to manage the time and it was not difficult to pass as I expected.Annual fest is springspree conducted between March and April and tech fest is Technozian conducted in December and we have a lot of fun in these fests.
All kinds of books are available in central Library.
Classrooms are designed with good infrastructure and smart classrooms .
There is lot of scope in sports & extra-curricular activities.
So many clubs are there in NIT Warangal like dance& dramatics club, Robotics club, Patriotic club, etc..

there are a lot of opportunities for internships.
from the very 1st year we can apply for interns and later the company might give ppo and boom you are placed.
From sde role to an analyst role to a designer role there are varieties of roles for which the companies come.
apart from this, there are few paid internship opportunities that the college provides itself.
internships are many and it helps us in making our resume for the final year.Placements are the reason why students prefer DTU over any other engineering college in Delhi or any other state.
There are almost 300+ companies visiting throughout the placement session.
It does not matter which branch you are in there are endless companies that are hiring students from every branch and are open to all branches.
without a doubt, I can say DTU has one of the best placement processes.

I had an interest in the electrical subject since class 10. So I wanted to do something in the same field. That's why I choose the Electrical Engineering course.
In a class, there are 50-60 students. For each subject 2-3 faculties are available. I think the ratio is quite good.
Faculties are well qualified. Many have done PhD,  some are about to complete PhD and few have completed their M.tech in their respective field.
3 exams are taken in each semester. 2 mid-seems and 1 end-sem exam.After the end of the 6th semester i.e. 3rd year in a 4-year course, students become eligible for campus placements.
Companies visited last year include Oracle, Adobe, TCS, Wipro, Mindtree, Infosys, Capgemini, cognizant, and many more. 
The highest package offered was 24lpa by Adobe for the software engineer role.
Almost all the students (who have no backlog and have 75% attendance) get the placement. The placement percentage is around 99%

I chose the course as I had an interest in the subject. Moreover, with the ongoing technological push, it felt like a safe choice. 
The student-faculty ratio would be around 1:20
The faculty are mostly PHD doctors having done their degrees from prominent IITs.
The exams strictly follow the academic calendar released at the start of the term and are doable provided you know the material.We have BREEZE, which is our annual techno-cultural fest that happens in March. 
The library is huge and well stocked with all prominent journals being available online as well. 
The classrooms are air-conditioned and the lectures are recorded. 
There are 30 active clubs on campus along with a football field, a cricket field and a well stocked Indoor Sports complex.

In 2021 we didn't get our fest due to COVID-19, but recently we had a fest in which Sunidhi Chauhan and India's Got Talent winner DVK performed. Each school also conducted its own set of events related to that course. Each department has its own library and there is also the central library with books available for all departments. Before the start of the semester, a pdf of the books is sent to us and the hard copy of the books is also given. Every classroom is equipped with AC and a monitor to display the PPTs. In the first year, every student is sent a mail regarding the university team selection process for the sports University has to offer. There are many societies formed by students like MSC(Microsoft Student community), GDSC(Google Developer Student Community), IOT lab, KIIT film society, etc.I chose the course Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) because that was the only course I had any interest in. 
The majority of the faculties here are either from West Bengal or Odisha. Almost all of them have doctorates from an IIT or NIT and are well-equipped to be teaching their own subject.
As for the ratio, there are almost 4000 students in CSE alone and 1 faculty takes 2-3 classes of 80 students each.
They have a handful of PPTs which they teach from and also share with the students.
The mid-terms happen almost 2 months after the start of the semester and end terms 2 months after the mid-terms. This goes for every university out there, if you study you will pass with flying colors.

I opted for EE that electrical engineering because somehow and somewhere was interested in this.
In my class, there are around 60 students and for each subject, according to the modules the professors are allotted. You will get one of the best professors, they are very friendly and with academics, they will guide you for different projects.After the 6th-semester final exams companies start visiting the college for placement and our college has had the highest placement package record for quite a long period of time.
There are some basic eligibility criteria for qualifying for seating for the placement.

Many fests like Deepavali, holi and many festivals are also held and farewells and feshers are also held and tech fests are held departments wise. All the books are available in the library and journals are in libraries. Amenities are fair in the classrooms. There are many sports like cricket and football and table tennis etc and many more extra curricular activities on the campus.
There are many clubs in the clubs.Because with this course I'll understand the basics 9f engineering after this I'll do btech.
All teachers are very qualified and good at teaching they all done phd and make good connections.
Exams done in mid-term and semesters wise and difficult medium of this exams.

The campus life at DTU is the best. If you do not want to study hard for 4 years, you should definitely come here. Every now and then, there is some fest organised by societies of the college. Engifest, North India's best cultural fest is held in DTU in the month of February. Tech fest too is organised in February. The library is quite well maintained and you will get access to all books and journals required. All classrooms are equipped with ACs and projectors. DTU has great sports facilities with badminton, lawn tennis, kabaddi, and basketball courts. There is Cricket and football ground too. Indoor sports are also available.Fees have to be paid at the beginning of the new session which generally starts in August. My first-year fees in 2019 were Rs. 1.7 lakhs and my fourth-year fees in 2023 became 2.14 lakhs. Students belonging to the Economically Weaker Section are provided with concessions. Scholarships are also given to merit students. I did not receive any scholarship or financial assistance from the college. The college provides a University internship, USIP, with a stipend of Rs. 3500/month. I worked in that for 6 months under the assistance of a professor. I also got a job from my college in Bain & Co. with CTC Rs. 14 lakhs.

The reason to choose this course is just optional, I want to do CA(Charted Accountant) so, I joined the course. In this college, the syllabus is more related to the CA syllabus which helps the students to study.  The faculty-student ratio is 40:1. In the B.com department there are around 35 teachers, but there are only 60% in house faculty and 40% visiting faculty. There are almost qualified teachers in the college and the visiting faculties are all professionals like CA, CS etc.
There will be 5 internal assessments and 1 exam in each semester. The duration of each semester is 5 months. The tests and exams will be not so easy to score, it will be challenging.There will be an annual fest conducted every year called 'ATHMA TRISHA ', which is one of the big college events. There will be big stage events every month and club activities every week. There are 50+ clubs on campus.
There are 6 libraries on the campus, which are both digital and manual and 4 study rooms on the campus. The classrooms are big where 100 students can sit in a class at a time. There are smart tv in every classroom.
Every sem There will be a sports fest conducted where all sports competitions will be there which is intra college competitions.

Economics is one of the premier courses of SNU, having a lot of people go to QS's top universities and business schools after graduating in econ from SNU. The faculty is extremely competent, most of them have taught at top universities around the world before coming to SNU. There is a lot of corporate exposure too as a lot of professors have worked in organizations like the world bank and IMF before joining SNU. 
However, there are very frequent examinations and assignments that kind of get overwhelming. All year-round studying is required to graduate with a good GPAThe annual cultural fest is called breeze which is the biggest event of SNU spread across 3 whole days. Also, there are about 70 clubs that also keep conducting events and fests depending upon their budgets and sponsorships. But all in all the campus is very happening. 
The sports here are amazing. There is a state-of-the-art indoor sports complex. There is also a horse race track where one can learn equestrianism.

I choose this course as BBA LLB provides me with both Business as well as Law field knowledge and if one wants to start up their own business then this is the best course. If we talk about the faculty then they are very supportive and always ready to help students in all aspects. In one class there are almost 50 to 60 students. 
All the faculties are very well qualified even advocates also teach students. 
The university conducts 2 Internal exams of 30 marks each and 2 Internal Assessments of 10 marks each in every semester. At the end of the semester University conducts 50 marks paper. These exams are not that tough if you were attentive in class then you can easily qualify.Well if one wants to enjoy college life to the fullest, then one can take admitted to this university without any doubt. 
The campus is too big, its cafeteria is just amazing. We have a saloon in the cafeteria. Every month 2-3 film stars visit the university for their movie promotion. Every year at end of March university conducts Unifest. 
There are various clubs and societies made in this college. 
This university has 4 libraries one is a central library, the other is a law library, and 2 libraries are in different blocks. 
The classrooms have ACs with projectors and whiteboards.

I choose this course because as the semiconductor industry going to boom between 2022-2030 so there will be more jobs available. faculty to student ratio is around 1:20 .all the faculty are PhD and above degree qualified. review the teaching of faculty is excellent. there will be two exams one is mid-term in which you will easily pass but in the end term, there will be some difficulty in passingThe fee structure of the college is around 40-55k per semester and there will be two semesters in a year there is some scholarship run by govt of India for weaker sections of society . yes, I have gate qualified so I am getting a stipend of 12500/month.

Placement statistics are quite good in our college, our seniors told us that the average package of cse students in our college is 10lpa. Only key to success is doing hard work. The highest package in the current year till now is 33lpa. I am planning to sit for placementsLife on campus is good, u can study anytime and anywhere. The library contains quality books to study and there are appropriate amenities in the classroom. Co-curricular activities and sports are also given importance in our college

review_text: Campus life is quite boring with limited events happening in the college. The fest is also cancelled for 2022 batch due to covid 19. Sports facilities are limited with no football ground. Classrooms are well equipped with adequate amenities. Library is well stocked. One can issue 3 books at a time from library.Faculty:Student ratio is quite good that is around 1:8. Although, college focusses more on theory and rote learning than students grasping concepts. Curriculum is tough and up to date and match industry standards. One can manage to score well by just studying a week before exams

Campus life is happening as we have a fully residential campus. There are two major fests every year - BREEZE and SURGE and a lot of events all around the year. BREEZE is conducted in the month of March after mid sem exams. 
The library is very big and has thousands of books. 
We have smart classrooms that are equipped with the latest technologies and air conditioners and heaters function in the summers and winters. 
Attention to sports is the thing that I love about SNU. It hosts a lot of sports opportunities including training. Competitions are conducted regularly.
There is a lot of cultural and technical club. I am a part of GDSC.I will become eligible for campus placements from the 5th semester onward. Tons of good companies visit our campus every year. To name a few, we have Amazon, Amex, Dell, Goldmann Sachs, HP, etc. 
The highest package this year is about 55 lpa and the average package is 12 LPA and the median is 11 LPA. 
I guess over 90% of students got placed. 
I am hoping to get a good placement after getting the degree.

Students get eligibility for campus placements from the 5th semester. Accenture, capegemini and hp companies have made offers to our college. The highest package a company offers is 7.5 lakhs and the average package is 3 lakhs and about 40 percent of students get placed in college placements. I want to do further studies after completing my graduation.The fests were very nice and it was held in collaboration with many more colleges and the internals was nice and the books were provided in the library and a card was issued for every student and sports between other colleges were very competitive and many prizes were won.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Thapar is one of the Most Prestigious Colleges in North India after IIT and NIT which gives an obvious reason to join the college.
For Lectures the Faculty: Student Ratio is 120:1 but for labs, it is 25:1.
The Faculty is highly qualified, most of them have Ph. D.s from Tier 1 Colleges in India, and the teaching is convenient just that in B. Tech. you learn more online and less in offline classes, but labs are good wrt to equipment.
Exams were not as per schedule due to COVID but now things are going back to normal.Saturnallia is the annual Techno-Cultural Fest of Thapar, including multiple games, hackathons, and concerts. Apart from it, we have societies having events around the year. Entrepreneurship Development Cell is one of the biggest among them. Thapar is known for the different opportunities provided by students to students.
Thapar also has one of the most award-winning libraries in South Asia giving the best of knowledge with the best books.

The students are eligible for campus placement after the 7th semester. There are many companies visiting the college campus like tata steel Wipro Tcs amazon byjus godrej bajaj and many more.1500 students are placed in the last three years and the highest package is 80 lakhs per year.The annual fest and cultural events are conducted after the summer season and there are many events like mismatch day, traditional day, character day and many more. There are grounds available for every sport like cricket tennis volleyball kabbadi kho kho and many more

The course of Computer science and engineering is in demand and secondly, it has a decent package that one aims for after B.Tech. Talking about faculties here, Faculties are well qualified with all teachers having expertise in their subjects and fields. The teachers attempt their best to impart knowledge to students but the teaching methods vary from faculty to faculty. The exams are conducted thrice per semester 2 mid-semester exams and one end-semester exam. The difficulty of the paper could be marked as moderate.College life is not too happening. There are 3 fests per year one technical fest called Technika, one sports fest called Aagaz, and one cultural fest called Adhyaay. Besides these, there are various sub-councils and clubs that students can be a part of. 
The library has a vast collection of books but unfortunately, the library timings are from 10 am to 5 pm. 
The LHCs are more maintained than the regular classrooms.

CS Course is in trends. Also, I have a lot of interest in it. There are more than 100+ faculty, and students in the college are more than 5000, on faculty opinion is mixed some faculty is exceptionally good some are difficult to understand. but they also have LMS online learning platform which will help you understand more, minor happens in around 35-40 days.From 5th-semester students get eligible for placements, placements are one the best reason to join this college, Top companies like Sony, AWS, Mercedes, TCS, Amagi, Twilio, Amazon and more, Highest package is 43Lacs, 30+ students got 20+ lacs package last year, almost all students get placed. I am planning to do job after getting my degree.

The annual fest's name is Amrit Mahotsav it happens in January, also many other programs happen from time to time like PUPA, and the Motorsports Association also conduct fest. Also, the library is good classrooms are good, and the sports facilities are also good there is a separate gymkhana for professional athletes also clubs like GDSC, Bits and bytes(coding clubs), GFG campus chapter, Codechef Campus chapter and KLE Motorsports, KLE Drama club are there, English club, Dance music etc are there.Students become eligible from the 6th sem for campus placements. Currently, placements are going on but companies like Amagi, Amazon, Twilio, and Juspay, have visited the campus and recruited. The highest package till now is from Twilio 43 lpa I think, the average must be around 10-11lpa. Almost all students are placed in product based.

Campus life at NSUT is very good campus is huge and the crowd of the college is also good. The annual cultural fest of NSUT name MOKSHA is very famous every year many known stars come there last year KK was there and sunburn was also amazing. Tech fests are also good anuv jain performed last year in a tech fest.The fees of the college are high but it is totally worth it the campus is very good and all the classes and labs are well-maintained fees of my course id approx rs.320000 which is lower than other universities offering the same course. Scholarships are also provided to students.

The B.com course is very interesting and there is a good scope for it after doing some professional courses like CA, CMA, CS etc or doing some extra courses like digital marketing, taxation etc. The faculty-student ratio is 1:40. There are also some visiting faculties who are professionals like CA, and CS. The exams are conducted semester-wise. The exams will be not easy and they will be challenging. The pass percentage is 85%.In the college from the 5th sem, the placement will start for b.com students. There are many companies visited our campus like capgimeni, Accenture, London stock exchange etc. The average package is 300000 and the highest package is 800000 and the low package is 250000. 40% of students get placement.

TATA Consultancy service, Tech Mahindra, etc. more companies choose our college. 
There will be several tests based on a company like the Written test,  Oral test and then Interviews, Aptitude test, etc. 
Based on these tests they will choose a candidate.Btech CSE is a future growing branch, in my opinion, there is Coeducation in it also.
All teaching faculty are also good and trained.
There are 4years and 8 semesters in between we have seminars presentations mid exams also.

Cse is the favourite for many who are decided to work as computer software engineers so I opted for this subject there is one teacher for 10 students on the campus and the faculty is highly educated and they had very good knowledge of the subjects and the teaching is good, especially some professors. There will be two mid-exams and final exams these will be easy if you study. It will average the exam.The percentage of students getting placed is growing as compared to the previous year and the student placed is around 80 per cent there will be many companies coming to the college for campus interviews. And many will join in campus placements. But few of them do ms in the USA after the BTech for better opportunities.

The campus is small, with not too much space to roam around but still campus life is good.
Annual fest is 8th mile but it won't happen annually, Esummit happens almost after every year which is also a good fest.
Fests are conducted after every 2-3 months, small or big doesn't matter.
Enough books are available in the library to make use of it.
The classroom contains a smart board and is good for a student kind of environment.
The sports culture is pretty good as uk Anil Kumble and lots of other sports personalities are from RVCE.
Ecell, RAAG, Rotaract, coding club, Alap etc. are some of the clubs.7th sem students are eligible for campus placement. Placements are better than lots of NITs and almost all the government colleges.
Average placement is >14 LPA this year for the ISC department and >8 LPA for IEM 
Deloitte, Capgemini, Mercedes Benz, Microsoft, Volvo etc. are some companies visit our college.
No. Of offers are maximum >250 for ECE department.
The highest Package is nearly 88 LPA this year
Almost all the students are placed in IEM.
I may prefer MBA as I'm already studying management at IEM.

If a student is determined, he/she can score enough to get a good GPA.Students are eligible for Campus placements in 3rd year. But students can also get internships from 2nd year itself. Students of our university have been placed at some of the highest-paying companies like Google, Microsoft, and Infosys. The highest package was 32.16 LPA. Students should make sure to maintain an 8+ cgpa and should also learn a skill to be noticed by the companies

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Thapar provides great placement opportunities for its students. Students here become eligible for campus placement from the 7th semester onwards. There were many companies that visited our campus including Microsoft, Samsung, Apple, JP Morgan, Optum, and many others. The number of offers made until now is approx 1497 and still counting. The highest package is approx 40 lacs and the lowest is approx 5 lacs. Approx 80% of students are already placed.We have around 5-6 subjects per semester and different teachers for all subjects. All the teachers are very cooperative and give us the best guidance. We have two exams per semester. First, the mid-term generally takes place after one month of the start of the semester and then at least we have our end-semester exam.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Annual tech fest occurs in a very short interval of time during the month of February and March, TechNiti is the tech fest of our college which is organized during the month of February and Utkansh is organized during the month of March, most of the books that we need for the study we can easily get from the library, we need to show library card to book issuers and we can issue one book for 15 days, our classroom is very well organized where we get the AC facility good sitting benches, and plane benches for the writing work, sports activities organized in very discipline way in our college we can play any game in our college, students play volleyball, badminton even in the night time as well, apart from the sports activities our college also have several social groups and clubs these clubs organized all the events in our colleges, each club and society having their different websites, some clubs and society of our college are TTA, GDSC, Zeal Society, SOME society, etc.The fee structure is different for different category students, the fee of any student depends on the category and the Income certificate, If a student belongs to SC and ST category then he/she don't need to pay only the hostel fee they don't need to pay tuition fee and If a student belongs to the general category then they need to pay full college fee, If the Income certified is less than 1 lakh then only hostel and other charges need to pay no tuition fee for these students, There is no scholarship for general and OBC students in our college but You can get the loan from bank easily without any extra charges, In our branch, more than 80 % students easily get placement in tech and core companies with an average stipend of 8 lakhs per annum.

The campus life of Mnit is what makes it the best. You will get ample opportunities to participate in various activities. The campus has 19 clubs including dance, literary, quiz, etc. MNIT has an immense library with books on various topics by many known authors. In sports, the campus is well facilitated with almost all games including badminton, football, volleyball, etc.All major companies including Google, Adobe, Texas Instruments, DShaw, and JP Morgan come to the campus for hiring students every year.

college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology - [Vel Tech]
review_text: The campus curriculum is CBC where students can choose their own faculty and subjects except the first year where it is common for all. The Faculty here are so friendly except for a few who will be very serious only about their studies. Mostly all the faculties are Done with their Ph.D. There are Internal and External Exams. For the external exams which are Final Sem Exams Carryout 60 marks and for internal exams, you will have for 40marks. Adding both the marks you need 50 marks to pass and 27 for sure in external to get a pass which is pretty easy if you study with some concentration at the time of internal exams. The gap between exams is not soo long there will be exams Continuously in the last month of the sem.Coming to my college life and others is totally different someone wants studies and some want fest. This is the place you can find both things. The university conducts a project expo every year known as VISAI and we have our own incubator in collab with Ashok Leyland and Goreh and AVNL avadi. The Clubs were so good here you need to join at least one club to get your graduation which is very useful in getting explore to others. And the college students won many tournaments in cricket and kabaddi football and the sports amenities are super good.

NASCENT is the biggest tech fest conducted by heritage.
Actually, this college is not famous for its fest 
There is a big library in college where you can study and there is a separate digital library
For the heritage, students have access to IIT Kharagpur online library 
In heritage, there are separate classrooms for every brack and every year students
The classroom is clean and has good lighting with good ventilation and air quality
Every sport is available in the classroom like Table tennis basketball football volleyball cricket and badminton. There are many clubs that are run by students and teachers 
Some of them are
1) Rotaract - social services club
2) Resonance - music club
3)Ghungroo- dance club
4)GDSC- coding clubI choose computer science because I love to code and I have a very much interest in web development. For every module there is a separate teacher suppose that there is 3 module in maths then there is 3 teacher for completing the module. Teachers use the green board and chalk for teaching and for complex structure teacher use projectors. All classroom have their separate projectors 
All teachers are well qualified and come from iit or jadhavpur like prestigious universities
This is an autonomous institute So all papers and syllabi are conducted by heritage itself there is no involvement of Maulana Abul Kalam Azad University of technology.
Semester papers are easy not so difficult

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
Chose ECE in sector 62 because the placements here are really good and the campus is situated in a prime location.
The class size is 30 students.
Most of the faculty is really good and helpful.
Some of the teachers are not that good but that's very normal.
Most of the teachers have masters or Ph.D. from an IIT, NIT, IIIT, BITS, or the reputed central universities.
We have lecture and tutorial classes separately. 
The term exams were held according to the schedule without any delay and the term exams are a bit difficult.The placements are good.
Students from the 7th sem become eligible for campus placements.
Companies like Atlassian, Adobe, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Samsung, Qualcomm, texas instruments, Infosys, TCS, IBM, etc.
The highest package for the 2022 batch was 1cr+ offered by amazon.
The highest package for 2023 is 83 lpa offered by Atlassian.
Avg package is 8.8lpa
I am also looking for placements but if I am not able to grab a good placement then I'll consider education overseas.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
I am a student of textile engineering in nit Jalandhar. To be honest I didn't choose this branch. I got it by jee cut-off. but at this time I know the importance of this branch. And you also get many job opportunities and placement facilities. The faculty to students ratio is 1:20. The qualifications of faculty are PhD or m.tech. and all teachers are experienced in their area. In the course curriculum of one semester the exam is collected in three parts ( two minor exams of 20 marks and one major exam of 50 marks and the remaining marks depend upon your assignment and performance). the exam is not so difficult. You can pass them easily by sincerely daily revising the things that are discussed in the classes.The NITJ provide good placement every year this year Highest placement got 1.2 crores and average placement every year was approx 14LPA and the lowest placement is approx 5LPA, 3rd year and 4th-year student are eligible for applying for the placement interview, many companies visited every year Microsoft Flipkart Google olx Amazon byjus Samsung Tesco cdot American express Goldman synopsys Paytm Honda Airtel and Reliance strand Vardhman and so may approx 250+ companies visited and provide 700+offices
Every year almost 90%student got placed in my plan after completing b.tech work in an MNC company

The social life is kind of chilled and kind of hectic as well. the college is good and big. the library is too good. the classroom is large and equipped. the sports and extra circular activities on the campus are a bit good and social groups for teaching poor students are thereI chose btech since no other option for me. here around 200 students in chemical, mechanical, civil, electronics, electrical and computer science engineering. the faculties are graduated and mtech in a particular subject is done by them

I opt this course for because I'm interested in web and app development. I love to solve problems. And at LPU the majority of the students are in Computer Science and Engineering branch only. For the year 2022 approx 15000+ students got admitted here in Computer Science Branch only. The faulties are also good here. They are supportive and helping also. But for that you also need to keep good bond with them so then only you can get a positive response from that side.Fees for Bachelor in Technology for Computer Science and Engineering branch are very high. And LPU takes a lot of money from students. For me after getting a 30% scholarship by giving an LPUNEST exam my fee details are like Tuition fees are 96000 per semester, hostel fees(4 seater ac) are 65000, and mess(standard mess) is 35000.

I think this college has the best teaching and official faculties. They support in every manner they can. They even provide their number numbers for help out of campus. They support in all of the things like how to prepare practical copies, how to get in interviews and all the things.Fees I can say are a little higher for middle-class students but this college provides up to 100% according to your results and also avail the government scholarships. Fees structure is 50k per sem. But one thing is this college accepts the fees only for the online method.

Life is very good at LPU. There is no raging culture and everyone is equal here irrespective of what they are studying and where they are from. They give the same amount of preference to sports too. There are many players from LPU who won medals for our country.The teachers are very friendly and supportive. They will explain n number of times if required. The curriculum is very simple and easy. Since my childhood, I'm into computers and mobile phones so I chose this computer science and engineering.

College life is very busy but good. The campus is always in news for various activities.
The educational aspect is looked after by the various workshops and seminars conducted. They even have open classes at times if the weather permits and I have heard that this makes education more interesting. Besides this, the big auditorium is always buzzing with activities like guest lectures from industry experts.
There are many fun activities like the events conducted for fun in the evening by the student affairs department where some celebrity or the other are always visiting the campus. The DJ evenings add to the fun fiesta. There is a fun zone, swimming pool, gym and so many other things.Lovely Professional University strongly believes in the fact that monetary constraints should not be an obstacle for a student to have access to quality education. So the University offers India???s largest Private Scholarship Scheme. Fee for successive semesters will be payable before the end of teaching of the preceding semester.
The examination fee (per semester) is Rs. 4000 and the examination fee for 1st semester is payable along with a fee for 2nd semester or before.

Students are eligible for placements in the last semester of their pre-final year. If no placement is found we can try offline campus and also there are chances for higher studies like postgraduate, MBA, and can do ph.d. MotorIq and made around 400 offers. HP offered around 300 offers. Also, companies like Tcs, Cts, and startups companies and they totally offer more than 1000 offers. Last academic year motoriq offered 1.02 crore per annum. This is the highest salary package last year. On average, students get 9LPA. 80% of students get placed. Some do their higher studies, some get placed offline and some don't get placed. People won't be eligible for placements if they have backlogs. My plan is to secure a job with a good salary package.Life is good on campus. We can be socialized with all states people and even other countries. We get to know each other. There are technical clubs. Nho clubs, literature clubs, etc., also our college has good lab facilities and good research facilities too. We conduct a cultural fest in February every year, this event is the 10th biggest event in India, also gravitas is a tech event that happens in October. 
We have a college team for cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, table tennis,  swimming, etc., each with a proper court. Also, there are gyms with ac facilities. Student welfare clubs are run by students and most of the clubs are run by students but coordinated by faculties.

The management faculty of this college is really good, prof. Are really helpful and well-qualified.
The term exams are always on time and it requires a little hard work to pass the exams.

review_text: Various fests are conducted throughout the year. Mainly sparsh, mindbend etc. Celebrity night is also there where reputed celebrity come to campus. But generally 62000 per sem tuition fee. Hostel fee is generally 12-15 thousand per year whereas talking about the mess it is 17000rs per semester. All scholarship student can get here if he satisfies its criteria both central as well as a state scholarship.

The fee structure varies for different income categories and also social division categories. The procedure for fee remission is quite slow though. The faculty is qualified and also takes our problems on the spot.

The only reason I took this course is integrated course is totally 5 years course so we can complete our pg degree in 5 years instead of 6 years

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text: I want to do B. Tech and that's why I choose this. The faculty is above average here. Each department has a proper teacher. Each teacher is teaching 2 to 3 classes in each session. Faculties are Ph.D. qualified and average in their teaching methods. Exams are conducted timely and easily pass.Fests like ethnic day, tech mart, and literary fest are organized by different clubs like literary clubs, film clubs, and coding clubs. Classes are well furnished with a digital board as well. All books are available in Library. Sports and extracurricular activities are encouraged on campus.

The fee per semester is 3,50,000 not including hostel fees and mess fees and other circular fees. However, are opportunities for getting scholarships via kiitee those who have a rank of less than 1000 have the opportunity to get scholarships. The other criterion for the scholarship is to have at least 60 percent in 10+2 exams.I have an interest in the field of computer science and engineering. Most of the faculty have very high degrees. Many of the are from IITs. Mid-term Exams are held every 3 months in the semester and end-term semester exams are held every six months exam has a medium level of difficulty but is still yet challenging.

My annual fees are around 1 lack 60 thousand and there is little bit fees hike every year I don't have any scholarship but apply for an education loan to lighten the fees burden from my parents.  Right now I'm in second and going to apply for an internship.I choose this course for exploring the tech domain in detail and get the opportunity to do research in a foreign country. Now the faculty Is nice. And student to faculty ratio is 4:1. The qualification of faculty is a min Ph.D.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: IIT Kanpur has a large campus of 1055 acres. However, you'll usually wander around only 300 acres. Life on the campus is nice. The campus is very beautiful and the sports facilities are well-maintained. We have here one hockey ground, one football ground, one PE ground, and several other grounds. The campus is full of greenery. One thing I especially like about IIT Kanpur is its culture. It has hall culture, wing culture, baapu culture, and many other cultures. The annual cultural fest is ANTARAGINI and the tech fest is TECHKRITI. Both of them are going to be conducted this year in March and I haven't attended them yet, so I cannot review them. Regarding the availability of books, the PK Kelkar Library has around 4 to 5 lac books and you'll find most of the books you need there. There are 20 lecture halls of different sizes and the new ones are very good. IIT Kanpur has the best OAT among all the IITs and is the main hub for cultural activities.Well, I choose this course because this is the best which I can get at my rank, but after joining the institute, this course sounds interesting to me. The faculty: student ratio here is around 1:9. The faculty is highly qualified here and have great knowledge of their respective fields, however, I would say that they somewhat lack in teaching. Most of the students have to study by themselves to get good grades in exams. Plus the academics at IIT Kanpur are hard. Within a few weeks of the beginning of our semester, quizzes were organized and after a few weeks, our mindsets started. Just after the midterms were finished, we got the dates for the end seems, which took place one and a half months later. In short, you will have to give exams very frequently. If you want to pass, you'll have to study one week before the exams, but if you want to excel, you'll have to study hard and regularly.

The fee per semester is 3,50,000 not including hostel fees and mess fees and other circular fees. However, are opportunities for getting scholarships via kiitee those who have a rank of less than 1000 have the opportunity to get scholarships. The other criterion for the scholarship is to have at least 60 percent in 10+2 exams.I have an interest in the field of computer science and engineering. Most of the faculty have very high degrees. Many of the are from IITs. Mid-term Exams are held every 3 months in the semester and end-term semester exams are held every six months exam has a medium level of difficulty but is still yet challenging.

My annual fees are around 1 lack 60 thousand and there is little bit fees hike every year I don't have any scholarship but apply for an education loan to lighten the fees burden from my parents.  Right now I'm in second and going to apply for an internship.I choose this course for exploring the tech domain in detail and get the opportunity to do research in a foreign country. Now the faculty Is nice. And student to faculty ratio is 4:1. The qualification of faculty is a min Ph.D.
