college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: The tech fest of IIT Delhi is Tryst and the cultural fest of IIT Delhi is Rendezvous which is generally held in the month of February or march. Rendezvous is also the biggest fest in north India and the second largest in India after mood indigo. The library is also well equipped with almost all the books that you can think of. Classrooms are also well equipped with a centralised air conditioning system. There are all sorts of sports and proper amenities to pursue any sport of your liking. There is a student body called the board of student welfare which works on making life at IIT Delhi easier for the students. Overall the campus life at IIT is amazing.From the 8th semester onwards students become eligible for placements. Almost all the big companies and MNCS visit the campus to offer placements to the students. The average package of IIT Delhi is 20,00,000 and almost all the students who sit in the placements are placed in some or the other companies. The highest package of IIT Delhi is 4 crore Which is an international package. The highest domestic package goes around 1 cr. After the degree, I would like to have my own startup and not get placed in any company.

Since KIIT was my backup plan for JEE MAINS and BITS, I thought I am not going to be a good engineer as I am not able to clear those exams.
This college changed my thought process as well as boosted my confidence with its teaching methodology. I talked about the opportunities and how to get the best out of myself from Dibakar Behra Sir(My mentor in the first year). I followed his advice and I am literally feeling that this is the place I wanted or more accurately needed. Though not as big a name as IIT or BITS still they provide us with every tool and tip to be a good engineer. KIIT is a very good platform that boosts your dreams in every possible manner. Faculty members are well-qualified and equipped with excellent skills in teaching. Faculty to student ratio is here about 1:12 . Exams here are challenging but our pre-exam preparation is so good that we find it easy. There are no skipping exams. Every exam is taken as per schedule.As I just completed my first year, here is my experience. It is going to be one of the best years of college life, making new friends, and adjusting to the new environment, classes, and all. During the first few days, you will be able to sort out the people with whom you will stay friends for the rest of your college life maybe even more. One thing that most people do is they will attend classes during the first year which will subsequently decrease in the succeeding years :P. One thing that you will hate is that in the first year, you will be caged inside the hostel as the timings are strict for first-year students. Boys will get involved in sports be it football cricket or tennis and some will start their journey towards Esports like Counter-Strike, Dota, or AOE. In short, your first year will be a smooth run with mid-sems and end-sems taking a bit of fun from the joy-ride.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
As this was the govt funded institute. The fees taken were very minimal for our batch the semester fee was 14500rs along with this we used to get an academic scholarship of 12400 per month from the government
In 3rd semester of their Mtech students are eligible to sit for campus recruitment
Many big MNCs came for the recruitment like GE, Mercedes, Caterpillar, Seimens, Lam Research, Bajaj, CNH, Applied Materials, TVS etc
If you're serious about your Mtech you'll easily get a job through campus recruitment
In our batch, nearly 90% of students were placedIIT DELHI provides the best placement opportunity amongst any college in India
Many big MNCs came for the recruitment like google McKinsey, Goldman Sachs, Facebook, world quant, etc
If you're really dedicated towards your Mtech course you'll easily get a job
There are 2-3 seniors from our branch who even got jobs in foreign countries like japan, Taiwan

review_text:

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text: College has a cultural fest called Spandan and a tech fest called techmart. All books on different subjects are available in the central library of CSJM UNIVERSITY which is issued on student ID. The classroom is well-equipped and has a smart board as well. Students can participate in different sports and extracurricular activities held in hostels and universities. There are different clubs such as film, literary, and coding clubs assisted by seniors.I choose this college as I got CSE at 129k to jee CRL rank. The faculties include mostly PhD qualified teachers. Each faculty holds two branches in a single semester and there are 66 students in each branch. Mid-semester and end-semester exams hold on time. The difficulty of exam papers also depends from teacher to teacher. Few are lenient but most of the teachers are difficult to score.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Campus life is great. The infrastructure is good too. There are over 40 clubs and societies which provide a place to learn and improve your hidden talents. There are many fests happening all throughout the year but the biggest one for me is the techno-cultural fest "Saturnalia". Sports facilities, grounds, and equipment are great too. The library is exceptional and is one of the tops in India and Asia. Lecture halls are large and well-equipped with modern technology for teaching.Students get eligible from the 5th semester for internship opportunities. Placements begin in the 7th semester. college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
The annual fest Rendezvous is held in our college which is Asia's largest cultural fest and is conducted in March-April
Our Annual tech fest is Tryst which takes place in March-April and a lot of tech companies visit our campus during the fest
The college ensures that sports and extracurricular activities take place such as the Group Dance by the IITD dance club, Stalwarts by debating Society, and many more
Clubs run by students include the BRCA which conducts all the events of our college related to Cultural Activities4th-year students are eligible to sit for placements 
Many companies visit during the placement season, including BCG and Big 4 for Consult and Amazing, Google for Tech, and many more
The highest package of the Institute is around 1.5Cr while the average package differs from branch to branch. My branch gives an average package of 12 Lakhs
Nearly all people who sit in placement get a job 
My plans after getting a degree are to do MBA from IIM Bangalore

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
The curriculum is very good. Unlike other courses, CSBS does not have courses like chemistry, and environment which are not necessary for a computer science engineer. There are many languages that will be taught like C, C++, Python, R, and MATLAB.
There were 75 students only. In other branches, there were like 250 students in a lecture hall whereas in CSBS there were only 75 in a lecture hall.
The faculty was good. Faculty was the same for CSE, COE, and CSBS.
The Faculty were all Ph. D.s from IIT and from colleges outside India.
The exams are pretty much dependent on your preparation, but I would that I studied it the night before the exam and I got 8 or 9 out of 10. So ya they were quite easy but there is relative marking so watch for those who study all day.Life at Thapar is the best out of all. There are many playgrounds for sport-loving people. There is a 6-floor building dedicated to a library.
The library is full of books and desktops where students can come and read with a very fast internet connection.
There are many places to roam around and eat like there is a COS market where a person can come and eat and socialize with other people.
The hostels are very good. There are options for a double seater and a single seater. During my time in a double seater, we used to be 4 friends living
together. Hostels for boys and girls are separate. There also an option with AC and without AC.

Our campus life is good I would say as our annual fest moksha is one of the best in north India and our tech fest conducts many activities for interested students. They were both conducted in the month of Feb and march.
Our library is also good it has many books related to every course and different magazines and journals for interested students.
Our classrooms also have facilities that are needed like smart boards, ac, and better sitting.
Sports facilities are also awesome we have every sport in our college and our college host sports fest and inter university tournaments also our sports faculty send us on different tours to play and show our skills.
Our college has almost clubs for every extra curriculum activity including drama, dance, singing, standup, social service,gaming,photography,literature,quiz, coding, etc.. I mean I can't even remember the no.of societies and clubs. Also, we have our official website and student sections for important information and instructions.I choose this course to get a mouth-watery package of course and another reason is my interest in the software field, especially in cyber security and ethical hacking.
The faculty is quiet good I would say a mix some are very good and chill but few are not friendly and helpful to the students and the student ratio is now improving as our college gives a 20 percent female quota.
All the faculty members are highly qualified and their teaching method is good needs to improve they need to connect the subject to students not just start teaching.
We have a back-to-back examinations from class tests to practicals to mid sem to another CT and finally end sem exam and it is not easy to pass the exam the students need to give a certain amount of time to each and every subject.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The campus offers good placement opportunities for students enrolled in BE program. Companies visiting the campus hire for Tech, non-tech, and core roles as well. However, tech opportunities are more relative to other domains. The highest offer made was around 40 lahks pa with an avg of 10 lakh pa. 70-80 percent of the batch gets hired easily during the campus placements.During my session in the college, the fee for pursuing BE was around 4 lakh annually which includes the academic fee, the hostel, and the mess fee(2019-2023). college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: I chose this course because I was interested in mathematics and wanted to build a career in the same. A rough faculty: student ratio is 1:6. Faculties are easily approachable and helpful. All the professors are highly qualified and have done a major contribution to research in their respective fields. Exams are held timely and you have to study consistently to score good marks.From the 7th semester onwards you become eligible for placements. Companies like Goldman Sachs, JP Morgan, Optiver, Uber, Quadeye, Google, Microsoft, and tower visit for placements. Placement rate is above 90%. I plan to opt for higher studies after my b.tech.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: I was always very passionate about computers, I was introduced to computers when I was a very young kid and this lead me to choose a career in CSE. Initially, I did a diploma in CSE and then a bachelor's course in the same discipline. The faculty we got at the institute was great. It was hard transitioning to the offline setting after the pandemic but they motivated us to do our best. Most of the faculty was well educated.The college works really hard to provide students with the best opportunities to boost their careers. As it was established a long time ago, the alumni support is really good. After my degree, I want to work for a well-established firm and gain as much experience as I can.

Yeah our seniors had said that so many popular companies visit dsce to recruit students and they also provide some internship work in there company itself there and approximately 95 per cent of students get placement every yearThe campus is very wast it has nearly 50 acres of campus in addition to that it has 3 campuses of which one is a university and a green campus it's a student-friendly campus over the life of dsce is nice.

BCA is close as possible to Btech. With a similar curriculum. And also with less money. BCA cost 3.5 lacs for 3 years while btech is 12 lacs for 4 years. 
Faculty of computer application here is meh! Not soo good not soo bad. There are 2-3 teachers who are loved! Whatever they'll teach you in the class you can't forget that. 
Exams are hectic here first practicals then theory. Mid-terms and just after midterms end terms. Assignments, seminars, mini/major projects. It really gets hectic during exam days.You get summer fest. There are many quiz events going on every other day. If you're a social you can easily get friends to create your group and have fun. Participate in different programs, sports etc. The campus is not huge but it's pretty with its lush greenery. Classes are totally digital. Lecture theatres are HUGE! And quiet with any to every amenity. 
There are several groups SPE, DBC, and dance clubs. Variety is there. You just have to be active.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: College life is awesome. The annual cultural and technical fests are Sparsh and mind bend and after that celeb, the night is also there. The library is actually very good all the books are there and the e-library is also available. the department buildings are newly made that's why it is very good. In svnit we have a big ground where we have two basketball courts, two volleyball courts, two lawn tennis, two badminton court, and a big ground for cricket, football, and other games. It also has a gym and table tennis. Svnit has many clubs such as Raag for music, Akriti for arts, dancing club, economical club, and many more.Fees are a little bit high if you do not get any scholarship or fee remission it will charge around 1.5 lakhs per annum but if your family income is between 1 to 5 lakhs per annum you receive 2/3rd fee remission in tuition fees which are 62500 and if it is below 1lakh per annum then your full tuition fees will waive. If you have SC/ST or PWD benefits then also your full fees were waived.

I'm a fresher in the Btech CSE branch. Technology being on the cutting edge made me take Computer science and engineering. At my college our class size holds approx 120 students, teaching is done in smart classes, teachers are highly qualified, exams are taken regularly and if a person pays attention in class he/she can easily pass the examBeing a fresher I'm not very much exposed to placement ideas and offers but would like to explore them. As per my knowledge on our campus, the average package offered to students is 20 lakhs. 
For a good package, a person needs to have a good cgpa as well as good interaction skills.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Rendezvous four-day cultural fest - has celebrities like Sunidhi Chauhan, and Benny Dayal.
 Tryst is an annual tech fest.
Literati, BeCon, and Kaizen are some other fests
Also different clubs also regularly organize events and competitions. For eg. the Debating society of iitd conduct stalwarts where basically students showcase their debating skills and compete with others.
Thus, every club regularly organizes such activities.
Also, sports are given great importance and are considered a central part of campus life, particularly through the General Championship (GC) which is an inter-hostel sports competition. Additionally, there is the Inter-IIT Sports Meet where all the IITs compete against each other in various sports.As a first-year student, I have not had any personal experience with internships, but from what I have observed and heard, many students have been able to secure valuable internship opportunities with a wide range of companies. These opportunities have varied greatly in terms of compensation, with some companies offering staggering packages of up to 40 lakhs, while others offer more modest salaries. Many of the companies that offer these internships are well-known and respected and provide students with meaningful and valuable roles that can help them develop their skills and gain practical experience in their chosen fields.

According to the university curriculum, teachers complete the syllabus timely and also take the unit test when it is scheduled. The syllabus is not very difficult and it can be easily completed with very less effort before exams if you are regularly in classes.College fees are highly expensive in comparison with other government colleges and fees increase by 10 percent after each consecutive year. But financial assistance is provided by University on basis of total family income up to 100 percent tuition fees waiver.

Many fests are organized around the year at NSUT. The main fest is "MOKSHA" which is organized roughly around the end of March. There are other cultural and technical fests like TeckWeek (Organized by IEEE), Resonanz, etc.
In the central library, one can find any book one's needs.
Classrooms are very spacious with high quality and comfortable seats with charging ports below the table for each seat. Classrooms have 4 ACs, digital screens, and computers for lectures, open Wi-Fi, speaker system. Classrooms are more or less like small auditorium halls.
Various sports facilities are provided in the college and one can hear about some sports competitions going on at any time.
There are a lot of clubs aka societies which have events every now and then. 
NSUTx is an app created by a student which has all the features from attendance, PYQ, syllabus, and Notices to exam results.I chose this course because my rank wasn't that good to go for a good branch in any NIT whereas ICE at NSUT is well-established and a good course for both tech and non-tech placements.
I believe that there are slightly fewer faculties relative to the requirement.
Faculty here at NSUT are well experienced and highly qualified and their teaching method suits all students.
Exams are held regularly. From class tests, mid-semester, and practical to end-semesters all are planned perfectly to enhance learning. The exams are easy to pass if one has a basic grip on the topics.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Life on campus is quite good. It is happening.
Rendezvous and Tryst are the annual fest and tech fest respectively.
Study books are available but novels aren't many in the separate library. 
Extracurricular activities are mostly situated in the boys' hostel side which is quite a bias and unequal for girls.The placements are quite good. But only for some students
Intern starts from 2nd year
Almost all famous tech companies like Microsoft etc visit
The highest package is around 1-2 crores and the average is 12 lacks approx

The internship process at NSIT begins in the month of august ie from your 5th semester. Generally the first company that comes for internship is Microsoft. After that you have some companies coming at an interval of 1 month for internship. All of them are generally for COE/IT people. I don't think there is any company that comes for MPAE/BT. Some electronics companies come (like Qualcomm, TI) and then people from ECE and ICE have a chance to get a college internshipMostly all the top companies including technical and non-technical companies visit every year for placement process. Placement process is very crystal clear. Students with more than 65% aggregate can sit in almost all relevant companies. Recruitment process includes percentage shortlisting, followed by written test, followed by group discussions and final interviews.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: There is a proper semester for Internsips. Generally the last semester is considered intern semester. College provides various companies with stipends varying from 0-100000. There can be different profiles like non tech profiles like consulting. Tech profiles and core GET profilesThe fee structure is not that good. The college is a bit overpriced however there are different types of scholarships that you can avail of. There is a fee hike policy of 10% each year. You can get a scholarship based on your semester marks

Life at the campus is amazing. The annual fest of NSUT is moksha and the winter fest is resonant. Moksha is held in month of March -April one of the tech week is conducted by the IEEE society every year . There is ample no. Books in library and we find every book regarding our curriculum. The APJ lecture complex is full of facilities and there is nothing to complain about it.NSS is a social group run by students.Because on my percentile I get only this branch.  The faculty members are nice and the overall student ratio is also nice.
Most of the faculty members are professors and they have done their Ph.D. in their corresponding subjects. The exams are on time and no it is not difficult to pass the exam.

The annual fest and tech fest are freshers fest kiit carnival and kiit fest. In the Central library, our university gets every book they and even every branch have its own library so the students can study easily. The sports group are very vast every sport have there a place to play we have a cricket stadium badminton court and etc there are mast social groups in the kiit and kiit has run a website called sap portal.From the 5semester students become eligible for campus placements if you are lucky and talented you got an internship in 2nd year this year the highest placement Is 84lakh in cse which is not realized yet but soon it will officially be the Percentage of students getting placements is very high because the mast placement is great you got placement. in this

Social life on campus is very good. There are many societies and clubs to which students can apply as per their interests. There is a society for every interest. For example Ashwamedh - the dramatics society, Crescendo - the music society, THC - the hiking community, etc. Sports infrastructure is also good here. But it needs to be improved. More badminton and table tennis courts must be built. Well-maintained smart classrooms are also there.The fee structure is high for a middle-class student. It is  2,19,000 /yr in the first year and it increases every year. The government provides scholarships like post metric scholarships, merit cum means incentive scholarships, etc. University also gives fee waivers to some students.

Well if I talk about fee structure well it is quite an expensive college for engineering I expect that 2,25000 on average per year we have to pay. Which is quite a high amount for middle-class students.  But if talk about scholarships there are many scholarships that anyone can avail of whether he is a category student or a general student scholarship is for all yeah I agree Percentage of scholarships may vary according to your category and family income. 
And every year a bit fee per year is increasing. 
Just two scholarships to students are provided such as the mcm merit cum scholarship which is provided to all students depending on their family income.Well, the social life and clubs and society of this college are awesome. Once we enter the college society are came for an orientation session and recruits freshers. If I talk about infrastructure it was also great sports complex is best. Even many opportunities apart from academics to proceed

The placements at our college are definitely almost the best in the country.
After the end of the 6th semester, you become eligible to sit in the placements.
The highest package offered was 83lpa CTC, by Atlassian, the lowest can go to 5lpa CTC.
Avg for computer sciens5should be around 20lpa, while for the college around 12 LPA 
All the eligible students always get placed.
Best companies in India visit NSUT for placements, however, a lot of students decide to pursue higher studies as well.Life at the campus is happening to say the least. Fests like moksha(cultural fest) are conducted in March every year and innovation is conducted in oct(tech fest), both see a very healthy attendance. 
The library is only filled with academic books.
Some classrooms are good, some are very old, and the campus has almost every sport available as a facility. There are a lot of societies and clubs in the college, in each segment.

I am in the electrical engineering branch at Netaji Subhas university of technology. The faculty members or teachers are good but some subject teachers are very rude. I choose this course because I have an interest in the electrical and electronics field.The campus life is good not much good in our college the biggest fest was moksha and I want to take part in moksha. almost all books are available in our library you can issue them easily. Colleges conduct lots of fests and sports for students.

My course is computer science engineering and according to its placement I selected this course because I am interested too much in it main faculty and guest faculty both are there. Hod and top faculty are best in their qualification and also guest faculty are passed from IIT and have MTech and PhD. Course Fees: INR 210000 per year. Internship opportunities are available on campus but there is a thing for boys and girls both are different a girl can get internships easily but for a boy, it's too difficult to on campus you have to be petty good at coding and with a GPA of 9 +. So about the fee, I would like to say it's a bit costly as other govt colleges are much more cost-effective but this JAC Delhi college is not. college + hostel+mess for my course is 12-13 lakh for 4 years which is costly and for scholarships, there is one merit-based that is available for everyone.NSIT Delhi offers a 4 year BTech program with 10 specializations. CSE is the most popular branch of the BTech program. It offers two minor specializations in AI and Data Science that can be opted for by a student, yielding them a BTech degree in CSE with AI or Data Science. Admission to the program is based on JEE Main scores and JAC Delhi counselling. The faculty members of NSIT Delhi are highly skilled and qualified professionals to provide the appropriate guidance and learning experience to the students of the institute. Most of the faculty members are postgraduates while some of them are ex-civil professionals and PhD holders.

I must say it is one of the best universities.

Admission to the program is based on JEE Main scores and JAC Delhi counselling. The faculty members of NSIT Delhi are highly skilled and qualified professionals to provide the appropriate guidance and learning experience to the students of the institute. Most of the faculty members are postgraduates while some of them are ex-civil professionals and PhD holders.

Our college is very good.  There is no problem here.  Faces etc. keep on happening here.  Good fests happen only after exams so there is no problem.  We enjoy college and enjoy college well and along with exams, we also get involved in straw striker kolam here and also join society so that we get external knowledge along with college.The nature of our college is very good and all the classes run offline in which all 75% attendance is good so that all the children attend the class our faculty continuously works hard and Lakhan runs all three classes.  Class tests and ant etc keep happening so that the faculty and students are constantly engaged in their studies.

Moksha is the annual fest, it is a grand event conducted in the month of March every year. We receive good sponsorship, with a large footfall each year. Big artists grace the occasion.
We have winter sports and an annual sports meet.
The library is a two-storey building with many books and a Wi-Fi connection. Yet a lot, of course, books are not available in the library and it is open for a short period of time; it is also closed on weekends.
Classrooms are nice, spacious, well equipped, and with a proper security system.
We have a gym, a TT room and a badminton court with a football, basketball court and a race track.
A lot of social groups and communities are there, tech and nontech.Chose this course as it has the best placement opportunities and due to personal interests.
Student to faculty ratio is average like most colleges 1:80 students
The faculty is well qualified but sometimes teachers don't show up for lectures for a long time
Exams were on time, but our semester started late the college is trying to compensate for it by making the semesters shorter, and easier for them but makes it hard for students to cope with the study and exam load alongside extracurriculars

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text:
I choose this course because I am deeply interested in forming designs and someday, I want to establish my own brand 
the faculty is interactive but strict and they don't accept any excuse at the student's end to maintain a good reputation and maintain the 75% attendance.
the professors are fairly qualified and have very interesting teaching methods but they require some contribution at the student's end
the examination marks distribution criteria are fairly simple. Firstly you have 2 minors (each of 20 marks)(happens every 40 days or so), then you have an end sem (50 marks) and the rest 10 marks are in the hands of the professor tutoring the course.life at the campus is pretty lively and very happening, there are lesser restrictions as you progress into next semester 
the infrastructure of the campus is above average but the classrooms and study rooms are well furnished and have an all-around nice vibe.
there are a variety of sports and clubs but the infrastructure for open sports is poor.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Students here also get the benefits of excellent internship opportunities both paid and unpaid or research interns both in Indian and foreign colleges. Min. stipend for paid internships is 30k per month which goes to a maximum of about 17 lakhs offered by Optiver for the year 2023. Although the CGPA required for such highly paying internships is quite high (Obviously).Rendezvous is an annual cultural fest and Tryst is an annual tech fest that occurs at the start of the year. Variety of books and journals available at 24*7 open library. Fully air-conditioned classes. Sports facilities are also good as compared to other colleges. A lot of clubs are run by student bodies such as Music clubs, sports clubs, dance clubs, etc.

Classrooms are equipped with modern technologies, especially for the freshers.
All types of sports and extracurricular activities are held inside the campus from time to time.
There are many societies and clubs in every interest and field for the students that are being run by the students.

After the 6th-semester students become eligible for placements and the highest package offered here is 7 to 8 lakhs per annum. My plans after getting the degree are to pursue MBA and go abroad. Many companies like Google etc are recruiters here.I chose this course because I wanted to pursue a management field and pursue MBA after the same. The faculty is quite good and attentive and had great degrees and qualifications. Timely notes and information is provided by the faculty.

College life at NSUT is very exciting, we get to meet a ton of amazing people with brilliant minds. And for every interest, there's a society from tech, sports, robotics, off-road racing, philosophy, photography etc. anyone can take part and excel in this society. The annual fest MOKSHA is among the best college fest in Delhi, students from different colleges come to attend it, and there are various tech fests held in college like HackNSUT, and TechWeek and many hackathons are held regularly. There are well-maintained grounds for Football, Cricket, Volleyball, Basketball, and Badminton and students participate in various events held in different colleges. The library is well equipped with every book and journal and has air-conditioned rooms. There is an app made by a society of college to check the student profile like attendance, marks, the notification from college etc in it.The academic fees of college for our batch i.e the batch of 2026, for the first year is Rs.219000 and it increases to Rs.10000 after every year the hostel fees for non-AC rooms for single seater are Rs. 51000 and for non-AC double seater is Rs. 49000 per semester (including the 19000 mess fees per semester) for AC rooms the hostel fees is almost Rs.60000(+ $750 mess fees) per semester. The college helps to provide the scholarship schemes from the government like the National Scholarship Program, E district scholarship, merit cum means scholarship etc. For a student loans, there is a branch of SBI inside the college campus.

Life at the campus is fun. The annual fest moksha is on the way and will be conducted in March. There is a lot of enthusiasm among the students. The tech fest resonance recently took place in the month of February. The library is well supplied with books. And coming to sports and extra curriculum, there is a plethora of societies to choose from.I chose BBA at nsut and I am very satisfied with the environment as well as the faculty. The professors are well-qualified and they explain the topics really well. There are 2 sections for BBA and about 50 students in each section. The exams were on time and the level of the examination was fine.

The NSUT is very well known for its placement. It has been ranked 7th among the engineering colleges of India for its placement. The student is eligible for an on-campus placement from the start of the fourth year. Also, the student is eligible for an on-campus internship from the third year. Each year more than 90% of students get placed.The annual fest of NSUT -MOKSHA occurs every year in the month of March. Another technical fest, Resonanz occur each year in the month of October.  Also, two sports events are organised each year-Summer sports events and Winter sports event. Also, the college is well connected and have restaurant club nearby

I chose this course in view that I would get to learn both about hardware and software. The course is good and I think I did a great job in choosing it because ultimately it is going to push me to heights to work harder on the course. In my course, the faculty-to-student ratio is about 10:60. Faculties are highly experienced and knowledgeable. They are really good in their subject and I am thankful for their support in course-related queries and everything. Term exams are held twice a semester. Between term exams, there are class tests that create a platform where we can keep up with the work. The exams were thorough. If one studied well, one can easily score good marks in it.Annual fests are lit. Tech fest is also really good and very informative. Books and journals in the library are up to date and we can always find good ones which help us with everything. Classrooms have a smart board that professors use. Faculty members encourage us to participate in sports and extracurricular activities. There are over 40 societies on campus where students can interact with seniors and learn new things.

Fees are quite less if compared to the other engg colleges/university.
As it's government engineering college fees is not that much. 
There is a scholarship scheme that provides scholarships on the basis of the tax slab their family comes in.Faculty is quite educated in their respective domains but they lack a way of delivering their knowledge. The faculty is pretty good in nature. Exams aren't that tough if you study even a day before you will easily pass through.

My course is Electronics and communications with the internet of things. I chose this course because it is a combination of tech and core branches, providing me with more opportunities for the future. The faculty is well-qualified and helpful and the term exams are well-spaced.The fee structure is moderate with around 10lakh fees for 4 years of BTech and seems worth it as the placement provided and environment is really nice. Though higher than some, it is still affordable enough, and options for scholarships are available.

There are a lot of societies in our university, u just have to say the name of which kind of society u want to join whether it is tech, dance, robotics, space, etc.
University conduct 2 fests Every yr moksha(name of the fest) which is 2nd biggest fest in Delhi
Every sports facilities are available and they conduct 2 sports competition every yr.Fees are a major issue for every parent but when u come to this University fees may be an issue there is an SBI branch that resolves ur all your issues with an education loan.
University increases fees every yr by 10k approx.
Keep in mind there is a lot of scholarship from central and state governments which will give approx 75% fees of 4 yr.

The course is pretty easy and all the facilities are friends as well as understanding enough. The teacher in first year have reputation and all are at high post and are doing freelance. The exams are so easy and can pass if you have studied for the exam before exam dayHere on campus, the cost of the study is the same as ip even is low than IP, and is better in infrastructure and study ratio. The teachers are friendly and cooperative. There are a lot of internship and scholarship programs for all students

The internship is offered in the 5th semester by various companies on as well as off-campus. Companies like Accenture, Microsoft, and Uber take interns by providing high stipends of 1lacs. Non-tech companies like Sprinkler also provide a similar stipend. The project for the interview should be made using web development knowledge and any other so skill. Every skill matter for the CV for interview cracking.The electrical engineering course is a mathematical course that is getting to realize in the subsequent semester. Subjects get tougher and tenacious. Faculty are greatly experienced in their field of teaching. Every subject has likely to have different professors in their field. In terms of exams, they are conducted every semester (normally 4 months) in between regular class tests and mid-semester exams.

Kiit provides you with one of the best placements. The average package for 2023 is around 8.5lpa and more than 1000 students got a package between 10 lacs to 15 lacs in their dream company. Kiit says it has a 100 percent placement but around 95 percent definitely get placed.The course curriculum is around 6 months. The student's teacher ratio is about 1 to 60. Which is pretty good. You will find one of the best professors in India here. Which are very helpful and kind. Terms exams are easy over here.

Certainly, I want cse branch but my rank in jee mains is 28k so I got ece now I realize ece branch is good. Especially in our college nsut placement of ece branch is very good. In our college many teachers are highly qualified such as op thankur of the physics department is Highly qualified and many teachers are highly qualified. The teaching methods of the teacher are best .timing of the exam is good so no issue here.The fee structure in our college for the first year is 219000 for 1st semester and for the hostel it is 48000 for 1st semester. I choose a hostel and for 1st year they provide ramanujan hostel. For obc and sc, there are full scholarship in our college and the person who has rasan card get a full scholarship. job opportunity is best in our college.

However, students belonging to poor families are provided with fee waivers and scholarships.The course curriculum is quite tough but interesting. It is very new for me and I am looking forward to learning many new things via my BTech. The faculty is okay and they are quite understanding in terms of our attendance.

The campus is lush green with good outdoor sports facilities. There are over 300 societies that conduct regular events and actively participate in the state to international-level of events. E-district and merit cum means scholarships are provided which can be availed by students to fund their degree. 
The internship starts at the end of 2nd year

A maximum number of students are placed. 
After getting I would get for MBA.

I choose mechanical engineering because I was always interested in mechanical objects, their design, and their work. Regarding our faculty, I think teachers are highly educated and knowledgeable, somewhat strict but yeah it's somewhat necessary. Also, many of our lab teachers are doing their higher studies (Ph.D.) from our college only while also teaching us. The student ratio is better than I expected, there are 20 girls in the batch of 100 students. I think most of the friend circles at least had 1 or 2 girls in them. 1st year really is finishing fast and so does our exams. The level of exams was moderate to difficult especially in electrical and mechanical subjects.College life is going really well. The campus is really large and beautiful and you get plenty of time to explore everything with everyone. I also made many friends within my class and other classes too. Seniors are really great and helpful

The exam is based on semester there are two semesters in one year and this is two-year degree program and four semesters in four year exams are of very good quality you will get cgpa after every exam
Placement job opportunities are excellent here with placements soaring up to 1.4 crores per a annum about 9 companies come on campus for placements and almost all students get placed.
Google, facebook come to intern students in this college, in my opinion it is best college in delhi to become an computer engineer.
The faculty of this college is very nice and educated. all the teachers are p.hd degree holders from reputed colleges of the countries. to name some of them are the Indian institute of science, Bangalore, and the Indian institute of technology, Bombay. hence the faculty is really very good.

Students are given many opportunities during college to have internships in many reputed companies

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: There is a tuition fee waiver for people with an annual family income of fewer than 5 lakhs per annum. Scholarships are also given based on merit, for example -  IITD Endowment Scholarship is given based on CGPA, and the merit list is released every semester, etc.Companies like Google, Amazon, Graviton Research, BCG etc. come for placements. Most people get placed in some company. The range of packages offered is very wide - a few lakhs to 1Cr+. I will probably pursue higher studies after my degree.

The course includes the basics of maths, accounting, marketing, and management as well as advanced levels. The student-faculty ratio is 1:60 for every subject. All the faculty holds a doctorate and is an expert in their field. The exams are of average level but 75% attendance is mandatoryThe college placements are pretty good. About 80% of people get placed on the campus itself, further who want to pursue higher studies have that option as well. The highest package gone for Btech is over 2 cr. BBA is a newly introduced course so its placements are still on the basic level

Campus Life is awesome at nsut. Moksha and resonaz are two major fests of this college and are held in March and October respectively. The library is very big and all types of books are available. The classroom has comfortable chairs and a smart board. Sports facilities are also up to the markInternship opportunities are really nice in the college. For girls, companies like google Flipkart uber start coming for women interns in 2nd year itself! So there are great opportunities. For others, companies start coming from the 5th sem beginning and there are so many opportunities.

Fees are affordable for middle-class people but you can even get a scholarship if you are from the reserved category or EWS so fees are not an issue if you are getting this college 
Many companies do come for internships and placements.Life is very good and enjoyable. There are 2 fests moksha and resonanz and also a technical fest innovation many societies are there is college so you can spend your time well and build connections. Sports activities are also available

Many annual fests are going on in the university every time. The library of the university is huge and you can avail of books any time you want for free from the library. The amenities in classrooms are moderate as there are all kinds of faculty some of which you may find interesting and some dull. The university gives special attention to students interested to participate in sports and extracurricular activities. There are many social groups run by the students like GSDC, Newton's coding club, etc.I chose this course because of my interest in computer science. In every class, there are 72 students and one faculty. All the faculty here are mostly Ph.D. holders and masters in their field except a few new joiners as they lac experience in teaching. There are two term exams mid-term and end-term. It is not that much difficult to pass the exams if you study decently. you also have the option to reappear in the exam five times if you fail the exam.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: There are a lot of scholarships for students whose family income is below a certain limit. Such students get support from the institute and the alumni. For summer projects if you get selected for projects like SURA then you also get a stipend for 2 months.I first chose the mechanical engineering department but after 1st year I got it changed to electrical engineering, the faculty is good and supportive and they periodically give you projects to evaluate your problem-solving skills as well.

The fees do not hike or the college doesn't do so but the average cost to study here is 4 lacs for 4 years which means 1 lac for a year. The students here mostly prefer taking the E.B.C scholarship and hoteliers take the Punjabrao deshmukh scholarship.The tech fest here is called techno blits it's not a huge event but it's quite popular and engaging. The classes here have one of the worst benches but they have projectors in each class.No sports activities are conducted here in the college.

A lot of events are connected almost every week. Students can enroll themselves in various organizations and clubs to showcase their talent in various extracurricular activities. The university has a large library with ample amounts of books and journals and much more for the students to access and use. Classrooms are air-conditioned and have a projector which the teachers use to aid in teaching. Also, internet connectivity is available throughout the campus. A lot of focus on sports is given as well.The university has a dedicated placements cell. It specifically focuses on placement techniques and classes. The students become eligible for campus placements from the 4th year but can bag internships depending upon the skill set from the 3rd year itself. High-profile companies visit the campus like Amazon, Microsoft, and VMWare to name a few. The highest package is approx. 30-35 lpa CTC. The average package is approx. 6-6.5 LPA. CTC.

The college claims a 100% placement guarantee.
The students are eligible for placements after the 6th semester.
Around 400+ company visits the college with an average salary of 8.5 CTC.
The highest package of the 2023 season was 62lacks CTC.The fees are pretty expensive at KIIT, but they do provide scholarships and financial assistance. The placements in this college are good compared to other private colleges in India. The only drawback this college has is the expensive fee.

My rank in JEE mains is the main reason I opted for this course. The faculty: student is equal to 2:20. All the faculties are well qualified and experienced as most of them did their Ph.D. from very well-known institutions like IITs. They are very helpful and very friendly toward their students, and always ready to our doubt.
The faculty members in our college are very helpful and always attempt to make students understand things easily.

review_text:

The course is very well defined and is intricately curated by keeping the industry standards in the mind. It enables the students to learn the latest technologies and keep up with the industry requirements. The faculty: student ratio is approx. 1:20. The faculties are mostly PHd holders and are well qualified in research and education. The teaching method is quite insightful and very up-to-date. It is quite interactive. There are regular assessment tests that are held at regular intervals to check the progress of the student. Also, mid-semester exams and end-semester exams are held which are quite challenging to pass and score good marks.Tech fest is conducted every month here and the annual fest is conducted approx in June. Every type of book is present in the library here e.g competitive exams books, journals, textbooks, programming lang books etc. LPU is most widely known for its sports. The LPU team everywhere they go they always make lpu proud. there are too many big names in sports from lpu e.g Neeraj Chopra.

The course curriculum is around 6 months. The student's teacher ratio is about 1 to 60. Which is pretty good. You will find one of the best professors in India here. Which are very helpful and kind. Terms exams are easy over here.The fee is around 1 lakh seventy-five thousand. Which is definitely more but compared to other pvt colleges it is pretty less. Scholarships are given to the top 1000 students who give the kiiteee exams. Campus jobs are also good

Many companies visit the college such as Amazon, Cognizant, High Radius, Capgemini, Adobe, CSG, JP Morgan, Delloite, etc. 
The average package is around 8.5 Lakhs. This year (2022) highest package offered was 58 Lakhs. 
Almost 95% of students get placed every year. 
My plan after getting a degree is to get placed with a salary of around 20 LPA.

my students have joined different hospitals,  blood banks, and labs for their dissertation work.

KIIT University offers a diverse range of undergraduate and postgraduate courses across various fields, including engineering, law, management, medicine, fashion, and others. The course curriculum at KIIT University is designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the subject matter, as well as practical skills necessary for their future careers. The curriculum is regularly updated to reflect changes in the industry, ensuring that students are well-prepared for the demands of their respective fields.
The faculty-student ratio at KIIT University is reportedly good, with a relatively high number of experienced faculty members compared to the number of students. The university has a team of well-qualified and experienced faculty members who are passionate about teaching and helping students achieve their academic goals. They are known for their dedication, professionalism, and expertise in their respective fields.The computer science department at KIIT University has a high placement rate, with a majority of the students being placed in top companies. The average salary package offered to the students in the computer science department is around 5-6 lakhs per annum, with some students receiving higher packages based on their skills and performance.
Some of the prominent recruiters include Amazon, IBM, Microsoft, Dell, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and Capgemini, among others. The university has also seen a surge in the number of startups visiting the campus in recent years, offering students with exciting opportunities to work in innovative and emerging areas.

The campus is one of the best campuses in India. Kiitfest is the annual fest conducted.
All the necessary books are available in the library. There is a separate infrastructure for each kind of sport ( cricket, football, hockey, basketball, rugby, tennis, and many more).  Some of the clubs are IOT, KSACI choose this course as I was interested to do so. The faculty ratio is around 15:1. The faculties are mostly PhD or M.tech ones. Teaching is good enough. Some are best and some are worse which is almost the same case in every other college. Exams are moderate and can be passed easily

I am not sure about other branches but for computer science and IT, we had special training programs organised during our holidays. They did charge some nominal amount, which seems to be a lot when you are in college, but later on, you realised its importance. Today, if I can write in my resume that I am OCJP JE6, certified then the credits solely go to those training provided to us.
Apart from that in our 3rd year of engineering Microsoft did come to recruit people for internships and even provided PPOs to some deserving students. I am sure such programs must be organised for other branches as well. If not maybe you could start an initiative and bring in companies for internship. You just need to think big.I have chosen Btech(Computer science Engineering) as my curriculum, my rank was 320 so I got the opportunity to select any of the courses provided by the college in a stipulated time frame.
In KIIT student ratio is 1:1, it's a healthy environment you will feel here.
All the professors have PHD degrees and many professors are from IIT, and IIIT and some are even from Harvard. Teaching is good they help us a lot.
Toral of 8 semesters are there with every semester there you have to give two exams, mid sem and end sem.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: Besides academics, we have many cultural and sports activities. Antragini is our annual College festival. It is the biggest festival in Asia.in which, students, from almost every part of the country, take part. Besides this, we have Techriti our tech festival, and Udghosh our sports festival.There are a lot of scholarship programs in our college. The government provides many scholarships. Instead of that pur college provides many other scholarships. I can say that if your family income is low and below 500000. then you will definitely get a scholarship if you apply.

Fees are really quite high but at par in comparison to most of the private colleges.
1.75 lakh institutional fees.
28000 3 non-bed ac room charges per semester.
22500 mess,
15000 extra money for one-time accommodation.
Ac rooms cost around 50000 per semester for 3 beds.College life so far has been great.
We just had our college fest where Sunidhi Chauhan performed which was quite amazing and a site to witness. 
We also had a tech fest and several big names came. Also, we had several dance events in our cricket stadium.

Faculty to student ratio could be improved a little like it could be made 1:40.

The library here is one of the best libraries in the whole of India I can say as its infrastructure is really great and gives a good environment for studying and every possible facility you can imagine it's also 24 hrs.

All the classrooms are equipped with lots of air conditioners smart tv projectors and decent benches and all

There are lots of societies for many other things which come under ksac.For the tuition fee, you have to pay 175000 per semester 
So the tuition fee for the 4 years will be 1400000
 And you have to pay 75000 for the laptops and all at the time of admission.
Hostel fee for 3 bed non ac - 28000 per semester 
3 bed ac 50000 per semester 
2 bed ac 60000 per semester 
And for the mess, you have to pay - 22500 per semester 
And you have to pay 15000 for the registration of the hostel that you have to pay at the time of admission.
The scholarship you can get only through KIITEE which is the entrance exam of KIIT university and you have to secure an under 1000 rank for that

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Fees structure here is very good. I also received a scholarship for tuition fees. Every student gets some kind of scholarship. Fees are paid on the basis of category and family income. Those under-scheduled castes and tribes get scholarships from schools.The course faculty here are very good. They teach in such a way that we understand in one go. They are amazing, and their style of teaching is very good. Proff teaches very nicely such that all students get to know every chapter clearly.

A fest happens every year during the month of February this year it happened as KIIT KARNIVAL of 15 days where the complete campus was decorated and different functions happened on different days. There are various libraries inside the campus for every department and there is a main library on 7 floors which has every department's books and it is very good for sitting and studying during our exams.
All the classrooms have centralised ac and digital screens.
The sports facilities here are also very good here and there are stadiums for every sport, there is a good opportunity for a good sportsperson in kiit.
There are a number of societies under various types of domains like social work, photography, digital marketing, dance, music etc you can join any of them and enhance your skills.I had chosen Btech with Computer Science as my branch as we can see how the IT industry is evolving very fast in today's world and the average package of every software developer is higher than other jobs in India.
So under 1 faculty 72 students are there and we also have an open elective for faculty in which we can choose under which faculty we want to study.
The qualifications of almost every faculty are good here, they are a very helpful and knowledgeable person and the way they teach is also very good.
Our Midsems and Endsemds also happen timely and students can score well if they study well before their exams.
You can pass your exams by just studying a day before the exam.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
The annual cultural fest is Rendezvous which is a four-day event. The annual tech fest is 'Tryst'.
These two fests are generally held in continuation,i.e. one after the other.
IIT Delhi has a huge and 'rich-in-literature'  library, where books on various engineering and non-engineering topics can be found.The course curriculum is very interesting and that too being taught by highly-qualified faculty members is an awesome experience.
There is approximately one professor per 100 students.
The exams were held two times in the semester, one in the mid-semester and one as the final exam.

The campus life at Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute, Matunga is the best. Given its location (Mumbai), the social life is 10/10. Travel access is good. You can come through any 3 lines of Mumbai local else you can avail bus facilities as well. You can get any book related to engineering in the library.  Many events take place on the campus.The placements are very good for Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute, East. Students are eligible for internships from Third Year and you might get Pre Placement offer depending on how you perform in the company. A lot of companies visit the campus for placement and the offers can be anywhere from 62 LPA to 5 LPA.

The annual fest of nit trichy is festember. Its tech fest is Pragyan. Nittfest is the inter-departmental college fest. Aaveg is the inter-hostel fest for the first years of the college. There are so many clubs related to every field and students can start their own clubsThe internships in cse have a stipend of around 1lac+ per month and the core branches have a stipend of 50k per month. The companies look for projects and internships in the resume to filter the students. So these are very important for good placements

The curriculum is similar to that of any other university. I'm a student of Cs branch and I pursue this course as I'm interested in coding and it has a great scope in future.
There are almost 15000 students in University including mba, bba, bca, etc., and about 2500+ teachers. A maximum of the faculty has a Ph.D. degree and were the students of nits, iits. In one semester we have to give 2 exams and they are moderately hardAll MBA and engineering students at GLA University are required to perform an internship that lasts one or two months at the conclusion of their first and third years, respectively. Most students don't receive an internship offer from their schools; instead, they must set up their own internships according to their schedules and relationships.

Cognizant, CISCO, DELLOITE, INFOSYS, and TCS are giving good internships. You can avail good stipend if you are capable. Just work hard and you can easily get internships.  Teachers will help you a lot. But you have to grab it on your own.Placements are good if you are hardworking. University claims about 90% of placements. The highest package offered this year till now is 33LPA. My plan after getting a degree is for good placement. The average is around 4-6 LPA.

A fest happens every year during the month of February this year it happened as KIIT KARNIVAL of 15 days where the complete campus was decorated and different functions happened on different days. There are various libraries inside the campus for every department and there is a main library on 7 floors which has every department's books and it is very good for sitting and studying during our exams.
All the classrooms have centralised ac and digital screens.
The sports facilities here are also very good here and there are stadiums for every sport, there is a good opportunity for a good sportsperson in kiit.
There are a number of societies under various types of domains like social work, photography, digital marketing, dance, music etc you can join any of them and enhance your skills.I had chosen Btech with Computer Science as my branch as we can see how the IT industry is evolving very fast in today's world and the average package of every software developer is higher than other jobs in India.
So under 1 faculty 72 students are there and we also have an open elective for faculty in which we can choose under which faculty we want to study.
The qualifications of almost every faculty are good here, they are a very helpful and knowledgeable person and the way they teach is also very good.
Our Midsems and Endsemds also happen timely and students can score well if they study well before their exams.
You can pass your exams by just studying a day before the exam.

All the faculty are Ph.D. and teach with years of experience and in a way that is easy and comprehensible for students.
The Mid sem and End-Sem exams have a gap of 1-1.5 months. The semester feels short and so do the holidays.The two main cultural fests of NSUT are Moksha and Resonance which are held in summer and winter respectively.

The APJ lecture halls have smart boards, AC, good lighting, and foldable seats. There are also separate charging sockets available at each seat.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text:
Annual fests - Here are the name of the fests in IIT Kanpur - ANTARAGNI (Cultural fest), UDGOSH (sports fest), and TECHKRITI (tech fest).
 Antaragni is conducted in the month of march, Techkriti is shown in the month of march or maybe April, and Udgosh is completed in October.

 IIT Kanpur has one of the most extensive libraries in India, called P.K.kelkar library.
Here are 223972+ books and 16000+ E-journals.

IIT Kanpur has the best classrooms. They are fully furnished, temperature controlled, and with internet facility.

Here is a compulsory course in IITK called CPA (compulsory physical activity) for first-year students. Students have to choose one of the available physical activities.

There is a student body called the student's gymkhana. Every clubs, society, and councils are in under that committee. There are many websites for students run by students like every fest has its websites.I chose this course because I am interested in research, and Kanpur has the best research culture. There is an excellent faculty-to-student ratio. There is 500 faculty member on 8000 students. All the faculties are Ph.D.-level qualified. There are some faculty which are not good at teaching, but there are lots of faculty which are very good at teaching. For the exam, it is not easy. You have to take classes regularly and complete assignments on time to at least pass.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text: Based on the rank I secured in JEE-mains and my interests I chose mechanical engineering in this college and its location is quite good as well. The faculty is supportive except for a few teachers which are worse. The faculty members are highly qualified and teaching methods are appropriate as required. exams and quizzes are conducted regularly within 2 months and it's not much tough to passThere were many companies invited by the career development and guidance cell with a stipend ranging from 10k to 160k based on the streams and skills of different streams students. Very good projects have been made by the students and faculty mentors always help students wherever required

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: Rendezvous is the Cultural feet and Tyrst is the tech feat. The library is pretty big. The classroom is very active. People are very active in sports. I am an active member of the debating club. There are all kinds of clubs here.The placements are in the 7th semester. The companies include Quadeye, Samsung Korea, Mc Kinsy, and Texas instrument. The highest package was around 1.4 CR. The average placements are around 14 lakhs. I plan to be a professor.

The campus is around 300 acres and it's fulfilled with greenery. We have a private beach it's very nice. The area around the college is very peaceful.
Our college conducts an annual fest incident and one tech fest Engineer. Our library is very big so many books are there. The classroom of our college is very big and well furnished. In college, we have 2 basketball courts 2 volleyball courts 1 cricket ground 1 football ground.4 badminton court 2 kabaddi court and sports faculties is very nice 
Our college websites IRIS is run by our college Seniors and many clubs are there whose run by studentsCollege Fees are 157000 per year college management takes fees per sem. Our college fees are not high compared to other IITs. The college provides financial assistance scholarships for sc, st students and some OBC and EWS students took benefit from college fees 
National scholarship program NSP is a central government scholarship scheme there are many types of scholarships we can take

It has a good infrastructure, laboratories are great and well-maintained.
The library has plenty of books and students can issue them. Sports facilities are also good.
The college has many social groups and clubs, some are Rotaract for rotary, ghungroo ( dance club),  and many more.The course curriculum is good. 75 %Attendance is mandatory. The end semester exam has 70% weightage and the rest  30% comprises the class test, attendance, viva, and assignment.
The faculty members are great and helpful. The professor is very knowledgeable.

From the 5th semester college will provide internships and we are eligible for internships when we don't have any backlog. The fee hike will be directed by the ministry of education west Bengal, then our fees will hike. There is many types of scholarship is provided by the government to the girls or the topper of the class.

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text: I choose this course because I was inclined towards the software field and our college has an excellent history of placements for the Computer science branch. The teaching of the faculty is nice too. The teaching methods are quite advanced too. The college has many professors with numerous degrees and they have been working in the software field for 20-plus years. The term exams have been regular and have been timely too. They weren't easy to pass but with proper preparation and regular study, you can pass them with ease.We have various fests in our college from talent fests like Culrav to technical fests like Aavishkar, and Hack36. Also, every committee has fests that they conduct. These are usually done in April and October. We have a wide range of books available in our central library. Classrooms are also well equipped. We have a website of a training placement office that is made by college students. Also Aavishkar website was also made by students.

For us, the fee was 80000 for the present batch which is 130000 and there is a scholarship provided for the rank students where every sem after the exam they give three scholarships like DAC, MRD, and CNR where the scholarship is given based on their cgpa.The placement will start in 5th sem and many reputed companies will come to hire the students.  The companies like Capgemini, Accenture, London stock exchange etc. For placement, The students should have minimum 6 cgpa. 40% of students get placement.

The course for our batch is constantly changing sem by sem which is making it a bit difficult but it's making everyone more competent and b.com was a course where u could learn about commerce and a bit of management. Students are about 250 in our batch, the faculty members are also approachable at times of any doubt or queries. There are regular tests after every unit in each subject. And it is CBTFor campus placement and employment opportunities, students must be in the 5th sem and there are a few sessions also conducted by the college on employment. There is a number of companies which come to college naming a few are Accenture, Amazon, Intellipaat, London stock exchange and many more. The placement package would vary from 2.5L to 4L.

I selected this course personally because of the family pressure plus societal pressure. There are so many claims from this course that mostly all students try to get this course no matter what. And there are so many jobs in it so I selected it.
There is decent faculty present on the campus but very strict in every manner so not too good thing but some faculty members are good too.
These are very qualified faculty members depending on what they do maybe best in their department of liking. Some have a very easy way of teaching and a fun way and some have a very careless way of teaching just like typical teachers.
Term exams are moderate and sometimes a little hard depending on how the teacher was who was teaching and did he was teaching properly or not. Passing is mostly easy every teacher passes the student but again mostly depends on the teacher as I said faculty is strict.There are two main fests one tech fest is known as Moksha and another is a cultural fest known as Resonance. Moksha is usually conducted in the month of March and Resonance is usually conducted in the month of October or November.
There are plenty of books and journals available for students in the library and are quite easy to avail.
There is so much exposure to other things than studying like all sports are present plus are being taught by the coaches and there are many societies which also help in extracurricular activities.
There is an app present named Nsutx made by students for the student profile and mainly attendance and there are a few pages after college on Instagram handled by the students.
