The faculty of this college was too cooperative, they always work hard with all students parallelly. they do their 100% with the students.
The student ratio was good.
Qualification of the faculty was taken on the basis of experience, the college takes feedback from all students when we came to this college.
Timely of the term exam was a bit short, but the difficulty level was 4 out of 5.The life of this college is moderate, because no fest, parties are arranged in this college. this college follows a very strict rule but one thing that is good is that this college arranged a freshers and farewell party for all the years on a big level.
All levels of books are available in the library, any time if you want then sure u go.
All types of sports are organized in this college every year a lot of students grab this opportunity and play very well and go to the state level.

college_name: IIT Jammu - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text:
The campus is designed to provide students with a supportive and engaging environment with a variety of clubs and activities to promote social interaction and personal development. Some of the clubs and organizations available at IIT Jammu include technical clubs for robotics, coding, and electronics, as well as cultural clubs for music, dance, and theatre. There are also Sports clubs for basketball, cricket, and volleyball as well as social service clubs and entrepreneurship organizations.
In terms of infrastructure, IIT Jammu is located on a sprawling campus with modern facilities and amenities the campus includes a library, computer center, Sports Complex, students activity center, and other resources for academic and personal development. While IIT Jammu is still in the process of developing its campus and student life it is designed to provide students with a rich and engaging quality experience with a variety of clubs and organizations to suit their range of interests.IIT Jammu is a relatively new institution having been established in 2016, it is part of the IIT system and is expected to maintain the high academic standards and reputation of the IITs. Students at IIT Jammu can expect to receive a challenging and rewarding academic experience with access to state-of-the-art faculties, cutting-edge research opportunities, and a supportive learning environment. The faculties here are very nice and cooperative they help the students very much with the research and the studies.

Moksha-Innovision is the annual tech fest of NSUT which is conducted in March.
The library is good, very spacious but many books aren't available here.
The classrooms are just perfect. They are big, with full AC, perfect lighting and comfortable chairs.
NSUT has a big sports complex. There are just so many sports for students indoors, outdoors every type. For other activities there are societies.
NSUT has over 45 societies which are run by the students for the students. Such as Mirage and Capella for dancing, Crescendo for music, DebSoc is the debating society, Ashwamedh is the drama society, Junoon for the club of photography, Tatsam is the Hindi society etc There are many other societies in the University 
After all this campus life is quite hectic because the university mainly focuses on academics.I chose this course because I want to be an engineer since I was a kid.
The student ratio is ok here better than in other technical colleges. The faculty here is mostly guest staff so they are not very good. Some of them are good while some of them are just mid-level. Some of the faculty are PhD scholars while some are PhD qualifiers. I personally don't like the teaching method of a single professor here.
Talking about exams it's not that difficult to pass, but the university doesn't give enough gap between exams and it pressurises the students.

my course is computer science engineering and according to its placement I selected this course because I am interested too much in it
main faculty and guest faculty both are there.
hod and top faculty are best in their qualification and also guest faculty are passed from IIT and have mtech and phdSo about the fee, I would like to say it's a bit costly as other govt colleges are much more cost-effective but this JAC Delhi college is not.
college + hostel+mess for my course is 12-13 lakh for 4 years which is costly and for scholarships, there is one merit-based that is available for everyone

From the 5th semester, the placement will start and many reputed companies will come to hire students. The companies are aperture, London stock exchange, cpagimine etc. The highest package is 8 lakh and the average is 3.5 lakh. 40% of students get placement. My plan after my degree is to do higher studies or entrepreneurship.I wanted to become a charted accountant so I took b.com. The faculty-student ratio is 30:1. In this college all teachers are qualified and experienced and some teachers are the best and some teachers are arrogant in nature. There will be exams for every sem and it will be challenging.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: There are many fests being conducted one is tryst which is conducted in march and the other is rendezvous which is conducted in October. The campus life is vibrant and lively, you have wifi facility on campus, a library where there are many books and published journals in which you can read everything for satisfying academic thirst, there is computer services center where you can use computer 24x7 for academic purposes. There are also many clubs and events organized by the clubs like a debate club, coding club, dance club, music club, Axlr8r, and economic development club. All the classrooms are fully air-conditioned and have wifi facilities with state-of-the-art modern teaching technologies and CCTV cameras. There are food outlets on campus for your hunger pangs and there is a night mess that is open till 4 am to satisfy your night cravings.I chose this course because firstly, it is a prestigious college and on the basis of my rank I was allocated this branch and I liked maths so I chose this course. The faculty-student ratio is around 1:20. All the faculties are Ph.D. from internationally recognized colleges. There are 2 minor exams and 1 major exam and assignments and quizzes all are cumulated together to determine the grade out of 10, A being the highest grade. The exams are not difficult you just need to pay attention in class and you are good to go. The first minor is after one month of the semester and 1?? months after minor 1 is minor 2 and one month later is a major exam conducted.

I get This Course On The Basis of My Merit (Rank in the Entrance Examination) so, I did choose it.
The student ratio in my class is Boy: Girl is 4:1.
Teachers are good, but you will have to put in the effort from your side as well to excel in your studies, as I said this College Curriculum is a bit strict, You have to do and complete everything within a given deadline.
There are CT(Class Tests), Quizzes, Mid Semester, Practicals, and Then End Semester Examinations.The fee structure variates and  Increases every year, it was 2,06,000P. A last year, this year it was 2,19,000PA Scholarships. yeah, there are many, you get that on the basis of your Financial Background, / your Merit Chola.
There are many like District(Merit Cum Means Scholarship) CVPVSK provided by NSUT or Through NSP (National Srship Portal) etc.

We have a flexible system where we can choose the course faculty and particular slot for each semester.
The faculty are friendly you can ask doubt they will help you.
Mentors will be provided for each student.
If you put in your efforts you can pass.The club there is like the coders club maths club literary club.
The library is there inside the campus you can borrow books whenever you want it has all department reference books.
Each year they will have sports day, college day, and cultural.

Campus life here is very chill and calm, it's all about studying and fun celebrations, they also celebrate all kinds of festivals as well. And there are many countless numbers of clubs and organizations in college music, dance, drama, literature etc.In my point of view, the fees were somehow very high they give scholarships  but very high I don't  think the BA department  gets the internship and stipend from the  college  but it's dependent  on the way you performed in the college

review_text: I took computer science and engineering because it was of interest to me and the field is very vast and offers a lot of opportunities. The faculty-to-student ratio is decent in this institute. All the faculties are highly qualified and their teaching methods are easy to understand and effective. There is a mid-semester exam and an end-semester exam which are held midway between the semester and at the end of the semester respectively. The classrooms are decent but not too good but it has all the amenities. There is a sports fest which is held in January. The websites for Culrav and Avishkar are maintained by students.

I am planning to give a gate as well as a cat.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
IIT Delhi is recognized for offering a comprehensive and challenging curriculum in engineering and technology. The course material covers various subjects and is regularly updated to stay current with the latest advancements. The faculty at IIT Delhi is well-qualified and experienced and known for their innovative teaching methods and expertise. The faculty-student ratio is favorable, allowing for individualized attention. Pursuing a degree from IIT Delhi provides a strong foundation in the field and networking opportunities with industry leaders. The term exams at IIT Delhi are conducted promptly and are challenging but not impossible to pass. Preparation and hard work are necessary to succeed in these exams.IIT Delhi's campus is a lively and dynamic place, with a diverse social scene and numerous clubs for students to get involved in. Rendezvous is the annual fest. Normally conducted in March -April. Tryst is a tech fest, conducted in March. The infrastructure is modern and well-maintained, with cutting-edge academic and research facilities and a variety of student amenities. 
All lecture halls and library has centralized AC. Located in Hauz Khas, South Delhi, students have access to the city's cultural and recreational opportunities. Campus life at IIT Delhi is enjoyable and supportive, providing students with a well-rounded experience for learning, growth, and fun.

review_text:

 All faculties are PhD. Some of them make you understand the concept clearly, while for others you need to ponder upon the youtube channels before the exams.
The pattern of Exams: 2 minors 1 End sem in a semester.
After every 1.5 months, you will come across these examinations and these are not at all easy like what used to happen in school.. so keep your seat belts fastened!!

The Delhi Technological University (DTU) has a strong track record of providing excellent placements to its students. The university has a dedicated placement cell that works closely with companies to arrange on-campus recruitment drives and internships for students.

Some of the top recruiters at DTU include Microsoft, Amazon, Google, Tata Consultancy Services, Wipro, Infosys, and Hindustan Unilever, among others. The university also has partnerships with several multinational companies and startups, which provide students with opportunities for internships and hands-on learning experiences.

In recent years, DTU students have been placed in some of the top companies in the country and abroad, with many students securing lucrative job offers with high salaries. The average salary offered to DTU graduates during campus placements has been consistently high, and ranges between INR 20 to 30 Lakhs per annum.The fees for the B.Tech program at the Delhi Technological University (DTU) vary based on several factors such as the course of study, the category of the student (General, SC/ST, OBC, etc.), and the mode of admission (through JEE or other entrance exams).

As of my knowledge cut-off in 2021, the estimated fee for a B.Tech program at DTU was approximately INR 1,20,000 to INR 1,50,000 per annum. However, it's important to note that these fees are subject to change, and I would suggest checking the official website of DTU for the most up-to-date and accurate information.

In addition to tuition fees, students may also be required to pay other fees such as examination fees, hostel fees, and miscellaneous fees for laboratory access and other facilities. Financial assistance in the form of scholarships and loans may be available for students who require support to meet the cost of their education.

HBTU has a thriving student life, with a variety of social, cultural, and sporting events taking place throughout the year. Some of the major annual fests and tech fests hosted by the university include "Adhyaay" (cultural fest), "Aagaz" (sports fest), and "Technika" (technical fest). These events provide students with the opportunity to showcase their talents, network with peers, and have fun.

The university has a well-equipped library that provides students with access to a wide range of books, journals, and other resources. The library also has modern facilities, including internet access and study spaces, to support students in their academic pursuits.

HBTU provides students with a wide range of sports and extra-curricular activities. The university also has several student-run clubs and organizations, such as the debating club, music club, and robotics club, that provide students with the opportunity to pursue their interests and develop new skills.The placement prospects for graduates of Harcourt Butler Technical University (HBTU), Kanpur are generally good, with many students securing employment in leading companies across various industries.
Students become eligible for campus placements from the 7th semester of their program. Over the years, HBTU has welcomed several leading companies from various sectors, including IT, Manufacturing, Consulting, FMCG, and others. Some of the top companies that have visited the campus for recruitment include Google, Amazon, Flipkart, Samsung R&D, and many more.

The highest package offered to students at HBTU is INR 60 lakhs per annum, while the average package is around INR 10-12 lakhs per annum, depending on the profile offered. 
Typically, a significant percentage of students get placed through campus recruitment, with the exact percentage varying each year. However, on average, more than 90% of students can secure employment through campus placements.

The Life at the VIT AP Campus Is Good. 
The Main and Upcoming Annual fest is VITOPIA in which there are many events organized with celebrities invited to interact with the audience. The Overall fest contains many exciting games and also workshops for enjoyment and is one of the major annual fests of VIT AP. There are also other tech fests like the VTAPP which has been held in the Month of November last year 2022, which was organized very well with the combination of different technological areas showcase i.e. the Main Real-world on going projects expo as well as many other fun games like robot soccer and Virtual reality experiencing etc. 
There is almost every book available for the respective courses for education like Computer science-related books. Mathematics and many more are only related to academics. A Very peaceful environment is very well maintained for the readers in the library to study. Mainly the classes are digitalized. The main Clubs are CSI,MLC,SM,ELA, etcI chose Btech CSE( Computer Science and engineering) because VIT has a track record for Giving Good Quality education, As I am passionate about Computer software stuff and for a good job placement I chose this Course.
Faculty student ratio is Good i.e 20:1, which means it has 1 faculty for 20 students. 
Qualifications of Faculty are very good. Many of them are from IITs, NITs, IIITs Background so it is Obvious that the Teachers are very knowlegeable and Good. 95% of the Teacher's Teaching method is very Good and 5% not good will be in every college but that Can be ignored.There are total of 2 CAT(Mid semester Exams) and 1 FAT(Final Semester exams) and it was Moderate to pass as they can be answered if You Listen to the classes properly and take proper notes and with practice. The Questions are application based so Understanding the Theorical part and Practicing well is the key to ace the exams.

It's around 2 lakhs to 3 lakhs per academic year. There are few fee concessions but they select a few individuals for that through interviews.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text:
I have chosen to pursue Data Science and Engineering as my B.Tech major because it is a rapidly growing field in the IT industry and plays a significant role in the development of various industries. This field is relatively new and offers a wide range of job opportunities globally. After completing this course, one can choose to further their education with a Master's or Ph.D. degree. IIT Mandi has the best faculty-to-student ratio among all the IITs, which is 1:8. Additionally, there are teaching assistants available to help students with their problems. The faculty members are highly qualified and many of them hold positions as Associate Professors or higher at various IITs and NITs. They possess extensive knowledge in their respective fields. At IIT Mandi, the course structure typically includes two quizzes and one end-semester exam, although the exact number of assessments depends on the instructor. With consistent learning, students can score good marks in exams.IIT Mandi holds three major festivals annually: Xpecto, the technical festival, held in March; Exodia, the cultural festival, held in May; and Ran-neeti, the sports festival, held in October. The institute has well-equipped libraries on both its North and South campuses, with a vast collection of books, both in hard copy and electronic format, as well as research papers and theses from students and faculty. Classrooms are designed to provide a comfortable and productive learning environment, equipped with blackboards and smartboards and suitable for accommodating up to 300 students at a time.

There are numerous clubs at IIT Mandi, including photography, writing, dance, robotics, programming, and clubs dedicated to specific branches of engineering, sports, and more. The Student Gymkhana is the governing body responsible for overseeing the activities of these clubs. Further information can be found on the official Student Gymkhana website at IIT Mandi.

college_name: IIT Madras - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: There are two main fests one is Saarang which is a cultural fest and one is Shaastra which is a tech fest. Both are conducted in January. The highest package is 44lpa and lowest package omis 4lpa and the average package is 7lpa. About 90 % of students got placed and after getting a degree I wanted to get a job with a good package.

Environmental Geo technology is a combined course of Environmental Engineering and Geotechnical engineering. Mostly Geotech-based course.
In 2022 MTech. Batch there are 26 students and faculties are about 12 to 15.
Well-qualified teachers with highly experienced. Most teachers are actively publishing theses, articles, and projects. They are very helpful in terms of teaching as well as guidance. The teaching method is satisfactory. Mostly they do blackboard work and presentations. They bind this 2 years course full of projects, presentations, and seminars. The laboratory is also very good. And students can go and do their experiments any time. No issue with that. There will be p.hd students who will help in doing laboratory experiments.
Here one mid-term paper of 30 marks, assignments of 20 marks, and end semester of 50 marks are there. A cgpa-based system where getting passing marks is easy. The minimum required CGPA is 6.5. Below this cgpa MTech students will not get their stipend of 12,400 Rs.Here in Nit Calicut, MTech fees structure is comparatively higher than in other Nits. At the time of admission, students need to pay 1.2 lakhs. This is for one semester. And in the second-semester payable amount is nearly 80000 rs. so to sum up total course fees are about 3.6 lakhs. 
Here the hostel fee is about 9000 Rs for sharing a room and 10000 rs for a single room. The mess bill is around 34 thousand. This is flooding expenses. Academic fees are about 37 thousand rs.
  Apart from a stipend, a scholarship is also there. But if you apply for a scholarship then your stipend will be cut-off. Carrying study expenses is not that hard but students can get loans easily.
Here campus placement is very good. Btech placement is very good. Mtech placement is above 50% in our department.
The internship is also provided by the college through the gen skill portal. Here also job conversion rate is above 80%. After 1st sem internship offers to start coming.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text: The course structure is very well planned. The micro schedule and the number of lecture hours for each topic are included in the lecture plan. The faculty members-to-student ratio is good enough. The professors are well-qualified, learned and helpful to students. However, Some professor cannot deliver their knowledge properly to students. Teaching is done in hybrid mode using both a smart classroom and a blackboard. Quizzes are conducted regularly throughout the semester and they carry marks to the end semester. Exams are conducted timely and strict discipline is maintained during the exam so that no one can do cheating. Even bio breaks are not allowed during the exam.The fee structure is similar to other IITs.Even the fee of IIT ISM Dhanbad is lower than most of the IITs.The course fee per semester is around 1 lakh. The mess fee is 23 thousand and another fee is 8 thousand. So, per semester total fee is 1 lakh 33 thousand. A total Fee waiver is given to those students whose family income is less than 1 lakh per annum. Those students whose family income is upto 4 lahks, have to pay only mess fee and other charges that totals to 33 thousand only. The scholarship amount of 6 thousand was given to tuition fee waiver students. Other scholarships are also given by the institute to the SC and St students.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: The dual degree course in chemical engineering is designed very well and is ranked among top institutions for pursuing btech and mtech in chemical engineering. Faculty-student ratio is around 25:1 here in the chemical engineering department at IIT Delhi. Most of the faculties here have done PhD from foreign top universities and then pursues there career as a researcher here at IIT Delhi.IIT delhi topped in the employability rankings from India, it provides placement to almost all the students who sits for the placement seriously in the UG programs, however in the PG programs the placement stats are a bit lesser relatively. UG placement percentage is around 85 %.

moksha is the largest annual cultural fest that happens in march of every year it is among one of the biggest fests in north India. the campus is enormous the library area is well-equipped with tons of the best books, and the classrooms are also well-equipped with ac and charging ports on each desk. the sports facility is available for almost every sport. there are a lot of societies in college like ashwamedh, ares,junoon etc.the fee for the first year is 219000 and it increases by 15 percent every year there is a scholarship option also available for financially weak students some of these scholarships include mcm the details can be found on e district Delhi website

college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology - [Vel Tech]
review_text: The placements are decent. The placements Star from 7 th semester. Many companies like Capgemini, cognizant, Accenture, TCS, Deloitte, KPMG, Sasken Technologies, Odessa, virtual, PWC, etc... have visited the college. college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Placements are quite good here around 95% of the students get placed every year 
The highest package in this college is around 90 lakhs and the lower package is around 5-6 lakhs. you can easily get placed with a good package if you have good skillsFest is done every month which is quiet entertaining. campus life is amazing. the library is huge you will find a huge number of books in LIBRARY and sports activities are also great .there are also many other extracurricular activities

I chose my course which is Bachelor of Technology - Instrumentation and Control Engineering (B.Tech - ICE) because it is a well-established and reputated course of NSUT (which is also a prestigious institute in the country). The course involves the core work around electrical and electronics networks and using necessary instruments to control the behaviour of those networks. The student ratio in my class is around 68 boys and 12 girls which is decent considering the average case in Engineering colleges. 
The faculty of the college is highly qualified with many of them holding a Ph.D. degree in their respective fields from the best institutes of the country with extensive work in their research fields. They possess great knowledge about the courses they teach and will always resolve your doubts. The teaching method is specific to each teacher but all are equally effective.
We have Mid-Semester Exams and End-Semester Exams in a semester and the question paper is of high quality so it's hard.The college and the campus life at NSUT are amazing. Firstly, because of the beautiful, lush green campus that spreads over 145 acres of land. Students can enjoy their time on campus in different spots. NSUT conducts 3 annual fests: Moksha (The cultural fest, also the biggest of them), Innovision (The technical fest, which is organized alongside Moksha), and, Resonance (The winter fest, intra-college cultural fest).
The college consists of various societies and clubs ranging from one important topic to another. So, you should be able to find a society that matches your interest. These societies also frequently conduct events in which you can participate. Such events provide you with opportunities to improve your skills and grow as a person.

My college life is good in Sharda because I have met my seniors and different type of students from different fields I learned a lot of things and the sports rooms are well furnished and available for all the games. 
There are many clubs as we met many stars from India.The fee structure as the same as all of the colleges and also they provide a scholarship who have more than 85%  and also they provide a good internship and good placement which gave a good rate of interest as compared to other colleges.

There are various government schemes for scholarships including CVSPK. There are many other schemes too, you can apply for education loans too through banks. We have a training and placement cell that looks after the companies.I have had a keen interest in fashion technology since childhood. I love to design and hence I opted for this course. Mid sems and End sems are taken for theoretical subjects, and a jury judges the practical subjects.

After 4 years the thing you will miss the most will be your campus life (playing at 12 in the night, roaming the campus all night, Maggi at mic mac).
After that are the fests of DTU, Engi Fest(North India's largest cultural fest), Invictus (Tech Fest),Yuvaan (Literature and film Fest) and apart from these. There are many societies, they invite many guests for student interactions.
The infrastructure of DTU is decent you have a sports complex (huge). The best thing is the wifi all over the campus for students. Classrooms are spacious enough and projectors are also installed.
All the societies are run by the student council. There are also some tech teams for tech enthusiasts which make cars, drones, mass rovers and underwater vehicles, etc.Fees if different for every semester, every next semester is slightly more expensive than the last one. It starts from 1.2 lacs. But the difference is not very big, so the fees remain around 1.5 lacs per semester.
And it does not include the hostel and mess fee(which is around 55k -60k per year.
There are various government scholarship schemes applicable but they are on the basis of your family income. So for that, you can contact the admin department of DTU.
There is a special department of placement and training which helps you to get a job out of what you have studied.

review_text:
There are different fees for students coming from different family background. Like full tution fees concession for the student coming from family whose family income is less than 1 lakhs similarly 2/3 of tution fee concession for 1-5 lakh family income and above 5 lakhs income you have to give full fee.

Techniti is technical fest conducted in second half of year and utkansh is cultural fest held in first half of year. Most of department have great infrastructure.

And it is not that difficult to pass but the scoring top is quite difficult here.The fee structure of nit Trichy is high when compared to other nits as it is a top nit and it is to develop in its research projects. it costs around 1,20000 to 1,50000 per semester. with including tuition fees the reservation candidate can be free from tuition fees. everyone in college is to avail to apply for scholarships like nsp and ffe. The sc and st students are better to apply nsp for scholarships they can get around 40000 per year. And other category students are better to apply ffe as it is a private one it depends on the capability of their interview process which is conducted by them online. Campus placement is very good here the avg package is 15 lakhs here.

fests are something we students look forward to and are waiting for

College life is pretty decent in NSUT if we put aside the 75% attendance policy and some strictness like there are lots of societies to join for a student as per their interest. NSUT conducts the annual fest Moksha which was so famous for the greatness and fun in Delhi and societies also conduct their annual events like Tech week, ethnic day, and all. The library of NSUT is pretty big and well ordinated, you will find all kinds of books and journals related to your interest and related to your course/branch. The amenities in the classroom for a first-year student are fabulous, for first-year students, their classes are held at APJ Halls and they are really huge and well advanced but after the completion of the first year, they shift to their department buildings that are a regular class like wooden desks and a blackboard / green board. There are lots of opportunities for sports and extracurricular activities on campus if you're interested and willing to do. There are lots of societies to join.I chose the course of CSE specialization in mathematics and computing because I want a CSE-related branch because all companies allow CSE-related branches to sit in their interview during placements session and I filled out the choice filling sheet according to the cut-off of the university and according to the rank of mine in JEE Mains I got the CSE'MAC branch. There is 1 faculty (professor) for the 89 student strength in each subject in my branch. The qualifications of the faculty are high, they are highly educated and trained, their teaching method is also very helpful and nice but there are always some exceptions are there. The term exams happen timely, the class tests happen regularly in the NSUT and the difficulty level of the term exams is moderate not to hard not to easy if you studied for it.

Companies like Microsoft and adobe come for internships.

Nsut has a strong culture of student activities.
Numerous societies are active on the campus that aim to inculcate team values & nurture talent within students. These clubs attract students from all walks of life, cutting across departments. This gives students a chance to experiment with their interests and hobbies.A student studying at the university needs to apply for a grant or scholarship by filling out a form TIS-I (Annexure 'A'). Bright Students seeking admission to the university need to fill out the form TIS-II (Annexure 'A') to avail of the incentives for taking admission to the university.

Fests are literally the best part of this college. Moksha is the largest cultural fest of Delhi organized in around March _ and April. And Innovation tech week is the technical fest conducted nearly a bit before moksha. Central Library is well equipped with almost every genre of books. Classrooms are very modernised with state of art infrastructure digital classrooms, cinema hall-like seating arrangements etc. Name any sport and you will find someone practising it in the sports complex and sports grounds. There is a no. Of clubs run by University students.CSAI is the premiere course of the university. You will get the most top-minded student on the entire campus in your class. Also, AI has got a very bright future in the coming years. The student-faculty ratio is nearly about 8:1.
The faculty is highly qualified, having deep knowledge of the subjects. Exams are kind of moderate, you need to prepare at least 1 week before exams in order to get a good grade.

The annual fest is Moksha held in Fe

Books can be taken and studied at the library and can also be borrowed.

college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI]
review_text:
Life on our campus is very good. We have an evergreen campus with an incredible infrastructure of labs, a Library, and a classroom. Sports on our campus are pretty good, we can choose various sports in our college like cricket, football, basketball, badminton, etc. We also have various clubs which are run by students like photography club, debate club, etcI choose this btech course because it enables me to explore various expectations of life. The faculty in our college is very good.
We have around 1 teaching faculty for around every 10 students. They teach us very well. We have mainly 2 exams mid sem and end sem exams and they are pretty easy to pass

Campus placements generally take place in the last year. There are over 200 companies that come to the college for placements, such as Amazon, Flipkart, Google, etc. The highest package was Rs. 1.25 Cr which was recorded in the year 2021-2022. However, the average package is Rs.10-15 lakhs. The percentage of students getting placements in the core branch is 85%, while in the tech branch, it is 100%. I have always wanted to be an entrepreneur, so, after getting the degree, I am thinking of creating my own company related to automation.The fests are one of the best parts of one's college life. The annual fest Moksha, which is held in March every year, is quite huge. Over 50,000 people attend this. There is a vast variety of books available in the library. The classrooms are also very spacious with comfortable benches and good lighting. There are a lot of extracurricular activities such as dance, football, basketball, and so on. There are many societies such as Ashwamedh, Crescendo, and Junoon for acting, music, and photography respectively.

There Is Great Employment With High paid Jobs in IT SECTOR SO I CHOOSE CSAI BRANCH in NSUT, NEW DELHI. Faculty Student Ratio is 1:90. Faculty are well Qualified and all have done Ph.D. OR MSC OR DONE BTECH IN IITS with huge Experience. It is very easy to pass the Term Exam If You study Daily and Effectively Hour a Day.Many Companies offer Internships with high paid amounts. Sprinkler Offers an 80,000 stipend per month. If you are in the CSE branch Then you have an 80% chance you will get an internship. College is branch specific. Coding is Must

Life is amazing on campus. Moksha the annual fest is one of the biggest college fests in Delhi and it is generally conducted in the month of March. Most academic books are available in the library and you may find several journals as well. All the necessary amenities like charger sockets, wifi, air conditioner, and comfortable seating benches are available in classrooms, at least in the first year. A huge sports campus is available at the campus which contains areas for many sports to play like football, basketball, volleyball, badminton, cricket, table tennis, lawn tennis, kabaddi, etc. Many societies such as the coding society(IEEE), robotics Society (ARES), and entrepreneurship soc(E-Cell) are very much active in the college.I am interested in tech placements and despite it being a core branch, I chose ICE as I have heard about students from ICE  in the past, cracking handsome tech placements in terms of salary and perks. The faculty-student ratio is decent. Faculties are very much qualified and knowledgeable and adopt great teaching methods to explain concepts to students. In each semester, two exams are held - mid-sem exams and end-sem exams. Exams are quite easy to crack and one can achieve very good marks just by studying 2 weeks before the exams.

Our course is superb. It is one of the easiest in the whole college where we can secure the highest CGPA in the college which in turn helps us to secure a good placement season. The faculty here is nice and helpful. THEY help us in some instances but are strict in case of paper checking. It is quite nice.The fee structure here is quite good. It increases by 10% every year. The scholarship here is not up to fine if you are in the top 5% in the first sem then you get your whole fees to return. But they take a lot of rounds for doing it and sometimes they just ignore us.

The fee for the whole 4-year course is nearly 10 lacs. The yearly fees would be around 2.25-2.50 lacs. Students can avail of scholarships through their merit and eligibility criteria. The students are eligible for placements after the 6th semester. Almost 90% of students get placed.Moksha is the largest fest that is the key highlight of the college. The college is well equipped with sports facilities including indoor as well as outdoor sports. The college also has a gym facility, and a library with almost all kinds of required books available.

Moksha-innovation is the annual fest and along with that we also have Resonanz. Moksha - innovation is held around march and resonant is held around October. The library is very well maintained and is stocked with books that are relevant. Classrooms are top-notch. There are many sports events that take place like winter sports and annual sports meets.I have always been interested in design and that made me want to apply for design here. The faculty-student ratio is good. Roughly one teacher per 30 students. The faculties are well qualified and experienced. Terms exams and internals are held on time

As I came to this college because of business, so I have taken this course to upgrade myself and my business skills. And the faculty staff here are very nice and There are only 42 students in our batch, our course name is BBA iev which means BBA in innovation and entrepreneurship venture. This course has come recently in our college. Most of our teachers have done Ph.D. And the method of teaching by the teachers is also very interesting and easy. Our exams are not over yet but will start in March.Moksha and resonanz these two fests are held in our college, one in March and one in October. At present, my syllabus mostly consists of 12th-class topics and a few practicals, so I don't need to go to the library, so I don't have that much idea. Our college has societies for every activity like a dancing society music society dramatic society and I am giving auditions for all I like the society named Ashwamedh very much which is the name of the Dramatic Society.

The curriculum is pretty decent, you do not have to waste huge amounts of time and energy trying to cover your syllabus for the exams in fact the spread-out schedule of our tests is good enough to help us cover everything so that we do not have to ramble around in the last moments. The professors and teaching staff is amazing and very helpful and the infrastructure is beyond compare.I really like my college life here, to be honest, the idea of the ideal college life is what helped me through high school and once I got to nsut it felt like it was everything I ever wanted. the people the societies the vibe the culture is just so immaculate.

review_text:
Life on our campus is very good. We have an evergreen campus with an incredible infrastructure of labs, a Library, and a classroom. Sports on our campus are pretty good, we can choose various sports in our college like cricket, football, basketball, badminton, etc. We also have various clubs which are run by students like photography club, debate club, etcI choose this btech course because it enables me to explore various expectations of life. The faculty in our college is very good.
We have around 1 teaching faculty for around every 10 students. They teach us very well. We have mainly 2 exams mid sem and end sem exams and they are pretty easy to pass

Campus placements generally take place in the last year. There are over 200 companies that come to the college for placements, such as Amazon, Flipkart, Google, etc. The highest package was Rs. 1.25 Cr which was recorded in the year 2021-2022. However, the average package is Rs.10-15 lakhs. The percentage of students getting placements in the core branch is 85%, while in the tech branch, it is 100%. I have always wanted to be an entrepreneur, so, after getting the degree, I am thinking of creating my own company related to automation.The fests are one of the best parts of one's college life. The annual fest Moksha, which is held in March every year, is quite huge. Over 50,000 people attend this. There is a vast variety of books available in the library. The classrooms are also very spacious with comfortable benches and good lighting. There are a lot of extracurricular activities such as dance, football, basketball, and so on. There are many societies such as Ashwamedh, Crescendo, and Junoon for acting, music, and photography respectively.

There Is Great Employment With High paid Jobs in IT SECTOR SO I CHOOSE CSAI BRANCH in NSUT, NEW DELHI. Faculty Student Ratio is 1:90. Faculty are well Qualified and all have done Ph.D. OR MSC OR DONE BTECH IN IITS with huge Experience. It is very easy to pass the Term Exam If You study Daily and Effectively Hour a Day.Many Companies offer Internships with high paid amounts. Sprinkler Offers an 80,000 stipend per month. If you are in the CSE branch Then you have an 80% chance you will get an internship. College is branch specific. Coding is Must

Life is amazing on campus. Moksha the annual fest is one of the biggest college fests in Delhi and it is generally conducted in the month of March. Most academic books are available in the library and you may find several journals as well. All the necessary amenities like charger sockets, wifi, air conditioner, and comfortable seating benches are available in classrooms, at least in the first year. A huge sports campus is available at the campus which contains areas for many sports to play like football, basketball, volleyball, badminton, cricket, table tennis, lawn tennis, kabaddi, etc. Many societies such as the coding society(IEEE), robotics Society (ARES), and entrepreneurship soc(E-Cell) are very much active in the college.I am interested in tech placements and despite it being a core branch, I chose ICE as I have heard about students from ICE  in the past, cracking handsome tech placements in terms of salary and perks. The faculty-student ratio is decent. Faculties are very much qualified and knowledgeable and adopt great teaching methods to explain concepts to students. In each semester, two exams are held - mid-sem exams and end-sem exams. Exams are quite easy to crack and one can achieve very good marks just by studying 2 weeks before the exams.

Our course is superb. It is one of the easiest in the whole college where we can secure the highest CGPA in the college which in turn helps us to secure a good placement season. The faculty here is nice and helpful. THEY help us in some instances but are strict in case of paper checking. It is quite nice.The fee structure here is quite good. It increases by 10% every year. The scholarship here is not up to fine if you are in the top 5% in the first sem then you get your whole fees to return. But they take a lot of rounds for doing it and sometimes they just ignore us.

The fee for the whole 4-year course is nearly 10 lacs. The yearly fees would be around 2.25-2.50 lacs. Students can avail of scholarships through their merit and eligibility criteria. The students are eligible for placements after the 6th semester. Almost 90% of students get placed.Moksha is the largest fest that is the key highlight of the college. The college is well equipped with sports facilities including indoor as well as outdoor sports. The college also has a gym facility, and a library with almost all kinds of required books available.

Moksha-innovation is the annual fest and along with that we also have Resonanz. Moksha - innovation is held around march and resonant is held around October. The library is very well maintained and is stocked with books that are relevant. Classrooms are top-notch. There are many sports events that take place like winter sports and annual sports meets.I have always been interested in design and that made me want to apply for design here. The faculty-student ratio is good. Roughly one teacher per 30 students. The faculties are well qualified and experienced. Terms exams and internals are held on time

As I came to this college because of business, so I have taken this course to upgrade myself and my business skills. And the faculty staff here are very nice and There are only 42 students in our batch, our course name is BBA iev which means BBA in innovation and entrepreneurship venture. This course has come recently in our college. Most of our teachers have done Ph.D. And the method of teaching by the teachers is also very interesting and easy. Our exams are not over yet but will start in March.Moksha and resonanz these two fests are held in our college, one in March and one in October. At present, my syllabus mostly consists of 12th-class topics and a few practicals, so I don't need to go to the library, so I don't have that much idea. Our college has societies for every activity like a dancing society music society dramatic society and I am giving auditions for all I like the society named Ashwamedh very much which is the name of the Dramatic Society.

MOKSHA is our annual cultural fest, whereas INOVISION is our tech fest, MOKSHA is conducted in the month of March.
Usually, students prefer soft copies i.e pdf forms of books and youtube videos although the college has its own central library, which has enough space and well-maintained books covering vast topics and fields. The college has its own cricket ground, football ground, athletics track, kabbadi ground, volley ball court, badminton court, TT rooms,  Gym, and other essential facilities for various other sports.In 7 semesters every student is eligible to appear for the placement drive. Companies start visiting NSUT for giving placements in various profiles from the month of August. Depending upon companies' requirements students of different branches are allowed, to keep a certain filtration of overall CGPA till the 6th semester. At the end of the placement drive, 70-80% of students get placement, where the average package is approximately 15 LPA for the Software Developer profile.

There are numerous societies, and club groups that are run by students.I chose this course because the design is my passion. The faculty-to-student ratio is fairly nice. Most faculty members are highly qualified and doing great in their own professional lives. There are two semesters with mid-sems and end-sems. Class tests are held before each sem. Term exams and internals are held on time

Life at the campus is amazing, there are places to explore where you want to be in life. The social culture of NSUT helps everyone grow into a better person. The library is full of books with helpful staff but the timings of the library are restricted which I believe can be changed. The sports culture is active enough to get engaged in it.The fee structure of the university is not that good. It is quite expensive but the government administrative process is good that helps students who're facing a financial crisis. 
There are various scholarships in our university for such students as CVSPK.

Campus Life is good. The campus is really big and green and the infrastructure is modern. The annual fest is moksha which is held in March. Diverse books are present in the library. Modern sports facilities are present and there is a number of societies to pursue your Passion.This college course was allocated to me at this rank. The faculty-student ratio is good. Faculties are qualified from prestigious colleges and are PhD holders so they have good knowledge in their field. Term exams are difficult they are on time.

Fees is quite high. Being a state funded college but it's fees is quite high. 219000 in first year with 10% increase in each year. Schlorships are provided by delhi government through edistrict. Cost of study and living is quite high but placemenys are too good.I choose the course with my interest. All Faculties are experienced and well educated. In a year two exams are their mid sem and end sems. Also, a class test takes place. The academics are good and the teachers are friendly and the labs are also quite good.

I have chosen this course because in childhood I have an interest in a mechanical department that's why I choose btech. The qualifications of faculty are high as you expect. To do btech degree first of all you will choose science and after that give jee mains exam and if you score good percentile then you got a good college for btech. Yes, btech is a little bit difficult. but if you do it properly then it is notIn Delhi jac college there are the fees of colleges is very high approx 2 lakh per year.but as per government they gives us a scholarship as a stipend. So the scholarship available for all categories genral, obc ,sc and st. Therefore there are some scholarship name is merit cum , post matric etc

A total number of 69 students in my class and the faculty are very helpful they give their helping hands in every situation.The library is very big and full of books with fully furnished computers and very hygienic also.
There are many types of fest organised in multiple months.
Classrooms are very big classrooms and fully furnished with projectors in each.

I am studying Computer Engineering at NSUT. The faculty in this department are pretty great and have vast knowledge in the field. They are great mentors doing research and projects. Exams in NSUT may sometime become a headache in the initial semesters as you may face tests and assignments every 2 weeks.The college library provides a wide range of books for academic purposes. NSUT proudly hosts 2 fests in an annual year. First is the inter-college fest - Resonanz and second is the intracultural college fest - Moksha which attracts more than 15,000 people every day and features many well know artists.

The campus Life is awesome. A big and beautiful campus with lush greenery and sports infrastructure. The campus is so big that you cannot see it all on foot. Some parts of the college have ac classrooms with projectors but the others don't. The are various societies handled by students themselves.The fees have now become a bit high if we consider the government college aspect but overall still less than private colleges. The fees add up if you avail hostel. The college provides financial assistance for those who have low family income.

Not so many companies are visited for internships. Since the implementation of the New Education Policy completing an Internship in every alternative semester is compulsory. But college not helping much with internships.
Fee Structure: Approx. 2.5 lakhs/year. Late fine charges are so high and strict guidelines for fee payment on time. The college didn't focus on its scholarship program. Mainly they consider National, State, and other private scholarships.The faculty of ECE is so chill. 
I opted for ECE because its covers smart electronic gadgets and almost every faculty of the ECE branch in DSCE are chill. In DSCE you have 3 internals ( monthly exams) and 1 semester-end examination. Besides it, Assignment submissions, Quizzes, and labs exam are also there.
Class Size : 70 and Course Fees: INR 1000000 per year.

I chose this particular course because this was the only course available at my jee rank and it was aligning with my interest and faculty ratio is 1:10, 
Teachers are highly qualified to have experience of more than 20 years.
This college is very particular about its exam regime and this process is quite hectic.
Exams are severely difficult to pass.Being a government-owned college the cost of studying at NSUT is very low compared to the private colleges offering the same course.
The ROI of fee vs placement is pretty good.
The packages range from 6LPA to 85PLA.
We have companies including FAANG to the BIG4 approaching us with internships and placement opportunities.

Every year we will have two mega events they are VITOPIA which is a cultural fest event and VITTAP a Technical events
and every Saturday we will have events that are conducted by college clubs. 
We will have an indoor court for badminton, and chess, caroms, etc. we can play in the indoor stadium, and a basketball court and volleyball and cricket ground  we can theme morning from 5 am to 10 am and evening from 4am to 7 am every day 
There will be a lot of books in the library and we can also access online eBooks. 
In university we will have different,  we will different clubs which are run by students there are around 80 clubs.I am interested in Computer Science so I have chosen the BTECH CSE as my course. 
There are highly qualified teachers for every 70 students each teacher per subject.
Qualification of the faculty is many are completed their education the NITs, IITs, and top universities.
In every sem, we have Two mid-exams and a Sem exam we have 40% total for the mid and final exams, they we get to pass the subject.

college_name: The LNM Institute of Information Technology - [LNMIIT]
review_text:
I choose this course to learn from the best of the best. The faculty-student ratio is around 1:40.
Faculties are well qualified and they have done their post-graduation and phd from reputed institutions like IIT.
The exam used to happen twice in a particular semester. The first one happens in the mid of the semester and they are called as mid sem exams and the last one happens are end sem exams which happen at the end of the semester. In between, there are quizzes and assignments also.
If you will study seriously and during your entire semester then you can easily score good marks.Social life is very good on campus. There are a lot of extracurricular activities that happen in college so different clubs are made for singing, dancing, drama, etc. A lot of sports activities also take place, library facilities are also good and a lot of good books to read.

Choose this course out of interest. The faculty is professional and very supportive except few who are usually from other departments. All the professors are Ph.D. holders, if not at least a master's holder with distinction. If studied and attended classes well the exams were easy, else it may not seem the same for others.The library is present in every department building and a central library which is open for all 24*7. The latest books weren't available, but can be accessed online via the computer system set up in the library. Sports are for professionals unlike for other students.

college_name: International Institute of Information Technology - [IIIT] Naya Raipur
review_text:
The campus life is quite good, our college has almost everything that is required for Engineering students. We have one of the biggest techno-cultural fests in India named Technovate it will be held for three days. We have cultural events almost every month. We have a good library facility
which will be open till 9 pm and we even have E-Library. We have full Wi-Fi in the ed Academic block and hostels were provided with ethernet so don't forget to bring ethernet cables. We have an annual sports meet named Aarambh. We have various technical(AIML, Cipercell, Comet Tsoc, E-cell, etc) and cultural clubs(Igniters, Capriccio, Take-the-bait, etc). We have almost all the sports facilities(TT, football, Badminton, Cricket, Volleyball, Basketball, etc).The main reason for which i chose these college was for its advanced curriculum, you need not to study any not required subjects ,you will have only technical subjects which makes you ready for industry.Our Director is from University of Tokyo.The faculty here were well qualified from India's best Institutions like IIT's and NIT's. They will be available to guide you almost at all times, they will be friendly, and right from the first semester, you will travel with them for making projects. You can maintain a decent CGPA if just follow teachers' classes so try to maintain good attendance.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
Annual Cultural Fest- Thomso ( conducted in October)
Annual Technical Fest- Cognizance (conducted in march)
There is MGCL (Mahatma Gandhi Central Library) which has 3 stories and one underground floor which has many books and journals. There are many monitors also and best in class internet facility so you can read online also.
Classrooms have 4 AC in one room and many fans, a projector, a smartboard, a blackboard, and sofa seats and benches.
There is almost every sport available on campus be it cricket, hockey, badminton, football, volleyball, swimming, basketball, squash, or athletics. Every sport has its own court/ground.
Besides these all outdoor games, almost all indoor games are available like pool, carrom, chess, foosball, and table tennis.
Extracurricular activities include sports, gymkhana, and participating in many clubs.
There are many clubs in campus. Software group, finance group, choreo group, robotics group, poetic group, and many more.I chose this course because, at my rank in JEE, I was getting this course only. I chose IIT Roorkee and not the branch.
Faculties are 25 and students 75 so the ratio is 1:3
Education is top-notch. There are lecture classes held offline in the lecture hall complex with smart class facilities. Weekly tutorials are also held in which students have to do assignments or presentations. 
There are practicals also held in some of the course in which we have to perform experiments in the respective labs.
There are 2 exams per semester for each subject- mid-term exam(MTE) and end-term exam (ETE). MTE carries 40 percent weightage and ETE carries 60 percent.
It is easy to pass the exam if one starts studying seriously one week before exams.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
There are 3big fest are organized in our institute "Kashiyatra" is the cultural fest of IIT BHU  conducted in the month of January and "Technex" is the technical fest conducted in the month of March. Many celebrities are invited to perform and guests for think talk in both fest. There is also one Sports fest "Spardha" conducted in the month of October. 
About books and resources, we have a library which has a lot of books, journals,  magazines and also an AC study hall.
There are many clubs and councils in our institute. Students choose according to their interests and continue for years.  Council related to photography, drama, Art, Astrology, Quiz, Sports, Modeling, Music, Dance everything is there. 
Apart from this, there is a social service council that has some clubs under it. Also, our students teach some poor students and celebrate every festival with them through "Gyan Udyaan" and "Sahyog club" and also donate old books for those needy students.I choose this course because IIT BHU has a very vast alumni network and mining is the oldest branch of this institution.  The placement offer and campus are very beautiful. Also, the campus is in the heart of Varanasi which is called baba ki Nagri.
IIT has a very talented proff. To take classes, apart from class there is a tutorial by Ph.D. students for b tech students. For girls, it's very good that nowadays reservation for girls has increased and many branches like mining and metallurgy also take admission of girls students. Also in our college in mining department, there is a scheme named the "   Sundora Banarjee Scholarship "
 through which every year one girl with the highest marks gets awarded by 3000/month.
About the exam there are 2 main exams mid sem and endsem in one-semester .apart from the exam quiz, assignments are held timely. For class, there are many lecture theatres to attend classes. Proffs are highly qualified and experienced.

life at dtu campus can be quite dynamic offering a mix of academic rigor and opportunities for social interaction and engagement in terms of social life there are many student clubs and organizations to get involved from sports clubs to cultural organizations as well as frequent events and parties. this provides students with the opportunities to make new friends meet people from diverse backgrounds and have a balanced university experience.delhi technology university has a good reputation for its placement record. the university has a dedicated training and placement cell that assists students in finding employment opportunities. in recent years,  dtu has seen a high placement rate, with many top companies visiting the campus fo recruitment. the university campus for recruitment. the university has strong ties with various industries, including all others.

Fests are held during February and March and consist of cultural and tech fests. Books are provided by the college itself and included in tuition fees. Classrooms are modern with AC, Whiteboard, Smartboard, Cameras, Speakers and Plug Points to charge laptops.
A lot of stadiums and grounds are built for different sports and sports participation is thoroughly encouraged and appreciated. A lot of clubs in various technical and non-technical domains operate too.Students are eligible to sit for placement from the 7th semester and internships from the 6th semester. Adobe, Microsoft, IBM, AMD, NVIDIA, Google and a lot of other MNVs visit each year and make multiple offers. The highest placement has been 52lakh/annum CTC and the average package is 4lakh/annum. college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text:
IIT Mandi is known for its strong curriculum and dedicated faculty members. The institute offers undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral programs in various fields of engineering, science, and humanities. The curriculum is designed to provide students with a strong foundation in the relevant subject matter, as well as hands-on experience through lab work, projects, and internships.
The faculty-student ratio at IIT Mandi is typically around 1:10, which means there is a good balance of faculty members available to provide students with individualized attention and support.
The faculty members at IIT Mandi are highly qualified and experienced, with many holding advanced degrees such as PhDs from top institutions. The institute has a strong commitment to academic excellence, and as such, the faculty is composed of experts in their respective fields who are dedicated to providing students with a rigorous and engaging educational experience.The students become eligible for campus placements after the 6th sem. The companies visited are Google, Microsoft, Oracle, Amazon and Hdfc, etc. The highest CTC offered is 62lpa and the average is 19 LPA. The percentage of students getting placed depends on the branches in cse approx 100% of students get placed and in the dse 95% of students get placed and in the electrical 85% of students get placed.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
Placements start from the 7th semester and go till the 8th semester. Many companies visit the campus for placements, some of the key recruiters include Reliance Jio, Optum, Oracle, Morgan Stanley, Google, Microsoft, Adobe, Tower Research Capital, GreyB Research, Procol, Futurefirst, Inframarket, Biz2Credit, etc. 
220+ companies visited the campus in the year 2022 and more than 1400 offers were made.
The highest package was 1.15Cr by Amazon EMEA and the average package was 11.01LPA. Generally these companies come for Software profiles or Data analysts. But there are other companies as well which come for the ECE and Business An, analyst domains. Above 99% of placements were recorded in the year 2022.
My plan as of now is to work in the corporate sector and if it goes well then will look for other options as well.There are separate fests held at both campuses of JIIT. Converge being the biggest fest of Sector-128 is organized around the month of November. At sector 62 Impressions is organized which is again a big party held annually. The college has a huge library on two floors containing books in every section. Classrooms are well equipped with AC and smart class. Sports at Sector 62 are not up to the mark but yes there are good opportunities at Sector 128.
There are a lot of clubs and societies at the college which are really helpful for personal development. For eg. Knuth (Programming Hub), Nrityang (Dance), Surkhaab (Bhangra), Crescendo (Music), MuCR (Electronics), etc.

review_text: Life of the IIT campus is the most awesome and chill life. With a total campus area of 2100 acres, it feels like a small town. review_text:

Life at VIT is good. The campus infrastructure is great, and there are a lot of good people in-campus as well. VIT hosts an annual fest called VITOPIA, which usually takes place in the month of April, and a tech fest called VTAPP, which takes place around November-December. They're usually very huge fests and fetch a lot of attention. The libraries are vast, with loads of on-topic and out of topic books available to rent and return. All classrooms are equipped with projectors and whiteboards, and almost all classrooms seat 70-80 students, other than the gallery classrooms, which seat 150 students. There are a lot of extra curricular activites on the campus. Besides a massive football court and cricket field, there are two basketball courts, a tennis court, a volleyball court, a badminton court, and a squash room. Coaches come regularly to teach arts like karate and boxing too. There are several clubs in here as well, and opting for a technical club (like CSI or ACM) gives you credits.The fees are divided into three sections- Hostel accommodation, Tuition fees, and a caution deposit. Hostel accommodation is always optional, and students can opt to stay outside the hostel in the nearing city as well. Tuition fees entirely depends on the course you have opted for, and also the category you have attained based on the competitive examination that you have written in order to get into the college. For instance, for BTECH-CSE core, a student will have to pay 1.5 lakhs per annum over a period of 4 years in college through category 1, 2.5 lakhs for category 2, 3.5 lakhs for cat 3 and so on. college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text:
Our college organizes the largest fest in North India known as PECFEST. Fest can be conducted either in the month of April or in October. Our Library has a collection of more than 1.5 lacs books. You can easily get a book issued if required on the basis of the availability of books. The classrooms are quite big and fully air-conditioned. Smart class facilities are also available in the classrooms. Talking about sports facilities college has an athletic ground, football ground, gymnasium, badminton court, cricket ground, basketball court, lawn tennis court, and also various indoor games equipment are also available inside the hostel.
There are various clubs for various activities such as coding, robotics, painting debating, quizzing, etc. You can be part of various clubs according to your interest.Fees for the BTECH program in this college are a bit higher than in other government engineering colleges. It is written in the college prospectus that fees can be increased up to 10 percent every year. Mostly students belonging to SC or ST categories can easily avail of fee waivers based on financial income certificates. For students belonging to the general category it is quite tough to get a fee waiver. Approx 50 percent of students got an internship in their third year of the program with an average stipend of approx 15-20 K and an average placement of 15 lacs per annum in the fourth year.

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text:

For sports, the college also has a sports complex with 8 badminton courts and 4 table tennis courts, squash, gym, synthetic running track, football ground, cricket ground, tennis, basketball, carrom, billiards, Kabbadi and moreAI and Data science is a growing field and IIT always provides you with tremendous opportunities so it was an easy decision to take this branch at iit.

Here continuous evaluation has a major chunk of grades so it reduces stress on the last days of the exam as the final exam carries only about 30-4%.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:

The highest packages are also given by these firms. In 2022, the highest package was 1.15Cr from Amazon. The average package was 11lpa.Life on the campus is pretty good and lively. Recently we just had our annual fest, Impressions'23. The 128 campuses have their own annual fest named Converge. The fests are conducted in the month of February or March. Tuition fee:80400 per sem,

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text: There are a lot of clubs at IIT. Students can do any extra curriculum activities in which they are interested. One of the largest fests is conducted in IITD called RDV every year. There are many fests conducted the whole year i.e. Tryst, Pravriti, Becon, Kaizen, I-connect, etc. Rendevous is a cultural fest and Tryst is a technical fest both conducted in the month of Feb-march. There is the central library which is 24x7 open and there is a huge collection of books where we can get all books, there is another library also in SAC where we can get books other than academics like manga and comics and others. Lecture halls are too big where at a time 300+ can sit and there are tut classes also for tutorial classes. There is a couch for every sport. There are grounds for every sport and even every hostel has a separate sports room and ground. There are many social groups run by students of NSS. Clubs also run by students.I choose Electrical Engineering because I'm interested in Electric Department and it is a circuital branch of one of the top most IIT. At IIT, we get the best faculty from all over the world. There is TA for every group of 30 students and a personal acad mentor. Profs are too good and very experienced most of them had a lot of research in their life. As they are experienced their teaching method is better than any other college. We have 2 minors and one major per sem. They are quite tough one should have to study regularly to pass the exam. Discipline in IIT is too strict no one can practice unfair means in exams otherwise they got failed that course. Here things are more practical than theory because they believe in making us used to the real world. We have labs regularly and we have to do experiments properly.

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text: We have three main fests called Concours, I.Fest and Synapse in which concours is a sports festival, I. Fest is Gujarat's largest technical fest and Synapse is our techno-cultural fest. Concours, I.Fest and Synapse were conducted respectively in November, December and March. Very good availability of books in Library. There are 23+ clubs here most of which are run by students i.e. IEEE student branch, cultural committee, student body government called SBG, Drama Theatre Group called DTG, DADC for dance, music club, sports committee etc.In the 4th year, students become eligible for placement but we have got facilities for internship eligibility from the 5th sem and if possible companies give us pre-placement offer also. We recorded 100% placement here there are many companies visiting here i.e. Google, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, Sprinkler, Atlassian etc. The highest package we got was 82 LPA for two students placed in Atlassian Australia-based company and the average package is around 20 LPA.

Total fees are 2,19,000 rupees in total including tuition fees which is 1,44,000 and various other parts like medical assistance, minority section relief donation and such. There are various scholarship opportunities if you are in need of it with enough low income. DTU concession (tuition fees return ), merit cum means (100%/50%/25% tuition fees according to condition ), post  matric scholarship ( for  SC/ST students only & 100 per cent fees return )Except for one or two professors most of the staff is top-notch and have a lot of knowledge. There are different experts in different fields. So they cover a vast area of knowledge.  Most assistant professors are relatively young from their late 20s to early 30s and others are above 40-50. Lectures are relevant to the placement but not enough practice. You have to learn everything and apply it in real on your own.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
I chose ENC because It gives me a peek into both the Electronics core and CS domains.
The student-teacher ratio is such that in the first year as subjects are common there are 250 students with one teacher
but in second-year in classes we have 120 students on  1 teacher further class is divided in to sections with each 25 having a personal tutorial and lab teacher.

The faculty are the best and have all done their PHDs from Thapar, IITs or Foreign universities.

The exams are divided such that you have sufficient time to prepare for each.
Here almost no students are failedThapar has its library nava nalanda ranked 7th among the most stunning libraries in the world.
for books all sorts of courses and fun books are available.
The classrooms all are equipped with projectors and Lab is equipped with ACs.
For sports, we have 4 basketball courts 1 swimming pool, many badminton courts(10+), a cricket ground athletic track and sports equipment available for lending. There are more than 20 clubs and societies focussing on technical and non-tech work.

The course curriculum includes all the basic subjects like english, engineering mathematics, engineering physics , basic electrcial engineering, basic electronics, python programming , c programming , special subjects like php and ai based on your stream. These will be provided subjects for you in your first year.
I chose AI as my course to pursue because i am really interested in this field and wanted to learn more here.

The faculy student ratio here is quite good. I would say there would be 1:70 ratio and there isn't any issue for teachers. Yes sometimes in first year , there are issues when new teachers join and they cant match up with students properly.
Most of the faculty in my first year. has done phd. Or mtech. From good colleges like NIT or IITS. There are lectures and tutorials to solve problems as well. You will be taught in lectures and you will be given sheets which you can solve and ask doubts in tutorials.

The exams here are semester wise and it wasnt very hard to pass.The placement provided by college is basic and depends solely on your skills.
 Students can start looking for placement from 6th semester or third year.
There are many great companies visisted here like google , infosys , etc. Around more than 600 recruiters came this year.

The highest packagae was of 44lpa in Microsoft. The average packagae here woulf be around 5-7 lacs and it depends on your stream as well.

college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
I had to go for UPSC civil services exam. So this course seemed to be the most appropriate one for it.
Faculty to student ratio was really great. It is not fixed. It rather depends upon the course you are pursuing. Could be anything like 30 to 600 students. Usually, 120-150 students are in a class. There are about 850 faculty members.
Most of the faculty have some higher education from renowned foreign institutes. About 40% teach really nicely. 30% acceptable. Rest is just to make you fall asleep in class.
Exams are held at regular intervals. Passing is not a big deal. 1 day before the exam is sufficient. However, getting a good grade is really hard.Rendezvous is the annual fest conducted on 9-12 March 2023. Tryst is the tech fest conducted on 6-9 March 2023. Dates vary every year. Usually, from Feb to April, these events are carried out.
Our library is one of the biggest in India. You can ask them to avail a book if it's not available. 
Classrooms are simply world-class. They have projectors, biometric attendance and everything you can ask for.
The sports facilities are really great. There is a sports complex and grounds for various sports.
Most of the work here is done by students. Both the fests are organised by students.

review_text: Life on campus is very happening. Yearly many fests are conducted by different student bodies on the campus.
