College life entirely depends on what you do with your life in college. LPU will give you all you want for a successful career and the skills thereof. But won't give you enough time to waste on non-productive and unnecessary things. You get tons of organizations to join, and a large number of events to participate in, organist, and enjoy. A large number of leisurely places, be it the central vista, Mall or playgrounds or gyms or swimming pools or indoor stadiums or skating rinks, or nets to practice. But it does not compromise in any case with your safety, your studies, your skill attainment, your employability, and finally your placements. Whatever the duration of your program, your campus life will depend on what YOU do with it. If you are sincere about your studies or any other activity, it will be great. The university charges a fee from your parents to enable a great career for you, and that's what LPU does best.Usually, the university collaborates with various reputed companies to offer internships to its students. Some of the companies that have offered internships in the past include Amazon, Google, Microsoft, TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, etc. The roles offered under internships may vary depending on the company's requirements and project needs. During the internships, students get to work on real-life projects that help them gain practical experience and industry exposure. The projects assigned to the students are carefully designed to match their skill set and learning objectives. The internships usually come with a stipend, and the amount may vary depending on the company and the duration of the internship. The internship opportunities provided by Lovely Professional University help students to develop their skills and get hands-on experience that can boost their career prospects.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology (TIET) Patiala is a private institution, and the fee structure varies depending on the program and specialization. Here is some information about the fee structure, financial assistance, and campus jobs at Thapar:
The fee structure at Thapar is on the higher side compared to other engineering colleges in India. The approximate fee structure for the CSE program is around Rs. 3-4 lakhs per year. The fee includes tuition fees, hostel fees, mess fees, and other miscellaneous charges.
Thapar provides various scholarships and financial assistance to eligible students. Some of the scholarships include merit-based scholarships, need-based scholarships, and scholarships for students from economically weaker sections. Students can apply for these scholarships during the admission process or after joining the institution.
Thapar has a dedicated Career Development Center that provides assistance and scholarships.TIET has a dedicated placement cell that works closely with the industry to provide internship and job opportunities to students. The placement process involves pre-placement talks, aptitude tests, group discussions, and personal interviews.
Students become eligible for campus placements from the seventh semester of their B.Tech program, and the placement cell provides ample training and guidance to prepare them for the recruitment process.
TIET has a robust placement network, with top companies from various sectors such as IT, consulting, manufacturing, and finance visiting the campus for recruitment. Some of the prominent recruiters at TIET include Amazon, Microsoft, Deloitte, Samsung, Oracle, L&T, and Mahindra. The highest package offered at TIET is around 43 lakhs per annum, and the average package offered is around 11 lakhs per annum.
TIET has an excellent placement record, with over 90%  of eligible students getting placed every year.

college_name: IIT Goa - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: The annual fest is cultural and technical fest is Cepheus. The library at IIT Goa is well-stocked with books, journals, and other resources. The library has a collection of over 10,000 books and subscribes to several online databases, providing access to thousands of research papers and journals. The classrooms at IIT Goa are well-equipped with modern amenities such as projectors, whiteboards, and comfortable seating arrangements. The institute also provides high-speed internet connectivity throughout the campus, enabling students to access online resources from anywhere on the campus. The institute encourages students to participate in sports and extracurricular activities. The campus has facilities for various sports such as cricket, football, basketball, volleyball, and table tennis. There are also several clubs on campus, including music, dance, drama, literary, and photography clubs, providing students with opportunities to pursue their interests. there are also social clubs on campusIIT Goa offers campus placements to its students. Students become eligible for campus placements in their final year of study, i.e., in the 7th or 8th semester. Several reputed companies visit IIT Goa for campus placements, including Amazon, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, IBM, Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro, Adani Power, etc.  Higher placements offer is of 1.2cr and average placement offers are16-25 lac per annum. The percentage of students getting placed during campus placements at IIT Goa is quite high, with around 90% of students getting placed every year. The remaining students generally opt for higher studies or pursue entrepreneurship. In case no campus placement is offered, students can explore other options for employability, such as applying for jobs through job portals, professional networking sites, or by directly contacting companies. They can also consider pursuing higher education or research in their field of interest or starting their own business.

college_name: University Institute of Engineering and Technology - [UIET]
review_text:
Well this is something, which really needs to be worked upon, as campus placements are very less for tech field, and almost zero for core branches. Here companies do visit, but at a very bad rate. On campus placements do happen, but the amount is not very fair. The average package is of about 3 to 4 lpa. Something which is good here is that, students take off campus jobs from here, and this college also provides, quite a number of good ranks of gate exam from core branches. Yes, if you study here, and have a dream of having job, then you need to work on your skills, so that you can grab off campus jobs. In recent years, companies like infoysis, tca, etc have visited here, and provided employment to students. But it's not constant. 
Nearly 50-60 percent of students from cse and it get placed from here, but highest package won't go above 6 lpa on campus. I do look forward for an off campus job, with decent salary from here. Fee here, for whole year, for cse, cse ai, and is 10400, while for the core branches, it's 84400 per annum. Fee structure is quite decent, because there is no extra money required, once the fee payment is done, nothing else is required. As per taking my experience, apart from fee, if you live in hostel here, your per semester expenses can reach upto 5-6 thousands apart from fee payment. Here, college provides internships, which are unpaid, and if you want, you can go for paid internships too, but for that, you need to help yourself, and college won't be providing as such assistance. People here are getting off campus jobs mostly, and some of them are also getting on campus jobs, but they are little in amount. About 3-4 lakhs can be considered the average placement here, which is offered to limited students, so job opportunity is not very well over here. For jobs, you mostly need to rely on off campus jobs opportunities.

review_text: This course intrigued me like no other. The faculty was very good according to the collegedunia website. The faculty members have Ph.D. in their respective subjects from the best institutions and teach in a manner that is very easy to understand. Term exams are every 4 months and passing is not very difficult.The campus is very beautiful and there is greenery everywhere. The social life too is very good. Fees structure of NSUT is slightly lower as compared to IIIT Delhi but still for middle-class family fees is so high. The college admin itself doesn't provide any scholarship but yah students apply for their scholarship on NSP or the e-district portal for fee reimbursement. An on-campus job opportunity that's the main reason I enter the college but competition in my college is so high regardless of good opportunities for internship and placement. Around 2100 students studying B.tech in different branches, CSE department itself has around 800 students ( includes IT, MAC CSDS, etc), so think how much competition is in my college. So I suggest you think about IIT and even IIIT Delhi where the CSE department has less than 400 students.The course curriculum is much more updated now as compared to the past but still requires improvement. I have 5 subjects in 1 sem i.e. Maths, Computer Programming, EEE, BME, Physics. If I compare my college to a few colleges i.e. IIIT Delhi, DTU and even IIT Delhi, IIIT Delhi have a much-upgraded curriculum and their faculty is so good at teaching.

I chose btech cse as I am fond of coding and have some great ideas, the class ratio is 70:5 there are men boys and fewer girls the professors here are top-notch and very experienced there are 2 exams in a sem, one mid sem and another end sem and like this, we have 8 sem,2 sem a year for 4 years of college.this college is famous for its fests and all, it happens in Jan and Feb. there is a big library full of books and lots of amenities for the classes there is a huge sports complex which has everything one needs, the societies are also very famous here.

Almost every company visit dtu, and they offered various roles like apple offered both tech job and core jobs, google come for software engineer, Microsoft also come for both profile tech and core and also core companies come, and various roles offered by companies is software engineer, full stack developer, data analyst etc.. the stipend offered by the companies is very good like Adobe offered 1lakh in hand stipend, sprinkr offered 1.5 lahks in hand also the stipend is fluctuating between 80k to 1.5 lakh.The lush green campus is absolutely worth it, various fest is conducted by the dtu, the biggest fest is engifest(which is north India's largest fest) Then (Invictus) tech-fest also comes, and there is also yuvaan fest which is a film and festival fest.. sports are also Good here there is a big sports complex and all the games are here, our college seniors run a website that is a resource. tech which helps students to get all the study material, pyq, notes etc.

To be very honest as I'm in 1st-year 1st sem. our faculties members are not good as I supposed that they all are in a very hurry. They want to finish the syllabus as soon as possible may be the reason behind this is bcoz of COVID. The college wants its curriculum on its track But yeah some teachers are good in DTU faculty: the student ratio is very poor on average it is 1:37..which is very poor in comparison to other engineering colleges exams are conducted on time but this year they are very strict end sem and mid sem ends in just 5 days. It  becomes very hecticIn this field, DTU is very good. They have very good infrastructure and are spread in all around 190 acres area. They have a very big playground area. they have lots of clubs. I'm in like they have a club for every field. for example, inferno for making rovers and all, and UAS for drones which is very popular. Invictus for cultural activities and many more. Fest in DTU is zamm big. This year around 3 lakh feet fell on the concert ground. Fest is good in my opinion

I choose this course as I want to pursue my engineering in mechanical only and also my percentile was not very much high and only left with these choices. faculty to student ratio is 1:73 but there are many PhD scholars who help us in improving our subject. also don't expect very much from the faculty as it is a government college. exams are easily cracked and most of the questions come from the teacher notes only and only 2 exams are held in one semester.
I must say exams are not very difficult to pass and even if you study 1 week before or in some subject one night before you can achieve good gradesLife on the campus is very chill .your social life will depend upon you and which kind of friends you make. also on campus, you can talk to anyone .there is no ragging on campus.
There are many extracurricular activities held on campus also sports events are also organised and fests are just awesome. There are many societies in which you can take part which improves your all over behaviour.

I chose B.tech in Mechanical engineering because from starting I have interest in Robotics and I want to pursue higher studies in Mechatronics engineering. The faculty-student ratio is approximately 40:1 and it is much better than most colleges in India. Faculty are highly qualified all of them are PhD holders and yes last year 16 professors from our institute were listed in the top professor list by Stanford University.
Term exams ended just recently, the only thing to say for good marks you have to start studying at least 15 days before it's not like you will score well studying last night.In the 4th Year starting you become eligible for campus placement. Various top companies like Microsoft, tower research, Apple, Adobe, Google, Amazon etc. The highest package last year was
1.8 crore offered and the highest domestic package was 65 lakh offered by Apple. Mostly 90 per cent of student who wants placement gets placed before the last semester. I want to pursue higher studies in Robotics Engineering abroad.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
The course curriculum is very well made and learning capabilities and met.
Learning Resource Centre is filled with course-related and research-related material.
The faculty of every department is also very helpful and do not over clad students with assignments.Campus Life is very enriching at Jaypee Institute of Information Technology.
Many different hubs are present on the campus all run by students.
To make classroom learning easier in any weather Air Conditioners are employed in every class.

The annual fest is moksha which is one of the largest fests in Delhi apart from that there are many tech fests for instance the tech week conducted by IEEE ( A tech society ) and many more such events. generally, you can find a wide variety of books and journals at the college library however the timings of the library are a little off as it does not function after 8 pm and opens after 8 am. The classroom is inside the apj complex which has all the facilities from air conditioning to washrooms, and water filters, the classes have cameras Wi-Fi charging ports etc. There are many sports events that allow you to showcase your skills an example of the same is the annual winter sports which include Futsal, cricket, basketball, kabaddi, table tennis etc. In the college itself, you can find many societies which can interest you from photography to standup the societies almost include all the interests a person can have.The branch which I have is itns ( information technology with network security) the main reason why I chose this course is because of my interest in the coding world and to understand the proper working of this tech world and its wonderful workings . As of now during the first year each faculty teaches only your section(itns ) strength 70 and similarly, all other branches have strength around 100( faculty ratio. On average 1:90) the faculties are all PhDs and the hods have even are having proper research background in the 1 st semester one two exams are taken mid-sem ( generally 2/5 units) and end-sem (full syllabus). During the classes. Class tests are included. Your Jee preparation along with proper practice is enough to excel in the exams

The social and campus life is one of the best in India. There are loads of events and fests in our college. The annual cultural fest is Engifest, which is also believed to be one of the biggest North India cultural fests. The tech-fest is Invictus which includes loads of competitions, hackathons, fun events and games. Sports facilities are the very best. Huge grounds and courts for each individual sport and even a huge indoor complex are there.I chose this course over Software Engineering because I really wanted to study Maths (I'm good at it too ). Most of the faculties are PhD holders. Many are good with their subjects and teach well while some don't (exceptions are always there). There were only 2 exams Mid Sem and end sem in the whole semester.

For internship students are valid to apply is the 5th semester and also there is so many opportunities comes after internship period comes placements seasons
The highest pakage is 2.41 crore per annum
Average 10 LPA
minimum 8LPASo because of my interest I choose this course 
Very great facilities also decent student ratio 
DTU is chill but yes it doesn't mean you pass the exam without study
And there is relative relaxation after jee preparation

The major reason to get admission in dtu is the college life it has nice college life you will have so much fun of course manifest it is best in North india you will so many books in the library sports complex is very bigI think fees are okay but It is less compared to iiits and bits Pilani. Yes, you can get a scholarship either by e-district portal or by dtu. But you have to get a 10 cgpa for a scholarship and 1 or 2 students will get

The main fest is manifest and that's the best fest I have witnessed so far may artists came 3 days show day and night and what a vibe I am sure this college is will not disappoint you for fest books can be issued from lib for 6 months or 1 sem than you need to renew it by your I card all types of books are available whatever you'll need in 4-year sports I haven't witnessed anything special for it but events go on but on a smaller scale you can enjoy in dtu grounds badminton court and all the equipment are also availableFrom the begging of my life I know I have to do btech and having a craze for cars took me to choose mechanical engineering every year about 3000 students come into dtu and the girls ratio is high only in cs so don't come
With expectations, teachers are highly qualified but u have to study on your own and btech students study 1 day before mid sem 1 week before end sem exams are not that difficult

The course curriculum is very good. All subjects are useful you will never feel that any subject that it is waste of time and the assignments are also relevant to our course and will help you in the future. The teachers are well qualified I got many Ph.D. teachers and they teach really well and they are so friendly. The term exam is after 2-3 months we have midterms after 2 months of it we have the end term and the level of the end term is good.Youth vibe is one of the biggest feasts hosted in the university in collaboration with many clubs. Many industry host tech fests and hackathons for students to get real-world knowledge. There are many clubs in the university you can join but the criteria are their minimum 6 CGPA. Should be  One of the best clubs is DYC which hosts the biggest fests in the university recently the magnitude.

There's everything you can ask for in this college, from a really vast sports complex to annual fests. 
DTU holds annual fests like Engifest which is north India's largest cultural fest, YUVAAN which is north India's largest literature and film fest, etc. The whole of February is termed as Fest-Feb since it's packed with exciting artists, concerts, and various activities to enjoy.
DTU also provides students with a large 3-story library with a vast variety, students are provided with every kind of book they would need during their college life. DTU also holds AHVAAN which is an annual sports fest between renowned institutions such as IITs and NITs. One can find a lot of societies and clubs in this college.I chose mechanical engineering because I was allotted this seat according to my preferences and my rank. Faculty at DTU are well versed in their field, they have correct and abundant information on their subjects. Famous authors such as AK JHA were also a facility here. Teaching happens normally in DTU, offline classes are held in proper classrooms with correct decorum and discipline.
University exams are moderate to pass and can be managed through consistency since DTU has relative grading.

life of campus is quite cheerful as there are many events and workshops conducted time to time for refreshing + there are many tech and non tech fest INVICTUS . bookk , magazines are various kindss of journals are always  available ranging on different topics in the central library . our classrooms as well maintend and fully air conditioned and they have many charging ports. also there many sports facilities  also both indoor and  outdoors sports . fresources is a websites run by the students and for the students .fees structure for the firs years is around 2.2lac for academic year 2022-2023.fee hike is around 5-10% for every academic year. there are many scholar ships offered to the students some are merits based while other are category based there are many private scholarships also includings reliance foundation scholarships and fast retailing scholarships. there are many on campus internships and jobs offers which goes upto 1cr per annum (for placements) and 2lac/month(for internships at sprinkler)

As I got electrical engineering based on my score in jee mains as it is my choice. But I really appreciate the faculty I got. The teachers and professors provided by the college are soo good and experienced. They are very educated and teach in a very good way. They provide us with notes, and tutorial sheets, and clear our doubts. The exams are tough but not enough, to clear. Follow the guidance of the teacher, and attend Every class, it helps very much during exams.The 4-year btech study program in dtu cost around 900000, along with other expenses. There are no fee hikes as such and the fee structure was mentioned in the prospectus at the time of admission. There are many scholarship programs provided by the college based on performance in exams and many other things.

Life on the campus is amazing ..as a hosteler, I can fully enjoy college life there r tons of time to do ..there is always something going on inside the college campus for students to participate like fest competitions interbranch matches clubs societies etc. Dtu 's main fest is engifest it is one of the largest fests in North India and it is usually held in the month of Feb followed by different tech fests and sports fest. The huge library in DTU provides every desired book classrooms are fully air-conditioned and have a beautiful infrastructure with important amenities like projector lecture stands etc .there r seniors and other students running website to help other students like resources for all the study material and pyq's. CC is the societies name that held all the fest and important programs in DTU.Dtu placement r just amazing . U can see every range of package. A lot of companies visit the campus students usually get placement from their fifth semester but u r always eligible for intership. More the 500 companies visit the campus including all major MNC's like Uber Google Microsoft Amazon etc the av package for CSE branch is about 24.5 for this year and highest offer was 1.85 crore .from this branch more than 90% students get placed and others opt for higher studies . I have planned to get a decent placement after my degree but I m also thinking of higher studies I haven't decided yet.

I think dtu has the best campus life in not only Delhi but in entire North India. In fact dtu conduct the biggest annual fest in India In the previous month it conducted three fests in which many famous artists like sunidhi chauhan, amit trivedi, nuclear, naseeb khan, rajat sood, and many others came.
Dtu has a very big library in which there are amole amount of books for all students.
In terms of sports, I love them.. Dtu has a very big sports Complex which contains separate ground and court for almost all sports like basketball, kabaddi, lawn Tennis, TT,  cricket, etc.
Almost all types of society are there like acting, music, robotics, automation, debating, etc.Because I have very much interest in computer science and its application and coding stuff.
There is an ample amount of faculty for students and we can also meet some of them after hours too.
I don't know about all but my chemistry professor is from iit jodhpur.
Terms exam comes very frequently and without any gap between them and it's moderately hard to get past them.

I chose this course because it gives the best opportunities. it has the best placement. the gender ratio is pretty poor. 10:1 or even worse sometimes. worst in the mechanical branch. The faculty is good but at the end of the day, it's you who have to study.The cost to study is around about 10 lac and getting a scholarship is pretty difficult. 10 thousand rupees are given to the top rankers at the end of the year. Books and hostel fees are extra expenses that one has to see.

DTU is famous for its college life and fests. there are many fests and events conducted here. The biggest fest here is Engifest, which is the biggest cultural fest in north India. Along with that other fests are YUVAAN  (literature and film fest)  
 ,Invictus(techfest).
Almost every book you will get in the library, each book has approximately 80-100 copies.  some classrooms are fully air-conditioned while some are not. there are projectors in each classroom and a seating capacity of almost 100 students. 
Talking about the societies and tech teams, there are many start-up socities also and the start-up culture here is very good.
sports facilities are also very good, there are cricket, football, basketball, and lawn tennis like for every sport there is facility except a swimming pool. 
there are many groups and clubs run by students such as 'fresources' run by second-year students where there are facilities for exam preparations and all college-related issues.The placements here are very good, every big company visits here for campus placements like google, apple, Microsoft, Atlassian, etc. with a very good package. last year the highest package was 1.84 crores. in computer science the average package is about 24.5 lpa like during 2021-22, the number of offers was 2459. given by a total of 403 companies .and highest domestic package was 64 lpa given by apple and the international highest package was 1.8 crores given by Palantir London.
my plans are of getting a good placement from the campus and to join the corporate world.

I choose chemical engineering, because I couldn't get computer science obviously, the faculty here is good, the ratio is 1:75, faculties are highly qualified and the best. the first semester exams are just one after another, you get no time in between, it was stressful, but it's not that hard to pass, you can pass if you study a week before.The placements here are very good, almost everyone gets placed, if you're dedicated your branch doesn't matter you'll get placements no matter what, the placement packages are also high, you don't need to worry about employability, 90% of students get placed, the highest package is around 1.5 crores, the average package is 15LPA

The campus life here is really chill and interesting. There are frequent fests organized by different societies. Engifest is the annual fest conducted 17-19 march and invictus was the tech fest conducted 13-16 march. Our classrooms are really clean and properly ventilated with a projector and white boars. talking of sports we have a vast sports complex which includes all sports cricket field, football, lawn tennis e.t.c so the campus consists of all the sports for students who have an interest in any sport.From 3rd year students become eligible for campus placements. The placements of this college are top notch with an average package of 24 lacs of the cs department and the highest package of the previous year was 1.84 cr. Many top companies like google microsoft amazon e.t.c have visited the campus. the percentage of students getting placements is around 95.  My plan after getting a degree is to get a placement in case I am not satisfied with the placement I will do masters it.

The course curriculum is well designed for the 1st year all the students are divided into GROUP A & B subjects for Group A are:- Physics, Chemistry, Maths, Basic Mechanical engineering, and FEC (Foundation elective courses), and Group B:- Physics, Maths, Basic electrical engineering, Programming fundamentals (C language is taught) and FEC (Foundation elective courses) the faculty to student ratio is quite decent as 1:15. All the teachers are well qualified and experts in their respective fields. The exams are conducted twice in a semester i.e. MID-SEMS and END-SEMS and the difficulty of the papers are quite standard but if you will be regular with your studies you can easily ace the exams.The annual fest is ENGIFEST & Tech fest is INVICTUS this year they were conducted in February 2023. All kinds of books, articles, magazines, and journals ranging on different topics are available in the Central Library. The classes for 1st year are conducted in Pragya Bhawan The classes are quite good completely airconditioned and charging points are available in the classes. There are many sports facilities available ranging from Outdoor sports to Indoor sports including cricket, lawn tennis, basketball, volleyball, kabaddi, Open gym, then Table tennis. billiards. indoor badminton courts.

Fests are the best part of DTU. You'll have Freshers, Engifest, Techfest, Yuvaan - literature fest, and many more. You'll witness the best concerts of your life here. The library is a bit off as you need to be fast as the books easily get out of stock. Classrooms are fully air-conditioned. Sports are damn good, a huge sports complex is right there with cricket, football, badminton, TT, kabaddi, Volleyball, basketball, and in short every sport.The faculty here is good, the teachers are enthusiastic about their field of expertise. They want to teach and so goes for the student also. Coming to the ratio it's around 50:1. Quality is topmost, the best you can expect. You'll have two exams one Mid sem and one end sem. They are easy to pass, just study exponentially, and don't pile up to the end.

The placements here are very good and its the key feature of this college. If with so many students intake the avg package of this college is at par with many iits.All the companies that visit IIT DELHI come to this college and the avg package of cs branch is 25lpa+. companies like google, microsoft, amazon, Atlassian, apple, come here for placements, the highest package of year 2021-2022 was 1.8 cr and avg package of the college was 15.6 and avg package of the cs branch was 25lpa+. the placement % is also good in top branches like 95% students get placed in this college also 5% left dont get becase they go for higher studies.The college life here in DTU is the most interesting part here people here are very genuine they are very friendly. The college is very beautiful to roam around and there is no strict rules or curfew timing for boys ( for girls its 11 pm) 
 The annual fest of DTU is ENGIFEST its 3rd best fest after IIT DELHI and IIT BOMBAY.
The library here is very great and clubs and societies here are also good you get a lot of free time here. you hardly have 3 classes a day.
You can follow your passion easily here if you have any.
sports and extracurricular activities are good but the gym here is a bit small for so many students.

Students get full time placement opportunities in fourth year. However internship opportunities come in the second and third year. Every big mnc like google,amazon, microsoft ,flipkart,apple come here for recruitment. Over 400 companies come for recruitment.
In 2022 the average placement was about 16 lpa for entire college and 25 lpa in cse branch. Record placements were seen in  2022. Number of offers were close to 2400 approx and almost all the students(about 90percent) were placed who appeared for placements in 2022. 
Highest international package was about 1.15 crore which was offered to 15 students form amazon dublin. Highest domestic package was 65 lpa given by apple. 

My plan is to get a tech( full stack devloper) job with a decent package in any of these companies.

However placement opportunities are really great, i feel that due to huge competition in dtu beacuse of large student strength, it becomes really competitive to get that dream package of above 40 lpa.College life at dtu is the best.
Our annual cultural fest is engifest. It is the biggest fest in north india. It is conducted in month of February. It is one of the best experience in our college to attend engifest . This time around sunidhi chouhan, amit trivedi and kaleshi chori guys came in our engifest and they just made the atmosphere crazy.

Library of DTU includes every book and journal which student needs. Students can go to library at any time during day and study in a peace environment. The infrastructure of the Library is very good.

The classrooms for first year students in the pragya bhawan are very good. But apart from that classrooms are not that up to the marks. They are older and don't have ac.

Sports facilities in the college are decent. There is gym , sports complex as well as big grounds for playing sports. Various inter and intra college sports competition are organised.

There are a lot of student societies in dtu for every damn interest.

All the teachers are professionals and experts in their respective subjects. Every subject teacher motivates students to ask questions and makes sure that they understood everything in the classroom. All of the teachers are good-natured, friendly people with whom you can interact freely and clear your doubts. The course curriculum is very good as we get to know about everything related to the field and gives knowledge about the industry. Semester exam papers are well-set on a professional level.Campus life is not so good overall it's boring as not many events take place. The campus crowd is quite good, guards make sure discipline is maintained on the campus and everyone is in a healthy environment. Sports are also encouraged and students may try to get selected for the college's team. There are many social groups and clubs for students which are run by them only.

High radius is a common internship provider in KIIT. It's a fintech company and it has its office in KIIT itself. It's quite decent. Stipend is decent as well so it's not a problem at all. You can get internships via various online sites as well.The placements of this college are quite good as per its standards. It had an average CSE package of 15 lakhs for its top 1100 students. So if you can work hard you can easily pull out packages. I wanna pursue my MS in the US after my B.Tech.

College life is an important part of an individual???s life, providing them with discipline, friendship, sincerity, dedication, struggles, and experiences. It is a sweet and wonderful time to learn new skills as well as enjoy the pleasures of college life such as friends.Right now I am not involved in any internship but it is very easy to be involved in it. The process is very easy and very comfortable you can get into it. My friends are doing an internship and they are satisfied. The College is provided with the internship.

Campus life at DTU is really amazing. We have our annual cultural fest called Engifest and our tech fest and literature and film festival all conducted in February. The library is filled with books which you can issue. Sports facilities are also great. There are also loads of societies which you can join.I always had an interest in mechanical engineering so I chose this course. I have 73 students in my class with a total of 300 in mechanical engineering. The faculty is quite skilled with almost everyone with a Ph.D. degree. The exams are usually for 2 months and it is quite easy to pass them.

I have to go to PSU. The faculty is too good with the vast campus. In one class on Average, there are 70 students. The qualifications of teachers are good they have passed out from a very reputable college and also have a good experience. If you daily study for 2 hours from a first then you will be the branch topper and college topper too. But most of us pass by studying 1 day before exams.In 4th students are able to get their placement. Placement stats are too good average package is 10 Lakh and the highest is 1.4 crore google amazon, and Facebook there are lots of companies coming for Recruitment last year 403 companies came to college for Placement. 80-90 of the students get placed. I want to go to PSU in govt sector.

Social life is very good at this college Engifest provides all opportunities to the students conducted in February and Invictus tech fest is also conducted in February. The availability of books in the library is very good, amenities in the classroom were awesome sports complex is also very big, 400 m running track, 1 football court, one indoor auditorium, and two basketball courts, two badminton courts, and two tennis ball courts. Also, the college has various societies for participation in the robotic society debating society. Run by studentsThe total fees of the college are 2 lakhs19 thousand rupees. 1.5 lakhs is the tuition fees and the rest of the money is the University charge. For the general category, scholarship is the merit cum means scholarship, available on the e-district portal.

North India's largest fest for eg; Aarambh, Yuvaan, Engifest, and many more technical fests, is usually conducted from Dec to Feb month.
An amazing and large amount of books were issued. 
Large and big classrooms encoded with AC fans etc.
Large sports complexes apply for national international sports, Good societies.
Mainly students prefer free resource sites for their papers and assignments.219k for the first year and gradually increases to 5k for every year.
MCM scholarship and DTU Targetx scholarship for Delhi as well as the outside region.
And nsp scholarship for outside Delhi.
Amazon campus Easy to communicate and enchancs skills

I am pursuing B.Tech (Computer science and engineering). For the general category, CSE closes at around 22-23k AIR in JEE Main. For me, the curriculum of this branch is slightly easier to understand and also, CSE has the highest placements with an average of about 12-14 LPA, and almost all students of CSE get placed. Overall, the average package ranges between 8-9 LPA. Faculties of every department are well qualified (almost all are Ph.D. holders) and they are experts in their fields, there are different teachers to teach different topics of the same sub. We have 2 mid-semester exams and 1 end-semester exam for each semester. Mid-semesters are held after every 3 months and the end semester is taken every 6 months. To pass the exams, you just need to focus on whatever is taught in the class, passing the exams is not a big deal but you have to maintain at least 75% attendance in the class to appear for the exams except that if you have a medical emergency, then min. 60% of attendance is required.Life at the campus is pretty decent. There is a central library on the main campus equipped with all the books that you will need for your program (there are no comics!). Classes are equipped with smart boards and fans, lecture halls are equipped with normal blackboards and air conditioners. Various sub-councils in HBTU take care of various clubs for your personality development. The sports sub-council frequently organizes various sports tournaments like cricket, badminton, volleyball, and many more in which anyone can participate after the screening. Then there is ASCPC (Alumni Student Connect Program Committee) which is dedicated to organizing different events like alumni meetings etc. in the college. The link for information regarding all the clubs is:-    https://hbtu.in/club
We have various events and fests like Aarambh, ChemCon, Adhyaay, etc. held every year, these fests are tech-oriented with some fun and cultural program.

college_name: Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management - [ABVIIITM]
review_text:
Aurora: this is a cultural festival held over a period of three days in the month of February/ march. Aurora should not look upon as just a cultural event but as an attempt to promote the friendship, teamwork and alliances. 
our tech-fest name is Infotsav: our college organises events that acknowledge technological innovation and it is conducted every year. our library is the central biggest library in which over 30000+ books are available & our library boasts almost 80 journal titles both from India and abroad. Amenities in classrooms such as projector, laser light, chairs, fans, AC, black & whiteboard. we have a sports complex in which you can play all the sports such as badminton, table tennis, squash, one basketball court,  one cricket ground & one football ground. In IIITM gwalior there are various club such as Rotaract (which work for social welfare), Uthaan(recreational club), SGM(helps poor children by teaching them), E-cell  & IEEE.I have chosen this college because of my interest in CS/IT field and I want to pursue this interest. faculty: student ratio is 1: 20 due to the fewer number of branches in our college there is less intake of students and that's why our faculty: student ratio is well maintained. In IIITM Gwalior all the faculty members are phd holders & have a grip on their concepts and teach us in the best way. generally, minor exams are conducted in the span of 2 months and after that major exams are conducted after 2 months then the next semester will be started. And If you give attention towards the classes and solved reference books then scoring a good cgpa will be a piece of cake for you.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: The campus life of iit kanpur is great we have such a huge 1200-hectare lush green campus, with a lot of trees. We got a lot of freedom we can roam on the campus at any time with no restrictions as well we are allowed to bring the opposite gender to our hostel within a certain period of time, which is not provided in any other iits. So this shows we are having a lot of freedom. In our college, we have lots of clubs like dramatics, dance, quiz, music, gaming, etc. any possible thing you want to explore we have clubs for it, and our clubs are doing great.We have great placement in our college, every year more than 400 famous companies visit our college. So almost every student who wants a job gets the job on the basis of their capabilities in various sectors like coding, quant, finance, trading, consultant etc. About 95%students get placed every year. The highest package in the year 2022 is 4 crore and lowest package is around 6 lakh and the average package is around 15 lakh.

I choose this course because it was a good option for my rank in JEE and I don't regret it.
The faculty was awesome and they clear our doubts.
All teachers are experienced and completed their Ph.D. in recognized institutes.
We have to give time for studies and if we study with strategies it is easy to pass and get good scores in term exams. We have to spend some time for study in the hostels. Time is not sufficient in term exams we have to manage the time and it was not difficult to pass as I expected.Annual fest is springspree conducted between March and April and tech fest is Technozian conducted in December and we have a lot of fun in these fests.
All kinds of books are available in central Library.
Classrooms are designed with good infrastructure and smart classrooms .
There is lot of scope in sports & extra-curricular activities.
So many clubs are there in NIT Warangal like dance& dramatics club, Robotics club, Patriotic club, etc..

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: Campus life is quite boring with limited events happening in the college. The fest is also cancelled for 2022 batch due to covid 19. Sports facilities are limited with no football ground. Classrooms are well equipped with adequate amenities. Library is well stocked. The university hosts an annual cultural fest, Moksha, and a technical fest, Innovision, which are conducted in the months of February and October, respectively. These fests provide a platform for students to showcase their talents and participate in various competitions and events. The university has a well-stocked library that is open to students throughout the week and provides a peaceful and conducive environment for studying.
The classrooms, labs and computer centre at NSUT are well-equipped with modern amenities, including projectors, Wi-Fi, and air-conditioning, providing a comfortable learning experience for students. The campus of NSUT provides various sports and extracurricular activities for students, including football, basketball, badminton, and cricket and the university has a dedicated sports complex. There are also various social groups and clubs run by students, including the Robotics Club, the Literary Society, and the Entrepreneurship Club.The fee structure for BTECH in Information Technology with a specialization in Network and Information Security at Netaji Subhas University of Technology, Delhi, is reasonable and is subject to revision by the university from time to time. The cost to study includes tuition fees, examination fees, library fees, and other miscellaneous fees.
The university offers several scholarships and financial assistance programs to deserving students based on their academic performance and financial needs. Some of the popular scholarships include the NSUT Merit Scholarship, NSUT Merit-cum-Means Scholarship, NSUT Minority Scholarship, and NSUT Sports Scholarship. The university also offers various campus jobs, including research assistantships, teaching assistantships, and lab assistantships, which provide students with hands-on experience and a stipend. Overall, NSUT provides several opportunities for students to receive financial assistance and secure on-campus jobs.

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: IIT Kanpur has a large campus of 1055 acres. However, you'll usually wander around only 300 acres. Life on the campus is nice. The campus is very beautiful and the sports facilities are well-maintained. We have here one hockey ground, one football ground, one PE ground, and several other grounds. The campus is full of greenery. One thing I especially like about IIT Kanpur is its culture. It has hall culture, wing culture, baapu culture, and many other cultures. The annual cultural fest is ANTARAGINI and the tech fest is TECHKRITI. Both of them are going to be conducted this year in March and I haven't attended them yet, so I cannot review them. Regarding the availability of books, the PK Kelkar Library has around 4 to 5 lac books and you'll find most of the books you need there. There are 20 lecture halls of different sizes and the new ones are very good. IIT Kanpur has the best OAT among all the IITs and is the main hub for cultural activities.Well, I choose this course because this is the best which I can get at my rank, but after joining the institute, this course sounds interesting to me. The faculty: student ratio here is around 1:9. The faculty is highly qualified here and have great knowledge of their respective fields, however, I would say that they somewhat lack in teaching. Most of the students have to study by themselves to get good grades in exams. Plus the academics at IIT Kanpur are hard. Within a few weeks of the beginning of our semester, quizzes were organized and after a few weeks, our mindsets started. Just after the midterms were finished, we got the dates for the end seems, which took place one and a half months later. In short, you will have to give exams very frequently. If you want to pass, you'll have to study one week before the exams, but if you want to excel, you'll have to study hard and regularly.

review_text:
