The classrooms are topnotch
There are a lot of sports opportunities and extra curricular opportunities daily occurring.
The board of student welfare is run by students regarding all the problems faced by students.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text: Indian Institue of Technology are famous for there placements only.  Almost 10 lakh students every year prepare for jee to get into iits and placement is one of the most important reason. As companies offer on campus placement with a very good average package of about 10 lakhs to almost 90% students.campus life is very good here. There are lots of clubs of all categories including cultural clubs, technical clubs, sports clubs, and all are managed by students very well. There is also a very big library in which almost every book related to the course is available.

The campus life of our college is very awesome, our fest is moksha fest which is our college's largest fest and one of the top renowned fests in India. It is conducted Every year in march. Our college has a library which is very big and has a large number of books. Sports include every sport is there and tournaments are also conductedThe fee structure for the course is for the first year fee is 219000 for the second year 229000 and for the third year 240000 and for the final year the fee is 250000. there are various scholarships that are available for different categories like merit cum means, nsp, cvspk etc and many more which leps students

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: Social life on the campus is very active and amazing. The seniors are very helpful, especially the ones who persuade us daily to join a club or take part in one of the events that come up, they also ask for volunteers which is very much appreciated. The infrastructure is still a little bit old but is improving rapidly, the library at SVNIT is one of the top ones in Gujarat.The course curriculum is quite vast and stretched out. It becomes difficult in the starting 2 semesters but later on, with only the core subjects and maths, it becomes easy. Would like the same to happen to me since I am currently facing it since I'm in the first semester. Faculty members are quite helpful and teach well.

college_name: IIT Bhubaneswar - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITBBS]
review_text: My course is civil engineering. I have chosen this because I want to improve my creative skills... Here all the faculty is good they are friendly with each other teaching method is very good. it is difficult to pass when we are not prepared day to day wise but there is no time to prepare every day because of this very busy schedule.My life at my campus is good but with a busy schedule. Here we have 4 Fests esummit,wisssner, Alma Fiesta and ashwmeda, ashwmeda is a sports fest and alma is a culture fest. many club websites conducted by students. One is e summit website.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
Annual fests in each IIT are a sight to behold. The cultural fest of IITR, THOMSON was held on 18 October for 3 days wherein many famous personalities like Sonu Nigam, Sanam, and Darshan Raval came to perform.
Tech fest is cognizance, which is conducted during the march.
The Mahatma Gandhi Central Library is one of the most extensive libraries where books of literary almost every genre are present.

Classrooms are quite spacious, with facilities of bright lights, fans, ACs and Heating Blowers.
Almost every sport and cocurricular activity opportunity are present here, A student can try anything to find his interest.
INTER IIT is the sports meets of various IITs
All the social groups whether technical, non-tech, or cultural. etc. are run by the students, here all the clubs are present wherein any student can join after a small primary interview round.Students become eligible for campus placements in the 7th semester.
Some of the companies that came into IITR were,
Jaguar Land Rover India, CISCO, Google, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs, Flipkart, Google, Adobe, Sprinklr, Navi Technologies, Paytm, Deutsche Bank, NVIDIA, VISA, ExxonMobil, Bajaj, Oracle, and many more
Thousands of offers were made with 2.15 crore international and 1.8 crore domestic packages in 2022.
The average package is about 25LPA.
About 100% of students get placed in IITR.
After getting the degree, I would prefer to have a job.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: I have chosen this course as it will be giving me better opportunities in the future. All the facilities here are well-qualified and most are Ph.D. holders. There are two exams in each semester one in the mid sem and the other in the end sem.The fee hike here is about 10% every year. College also provides scholarships to students based on their merits. And also provides stipend internships for the students after the third year of the course

Placements are good with a percentage of above 90% every year. The training and placement cell along with the CAAS i.e. Career Advisory and Augmentation School works out everything for the students. Many tier 1 companies like Amazon and Adobe visit the college with a decent number of offers. Companies like TCS, Cognizant, Wipro, Infosys, Accenture, IBM, etc. visit the college with a huge number of vacancies. Last year 4 students got, the average salary package offered is 40 LPA, and the profile of SDE, while the average salary package offered, is around 6 LPA. I will personally prefer taking up a job after graduating from the university and doing it for 2-3 years to get acquainted with the trends of the industry, and then I will opt for a master's degree.If you get ranked under 1000 they will provide you with a 10000 Rs scholarship per month approx. 480000, you will get from KIT, when you are a scholar just you have to pass KIITEE study hard placement is not hard. Fees are costly but value for money.

There are many internship opportunities available in this college, but I prefer to focus on my study and get experience from good companies. I have created the project which is based on the eCommerce website. On this website, we have a login & up module card. Many more. One company like this provided me with the opportunity of an internship in their Projects.I choose this score because I am interested to learn new things. SI has an interest in computer applications, learning new technologies, editing, and graphic designing. I also submitted some projects on this course completion. Faculties of this college or Ph.D. holders. They have some degrees from this college.

The campus is huge. It's all about the Ramaiah educational institutes. The engineering campus is Lil bit smaller as compared to the medical college here. The main gates will be opened at 6 am and closed at 11 pm. Students can have all kinds of facilities which could be afforded them easily. Also, there are 4 libraries in RIT. Even they conduct fest but rare although club events took off often...In 2020 mechanical department started a start-up of designing an electrical cycle for the students to ride across the campus with charges made for an hour. It was so helpful for zomato and swiggy delivery people who used it in a meaningful way and it's growing well... there's also the multi-purpose building where students in their final year are allowed to research...

Our college is known for its campus life. We have fests every month but the main fest is in March which is known as the breeze. We can get books in the library by scanning our ID cards. But it isn't that necessary as lecturers mail us the books in the online format. Classrooms are well equipped with smartboards, projectors, and proper ventilation. It is also air-conditioned. Our college has many sports such as football, cricket, badminton, table tennis, lawn tennis, basketball, horse riding, a well-equipped gym and a golf course too. We have SNU explorer it's a website founded and run by students which helps other students to navigate through the huge campus in case they get lost or acts as a map for newcomers.The fee is almost 5 lakhs for the 2022 intake batch. A student can avail scholarship based on financial background or sports scholarship. You can even do on-campus jobs. Ph.D. students can teach B.Tech students and be their tutors which is an on-campus job. The fees of 5 lakhs include everything from tuition fee, hostel to mess fee.

I am a student in the Electrical department. Why did I choose this course over the cs and ece branch? I choose this branch because of its versatile nature, apart from that electrical engineering is considered the toughest branch of engineering. If you can choose this branch then you can also give exams like IES and also you can take this as an optional subject in IAS. You have also a chance to sit in the placements of other branches as well if you have good coding skills. In NITP faculty: students is 1:60. Qualification status is also good. All are graduates of NITs or IITs. There are two terms in 1 semester the paper level is also tough here. You can't pass an exam without studying.Here recently there is one cultural fest Eccentrica 4.0 organised. Also, Technotone and many more fest are conducted. In the library, you can find the book of your course. NITP has a nice collection of books. The sports culture is very nice here. Recently our seniors have gone for inter NIT competition and I am feeling proud to say we have got many gold medal silver medals also in kabaddi and all.

Fees here are slightly more than any NIT IIT or IIIT.
Like I paid 2,19,000 for 1st year and it'll cost around 10-11lakhs for the whole course.
Although scholarships are given on an income basis by the college itself and most of the govt schemes are eligible here.
Talking about the placement scenario of 2022-23, the average package is 15-16lpa and 25lpa for the CSE branch
14 students grabbed offers of 1cr+ and the highest package is 1.83cr by AMAZON IRELAND
There are many internship opportunities here average stipend is 59,000 and the highest is 1.8 lakh per month by sprinkler.The ENGIFEST, the annual fest of DTU is the biggest fest in North India along with it, there are lots of fest conducted by the Cultural Council of DTU and different societies also.
Sports Complex is one of the best places at DTU.
All major games and sports playgrounds are there with a well equipped gym and outdoor gym also.
There are lots of societies which will help in personality development along with this, tech societies, dance societies and literary societies are there.

college_name: IIT Dharwad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITD]
review_text:
There are not many fests in IIT Dharwad as expected, IIT Dharwad has an annual fest 'PARSEC'. It is a tech-orientated fest held in March every year 
We have a huge library which contains almost every genre of book which has at least 10 copies per book   you can take the book to the room after getting issued by the librarian 
Classrooms at IIT Dharwad in not soo good but yes it is sustainable 
IIT Dharwad has all sports clubs if you are interested in any game, you will get 100 support from college authorities
Despite all, we have a cricket ground, football ground, basketball court, lawn Tennis and two volleyball courtThe tuition Fee for a general student at IIT Dharwad is 1 lakh per sem and 25k for other expenses which include hostel and mess food, if you belong to SC and ST caste then there is total remission in tuition fee and you have to pay only for mess food and hostel. 
If you belong to general and obc and your family income is less than 1 lakh then there is a total remission in tuition fee and if your family Income is less than 5 LPA then you get a remission of 2/3 of tuition fee

I have chosen this course as I am interested in Mechanics since childhood. The faculty is okayish not so great as well as you won't be studying in the classroom as you have been studying in school. Some of the faculty are great no doubt. Haha, student ratio? Why? Well, here we have like 70 boys and 3 Girls on average. If you want a better ratio, choose NITs. Term exams were on time but due to the delay in admissions, our semesters have been shortened which is why we are facing problems in completing the syllabus on time.The well awaited annual fest known as EngiFest is held in February this year. Most of the required books are available in the library.
Classrooms are well equipped with Projectors and ACs.
The sports complex is a good one and has all of the sports you can ask for well except the swimming pool.
A lot of societies are run by students.

So I opted for this course because I didn't have a good rank but at my rank, I am getting some upper branches also I opted for chemical engineering because I am good in chemistry and it is a unique branch from others. And you have some much free time for co-curricular activities and freelancing. The faculty is very awesome and experienced here. The professors are very helpful and kind. All faculty members are very highly qualified. I didn't know the exact qualification but all of them are PhD holders aur extra than that. So don't worry about the faculty and teaching. So our semester started late so we have early exams almost 1.5 months before entering college. That's why we have to study hard for the mid-semester examinations. But if you study well and give some extra time to your studies you will definitely score good marks in your exams. So don't worry about the examinations.Annual fest take place between January and February. The annual fest is called Engifest. And tech fest also take place between Jan and Feb. Life here at campus is so awesome. You have a great exposure. You can join various clubs according to your interest. Some of the famous clubs and societies are Ecell, Yuvaan, Pratibimb and Cultural council etc. So talking about the library is that the library is very big. It is a three-floored AC library where you get all books your need or can read books in the sitting area of the library. The campus has great sports facilities. There is a very big sports complex which comprises an outdoor hall and an indoor hall. In the outdoor hall, you can play lawn tennis, Football, Cricket, volleyball, Athletics track and Basketball. Inside the indoor hall, you can play Badminton, Chess, table tennis etc.

There is very less time to prepare for the mid-sem or end sem after admission you will just have one and a half months for the mid-sem exams and in that one and a half months there are some fests too. after giving your mid sem you will have your end sem in the next two months only. Exams are easy to pass if study if not then obviously you'll get failed.I am a fresher here so I did not  get any projects so I can not say about the outcomes to clarify the tasks but yeah I have heard a from my seniors that they will you a role of a entry-level work in the internship then rest it all depends on your skills so who knows you can get even better position stipend are good from 20k to 2.5lakh

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: Campus life is great. Three fests are organized in a year, Techkriti, the science and tech-based fest; Antaragani, the cultural fest; Udghosh, the sports fest. Everything we can imagine is available through clubs and societies. Well-developed infrastructure for sports. All the clubs and fests are run by students.Fees for the course are not much higher as compared to private colleges, and placements are enough to clear all the fees in a year or so. Fees payable is even less for SC/ST communities and EWS. Education loans are provided by national banks at a lesser rate of interest.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text:
life at the campus is decent, you get high-speed internet facilities across the campus and a hostel 24x7.
the best part of SVNIT is its library with more than 2.4 lakh books and a variety of e-book platforms.
the clubs at svnit provide you a chance to explore your hobbies and during the journey, you learn how to manage events, studies with extra activities, etc.I choose this course because of the faculty of EE dept. is the most experienced and is considered the oldest and best branch in SVNIT.
the student ratio is 1:5 which is decent.
the faculty is excellent and the prof. are friendly.
every faculty is a Ph.D. holder.
the exams are moderately difficult and occur at a frequency of 2 months.

Talking about the fees ....the fees are high if considering government college ....it's around 2,20,000 per year and it increases around 10 percent yearly.
Many scholarships are available at college and also government provides scholarships or financial assistance for people with low family income and the reserved category.
You can be among the top 5% of the students to get a 100% scholarship from the college.The best I can get on my rank is ECE so I choose it .....Btw in starting you will regret but later on, you don't have to... ECE is among the top branches and also you will have better exposure to other things which you will realize later ...
Some faculty members came to teach and they are good but some faculty (the majority) don't mind whether you study or not and some come only to complete the syllabus

There is a lot of opportunities like internships, and full-time placements. Students can apply for internships from the 3rd sem and for placement, students get eligible from the 7th sem. A lot of companies visit its campus according to my research except apple, tower research every company visits here with multiple offers.My 1st year's fee is 2.20 lacs and there is a 10k increment every year. Fees are too much for even a person belonging to a good financial background. There is a scholarship opportunity for every category of gen news obc sc st PWD.  The name of the scholarships is district, up

Our campus life is so good and happy enjoy here so much here social life is so happy there are so many clubs at NIT Patna like Tesla, Hackslash, Natvansh a drama club, aarthi -music club, dance club, American Society of civil engineering, Sankalp, Robotics club, etc. And also we have a separate gym for boys and girls.
There are so many books,  journals, and magazines which are available in the library and you can access it online also.
In our college, there are many types of sports and extra curricular activities like basketball, kabaddi, football, cricket, badminton, squash, table tennis, chess, volleyball, etc.
Sankalp is the group which is run by our college students in which they provide free education to classes 1-10 and also provides the basic amenities like pen pencil books copy etc.As per my field of interest and jee mains rank I'm getting this college only. As it is a very old and good college so I go for it. 
The faculty-student ratio is quite impressive which is nearly 1:10 . And also the faculty are well qualified most of the professor has done m.tech and Ph.D. from renowned Institute like IITs, and NITs, and also some has done their Ph.D. from foreign universities also. And their methodology is quite good but sometimes I feel bored. And exams are held timely and it is not as difficult to pass the exams here.

The course is a bit outdated but it's interesting.
Faculty to student ratio is 1:60. Faculties are experienced but teaching quality depends on teacher to teacher. Term exams are very easy and can be cleared even with slight hard work at the end. Questions are repeated from previous years for the majority of the subjects.The social life on campus is really good.
There are various events throughout the year which include tech fest, film festival, sports fest, and inter-college events.
The library has a wide variety of books, but the quantity is limited.
There are various clubs and societies as well like pratibimb, IEEE, scee, Macs, etc.

college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology - [Vel Tech]
review_text: In the fourth semester the students are eligible for the placements and the Internships also they are eligible for the campus placements the college is responsible for the placements A lot of companies are visited for the placements like Amazon and Accenture and Wipro and Tech Mahindra and Zoho and Cognizant the highest package is 45 lakhs per annum and the percentage of the students getting placed in the companies is 95%I chose Electronics and communication engineering because the upcoming future is based on the Tesla cars so the contribution of the electronics engineers is very useful to the manufacturer of driverless cars here the faculty members are very friendly in manner and they have done the doctorates  and Ph.D. in NiTs and Vits and Even in IITS ALSO

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
I was always curious about competitive programming and this college has its iron in this field. Our college has given kickstart world rank one and is also representing India for ICPC for two consecutive years now. The faculty-student ratio is around 1:40.Faculty is well qualified to be in IIT. Exams were held semester-wise and were of moderate difficulty.Annual Fest: KASHI YATRA (JANUARY)
Technical Fest: TEchnex, Codefest(MAY)
Library: very good AC rooms and almost all course books available
Amenities in the classroom: Clean and some also have AC
Sports: Spardha, Olympics and all facilities available
Clubs: COPS, Ecell, Biz

The annual fest of the college is called breeze and is conducted in March. Also, there is a sports fest called surge. There is a huge number of societies/clubs in the college ranging from dance, photography, horse riding, golf, drama, cooking, and many more. The indoor sports complex has a built-in wooden flooring basketball court and badminton court and table tennis. There is a huge library with books from almost all genres. The campus itself provides pretty much everything. So the outline is pretty dull. There are several malls but you will have to travel there by cab.Students are eligible for placements from the 7th semester. Avg placement for computer science 15Lpa. Highest placement 60Lpa (BTech Computer science student). Various companies like Amazon, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, HCL, and many more visit the college during the placement season. The placement record is 100% for computer science students

Blitzshlag is the rajasthan's second largest college fest, which is going to held in February. Library are also there thousands of books are also there you can avail them by showing your ID card. Sports complex and gym are also there. Many equipments are there , we have tennis ,football ,basketball and cricket ground. There are many clubs for dance, music , quiz, travel and many more things. You should just need to explore yourselfAfter 6th semester students become eligible for placement.  Many companies and psu are also come to college , almost every student got placed if you have a nice cpga. Highest package offered was from cse branch of 60 lakh and from chemical it was 48 lakh this year and average for chemical branch was 8.8 lakh per annum. I will peruse for gate Exam as it provides government jobs and psu.

I chose ECE because I just love electrical engineering and it's my passion, our faculty is really really good and helpful and our all faculty members are PhD and IITian,  our exams are mostly each week and the exam level was high.After 3years people are eligible for placement, many top companies like Goldman Sachs, google, Microsoft, Amazon, Flipkart, ubuntu, and many more companies visit for placement, and almost 90% of students get placement.

college_name: IIT Indore - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITI]
review_text:
The Campus life at IIT INDORE is very nice, such a lush green campus, and full of technology with full infrastructure. In  IIT Indore the annual fest name is FLUXUS, and they are conducted in the month of march 2023.
IIT Indore provides a library, there are lots of books, and a Free study zone center also on the campus where you can study any time FSZ is open 24*7   hours. The classroom is fully furnished with an air conditioner and the teacher teaches in a smart board projector all slides they teach provide by mail.
sports facilities are very good, they provide Cricket ground, football, basketball, volleyball, and many Indore games like TT, Carrom. There is one Student community named Students Gymkhana which is run by students. Lots of clubs on campus programming club, dancing club, robotics club, etc.There is a need and demand for engineering graduates in the country. Attractive salary packages for B-Tech graduates, and listen from many seniours and school faculty that if you graduate from top IITs and NITs your annual salary is offered around 20-25 LPA.
In the IIT INDORE  faculty: the student ratio is 1:5, there is a group of 4-5 students one faculty advisor, and one student mentor also, and all the faculty teaches very well and properly. The maximum faculty are PHDians, their thinking and imagination level is very nice. The academic calendar released by the institute follow strictly, all exams and events occur from time to time, although some subject like maths is difficult to pass you go regularly in the classroom it is easy to pass an exam.

I choose this course because I like to work on computers and I hoped for a good career after this course. In our computer science branch, there are approximately 4000 students this year and the faculty provided them including Deen  HOD, and all faculty they are approx 300. The teachers are very good and almost all of them are supported also. They teach us well and every time ask us if we have some doubts. And clear them later. there are 6 IAs and 3 CAT exams. All IAs is online mode. Cats are in offline mode. Each exam of a cat is at 1:30 time. And the level of questions is moderate.First, you have to do your registration with 1100 rs online on the galgotias University website. Then they provide you with the application I. Then you have to submit your documents and you have to pay 25000 minimum fees that time to confirm your admission. There is almost no scholarship other than sports quota as I know. You have to submit your fees for 1 year before attending the first lecture of your college. but there are job opportunities also. companies like amazon, Wipro, syska, and Flipkart visit every year.

From 3rd semester students become eligible for campus placements.
Various companies like Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Infosys, TATA, etc. Visited our college and conduct placement exams and interviews.
In my course, at least 40% of students got placed in these companies.
After getting the degree I would like to apply for jobs interview or would start my startupI choose this course because I want to explore new things about computer technologies and I want to learn to code so that's why I have chosen this course.
The college takes semester-wise exams and between semesters there are four internal assessments also which we have to give online on the website of our university.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
There are many fests but two most important ones. Thomson, the cult fest, and cognizance, the tech fest!
They both are conducted in October and March respectively.
I don't know how many books are there because even after staying in college for so long, I still haven't been able to explore the entire Library, it's really that big.
Classrooms are very high-tech and new. (on every desk, there's a charging Port)
IIT Roorkee is famous for its sports, the inter IIT meet of 2022-23 was won by IIT Roorkee itself. The club activities are something that'll take up your spare time and convert it into something productive.
There's an entire chain of social media pages for every club, and their websites are run by the students themselves.I chose this course because I wanted to step into the industrial world. The faculty-student ratio is about average because the faculty has to be chosen from among the best. All faculties are Ph.D. graduates from the most established universities in the world. Their teaching method varies from prof. To prof. but all of them have innovative ways to teach the concepts to students.
The exams were all oriented toward a basic understanding of the concepts and if you know enough about the same, congratulations you're the topper.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The faculty here are well qualified. Almost 90% of professors here are PhD holders. The teaching quality is good. They are available for doubts clearance. The curriculum is good, it focuses on the all-round development of students. exams have pretty good questions and are competitive!Classrooms and Libraries are very premium here. All are designed with the latest infrastructure. Hostels are renovated regularly. Food quality in Canteen and Hostel is decent. There is a dispensary from where u can get a free check up! Games and sports facilities are also gr8 here

Blitzschlag annual fest is held in February, Youthfest in January, and SPHINX tech fest is held around November. A wide variety of books and journals are available in physical as well as our online library. Various sports like volleyball, cricket, wrestling, kabaddi, etc are there and various clubs dedicated to enhancing our soft skills are present like debating, dancing , think India, NSS, singing, etc.from the 5th sem, they are eligible for internships.  Companies like Adobe, Amazon, Apple, TATA motors, etc visit our campus. the highest package offered was 64 LPA in the year 2022 with an average package of 12.43 LPA. Play. cement rate was 90%. one can do M-Tech from this college also after receiving their B-Tech degree.

college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text:
Annual Tech fest in IIT bhilai Is Meraz. 
In Meraz 2022 Many events were there. 
Gajendra Verma, band, DJ nights, etc.
Was there. 
Also, There were So many Events such as Speed Dating, 7 ups, Robo Kabbadi, Drone Racing, etc. 
Many Students from different colleges such as NIT Raipur, IIIT Naya Raipur, etc. Were participated in that event. 
Various Clubs such as Drishya ( acting), Enginuity( coding), and best hacker ( dancing).
Etc, clubs are there.My Course is Quite popular everywhere and also I learned some coding in 11 -12 the standard which increased my curiosity about the CSE branch. 
Faculty members are very supportive and available every time. 
Term Paper was there In Every month. 
Exam pressure is very high. 
The Girl to boys ratio in our branch is quite good in comparison to all other iits 
It is about 3:1.

The course curriculum is the same for most of the branches of the B.Tech course for the first year. The curriculum contains 5 subjects and one optional subject. I have a special interest in mathematics,  thus I chose this course. The Faculty members are very good. They are experienced and well-qualified. Every teacher has a minimum experience of 5-6 years and all of them are either Ph.D. or have completed research and higher studies from reputed institutions. Since the semester started late, the first semester is of only 4 months this time. After 1.5 months, the mid seems to start. Other than the mid-semester and end semester, there are also class tests, at least one for each semester. The difficulty level for the exams was moderate to difficult.The college life at NSUT is amazing. The campus area is about 144 acres. There are two fests each year and they are very great. The crowd of the college is also very decent and well-mannered. The library contains well-kept books and one can find nearly any book that he wants to read. the classrooms and washrooms are well-maintained and hygienic. The college also organizes intra-college sports competitions frequently where students from different branches of the college form teams and compete with students from other courses. There are many communities and groups in the college like DebSoc NSUT, IEEE NSUT, Venatus NSUT, etc.

college_name: IIT Bhilai - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITB]
review_text: Really well faculty and have a broad knowledge about the subject. the tierce exam is held after every 2 months, it keeps the students focused on study. we all guys really appreciate such a system in modern colleges, as we know nowadays many colleges are not too focused on academics but IIT Bhilai is more concerned about academics as well as sports and extracurricular activities.If you talk about the campus of IIT Bhilai, it is true that this college currently runs from its transit campus. The current campus of IIT Bhilai is shared by the Gec Raipur campus. But if you talk about its permanent campus in which the students will shift very soon, in one of the best CPUs among all IIT. Recently in Delhi, IIT Bhilai won many awards for its greenery campus.

Engifest is the annual fest and Invictus is the tech fest they are in Feb and also there are a lot of other fests. 
All of the books that have been given in the syllabus are present in Library.
All basic amenities like space, ac, and projectors are there.
There is a lot of competition are organized by the sports council. 
There are a lot of clubs present NSS dtu (social group), cultural council, yuan, ecell tech teams, and coding society are there.This course is the perfect combination of mechanical, management and industry and the core courses of the college are quite good.
Students have 1:80 approx. the ratio for lectures and 1:20 for practicals. 
Faculty are mostly PhDs from colleges like IIT and NIT. Teaching depends on faculty some uses ppt and other uses notes and some uses their way but all are good based on an understanding of students.

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP]
review_text:
The students can start applying for placements in their last year. Many companies including top MNCs visited the campus. IITT doesn't promote the idea of advertising the college through placements, but the placements are actually jaw-dropping for a new college like this. Despite being a 3G IIT, the location plays a crucial role in the placements here. Tirupati is a prime location- 2 hours from Chennai, 3.5 hours from Bangalore, and 6-8 hours from Hyderabad. It has a lot of connections with close institutes like IITM, IISc, CMI, IITH, IIITH, IISERTPT, etc which makes it a must-visit spot for companies and their HR agents. 
The highest package in the year 2021 was nearly 72 lakh, which is insanely high for any new IIT and had overtaken the highest package at IITH and IIT Indore.
In 2022, in phase 1 of the placement, a student from civil engineering got the highest package of nearly 83 LPA, which again was the highest among all the new IITs by a huge margin (IITH had a highest at 63.5 L)I chose Chemicals at IITT because the chemical department here is very good at teaching and research. Having IISER Tirupati at a distance of 2 km is another huge benefit to improve the research culture of the college. 
The faculty-student ratio at IITT is the best among all the IITs (5:1). 
All the faculty members are very enthusiastic, young, open to ideas, and Ph.D. graduates either from IISc, IITs, or some university abroad.
IITT follows a continuous evaluation method with 3 exams- Quiz 1, Quiz 2, and End sem. The papers are very normal and scorable too. 
There have been alumni (students) who have gotten into top colleges like Stanford, ETH Zurich, Princeton etc from here too.
The packages here are also really impressive for a 3G IIT. 
This year, after the Phase 1 placement, the highest was 83 LPA, and last year it was around 70 LPA. In both years, IITT had the highest package among all the 3G and the 2G IITs (including IITH and IITI).

review_text:
Life at an IIT is always great, but at IIT Roorkee, it's a special blend of modern life and traditional values. Our homes away from home are as modern as they can be. More importantly, though have spawned the cultures that make the campus an unforgettable experience, it's here that midnight cricket tournaments, early morning bakar sessions, community computer warfare and so many other things find expression.
Clubs at IITR are the best place to relax and recharge with indoor sports facilities including seven Billiards & Snooker Tables, Carrom boards, Chess boards, Table tennis facilities and football tables. IITR has a 24/7 wi-fi connection throughout the campus. IITR library has access to e-books available to every student at any time.The total 4-year fee for the B-tech course is around 12 lakh. There are several Fee waiver programmes for economically weaker candidates in which 100% tuition fee waivers for SC/ST/PH students. Full remission of the tuition fee for general and OBC students whose family income is less than rs. 1lakh per annum and remission of 2/3rd of the tuition fee for general and OBC students whose family income is between Rs.1 lakh to Rs.5 lakh per annum.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: The annual fest of IITG is Alcheringa which will be conducted in February from the 3rd to the 5th Feb. Our Tech fest is Techniche and it's the biggest tech fest in the NorthEast. It is conducted mostly by students and the attractions are mainly arranged by the students. Almost all books and generals needed are present in the main library. Even the hostel libraries contain a lot of books. All in all, there will be no shortage of books if you're there to study. The classrooms have all the amenities. There are two types of classrooms. 1) Digital and 2) Normal classrooms(with a blackboard and a projector). Now the digital ones have a charging port and all the things you need. While the normal one just has about 500-600 seats with a proper and clear vision of the board. There is no end to sports and activities you can do here. You have a full-sized ground for any sport you can name. Tennis basketball football volleyball squash table tennis badminton. App-->OneStop. Club---> E-cell. These are the major onesI chose this course because chemical engineering is one of the most chill core branches that are out there. It also provides ample opportunities for big petroleum companies, in pharmaceuticals, extraction sites, etc. Or you can take that free time and use it to code and go into the IT sector. About the faculty-student ratio for the first year, the course is the same for all the BTech students irrespective of the branch. That is because everyone should get a fair chance at branch change. So there is about 1 professor for each subject per 200ish students and 1 teaching assistant per 40ish students (for tutorials and such). All the faculty members are highly qualified and they have PhDs in their respective courses. Their teaching methods and style are amazing and the profs as well as TAs also take time out to clear your doubts (if you're willing to ask). About the term exams, they are difficult and have their standard. If you study you can easily score and if you don't study you can fail.

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP]
review_text:

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
There is an annual fest every year which is mainly held in the first semester but due to the disbalance caused by the covid, it could even be held in the second semester. It lasts for 3 days known as JIIT converge in which the whole college participates and different societies perform on different days in these 3 days' time. The last day is the most interesting one because one of the famous artists comes on this day and we enjoy ourselves a lot. It could be held either in November after the first test of college or in Feb in the second semester. 
There are mainly books related to studies in the learning resource center(library) but sometimes you could find a few interesting ones too. 
The classroom is full ac ones with a board as well as a ppt viewer and the seats are well organized so that one can see the teacher from the back seat too. 
There are sports clubs, especially in JIIT 128, and many societies like dramatic societies, photography clubs, tech societies, and sports clubs too all run by seniors and us.CSE is something that has gained its importance for quite years now and if you want a decent high salary job then its always advisable to choose it as your branch and all above that if you have an interest in it gives the joy of another level. The faculty: student ratio is generally 1:30 but in some classes, two batches are combined so then it's 1:60. 

The faculties here are highly educated and knowledgeable and supportive. For subjects like engineering maths and physics, you can just cover it by revising what they have taught, and then ppts are provided after every lecture so it's easy to recall for sdf they teach nicely but you need to practice a lot programs to score well. 
There are in total 3 exams
Test 1-20 marks
Test 2 -20 marks
And the last one ends the sem exam
The last one is quite difficult as the complete syllabus comes in it but if one could score in the initial one it becomes easier to pass the subject.

I choose this course because I liked computer science and had a great interest in technology.
The student ratio is less female because there is no female reservation.
Faculties are strict but only a few otherwise majority are chill but attendance is strict to be 75% mandatory.
Faculties are above average but a few are exceptions.
The faculties are too qualified.

Gp Birla scholarship is there and gets that and also there is an e-Kalyan scholarship where you can get 1lakh per year for obc/sc/st income below 1.5lakh and for Jharkhand only GP Birla is open for all categories and all states.
Jobs and internships are provided on campus and are great...

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
The course is not very hectic and so a lot of time for extracurricular activities. The course load is less so there are opportunities to explore other domains also. There are opportunities for core sector jobs as well as noncore domains.
There are 156 students in one class.
All the professors have done Ph.D. and also work experience in the US.
Review exams and quizzes are organized from time to time as well as the term exams are easy to pass if studies are done sincerely.The student body organizes Kashiyatra every year and is the second-largest fest in north India.
It is organized in January and is a three-day event. 
The tech fest also attracts students from all universities across India.
The library in the institute is spacious and one can find books on all subjects. 
There are over 60 Clubs run by the student body hence a lot of opportunities for extracurricular activities.

college_name: IIT Roorkee - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITR]
review_text:
As of now, I didn't know about the fee hike policy but the fee is pretty high for a government institute. It's 2 lakh rupees per year and including the hostel and mess facilities, it goes up to 3 lakhs for general and obc candidates, however, the sc and st candidates do not have to bear the tuition fee, so for them, there are only hostel and mess charges which are around 1 lakh rupees a year.
But also we have many scholarships like the fee waiver scheme is there according to which if you are a general or an obc candidate and if your family annual income is between 1 and 5 lakhs, then you get 2/3 rd of your tuition fee returned and if your family annual income is below 1 lakh rupees, then you get all of your tuition fees returned. 
Also, other scholarships are there namely the merit cum need(mcn) scholarship which is provided if you are in the top 25 percent of your batch and your  family annual income is below 5 lakh rupees.So the course curriculum is what attracted me the most. I was always interested in coding, but my bad, at my rank (which was 4367 in JEE Advanced 2022) I was not able to get a circuital branch in the top 7 IITs so I decided to go with chemical engineering so I discussed it with my seniors and teachers that which branch to go for and chemical seemed to be the best option.
Faculty to student ratio at IIT Roorkee is almost 19 to 1 which is pretty decent. 
All the faculties are well qualified and hold degrees from various institutes of national as well as global importance. Most of the faculty members are Ph.D. holders and the teachings are very helpful, but you have to devote extra time to subjects like maths and programming on your own. 
Currently, the first sem is a compressed semester, so we are having exams almost every fortnight, but generally, they are every month.

Collage fees are like 59000rs per year and there is a fee hike for police which will increase like 4000rs per year.
There are 2 types of scholarships like
If a student got a percentage more than 95, that student will get a scholarship of 100% in academic fees.
If a student got a percentage between 85%-94%, that student will get a scholarship of 50% in academic fees.
Students who have financial problems will take education loans from banks.
I completed a job in 1st sem with a stipend of Rs.5000.I have an interest in computers and technology so I had chosen this course.
Faculties are very friendly with students.
The student ratio in this university is more than 3000 students.
All faculties are graduated and some are completed their master's in foreign countries.
The teaching methods of faculties are very nice and good, so many students are interested to listen the class.
Term exams will conduct every 5 to 6 months once, the first 2 sets are easy to pass.

As students with income less than 1 lakh get a full fee waiver, students with income 1-5 lakhs get a 2/3 fee waiver. I managed to grade god in my first mid-term exams.

Hence, JU has the highest return on investment value compared to any other college.

Clubs like music, dance, sports, cell, ECS, and NCC are also available.The fee structure is average as per the government rules. For category students, scholarships are available for tuition fees. And many scholarships are available on the government websites of their respective state and central governments. Education loan is also available in campus bank in college.

I opted for Bachelor in Technology in Computer Science and Engineering as I have a great interest in computers and associated devices. The future scope of this course is great as well.
The faculty: student ratio is 1:80 in the CSE Department for the UG course.

Nearly all the faculties are passouts from elite colleges such as IIT Madras, IISc Bangalore, IIT Kharagpur, etc., and hold Ph.D. degrees. Hence, they're more than qualified to shine their light of knowledge on us. The teaching methods followed vary from faculty to faculty. Some prefer teaching using presentations while some prefer the old-school method of teaching on board. Either way, they try their level best to deliver us the content we need, and make sure we understand every bit of it.
I haven't given a term exam as of yet, as our classes had begun on 28th November due to the COVID Pandemic. But we do have received a schedule for the exams, and our mid-semester exams are going to start mid-January, and end-semesters in March.The fee structure somewhere lies between that of prestigious institutions such as IITs and NITs and that of other prestigious institutions such as BITS Pilani. The overall fees for a 4-Year course in BTech are somewhere between 16 to 17 lacs, including hostel and mess charges.
However, there are various opportunities for scholarships as the GP Birla scholarship, where a student has to lie among certain top students of the college in a semester and can get up to 100% scholarship in that particular semester. Other scholarships include ranks in JEE Main examination etc.

There is financial assistance available for students whose parent's annual income is less than 3 lacs per annum.
There aren't any official campus jobs as of yet. However, there are various startups by seniors of the college which provide various tasks such as content writing, prospecting, etc., and one can earn pretty much enough by joining one of them to carry out their monthly expenditures independently.

I choose Btech in cse because I love to deal with computers and software and the placement of CSE student are pretty decent. There is a mentor system in kiit there is 1 mentor for every 50 students. Some of the mentors are very good some are not that great.The fees are a little bit on the higher side but it's ok because the college provides a nice facility. There is not so much opportunity of getting a scholarship because only a thousand students get the scholarship for half the fees.

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text: My course is very good. The curriculum and faculty members are also very good. I've got this course not by my choice but by my rank.  Faculty members are well qualified. Most of them are Ph.D. All of them have different teaching methods. Our mid sem exam is on 25 Dec. It will be my first exam to give here. But as get from seniors one can paas easily by studying daily.Placement is the only reason that attracts students to join MNNIT. Placement records of MNNIT make it in the top 3 NITs. In cse highest package this year was 1.18cr and the average was 30lpa. In my branch highest was 25lpa with a 10lpa average. All the branches have the best placement records.

BITOTSAV is the annual fest of BIT Mesra which is conducted at the national level. Generally, it is conducted in the month of February and many eminent personalities visit our college during the fest.
The college offers a large library with a vast collection of books from all domains. The library is totally tech-enabled with zero human interaction.
All facilities including a Gym and indoor sports facility are available for sports enthusiasts.
BIT Mesra boasts of having one of the best coding cultures in the country with top-notch coding clubs having their centers here, like IEEE, IET, ACM, etc. The college also has other clubs like the Literary Club, Photographic Society, Dhwani Music Club, etc.When it comes to fees, BIT Mesra charges a little high in terms of its competitors (Top NITs and IIITs) but the money is worth it considering all the facilities the institution provides and the amazing placements.
GP Birla Scholarship is provided to the top performers of the batch, every semester.
Top 25 students: 100% tuition fees waiver,
Next 25 students: 50% tuition fees waiver,
Then there's merit cum means GP Birla scholarship for people needing financial assistance and who have performed considerably well in the institute examinations.

The university provide internship program to the students for exploring their knowledge against machines and their subjects. Here you can also prepared field projects for build basic knowledge. It's compulsory for all.There are so many clubs and societies in university which is run by the students and they can also organised fest and cultural program at regular time of interval. They can also held guest lecture for knowledge

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: The campus life and culture at IITG are great. U get exposure to a lot of things. Not only in academics but u get a lot of learning in many different fields like sports, clubs, dance, music many others. There are many clubs available like music club, coding club, analytics club, dance club etc.. where u can join any and learn anything. The annual fest of IITG is called "Alcheringa" which is generally held in February and is conducted in a very flashy and grand way. The tech fest of IITG is called "Techniche" which gives chance to students to share their ideas and improve their practical skills. Classrooms and lecture hall complexes are very comfortable and do have high technology and they are very modern. IITG has a sports complex cum gym called "GYMKHANA" where students can enjoy playing indoor sports like badminton, table tennis, etc. And for outdoor sports, u get enough grounds to play anytime. Many student groups and social groups are being run by students which conduct many activities.I was getting computer science and engineering(CSE) in 2nd gen IIT but I chose EEE in IIT Guwahati because it is an old and prestigious IIT and ranks 7 in the NIRF ranking among all engineering colleges in India. Electrical engineering is also a core branch so u can also get placed in core companies. I also feel that the Electrical branch has great potential in the future so I took this branch. The student-faculty ratio at IITG is pretty good it is around 1-10. U also have other teachers other than lecturers who will help u with your studies. Tutors, Instructors are also present who will help u to get a better understanding of lectures and they will help u solve questions after the completion of a lecture. The lecturers here are highly qualified and seem pretty intelligent but they fail to make average students understand the subject. There will be no person-to-person interaction always in the class so u have to work hard your self in order to get good grades and survive the competition.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: IIT Guwahati doesn't only focus on academics. Sports and culture are also the main things IITs are famous for. Spardha, Spirit, and Alcheringa are a few of the cultural and sports fests happening here. The campus life is really very thrilling and good. One can get all the facilities right from a huge sports ground to a good swimming pool. Thus enhancing students' athletic as well. The classrooms are really very huge and good with very spacious seating arrangements. Facilities like water dispensaries and washrooms are also the in the classrooms. Ecell of iit guwahati is one of the website in iit guwahati.I am very much interested in mechanical which made me choose mechanical as my career option. The student ratio at IIT Guwahati is around 1:5 .The faculty here is highly qualified and well-educated. The Ph.D. students and teacher's assistants help us clear our doubts very easily. Each term is of around 3-4 months. It is not much difficult to score well in the semesters. A daily study of around 2 hours is even enough for a good score.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: The college host 3 major fest in a year. SPARDHA is the annual meet to showcase the sports and athletics strength of the campus. TECHNIC is the annual techno-management fest of the campus where people come together and collaborate to make the world a better place. KASHIYATHRA is the annual socio-cultural fest which is also one of the prominent fests in India. Besides these, there are other fest and workshops conducted by clubs and councils. The library on the campus is well maintained and almost all books are available in either physical or electrical form. There is even a special amenity for SC-ST students an SC-ST Book bank. The classrooms are also good. Every class has its projectors and screen and can accommodate at least 100 students. The campus has 3 grounds and a student activity center (SAC) to promote the athletic and sports hobbies of students. The campus has many achievements in sports, social and cultural activities run all India level.I choose BTech+MTech in Pharmaceutical Engineering and Technology at IIT BHU because the college is a pioneer in providing in BTech degree in pharmaceutical engineering. It is the first university in Asia to do so. Also, the course has a very high scope in the research field.  The faculty here are very good. Almost everyone has done their Ph.D. from premium institutes in both India and abroad. Most of them teach in both Hindi and English and use slides to teach. Although there are some teachers whose teaching methods are not so good. This is mostly because many of the students are from a non-biology background, but the course expects us to study some basic biology. The professors are very helpful and are available 24x7 for doubts and other help. We usually have a mid-semester exam and an end-semester exam. I have not yet attempted one, so I cannot comment on exams. But overall academics is good if you are willing to put in some effort.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: The course curriculum is common for first-year students with maybe one elective change for each branch which is again common to a number of branches. The faculty-to-student ratio is as expected the professors are well-qualified and many have a passion for teaching. Professors who have studied at Massachusetts etc take your course. The course is offline but all ppts of classes are promptly updated in official class groups. The midsems are around December and the End Sems are in February. They are fairly hard as students are introduced to a very vast syllabus, especially in PhysicsAlthough the campus is older, most of the infrastructure is kept up to date and in very good condition, especially the air-conditioned reading rooms and library and the well-equipped gyms and Student activity centre. The grounds are in a league of their own and are extremely large and well-suited to the needs of the students. Clubs are aplenty ranging from Western Music all the way to Entrepreneurship and Business. Almost all niches have recognition here. Kashiyatra is the annual fest and is hosted around January and is one of the biggest IIT fests.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The annual fest is Saturnalia and it happens in November when 2-3 singers are called. Like in 2022 Saturnalia, Gajendra Verma, and Zaeden came to perform. In the library, there are around 2.5 lakh books and Thapar has one of the biggest libraries in India. There are separate Group Discussions rooms in the library to study separately with your friend's group. Classes have a projector. There are AC classrooms. Here students get separate grounds for football, cricket, badminton lawn tennis, basketball, and table tennis. A swimming pool is open for a whole year. For extra curricular activities, students join the entrepreneurship Development cell, pratigya(for social work) adventure club, and academic clubs which are run by students and seniors who teach juniors.Students are eligible for placements after the 5th semester. Apple offered a 90k per month internship. Amazon, Flipkart, Swiggy, and Zomato offered great packages to students. The highest package was offered by De/shaw of 60lpa. More than 90% of students get placed through campus placements. Talking about my plans after my degree, I would love to pursue MBA and then maybe a job.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: I am doing BE in EIC (Electronics Instrumentation & Control), and the course curriculum is good as it is set according to the need of the time. I chose this course because this branch of engineering studies the measurement and control of process variables , and design and implementation of systems that incorporate them. Choosing EIC pays off well as the placement of TIET is good. The faculty: student ratio of lectures is 1:120 and for practicals 1:30. All the faculty of TIET are well qualified and studied at IITs or abroad. They mainly are doctors in their respective fields and teach in the most effective ways using projectors and different visual methods. They clear all the doubts of students and help them in every way possible. The exam papers are difficult in ESTs and easy for MSTs and always take place timely.Campus life TIET is very nice and students get to experience a lot of things and get to grab many opportunities. Students from  different states come here so you get to know about different cultures. The annual fest here is Saturnalia which goes for 3 days in which we have different events in which we can take part and on the 3rd day we have Bollywood night where a celebrity is invited. TIET has library which is of 4 floors and have different section for different departments. Nava Nalanda is one of the largest library in North India.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The best part about TIET is its Infrastructure be it the magnificent Library or the stunning Lecture Halls. The amazing infrastructure of TIET is enhanced by the people be it your batchmates or seniors, I personally feel that each and every member associated with TIET is an integral part of every Tiet student be it the mess workers, or the guards every one has a unique personality which makes the TIET experience unique.I personally feel that TIET offers highly qualified teachers for almost all subjects and you when in class will feel the same as well, however, the problem comes when we sit in the lecture hall due to a large number of students in a class it sometimes feels that you are sitting in a coaching institute and thus lose interest in the subject despite the talent of the teacher.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text:
There is an annual sports fest Rann neeti conducted in the month of November and also pro nights are celebrated with it. Everyone enjoys the fest.

All website of clubs are run by students and also photography and video making is also done by students for which extra club is there.There is so much fees taken by the students. Only the tuition fee is 2 lakhs so how everyone can afford that.
There is a scholarship but only for students having a family income of less than 5 lakhs. What about students having a family income slightly greater than 5 lack ( such as 550000) they will not get any scholarship benefit. This is very wrong with this institution. college_name: IIT Delhi - Indian Institute of Technology [IITD]
review_text:
Fees for general category students in IIT are 1,66,500(including mess, hostel, and other fees) per semester. So fees are roughly 3 lac per year with slight variations of around 10k-20k in other IITs.

If you are SC/ST cat. the student then your fees are around 18k-20k per semester or even less. You will also get a scholarship of 1000 per month from Institute no matter what your family income is.

If you are a gen/OBC cat. a student with a family income less than 5 lac/annum, your 2/3rd fees will be waived off.

If your family income is less than 1 lac/annum, full fees will be waived. Moreover, you will be provided financial support from Institute.

P. S. Banks will give loans up to 5 lac without interest, without any guarantee for all IIT, NIT students no matter what their financial background is. You can even avail loan of up to 9 lac and its interest will be paid by Institute, you just need to pay money. So, don't worry about fees, all things will take care of and aid will be providedIITD has the smallest campus at around 320 acres, but on the plus side, it's bang in the middle of Delhi. The campus doesn???t feel small though. On my first day here, I was totally blown away by the sheer size of it.

The academic area is pretty decent, and I really like the Bharti Building, and in particular the GCL (General Computing Lab), which is like a second home for our department. The classrooms vary from huge comfy AC-equipped to antique stuffy ones.

The studies are no cakewalk - this is an IIT after all. And you'll come to get exasperated with the multitude of exams: two minors, one major. So mostly you'll be dreading, preparing, or giving exams throughout the semester. Not to mention quizzes, assignments, practicals, etc. Your mileage with marks may vary, but it's not extremely hard to scrape through most courses.
The quality of faculty varies, but CS profs are thankfully comparatively chill. Overall though, IMHO, the faculty could be better

Academic fees per year is 78000. If someone wants to avail accomodation too there are additional charges of Rs 15000 per year. Transport facilities for day scholars are not provided by college. Students get in campus rotatory internship facilities as well as job opportunities from other renowned institutes in final year. Students can also avail different scholarships according to their performance based upon their merit. Although there is fee  hike policy every 3 years, but it is fixed by management committee.College organises Grand Annual Fest in the month of September every year. Apart from that students can take part in different inter college sports, music competition held throughout the year. Inter college Online gaming Tournament is well known game event hosted by this college every year. The college has 1 library specially dedicated for e materials of computer science students. Classroom is smart board aided.

The college had a good placements record in the year 2019,2020 and 2021. This year also the highest package offered was of 30 lac per annum. Talking about session 2021-2022, more than 100 companies had visited and out of this 50 companies are new. The students who got selected by these companies were offered an average package of 10.20 lac per annum. The registered number of students in this session was 588 and more than 600 job opportunities were offered to them by the companies. As a whole nit Patna placements had still shown an upward trend. As the session has not ended more companies are likely to visit till march. Hoping for more students to be getting placed in the session 2021-2022.The college had a good placements record in the year 2019,2020 and 2021. The students who got selected by these companies were offered an average package of 10.20 lac per annum.
