college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: There are different no. Of lectures for different course,but generally there are 3 lectures per week. Professors are excellent and also hardworking. They try to teach in very effective manner and also try to increase participation of students. Professors are very interactive in class as well as in personal also. They also clear our doubts time to time.I cleared JEE exam and my rank was 7000. First of all IIT Kanpur has very safe campus,excellent in academics. I had to submit all my documents in two steps. And also had to fill my preference for the college as well as for branch. IIT Kanpur is very rich in culture also. And there are so many other option to explore like robotics, aeromodelling etc

For the batch of 2012???2016 the fee structure was
If you are taking admission via COMED- K:
It was 1. 5L per year (inclusive of 2 semesters). Hostel fees not included in this. For semester end exams again have to give around 1K (200 per subject). If you taking admission via CET:
The fees is around 53k per year. Again this does not involve hostel fees and 1k for semester end exam you have to pay. If you are taking seat via management quota- the fee structure remain same aa that of students who got the admission via comed- k. For management seats you have to amount which vary from branch to branch and that will be one time payment exclusive of the college fees.I can tell you about placements at year 2015:
1200 approx students placed.
Mass recruiters take a chunk of people 200???300 each, package 3???3.5 L.
Mechanical and civil core jobs are few. Highest packagein mechanical 6L (1person), second highest 4.5 L.
CS & IS have companies visit daily in 7th &8th SEM. CS &IS average package 6L, highest double digit figure. BT, medical electronics core jobs are non extinent. For more information please contact MSRIT placement office.

college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Sri City - [IIIT]
review_text: Good with academics but not at all good with the college life. Location is a hit disappointing so as the weather. But the infrastructure is forgivable since the college is under construction. Above average faculty with a good encouragement in the research field. College sport is less and is something which should be taken care of.Seniors have always been helpful and they did result in a change in the juniors. Since I know some of the seniors I know have been here from the time the college started, they gave us good knowledge on how the college is still and under work and how to cope up.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text: The gender ratio is too bad i.e 15:1. Both the campuses have super sports facilities. We have a number of hubs for different activities. Parola and eloquence as literally society handling debates, open mic, MUNs etc, Jhankaar, vamunique, bds for dance, crescendo for singing, Robotics, IEEE, rapid programming, cinematography, abhivyakti and many more. Ample number of societies to keep your college life happening round the year. We have a temple too in the college campus. All the students are from good families, most of them from upper middle class families.The college has two branches, one in sector 62 and another in sector 128. The main campus is in sector 62. The admission criteria is JEE Mains rank. My rank was 67213 in year 2018, so i got admission in sector-128, cse. As JIIT comes in top 100 engineering colleges in India, has a nice location, and I was sure to get cse so I chose to get admission in it. We just have to mail them the required documents along with the application and attend the respective counseling to get admission. Detailed procedure is available on their official site.

college_name: IIT Jodhpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITJ]
review_text: The admission is thourough the joint entrance examination. I gave the JEE exam in the year 2018. For getting admission into IITs one need to appear for JEE mains and need to get a decent rank. Top 2 lakhs candidate are then selected for JEE Advanced. You need to have a rank under 10000 to get admission into any of the IITs. The subject you need to prepare well are Physics, Chemistry and Maths. I am enrolled in Biotechnology course in the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. I decided to choose this course because this course has a great scope in the coming future.We have a good placement statistics. Companies like Microsoft, Adobe, Amazon, Goldman Sachs, Wipro, LnT, TCS and many other big companies visit our campus every year for the placements. Companies from Japan, Indonesia and many other countries also offers job to our students.Our students lands good internship opportunities be it a research internship or Industrial internship. Many goes to Germany and Spain for research internships.

Daily schedule of classes, frequent lab practicals, Conducting exams frequently in a fair way in the way that will definetely have on-campus placements and industrial visits are also organised for students. There are also projects made by students which can be also funded by College management.Every semester, according to the total subjects in the semester will be equal to number of lecturers. The average age of lecturers will be from 30-35 years and Ph. d will be the qualification of many lecturers. Every faculty allows students to their cabins at anytime for clarification.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: I got an average rank of 47,000 in my JEE MAINS. So I had a few private colleges to choose from. What made me choose THAPAR instead of others was that it was specialized in the stream I wanted to choose (Electronics and Computer). Moreover, I know from the beginning that I was interested in Computers and Electronics and I knew it for sure that THAPAR had one of the best faculty for the subjects. I would recommend the college to people who are interested in Computer Science and Electronics. Also, the social life is amazing and great for teenagers. The vicinity near the college offers good restaurants and good food. The food inside the campus is also good with inbuilt messes inside the hostel. So all in all, it is a good place to built your careerThe campus life is amazing and does make a person a much better person when it comes to communication skills. The gender ratio is much better than government engineering college but still, it does need an improvement.There are various extra curricular activities organised by the college including events like singing competitions, dancing competitions, debates,MUN's, coding, robotics, line followers, hackathons etc which encourage the students to improve themselves. Also, the college is not that diverse with students mostly from North India. Also,there is a 50% reservation for students from Punjab which means half of the students are from Punjab (again, their selection criteria is same as that of non -punjab students)

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text: The campus life is really diverse. From academics to social and cultural events, students can indulge themselves in various events. We have techno-management fest Kshitij, Social and cultural fest Spring fest, Global entrepreneurship Summit, Alumni meets and other events as well. All these activities are managed and organised by the 3rd year students, from the ground up to the top. The gender ratio is one thing that lets you down, we have aa drastic difference in number of girls and boys, on an average the gender ratio is around 1:10(1 girl for 10 boys, duh.). We also have inter hall competitions for sports, tech, and social and cultural, involving exciting events like, product designing, musical events, athletics, water polo etc. Our halls compete for the General Championship trophy in every fields. We also have different societies for students with different interests. We have debating society, robotics society, musical society and others too. Just name it and we have it. We recently started anime and game development societies as well. To keep this short, I'd like to quote one of my senior, "IIT Kgp is a blessing in disguise".Everyone knows about the overhyped JEE mains and JEE Advanced for pursuing engineering in India from a government college. And if you don't, in simple language, around 10lac students appear for the exam every year and 1% out of them get selected in the collegesall over the nation. The mains paper is held in the month of December and April (The one in December was announced recently), and the advance paper is held in the month of May. Once you clear both of the exams, you need to fill the college and course preference list, and once you are alloted the seat, you need to visit the nearest IIT(if you got an IIT) or NIT(If you got a seat in any NIT) and accept the seat by getting the documents verified. After this all you need to do is get your bags packed and book your tickets to start your college life.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text:
There are many societies related to debating, music and singing, dance, dramatics, photography and film making, robotics, open-sourcing, soft skills, fests etc that aim at developing the skills of students who join them in first or in some cases the second year. Sports facility in really great. We have a court for all sports and stadiums too. The administration invests a lot in these.
Labs are okay but a little outdated. It will be changed soon as per rumours.
There are also societies for gay community students and to fight homophobia and soreD knowledge about it.
Gender ratio is improving due to women's reservation.
There are also many ethnic groups.
No cases of discrimination on the basis of caste, religion, sex, etc have ever surfaced.The job and placement opportunities are great. Several companies come to recruit in diverse fields, core, non-core I.e. programming, ML and DA, consultancy and Finance. You can choose any of these fields and will always find someone to guide you thourough. Also you can take a project under any prof and continue working on that and publish papers and opt for higher studies. The IIT tag helps a lot.
The internship are not loaded and mostly well paid.

The course curriculum is very good and way far better than other universities as i compared with my friends.The learning is not only the bookish knowledge, you het hands on experience, you write your own codes run them improve them with the help of teachers. There is a place known as explore hub which is damn good built for the ones who want to work on startups and the ones who want to write codes and codes. There are thouree mid term tests out of which you have to attempt at least 2 at the gap of 3-4 weeks and the syllabus is well divided anong them and then the final end term exam. There are many events going on around the college so the learning is not only limited to the classes. The placements are very good at this college as far as i can see as the half of the batch who will graduate in 2021 has already been placed.We have around 5-6 lectures a day and each lecture is of around 50 minutes. The faculty is quite good in teaching. Most of them have done PhD in their respective subjects since aging around mid 30s. They do have industry experience and one or two of our professors even have their own startups such as the learning platforms which they are even using for the college learning.But also there are some teachers who don't know very much about the subject so you'd have to learn it on your own. There are some extra certifications which college is providing in sponsorship in red had, cloud computing.

Bennett univeristy is a univeristy by its own so course is designed by bennett itself with help of some other foreign universities.
We have regurly workshops and my course is more focused towards AI. Moreover we are given both practical as well as research driven knowledge. Ours is a fairly nww univeristy and its was the first year for first batch of placement which went preety well with Microsoft recurting one of our student.Ours is a very new univeristy so there is less no of big companies directly comming to our college. The first batch of Btech passed out this year with bennetts's very first placemnt. Its not too good i think for the first time its okay.
More than 90% of students got placed and more tha 80% got intership in 3rd year.
placement and internship cretieria is based on CGPA

I prepared for Entrance exam which is named JEE-Mains.And I got 202 marks and AIR 5445.This is enough rank to take admission in any NIT and almost any branch there.
After result JOSAA organise an counseling.In which we have to make preferences according to college and course.
I was not much aware of different engineering branches,so did some research on Internet and took advise from my peers.
Good curriculum,good placement,being near to my home, cultural rich were all the factors that made me choose this college.If we talk about curriculum this is outdated and not been updated since years.It's good enough to understand theory part but gives very less hands on experience.
Exam structure is not much practical and needs a lot improvement.
I want to give an example, there are many labs in which we don't see faces of our alloted professor for weeks.they just comes at the end of sem to take exams.
It's need a lot improvement in terms of exam and research.

The campus life is really good. Everyone is friendly. The campus is couple friendly. The campus is lush green. There are many international students and also students from other colleges. Each campus has 3 security guard at the entrance which provides security for every student. There are 2 football fields 3 basketball court for each department. There are courts for tennis, badminton, hockey. We have a whole students activity centre. The campus life is balanced in Sports, Technology and Cultural activities.Kalinga Institute of Industrial Technology hosts different events every year. The KIIT fest is the biggest event that holds every year. Fest is organized by the 2nd-3rd year students. Top music artists from India come every year to perform. Many individual cultural clubs also make events for students to participate. There many technical societies in Kiit that holds special events for students to participate. Hundreds of students participate in this fest.

There are two fest held in my college first technical, literature & cultural and the other is sports. The college budget for the cultural and technical fest is so less about 4-5 lakhs of which technical gets only 15-20 percentage of it. The event of technical need more many for the arena for the event the main event is robo war which need a best arena but due to the budget we can't arrange it. We get the sponsorship but the cultural get the money there is no scheme for technical and literature how do we manage only we know cultural got everything because of Bollywood night and EDM night. But the college should also get funds for technical also. As per student register to the website of festival they can participate.The campus life is best I love my friends. About gender ratio in college it not good about 25-30 percent girls in my course only 3. Scene of labs are also not good there are equipments and machine which do not work and some which are costly so faculty are afraid to run because something happens wrong they have to deal with that. I have participated many robotics competition like manoeuvre, line following Robot, robo war, drones etc in my college and other IITs and NITs etc there are some clubs for these. They are not good.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: The computer science department of thapar is among the best in india. The tentative lecturers are above 300 and average age is around 30. All the faculty members, professors, assistant proffesors etc have completed their doctoral degrees. They have expertise in their respective fiels like database management, networking and coding etc. All of them are holding adeqaute experience and exhaustive research experience in their respective fields.The gender ratio is equal and diverse culture since thapar has students from many cities of north india. There are many extra curricular activities and competition which are much helpful in providing a gap from the daily hectic schedule. There are numerous clubs/societies a student can join and do work for them. We at thapar with diverse culture still live with peace with people befriending students irrespective of caste or religion.

The fee structure is feasible. As a DASA student, getting in thourough the ciwg quota gives you a fee waiver which reduces the fees to the same fees the JOSAA students pay. The college does provide transparency in the fee breakdown. However there is an extra Rs. 500 every single student in the college has to pay for along with the tuition fee. They say the money is for SpringSpree. The money doesn't go into good use.There are 2 main big festivals. One is a technical festival called Technozion. Other colleges are invited for the festival and there are many events within the festival. The other festival is a cultural one called spring spree. There are many cultural events in which prizes can be won. There is also a management festival called Cura. It is however mainly for the students doing their Masters.

It's really a good college with great opportunity to get a good job in life and also go for any research field if you are interested for further study and degree. Also gives separate training program to appear for several competitive exams like Gate, Cat etc.Other co curricular activities like sports, music etc are very well taught. Dedicated students also take it as their own profession. Even dancing, photography, singing, debate etc are also done by taking several events respectively.

I live in varanasi. I had a lot of options for the college. I gave the entrance exam for many colleges I got selected in most of them but I got best reviews of the the college I am in. I gave the entrance exam which was an online exam from a centre. Thourough which I got selected and offered subject was cse.There are lot of cultural and sports event in our college. Our college produces a lot of sports celebrity like dutee chand. This year our college hosted the khelo india national game. There are lot of event s in which students van participate.

college_name: Jaypee Institute of Information Technology University - [JIIT]
review_text:
Steps:-
1. You have to qualify jee mains. 2. Fill the application form of the college Which will be available on the college Website and send it via post/mail. 3. According to the applications received College will prepare a merit list and you Will be called for counselling. 4. You will be awarded seats in various Courses as per availability. 5.Once counselling is done, after two weeks interval registration and welcome to my college. I chose btech Cse because I love it.According to me, course and curriculum are good, thouree exams are held per semester first two of 20 marks each and third of 35 marks rest 25 teachers assessment. Without assignments, quizzes, lab tests engineering is incomplete. If you manage time wisely life will be chilled.

We have to complete 180 credits at the end of our 4th year. The most unique thing about VIT is that it follows the FFCS system. That is the fully flexible credit system. According to this, we can choose what subjects we want to study in that particular semester and we can also select the session we want to attend i. e morning or evening. And we also have the pleasure of selecting our own teacher. Therefore it is upon us whether we want to make our semester hectic or not.The main events of our college are Gravitas and Riviera. Gravitas is a technical event and there are almost 600 events where we get to learn something about the various things taught by chapters and clubs. Riviera is the annual fest that happens in VIT and it is one of the biggest fests of India and it happens for 4 consecutive days. Every day more than 1 or 2 celebrities come to perform here like ayushman Khurana and Sonu Nigam and many more.

Placement are pretty decent around here. Most of the reputed companies like FACEBOOK, AMAZON, MICROSOFT, GOLDMAN SACHS, ADOBE are some of the companies that visit every year in the college to provide internship and placements to the students. Core branches like mechanical engineering also have good placement. Some of the companies which visit this college are TATA MOTORS, TATA STEEL, HINDALCO, MARUTi SUZUKi to name a few.The course curriculum has recently been updated. It now follows CBCS (Choice Based Credit System) which offers much flexibility to the students. It offers program elective right from 3rd semester which helps the students to take courses based on their interest. It also offers minor degree in other branches which is vey good initiative looking the current trend where you require to have knowledge in multiple domains.

Placement are one of the best in the country and certainly the best in the state of Karnataka So every student has a great job opportunity. Yes internship are provided in our college. Since I am in my second year I have not applied for any internship in any company. I don't have full idea of all the company that visit our college as I am in my 4 th sem now. But the few companies that come are quolcom, analog devices Samsung etc for our branch.The College overall is a great technical institution to persue engineering and masters, the infrastructure is on of the best in the country. its spread across vast area of about 55 acres and as has all the basic and luxury feature in the campus. And lastly the institution is well know for its placement and repute in the it world.

Course curriculam in my college follows aicte course curriculam.but aicte curriculum is more of bookish knowledge rather that practical knowledge.there are two internal took place in each semester and than main sem exam took place.even semester ends in may and odd sem ends in december. Lab exam consist of any one practicals which we did in lab we have to perform.viva took place regarding practical exam subject.then semester exam can easily be cleared with good marks if you attend classes regularly.Some Teachers are good with good knowledge while some are not good.but overall i can say teachers are nice and helpful.some teacher teach their subject in interesting way and enhance the student interest towards subject while some come and give lecture on some topic and leave the class, But majority teachers are good.

Our curriculum aims at providing the basic knowledge of all the branches to all the students before teaching the core subjects. Afterwards it gives us both the theoretical as well as the practical knowledge of our branches pretty well. Industrial visits, seminars, guest lectures and lab sessions give us an all round knowledge. The exam pattern is also satisfactory which aims at checking the knowledge of the student and what all he can retain from whatever is taught in the class. It is intermediate, not a hectic neither a chill one.Our college provides fee consessions on institute fees for the ones who need them. The institute fees for the SC/ST candidates is free. For the candidates who have an yearly income less than 5 lakhs, a consession of two-third of the fees is provided. For income less than 2 lakhs, full fee is refunded. Students can also avail for education loan from several banks

I got admission in this college thourough online counseling process conducted by WBJEE( West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination) Board. Regarding the councelling process :
Students can fill their choices of Stream and College in the descending order of their priority.
For example, a student will fill like
1. CSE in Jadavpur University
2. CSE in Institute of Engineering and Management.
3. ECE in Jadavpur University
.
.
.
This means that student wants the 1st choice first, if not possible then 2nd choice, and so on.
Students are allotted the best possible choice of stream and college according to availability of seat and better rank holder gets the opportunity first to fulfill his /her choice.
After getting the final allocation of stream and college, I went to that college to get admission. I was not interviewed at the time of admission process. I had to submit the fees of 1st semester and other one time charges and fees to complete my admission process.Students are mainly placed in IT companies regardless of stream with an average package of 3.5 LPA.
But few students from each batch become able to grab jobs of high salaries(highest package was of 40 LPA offered by Adobe) with their own talent and hardwork mainly thourough off campus placement.
Our college doesn't provide internship. But HODs of each stream help the students to get free internship in different companies and IITs by recommendation. Again, good students are able manage paid internships by their own.

Everything is good and beautiful about our University, but according to be there is one negative remark too. There are very restrictions for girls in comparison to boys in case of hostel entry timing, and girls have a rule to apply leave before 6hours, which is not there in case of boys.There are so many time of events goes on in the University, like one India, one world, visstar, youth vibe, tech fest, different types of marathons, effi cycle competition among the Universities of India, the most grand fest among these are youth vibe and effi cycle

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: Fees is 120000 for general and obc category and 6400 for sc and st categories. This fees structure also varies according to the family income status if a family is earning more than 5 lakhs per annum then they have to pay the whole fees, if the family annual income is less than 5 lakhs but more than 1 lakh they have to give 33% of total fees. And for family having annual income less than 1 lakh have full relaxation in tution fees.this is fees for a semester.Course structure is hectic. We have 3 labs in a week and one building drawing class and 3 lecture of each 5 subjects.In each semester we have 1 mid semester exam, quiz and one end semester exam. Since I am civil engineering student and our institute is quite old and bkg so our labs are well equipped with equipments.staff is adequate in ratio of the students studying.

The selection is purely based on merit. There is an Entrance examination conducted by Amrita. It's an Objective paper which can be taken either online or offline. After clearing the entrance examination, seat allocation is based on all india rank. There is more than 5 campuses to choose. There is also home state quota. The counseling session is offline.Amrita is the best place where you can be. It is safe for all individuals. The climate here is too good. And the campus is lush green and energetic. Academic wise it is the best you can get.
The stay in here is also too good. You get to mingle with diverse people and learn new cultures and languages.

The course curriculumn is perfect, it provides every lab from concrete to aerospace, botany it has plenty if good faculties also provides research opportunity to enthusiasts, regular mid term exams before the majors are conducted that give an insight of preparation status of students and the checking system is also authentic.Placements are good as told by seniors, this year the highest package was of 11 lac offered by Extramarks to the student of 4th year, rest average of 6 lakh p.a. to most of the atudents who sat for placement interviews, CRC arranges internships to 3rd year student in very reputed companies like AT&T.

The students are required to write the PESSAT or KCET examinations and take up seats in courses on the merit of their ranks. There are only two rounds of councelling in PESSAT but with higher chances of getting a seat and multiple rounds of councelling in KCET but lower chance of securing seat. It is a prestigious college in Bangalore and is ranked #1 private university in Bangalore and #2 college in BangaloreThe college has state of the art sports infrastructure. The college has a full sized Cricket ground which hosts a lot of tournaments. The college is well equipped with two tennis courts two basketball courts two badminton courts a tt room and indoor game facilities. The labs are well equipped with best apparatus and facilities that enable the students to learn as well as do research projects in their free time

The Gender ratio is BAD. For instance in our batch of 56 we have 10 girls only. The fests and college events are amazing. The fresher's farewells and reunions are also very well organized every time. We have Debating Society, Dance Club, Music Club, Drama Club, Photography Club, Science Club, Mountaineering Club, Quiz Club and Painting club as well. Freedom is atmost priority in our campus with regards to anything. Very low gender/race/religion intolerance. Students from Bihar and Bengal study together. Students are politically aware and active. An average student is below middle class to middle class family.1. SRIJAN- Annual Tech fest of Jadavpur University. Largest in the city. Participation from around Kolkata and Odisha Bihar Jharkhand. 2. Sanskriti- Annual Cultural fest of Jadavpur University. 9 day longest college fest in Asia. A crowd of 30k from around the state and country. Apart from these, we have a lots of clubs and societies which hold their events. 1. Jadavpur University Model United Nations. Largest MUN in Eastern India. Hosted by Debating Society. 2. ESUMMIT by JU ECell.
3. IBTIDA by Music and Dance club

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: Our Alumni network is very strong as our college is 100 year old. and they are very helpful. In the institute an Alumni Visiting faculty Program is running in which reputed alumni come to college on alternative Saturday and Sunday and teach courses like economics and finance, media and environment. Our seniors are very helpful, we also have a Technical family system in which a particular roll number is a tech family in itself. 4th year called as great grand Techdad, 3rd - grand Techdad, 2nd year - Techdad, 1st year - tech son.we have 4 annual college festivals, Spardha- Sports, FMC Weekend- Film and Media, Kashiyatra- Cultural, Technex - Techno Management. with around 2000+ participants in Each event and around 60, 000 Footfall during the fest. Fest are big and stars like Guru Randhawa, KK, Salim Sulaiman, Ritviz, Divine, Shirley Setia, Zakhir Khan, Nishant Suri, Akash etc, have come to perform in the fest.

The college doesnot provide any loan on direct there is an sbi bank or other banks which provide the students with the study loans for their study but the scholarship are provided by the college and government for the economically backward studentsCourse is good according to me the way college pursues the interests of the students and exam structure is very tough there were 90 questions which carry 4 marks each and need minimum marms to qualify for the college admission

Extracurricular activities are good. I wouldn't say the best. We have to go thourough a lot of permissions to do something in college and most of them don't get approved. But college tries the best to keep something going on in college. Sports department does really well. Labs are good. Students stay back after college hours to indulge in lab sessions. We don't see any problems wrt ethical, race and religion in college.We have many events happening in our college. There are many clubs which are active. We have our annual cultural fest called MAAYA. There is interdepartment Activity day. Intercollegiate sports day. Interdepartment sports day. Hackathons every semester. Tedxpesuecc. And the club activities. We see a lot of participation in these events. Organizing team makes sure every year they do the best.

Placement in Lovely Professional university is very nice. Each course in this university provides a great placement with good salaries. Many famous companies like Google, Yahoo, Microsoft, Facebook,etc are visiting the campus. Companies set a CGPA criteria and take interviews to place students. 90% of the students are placed each year. Before placement university does a soft skills class which improves the confidence of interview.In computer science lovely Professional university is a fabulous university. It has a quality type of teachers which explains every concept in a very detailed manner and is Very helping. All the syllabus are well designed to start from a beginning and become a job ready. The flow of the course and Teachers explain everything in detail which leads to become a better professional.

Every year there will many events in this events main are fresher's day and annual fest sports meet and convocation ceremony for graduated students annual fest will be celebrated for 3 days celebraties are invited to this ceremony and on founders day there will be a grand cultural evening for students and teachers and staff.As iam studying electrical and electronics engineering as I know this is the tough branch in any college the course curriculum is good easy updated syllabus as compared to other universities there will be placement classes from 2nd year for students to attend placement training for good career selection.

Course curriculum is good, not so stressful. 1st year consist of a bit advanced subjects from your 12th. 2nd year subject starts focussing on coding and other subjects such as Linux, OS, DBMS and the intensity keeps on increasing year by year. As for the labs, tey are less in 1st year(2 per week ) but of second year it gets to nearly 5 per week( as subjects are mostly coding). As of exam structure, each subject has 4 fa's 20 marks each (best of thouree for consideration), 3 sessional tests each of 40Marks (best of two for consideration ) and a final examination (60M).The labs are well maintained and spacious accomodating nearly 70 students per lab. There are many labs in our campus from computer labs, electronics labs to fluid dynamics lab and physics labs.the sports room is also very spacious with space for two badminton courts, 4 table tennis courts, 2 pool tables. They also have a gym in the same area. Other than that the campus has a wide open ground for football, cricket and running.they also have a basketball court, lawn tennis court and a volleyball court.

In VIT, all the courses are divided into 4 types i.e. Program Core, Program Elective, University Core, University Elective. Any core subject is a mandatory subject. However, electives can be taken up according to your interests. VIT allows for a flexible credit system so you can advance your skills in your preferred domain of interest. There are 3 examinations per semester, CAT-1, CAT-2, and FAT. CAT-2 is often an open book examination and is entirely application based. The project component involves the student to think out of the box and deepen their learning on the subject and are hence helpful in placements. Industrial visits are also organized by the faculties.The VITEEE (pronounced "witty") examination takes place a few days after the JEE Mains examination. VIT University sends a booklet to your address with some sample questions of the VITEEE examination. It involves MCQ from Math, Physics, Chemistry, and also 5 questions of English. It involves the application of simple formulae and is very easy as compared to JEE Mains. The admission process is based upon the ranks you get in the VITEEE examination. Each branch consists of 5 categories which have different fee structure. The seats start filling up rank wise during the counseling in all 4 branches of the University- Vellore, Chennai, Bhopal, and Amaravati.

Course curriculum and exam structure are quite helpful for the students. The students are given their priorities of choosing their timetables and teachers of their choice. It is research driven and the exam structure is well planned by the Administration. It is an industry based curriculum and there are industrial visits organized every semester.Campus is a huge one to discover. The gender ratio is quite interesting here because girls are equal to boy's. Extracurricular activities are very interesting in this college. There is a wide scope of activities candidate can avail in here. There different chapters and clubs in this college to entertain such activities.

Campus Life is too good.One will enjoy their 4 years of college.The gender ratio is bad in Faculty of Engineering but it's good in Faculty of arts And Science.
Extracurricular activities are there in the college and if you want to take part then there are many clubs and societies are there in college.Yes course curriculum is quite good. It's not too heavy or light the way teacher arrange the subject are quite good to enhance the skills one by one. The practical classes are there to give students practical knowledge about subject and also there is academic tour

Yes, I am a student of this college. There is an entrance exam known as VITEEE which is generally after the 12th-grade board exams. VIT releases an application form that is supposed to be filled by the applicant with necessary details such as the Aadhaar number, permanent address, etc. There are a fixed number of options for the slot and dates. Choose the one suitable for you and give the exam. A few weeks later, the results are announced and there is a date assigned for counseling. Once you attend the counseling, you will be shown the number of seats left for each course depending on your rank. Choose the appropriate course and your details will be taken. The fee structure, the allotment, the ID card photo would be discussed with you. I have opted for computer science. I am interested in data science and I want to learn the appropriate course necessary for that domain. Vellore Institute of Technology is a college that offers exposure with ample opportunities and chances for students to prove their worth. The teaching, the environment, and the management have always been well organized and combined units with experience in the field of education.The placements are a huge factor in the reputation VIT has earned. Companies such as Microsoft, Amazon, PayPal ranging from multinationals to startups have visited the campus for their placement drives. The students are asked to first register for placements if they are willing to sit for interviews. There is a special placement cell that guides students for placement related activities. There is specialized training that starts around February or March that focuses on training students for the placement process. After a few months, companies visit the campus and placements take place. Approximately, 74% of the students have been placed.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: It is national scholarship and first we need it to be apply in the national scholarship portal and after than we should take the print out of the form and then it need to be signed in department and then need it to be submitted in the scholarship sectionThis is the best course one could have ever thought about and this is the core of all the remaining branches every branch is thus connected by mechanical branch and iit bhu mechanical is just worse department because of the faculty we have

student gender ratio us 50 - 50, there is no unequality for each of the gender, In every field every student is given opportunity to participate and further progress in all fields. there are many extra curricular activities such as in field of robotics, we use to visit many colleges and win prizes in desired fields. we also participate in motor sports competitions,debates, many small events1.Grafest:- it's a college fest, A three day fest where there are stalls, dj,night parties, visits of celebrities( last year the dj Don diablo, singer Neeti mohan and Hardy sandhu visited our university.
2. diwali carnival:- it is similar to grafest also called as mini grafest, but the difference is just the celebrity doesn't visit

Vit Bhopal is a new branch of VIT(Vellore institute of technology) which is situated near Bhopal and right now I don't have any idea regarding the job opportunities and placement on our campus but in Vellore and Chennai, they always have good placement and get a good job. Although they haven't provided us yet in our campus teachers were telling us that they might provide an internship at the end of 3rd yearSports and labs that are provided to students are good. We don't have any dorm parties and there is definitely no tolerance in regards to gender in my college. It has a PDA fine which has quite a big amount. Even holding hands b/w two friends of a different gender is not allowed other than that my colleagues have quite a large no. Of diversity in terms of cultures, religion, etc.

The alumni network is extremely large. Every year, our college has a regular alumni meet where all the present and pass-out students come together. Even if there were some faculty who have left the college due to various purposes, they also come to this meet. The seniors too are very good. There is absolutely no ragging in our college and a very co-operative and positive environment prevails. I have met a senior through a friend who is like my big brother now. I go to him for almost every advice I need and he treats me like my little sister.The courses are extremely good and have been done keeping in mind the latest technologies and research going on in the world. The faculties are extremely helpful and always ready to assist you. As far as the exam structure is concerned, in the mid of the session you have you mid-semester examinations(20 marks) and at the end of the semester you have the end semester examinations(50 marks). The rest 30 marks are internal marks where your assignments, regularity and maintenance of class notes would fetch you those marks.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: List of companies that visited the campus for their placement drives, for your course are Honda, Hero, Godrej etc. the general steps followed in the recruitment drive of these companies are written test and interviews. the tentative percentage of students placed every year from your campus is 100%? Yes the institution take special steps to prepare the students for the placement season.I Got the admission in this college through JEE. I opt for this course because I love mechanical engineering. the factors that made you choose this institution is because it is one of the India's premium institution. the steps followed as a part of the admission procedure for the institution is good rank in JEE and counseling.

The placement and job opportunities in KIIT are great as the main aim of the institution is to provide a good placement along with a decent package to it's students. The College also provides internships from 6th semester onwards. The companies that visit KIIT for placements are Capegemini, High Radius, My Sigma, Infosys, TCS etc. The package is usually between 6 and 10 lakh per annum for the students initially.There are a lot of events happening in KIIT at any given time. They are mostly organized by the students as a member of different societies with KIIT. I have personally had the pleasure of organizing a national level quiz in KIIT as part of the quizzing society of KIIT (Qutopia). The events are usually big and see participation for various people nationally as well as internationally.

it's infrastructure is good enough. 98% of students should not be here. they're just a burden on the college. should've been built in a more sophisticated locality rather than a jat majority one. admin sucks. period. professors suck. period. students suck. period. placements rock. period. infrastructure rocks. period.fests are huge here. although management sucks. everyone is allowed to come since it helps the sponsor. its a jat majority area so there's no way I'm inviting my female friends in engifest(cultural) since some of the big name artists perform in it. yuvaan(literary) is good though.

The course curriculum is advanced and filled with updated courses, the course structre will be different from that of other colleges. One needs to strive hard to survive this kind of curriculum with th competition being faced here.
Here the grading system is relative that is the pass Mark depends on the class highest and also on class average. So this is quite a new thing for us. Coming to examination pattern we will be having 3 cats per sem for 50 marks which be open notebook(you should understand the level of examination, not as simple as you think) and depending on the course you will also have lab evaluationThere will be tech and cultural events being organised on every Mondays and Saturdays. Apart from that we will be having a 3 day cultural fest named Vitopia which gets country wide recognition and also a 2 day tech fest vtapp conducted every year
We will get a chance to meet the alumni during these fests and we interact a lot with them pre and post fest which I found very helpful.

it is one of the thing that lpu do in most interesting ways.after all this is the most important part for any University.
Lpu has many exams like CA,mid term practical,and finally end term.
if you want to score well you have to work hard and study regularly it is not like you will study one day before exam and can score well.overall if i say about my college is that it is the best college in india you have many opportunities here in academic, sports and many more other thing.The fees is feasible and faculty here is just awesome and helping nature. They have different digital platform that you can use

The institute has a full- time faculty strength of nearly 200 professors. There are highly qualified professors, many of them being Ph. D. ???s and IITians themselves. Apart from this, there are 500 full- time research scholars. Some of them have even completed their higher studies from universities prestigious across the globe such as Cranfield University, England, University of Florida, University of Southampton etc.Yes, alumnI network are very helpful. The AlumnI already employed in reputed organizations in India and abroad help the final year students in their project work and also inform them about the employment opportunities in their respective organizations. The University in turn provides free access to the library and assists budding AlumnI entrepreneurs like you to incubate your technology venture.

Technosis is the technical fest celebrated in college in every odd semester. And there are lots of events in it. Incandesanse is the fest celebrated in every even semester in college. Apart from them there are lots of events that are celebrated in college.NSP scholarship Provided by central government and it is only to those who have secured above 98percent of marks in 12th standard. From 2nd year onwards it will be based on our cgpa. If we have cgpa above 7 then we will get scholarships.

Basically, I am interested in learning stuff about computers. Though I chose Biology as my main subject in higher education. I love to learn about computer science. So I chose this M.Tech Software Engineering. Now why I chose this VELLORE INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY. Here are some points 1) The infrastructure they have is very fantastic which keeps me refreshing and motivated every day. 2) Teaching professionals who make me feel comfortable to learn more. 3) The Surrounding and the classmates where I met different people from different countries. 4) The practical knowledge about each and every topic in my curriculum. 5) The projects we are doing for every subject give me a clear understanding of concepts. Now the steps of admission procedure 1) At first I applied for three courses but I chose only one in this same University through an application form which was offered a month after the 12th exams get over. 2) They allotted date and called me for counseling. 3) Punctually I attended the Counseling where the higher officials asked me some technical questions. 4) After two or three weeks I received a mail that I approved for the selected course. 5) After three months the college started with orientation program and i am provided with my unique registration number.The campus life is fantastic where are set free to work and study. Usually a group of friends contains both boys and girls so no ragging or some undisciplinary activities can be seen. There are many workshops regularly conducted by many departments some are free of cost and some are paid. These workshops are informed us by mails. Since we all are belong to same country we don't separate ourselves by any caste or religion. We follow all cultural activities. No racism or religious flights between our students. The management teaches us good discipline in order to obey all religion and community. There is no sexual teasing between the students or any political conversation or fights among us. We all United to learn discipline. Though our economic status vary for all we all share the discipline and all to make peace and friendship.

Coming to campus life we have a very rich diversity with large number of students from different states and countries, everyone let us to communicate easily, we have various labs to explore our knowledge and try something new with many costly equipment imported from abroad, coming to ratio there are large number of male population like in 3:1 ratio, with different kinds of race and religion, irrespective of caste everone is really very freindly and helpfull without any conflicts, as we can see the fee structre there will be large number of high class students.
We have many clubs such as techinal club which includes Machine learning, AI, ROBOTICS. Sports club, dance, music, tostmaster, theather, arts, photography etc which helps us to boost our creativity skills, we have debates, Jam etc in literature club etc. We have competitions such as tech quizes and on a club activities.After attempting mains i couldn't fet expected mark so i want to join a university which is of that standards and i have chosen SRM UNIVERSITY AMARAVATHI, coming to my admission process first i wrote srmjee exam and then because of my merit in the exam i got a call from university to attend counselling process with all my valid certificates and DD of 2.5 lakhs as tution fee.
I have choosen ECE because I thought that it would help me to cover both electronics and conputer science and i got the best review from senoirs of my college about ECE so that made me to choose that course, apart from other college SRM UNIVERSITY gives students opinion the utmost priority, it gives the freedom to choose our own electives teachers and all, coming ro labs they are the best you can find all over INDIA

Every September, there will be a technical fest called gravitas which is a national-level event in which several technical workshops will be conducted, where students get hands-on experience in stuff like iot, ai, ml, etc. Students from any college can participate in gravitas.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text: I am currently a 2nd year B.tech Student, in METALLURGY BRANCH. I got admission in 2018 through JOSAA (based on JEE mains)counselling in 2nd special round. The admission in PEC is only through JOSAA counselling. Firstly student have to fill JOSAA councselling and have to fill for various branches. Our college have total of 8 branches namely-: Aerospace, Civil Engineering, Computer Science Engineering,Electrical,Electronics and communication,Mechanical, Materials and Metallurgy,Industrial and Production. I had chosen Metallurgy branch, as i was having interest in this field and got admission in 2nd special round. At the time of reporting student has to bring some original documents like Aadhar card, 10 and 10+2 Marksheets, Birth Certificate,Migration Certificate,Character Certificate(issued from school). At the time of admission student has to pay one semester fee and Hostel fee(if opted), Chandigarh students are not allowed for Hostels. And after this process student get a fee receipt for further process at hostel.Our College conducts one of the biggest witnessing a total footfall of 45,000,Techno-Cultural Event of North India i.e. PECFEST. It is a marvelous event of 3 days comprising many of technical and cultural events.Many societies organize their events in this fest and winners are rewarded and awarded handsomely. On last night of fest always there a star night, previous it was GURU RANDHAWA who banged the night, so much fun there. Besides this our college organizes an event named OPEN HOUSE, where student share their ideas about innovation and what they want to do etc. And, also a 3 day sports meet is also conducted every year where college witness a massive participation from students of college. Other events are organized by the technical and Cultural clubs we have Dramatics club, Music Club for self development we have societies-: VOYAGERS(event organizing), IMAGES(event organizing), Les Amis, NCC, NSS. These all give a nice surrounding to the students and one should take part in any one at least.

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text: Got into the College through jee mains and got seat in this College and this was the best branch i could get. So i opted for ece in mnnit Allahabad after getting my rank in jee mains entrance exam. I was preparing for IIT but it turns out this was also a good College.Some of the faculties are good and well trained. Whereas most of these faculties don't want to teach or they teach in an old fashion which in not understandable to any children. So most of the times, we have to study ourselves.

Usually, VIT refers to the Vellore institute of technology conducts an entrance exam every year for engineering and other branches through which the admission process takes place. Usually, the exam takes place between March-May time for which one should fill an application form which will be opened by December on Vit's official website or you can get it offline through if one visits College. The application fee may be around Rs1400/- at my time. For BTech entrance exam is compulsory and based on the marks scored merit list is given through which one can join based upon his rank. Whereas for my course Mtech software engineering integrated one doesn't need to write an entrance exam but the merit list is based on a 10+2(PUC) percentage of Maths, physics, chemistry. Based on your rank there are ranks allotted and a specific counseling date given on which you must decide under choosing the College branch (Place) basically Vellore is the first priority, next comes Chennai, Amaravati, Bhopal. There are nearly 5 categories of fees that are given based upon one's rank. I got 93% in My 10+2 and I got the Chennai branch in the 2nd category where Vellore seats were done. So one get easily get into Vellore institute of technology as there are many categories of fees ranging from 1lakh to 5lakh. But if you want to get a low category fee then your rank must below to get the main campus i.e Vellore. But one needs to remember in order to get admission one should compulsorily submit the application form without that you can never get into College.Vit follows a different curriculum i.e as it a deemed University it has its own one. Basically, the course curriculum in Vit is really good. Like for example, the course curriculum is prepared as if it is connected to a real-life problem and in a practical way. For example, I have many courses like Programming language Java, Software architecture for each one we have a J i.e project and lab where one can take real-life examples and connect it to theory. The exams in Vit as usually the same as others we have two internal exams i.e Cat(Continuous assessment test) where CAT-2 will be an open book exam. Though it is an open book it will be a bit tough. And finally one final assessment test for lab and theory. If one has a project component the evaluation is based upon the review submitted. And we have three Assignments which is the only one thing one can score directly and easily. So totally it will be like 30% for Assignments, 30% of CAT, 40% of FAT. So one doesn't need to worry about CAT marks. But should make it good in Assignments and FAT. If one fails to clear a course then it's a different way in Vit, like you should re-register the course again with a fee of Rs6000/- and go for all classes and write the exam again. So please be a bit careful with your marks.

The course structure is okay. But depends on the department you choose and your likes and dislikes.
Each semester we have two class tests and one semester examination for each course. Mostly the daily routine is not that hectic and you have plenty of time in hand to do something productive at home. But during exams it gets hectic with lots of syllabus to cover at the last days (if you are like me?).Our University is known for big events like Sanskriti, Srijan, Jadavpur University Photofest, JUMUN, TEDx etc.
Most of these are absolutely free for participation. Though we don't get as big sponsors as many other engineering Colleges in the country, but the alumni help us in arranging everything for pulling off these events at such scale.

All the teachers are not bad, but most of them are mediocre. They give us the question paper for midterm and end-term exams coming from the question banks. No one knows how teachers check copies. The exam cell is unreliable. If you have a good relationship with teachers, then you will get good marks. The course is up to date, and they try hard to make students industry-ready. But the fault is at the teaching level. Teachers always threat students that they will cut our marks in the exams. Techniques devise by the college are just basically to harass students. Students have to maintain at least more than 75% of attendance, if not, then you are debarred, and you can't sit in exams. You will have to wait till summer breaks and give them an insane amount of money for each subject only to stay here and give the exam later. Their rules tend to change frequently, and no-one knows what's happening. To get one work done, you have to go from one place to another. The course is relevant, and if someone does works smartly with the course, then you will get a good salary package.Overall I would not recommend anyone to go to this college. Because the college management will not help you in any way. You will suffer even if it is your mistake or even if the college management did any mistake. If you talk to any student currently studying they will also give you a hundred reasons not to take admission here. If you are in any problem then the college management won't help you and that's final. They didn't even pay the cleaning staff of the college for which they protested also. And one of the worst problems is the local students who are ready to fight with you for no reason at all and a lot of other students who ruin each and every fest and party organized in university.

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text:
College have good placements record. Almost every branch observe placement percentage of more then 75%, in which cse students get the highest packages. Mainly the recruiters of the college demand software profiles so, many students prepare for software irrespective of there branch. Internship opportunities also have the same scenario as that i have described for the placementsGender ratio is 1 girl per 5 boys
There are strict rules and regulations in the college against ragging.
Over the year so many technical and cultural activities take place in the college in which students get opportunities to hone there extra curricular skills.
College has a very peaceful environment and there is no discrimination on the basis of gender, cast or money.

You got admission in this University through several ways.
1. University conduct his entrance exam whose name is Lovely Professional University National Entrance and Scholarship Test (LPU NEST) which happens usually in 1 st week of April through this exam you can get upto 80-90% scholarship.
2. If you don't give LPU NEST exam then you can try JEE Mains marks basis for B.tech courses only.
3. University also take admission on the basis of 12 percentage unusually it's criteria is 90 % or more than 90% (for direct admission). I got admission through LPU NEST it is very convenient and i also got scholarship of 40% per semester through this exam.Fee Structure is varies on the basis of which course you have to opted, now In my case i am a student of B.TECH, CSE so my Fee is 95000/semester but i am getting 40000 scholarship/semester so it is ok, but some student not getting scholarship then the fee structure is expensive for them on the other hand some student get scholarship 75000/semester so in his case the fee structure is feasible.
so overall conclusion is if you get scholarship then it is feasible for you otherwise it is expensive.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text: The course curriculum considering that this is the oldest of all IITs is kind of outdated in some cases. Although most of the subjects that I have come across are in some way or other inclined towards engineering research fields but the fact that in a time where we have so many CAD softwares and simulation softwares yet we have to draw using pen paper in most labs is kind of hectic. The exams are kind of tedious and tough although labs and classes are mostly evenly spaced out and gives ample time for one to explore interests.The two major fests include Khsitij which is Asia's largest techno management fest. Sees participation of students from all over the continental in fields like robotics, business studies, data analytics, software and many more. The other is spring fest which is a cultural fest flocked by people from all over the country in what is called as the best 3 days in KGP sees dance music beatbox and a load of fun activities. Apart from these there are department fests and also a global entrepreneurship summit.

we have so many events that happen in the campus
1.YouthVibe (youthvibe is one the biggest youth festive in the campus where student from different university participate in different category of the event its has highest number of participants and audience)

During my 2 year i got a internship at company in Delhi where my stipend was 7000 per month for 3 months the internship provided by the university are so helpful for the student to have real life cooperate experience that help me Choose my minor and major subject combination.

Our college conducts two big fest annually one is innovision and resonance and MOKSHA. Moksha is conducted in march. It has fruitful of almost 60k veiwers and participants every year. It is 3 day fest with star night on last day of fest. If you are in a society of college then these fest are like festival to you also everyone give their best to represent college in front of others.The fest contains cultural , literature and technical events.Maximum faculties are PhD holders.Mostly supportive, clear every doubt related to theory  and also tells research opportunities of subject.The teachers always encourage you to do research in your subject. In the starting of semester all teacher will suggest you best book to be followed.The college also provide a black book which contains course and subject list with syllabus and book to be followed.

college_name: Punjab Engineering College University of Technology - [PEC]
review_text: I knew I would be joining mechanical engineering as I was fascinated by mechanical physics and it was the only thing I wanted to do. The entire admission process is explained stepwise as under: First of all, you need to fill a form for jee mains exam,this exam is based on the knowledge of your pcm subjects and need to get in merit. Then you need to apply for jossa application and choice fill for the PEC UNIV. Of Technology along with the stream choice as mechanical engineering. Wait for the different stages of counsellings. Got the confirmation of my name in the merit list and went to college on the dates of further registration and documents verification by faculty. Got a student id and enrolled into the course. Talking about the course,The course structure is sufficient enough to generate skills in the students to enhance a student's career.The course offer good knowledge on technical subjects which help students in advancing their careers further. Labs are a chill ,further simmens also has a research facility in the institution thereby promoting research. There are two sems every year and seem last 4-5 months .exams are a headache due to less availability of time but cool because not long lasting we have midsems- 2 each day without any day gap and one each day for endsems. exams have generally 4-5 questions.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: Many companies visit our institute to hire students. Top companies are microsoft , Google, Facebook , Adobe , Goldman Sachs , nutanix, flipkart, Amazon . Training and placement cell arranges placement session and every year many giants tech and core companies visit our college. Placement at IIT BHU is one of the best thing about it. Students get highly paid for brances like CSE or ECE packages average about 25lakhs per annum.Our college mainly has three fests. kashiyatra, which is annual socio-cultural fest. Technex , which is annual technical fest and Spardha which is annual sports fest . This fest are considered as one of the greatest fests of North India having footfall of around 60,000. Many great artist visit in this fests some examples are kk, ritwiz, jubin nautiyal , Salim -suleiman. All together fests are really wonderful.

Since childhood, I enjoyed doing stuff related to electronics. Also, my father being an electronics engineer motivated me a lot. After attending the symbiosis counselling, there was not a second thought and I decided to take admission in this college. The moment you enter the college you fall in love with it. The aura, ambience is excellent. The teachers are all PhD or undergoing PhD so you don't have to worry on the academic part.Faculty is very well trained and knows what is to be taught to students. They are also involved in many research work and if you are a good student you can be part of their team. The class is very interactive, so you shall never feel backhanded. Last but not least the assessment pattern is strict and adequate which I think is necessary for us.

There are a lot of events that are celebrated in our college. First of all, it is 
1)The Reverb-which takes place in the month of September and a lot of celebrities visit our college at that time. In this a lot of events are there like fashion shows,comedians coming and entertaining.
2)Techfest were students show their creative ideas and create events where people from anywhere can come and participateI got admission into this college through the set paper which is conducted in the month of may at different institutes. The counseling was held on 3rd June for open category students. On that, according to our score, we were getting branches. Mine score was 106 so I had It ,Etc And Mechanical branch available. But my interest was in electronics so I opted for it.

Campus Life is great. You will meet students from all parts of the country and we even have many international students. Sports also has a very crucial role as we an annual sports fest 'Prota' where all the branches compete to be the best. The college also has a robotics team that competes in various events.I got admission through the SET SITEEE. Symbiosis Institute of Technology has a very good course of B tech which provides a great opportunity for students to get hands-on and experience practical application of the subjects. Admissions are based on marks and the seats are allocated in the counseling session

I had opted for this course as I wanted to study any stream related to technology whether it be electronics or computer science or information technology. I chose this institution as I always wanted to study in Pune and Symbiosis had its name in the field of electronics for years. When I visited their website, I was completely convinced to study in this college. When I had applied for college, the entrance exam was over. I had applied through my JEE score. After a few days, I received a confirmation email from the administration and then I was called to Pune, I had given the choice of courses and then I chose electronics.We have eight semesters in total, and each semester has two sets of evaluations, internal and external. The internal evaluation includes small class tests, assignments, unit tests, projects, and quizzes. The external evaluation has practice exams and final exams. We have five to six subjects per semester. The curriculum is designed in such a way that we get to learn the subject and its an application in real life by means of projects, industrial visits.

For CSE, this year all the students were placed and the average salary was 4.5 lakhs/annum. Our placement cell works very hard for the placement of the college and keeps regular updates with the HRs of the other companies. I being a member of the placement cell for 2 months have witnessed this and our placements are witnessing immense growth every year. We have top companies coming like IBM, Tech Mahindra, Google, Microsoft. Crowd works which are a foreign company offered a student a package of 31 lakhs/annum. We also witnessed companies like BookMyShow, Shopify, Gomo group, Vodafone and many more. Also, we have an entire semester reserved for an internship where we are supposed to go for a 6-month internship in the industry and apply and learn practically.Every year, we celebrate a lot of events. Our tech fest called Techela is held every year and has lots of technical and gaming events. Our college's Entrepreneurship club also conducts some events related to marketing, entrepreneurship, designing, creative thinking during Techela, and the entire fest experiences a lot of participation. Also, every year we have a cultural event called Reverb, where students get opportunities to show their talent. Not only students, but we also get participation from faculties with full enthusiasm. We have DJ Nights, local bands performing and tasty food stalls lined up for the evening. Other than this all festivals are celebrated with full enthusiasm and energy.

Well, By the first year that was not a core region of the engineering but from my experience I had a wonderful experience  with the studies here never ever in the 12th also because teachers are friendly and co-operative and entering into the second year I am in love with the electronics because of the tech event where you will get to know how to have work on Arduino, robots and many more things, like I did the project of music reactive Led and from the final year project event you will learn about more things like raspberry pie solar  Car and many more thingsAccording to the credits of the course number of hours and lectures happen. I got admission in IEM through WBJEE, my rank was 3904 and i got IT branch in IEM in 2nd round of counselling. So first of all Give wbjee and if you secure a decent rank( upto 5000 generally) then you can get into IEM. But if you want cse then try to keep your rank upto 2.0K, in case of ece around 4.5K will be safe. And for the other streams a rank around 4.5K will be more or less safe. Put your desired branch in IEM in the choice list of wbjee counselling portal.You have to pay 4 lakh in 4 years. Each semester you have to pay 50000. It's very much expensive for middle class and poor people. But i can say your 50000 will not go in vain, it's worth.Though fee structure is high, college provides opportunity to get loan from banks. Through different technical events anybody can earn money also as an award. Apart from all these things, there are so many scholarships available from government.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text: There are various types of scholarship that our students can avail. The main scholarship is MCM scholarship which means merit cum means scholarship. This scholarship is based on merit obtain by students. Also there are various other types of scholarship based on the category of students.The teacher are well educated and well qualified. But all teachers should be given a period of training on how to teach. No doubt that teachers are having a great knowledge. But to ensure that there knowledge is getting shared to us, there should be a training for them

The campus is fantastic. The atmosphere to study here is great. Hostel Life is great and you get many opportunities to interact with people not only from India but the international student also. Hostel, as well as the mess, is very nice. Once you get acquainted with the college life here then probability there are very fewer chances of homesickness.The course curriculum and examination structure are great. Facilities try their best to teach in a healthy atmosphere. One can gain knowledge theoretically as well as practically. One of Our seniors has been placed on Google. Every year many companies come for placement interviews and once and get a brilliant chance for their future.

As the college is deemed the curriculum is changed in every 2-3 years, you receive knowledge of new machines in the market and methodology along with it. The curriculum is groomed to provide industry-specific knowledge. Six months of company internship is mandatory during the internship semester so you can gain field knowledge. Industrial visits are organised every semester and there is an extra course like disaster management, entrepreneurship etc in curriculum too.I opt for this course because I have interests in this field. I chose this institute after learning their syllabus, the opportunity they provide to the student (research internships, chance to work on projects, social services, health care, extracurricular activities etc). We can also perceive a diploma in business management along with BTech. Admission depends on the percentage scored by the student and their interview.

The Facilities in our college are really talented, supportive, motivating and friendly. This college has its own way of updating students for their incredible growth. Placement here are good enough and top MNC's are recruiting skilled students. Entrepreneurship are really encouraged and college does its support to students to motivate their interest.I have opted BE civil engineering and i opted it by my own opinion and i our college we have 2 internalls and 2 assignment test and one lab internal and all internal and assignment marks was reduced to 50 and we have semester end exam for 100 and it is scaled out for 50 and then both marks are combined and grade was give as per marks.
