Fee structure is feasible as it is among top government colleges. Its generally as other institute of technology in India. But its fee is bit higher than other 30 national Institute of Technology. Generally it is around 90k per semester for open category. 55 to 48 k for obc students having family income (1 to 5 lakh). And 27. 5 k per sem for obc students having (less than 1 lakh (family income)) rest sc, st, and pwd have to pay only 27. 5 k per sem as per rules.The National Institute of Technology Tadepalligudem is the 31st institution among the chain of National Institutes of Technology. The most positive point is that it got Its permanent campus within 4 years. It is an Institute of National Importance, being an NIT. NIT Andhra Pradesh was established in Tadepalligudem, State of Andhra Pradesh and it is functioning from the academic year 2015???2016.

Placement for civil engineering is average. Approximately, 30 to 40 percent of students were placed, as my senior says. The companies like L&T, Bigrade, TCS, Reliance visited the campus. L&T took only 2 or 3 students from the whole civil engineering branch. Most of the students got internship either in private sector or government sector.Admission to the B. E / B. Tech courses is done through JEE mains or KCET. which is conducted by Karnataka Examination Authority (KEA). Joint Entrance Test (JEE) which is an all India common engineering entrance examination conducted for admission to various engineering colleges and courses all over the country.

First all the students have to apply for the college through online portal.There are 2streams to get admission. Through jee main and 12th Mark (70% Of seats are filled in this). Through 12th mark only (30% filled in this) Once you have applied they will realese rank list based on your mark for both stream,you will be called for counselling first for stream 1 then for stream 2.You can attend both if you are eligible and there are vacant seats left.There are separate tamilnadu, all India and trichy thajavur quotas are present. Once you have been called for counselling select the desire branch based on vacancy and give the fee and you can come back home happily.The curriculum is designed by sastra itself and it's very much updated.The syllabus keeps on updating.if you are interested in research then this will be one among the best in india.The labs are very well equipped and if you are idea is so great you will offered to do research in worldwide labs(one or two). There are 3 internal exam and a sem exam. The grade will decided from both the exams equally.Best of two intenal will be taken for 50 and the Sem for 50 so with this the grade will be decided. If you get less in an exam don't need to worry other 2 are there.

The college curriculum is set in a way that all the fields will be covered by the student before his graduation and will be in a position to get placements in any field he is interested in. Exam pattern is like we will have 3 exam and the cumulative of all the marks plus assignments will be considered to give the grade to students.The faculty in our college are very talented and experienced in their field of teaching. They are very good with students that is they are very supporting and they fell responsible for the student. If the students behave good with them then the students can have a very good college life.

college_name: IIT Mandi- Indian Institute of Technology - [IITM]
review_text: The fee for each sem is around 1, 35, 000 including the tution fee, mess fee, hostel fee, medical fee, transportation fee, etc. I don't think it's that expensive. Well the figure I told was for general boys whose family income is more than 5 lacs per annum below that we have to pay one-third of it and if it is below 1 lacs per annum we have to only pay hostel and mess fees. For OBCs, SCs and STs and all the girls the tution fee is 0.Placement and job opportunities are really good here. Companies like Microsoft, Adobe, Amazon, TCS, Goldman etc. come every year to provide jobs. The average package of a student graduated from IIT Mandi is around 15 lacs per annum. Yes, my college provides intership opportunities in the field of research, data science, machine learning and many more.

In this I am taken computer science engineering I think computer science courses has good future then exam structure in our college is very good because they conducted test in every month I think that will make the good preparation.The fee structure in our college is very low and quit less expensive because I come to this college form consoling so it refuses the fee college give time to fee and they provide a instalment system in our college.

The rate of placement is good in my college. But sorry to say, all the opportunities are solely based on your knowledge based on CSE. Only IT companies approval are available. Talking about internship, I'm now in my 4th semester & I've not any idea about what internship is & how to approach it.Sorry to say, I've been rejected to get any kind of scholarship, as according to the college administrative body I'm belong to TFW scheme. But nowhere it was mentioned that a TFW student will not get any scholarship. TFW is solely based on WBJEE rank & family income.

I strongly believe that the curriculum of the course here in jadavpur university is too good, the teachers are awesome and the course outcome always makes sure you learn something better than nothing. The exam structure is something like, for every subject you have to give 2 class tests and then a semester, and two semesters a year. The thing is like the examination hall has restrictions but not too much like calcutta university. Though Some of the departments have strict rules but they are not many.This is the best part, as far as i know, jadavpur university has one of the best alumnus network in west bengal. My university made stars like rituparna ghosh, Parambrata, rupam islam, rwitobroto and many many. And you want to know about the alumni network? As far as I know, the fund for the fests does come from only two things 1. sponsors 2. alumni fund
College does not spend any penny during these fests. The seniors are very helpful but you have to build a strong connection with them first.

college_name: Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology - [SVNIT]
review_text: B. Tech is offered in specializations like Civil, Electrical, Chemical, Mechanical, Computer Science etc. for a course fee of INR 1,25,000 per annum. The fee structure of hostel and mess facility at Sardar Vallabhbhai National Institute of Technology varies between INR 33,867 to INR 49,460 per annum. The fee structure of M. Tech in Computer Science, Chemical, Mechanical, Electronics, etc. is INR 70,000 per annum. Remission of 2/3rd of the Tuition Fee for General & OBC students whose family income is between Rs. 1 lakh to Rs. 5 lakh per annum. Full Tution fees remission for those whose family income is less then 1 lakh.It is a scholarship for btech students 1st year. Candidate's family income must be below 2 Lacs per annum. Students are awarded with Siemens Scholarship throughout four years of BTech. Despite offering approval to postgraduate and undergraduate programs under specified categories, AICTE also offers a number of scholarships for students to ensure that financial constraint does not become a blocking stone in their professional carter.

The course curriculum has changed from Bachelor of Engineering to Bachelor of Technology that is the Choice Based Credit Sysytem is implied from the year 2018, my admission one. This curriculum is a much better version of engineering course curriculum. We have options to opt for subjects apart from the ones related to our branch. Also we have a relative marking system in which marking of students are on a scale level of the one who scores the highest in that particular branch. Exams take place in two semester. Each sem comprises of quiz1- midsem- quiz2- endsem. With a total marks of each subject being 100 and the credits may vary according to their importance.The fee structure is nearly ?1,25,000 per semester of 6 months. It includes mess fee, tution fee. And many other miscellaneous services as well. In a glance it might seem to be an expensive deal, but the college is not a government one but a deemed university so for obvious reasons the fee is a bit high. Also there is not much difference in fees when compared to an IIT. The infrastructure is Splendacious and hence fees is managed accordingly.

Most of our faculties are PhD holders some are even from the India's top colleges like iits and nits. In labs we are assisted by on going PhD scholars who are in the research team of senior staff. Some faculty even complain that they our college is not giving them enough time to do research and ask them to teach more.No. college never let us mingle with alumini. We were not given any guidelines about interviews. seniors are so helpful. They are like our friends. No ragging in the college premises. We do all nasty things with seniors. one of my senior guided me in applying for internships and some academic stuff.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text: Indian Institute of Technology are taking students on the basis of JEE Advanced examination. All the candidates who qualify JEE Mains exam are eligible for the JEE Advanced examination. I qualified the same with AIR-5431. When I appeared for the counselling, the best course available at my rank was Computer science and technology. So, I took admission in IIT Dhanbad.Yes, our college provides intern in the field of research. The placement season in all IITs starts in the end of December and continues till March. Many companies visit the campus for recruitment and they also provide internship to the 3rd year students. The internship program from college is generally in research field and they are paid a stipend of approx 25000.

Course structure are good but there are some boring subjects in 1st semester i. e law for engineers, history for engineers and evs for engineers. From the 2nd semester onwards, these type of boring are cancelled. But from 4th semester news analysis, behaviour science, library class are included. In Amity university patna, french class is also included.In our college, we celebrate amiphoria, guest lecture, sangathan and many more. Amiphoria is a cultural fest of amity university patna which is held on the month of April every year. In this fest, all the courses of Amity University Patna gathered and enjoy the DJ NIGHT and other performances.

One must give MHT-CET exam and then secure a good rank in merit list. Then he/she should apply for the DTE Mahrashtra CAP rounds in which you need to fill the form of certain details like 12th percentage etc. Then after that DTE declares date on which you need to fill the option form for choices of college as per preference. There are usually 3 DTE rounds with one spot round of DTE and if you wait further, the college may conduct it's own spot round. One needs to be patient during the process. Then you need to visit certain centres (guided by DTE on their website)to authenticate documents. Finally, you need to visit the allotted college and submit documents and pay the fees and get the admission receipt.Don't know about other branches, but for civil engineering, the schedule is hectic. You may feel tired at the end of day since the college time is 9Am-5Pm(you may get free slots in between). The college is not too inclined towards developing research minds in students. Exams are of moderate to high level and labs are something which you can't miss. The laboratory facilities are the worst here.

Every year we hold 2 different cultural fest one for technical event which is called technovit and the other one is a non technical fest which is vibrance. So like each sem we have atleast 1 big culturals and a minimum of 3 days off to participate in various event held inside the campus.90 percent of the things about the college is positive. The only downsides of the college is transport and the college always gives preference to hostellers in terms of facilities provided. They system is very flexible and very comfortable for everyone. Very much satisfied with vit.

Yes, there are lot of chances to get placed and get internships through placement drives Interns are offering by many mnc's like microsoft and google and amazon india and many more
And jobs offered by amazon, qualcomm, bosch, accenture, reliance, airtel and many more
Internship by college provided to only cse students in it's software departmentYeah when we have good there will be bad also
Very strict in indisciplinary things,
Holidays for festivals,
Strict security guards Everything is related with cost
Cost of living is somewhat high
90% Attendance is needed to get 5% of marks otherwise 75%must need for exam appearance with loosing attendance marks

DTU boasts of an amazing sports complex. The sports complex is equipped with all modern facilities. The sports complex houses a 400 metre running track, a cricket pitch a cricket ground, football ground, small hockey turf, an indoor sports complex with three badminton courts which are lined with synthetic mats, indoor complex for table tennis, an open place for Kabaddi, kho kho and volleyball, also 3 well maintained lawn tennis courts are there. There is also facility for bodybuilders in the form of a main gymnasium, there is also a smaller gymnasium on the other side of the campus, there also a couple of open the gymnasiums in the campus. Plus the campus of over 150 acres has running tracks all across. Student politics is mostly an underlying phenomenon with absolutely nothing at the forefront as such. The gender ratio is poor as is with all top engineering colleges across the nation. The culture on the campus is one of harmony and peace.So, like any other engineering aspirant, I prepared for the dreaded Joint Entrance Exam Mains and secured a rank of around 7,000 and qualified for the Joint Entrance Exam Advanced, which is primarily for the Indian Institutes of Technology, more commonly known as the IITs. I was successful is getting a seat at IIT-BHU, and ended up taking it through the JOSAA counselling. However, I had also got a seat in Software Engineering, Delhi Technological University through the JAC Delhi Counselling process. So, I had two options,
1. A not so popular branch at IIT-BHU
2. Dept of Computer Science at Delhi Technological University. I chose the later, and after having spent close to 1. 5 years, I'm more than sure that I made the right decision.

college_name: Dr BR Ambedkar National Institute of Technology - [NIT]
review_text: Course is very good for blooming engineering students. we have well set up labs in every department. Research sector is also very good in this college. but curriculum is bit outdated. We generally have one half of day for theory and other half for labs. Its hectic but its necessary. students in college got very good placements. This college also have a strong alumni network, which helps a lot in future.Gender ratio is very poor here. its about 1:6. College organise at leat ine workshop a week. Extracurricular activities are really appreciated in the college we have 12 clubs for different intrest. And all kind of playground for any popular sports and games. There is no politics in college nor any student union which is good but also bad. College have a healthy environment.

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:
Mainly 3 fests are held in our college
1. Spardha-It is the largest sports fest of the country where teams from various colleges come and compete. and win prizes worth lakhs. 2. Kashiyatra -It is the socio-annual cultural fest Many big celebrities and live performance take place. Technex-It is the technical fest of the country,lakhs of funds come in this fest various gaming competitions take placeAs we all know IIT's are among the best research institutions in the whole country,so research is our topmost priority. Course curriculum foccuses on all round development of the students. Days are a little hectic but if u have intest in studies than it's easy. Labs are outstanding with all kind of facilities.

Our university need to cope with the technology. Though it is a very reputed college it needs to adapt to the changes. Though pursuing a course in such an esteemed college has its perk but even then the lack of extracurricular activities makes us regret sometimes our decision of joining this college.Extracurricular is not prevalent in our salt campus. There is nothing in the sense of extracurricular. We dont even have a proper basketball court. We just have a ground called CAB which everone boasts of but the truth is there is nothing for extracurricular.

LPU holds ONE WORLD where students from each country showcase their culture and traditions. It holds ONE INDIA where culture and traditions of all the states of India are shown. Except this there is always something going on in university either in technical field or calling big personalities to interact and share their experience with students.Alumni network is also excellent and alumni visits every year to interact with juniors. Seniors are always helpful in clearing doubts about anything related to university principles or academics. There is completely 0 ragging case in university and a student friendly environment.

Based on ipe marks and iit mains a merit list is filed after application. We have to apply online and after that we get a rank based on the list and then for counselling we have to go to the college and select the required stream as per the number of seatsThe fee structure is very feasible compared to the other colleges, it's around Rs150000/- and comfortable even for middle class families. Many people chose this college for its low fee structure compared to other colleges and for the security of girls.

gender ratio is 60 males and 40 females per hundred individuals,their in the campus the parties also held their is not any gender discrimination as all are equal the environment for girls is best without any issue they can learn anything. and their is no politics in the college as it always go beyond their deciplinethe faculty is really sooo soo excellent as all the teachers are very knowlegeful, kind and helpful they always try their best to make the student clear about their doubt and help the students in very difficult situation anyway the ai couse is strongly teached by our respected teachers.

college_name: IIT Guwahati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITG]
review_text: Admission was on the basis of one of the world's toughest entrance test called JEE. It took place in two stages. Mains and advanced. Advanced is a really tough exam. Over 1500000 students take Mains every year but only around 10000 are able to make it to an IIT or any other good College. I chose this course because this was the only course I could get at my rank in top 7 IITs in India. IITs have a great exposure and competitive environment.I think course is not something that teaches us everything. Most of the things are taught by co curricular activities. Being a part of club, fests teaches you to manage time. 2 exam take place for each course called Mid sem and End sem. apart from these quizes, assignments and attendance also carry marks sometime.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: Every laboratory is fully air-conditioned and have State of the art new equipment equipped where you can do many experiments. The assistance are also provided by the lab assistant inside the laboratory. And also big two grounds are available in our campus.It's very flexible. The syllabus of the course is not too vast all you need to study regularly. Electronics is something if you want to do something you should have a deep knowledge of the subject. I think that structure is available here

The main fest of our college is Corona and melange which is held in between January and February every year in this fest about 100 event happen in which some of them are technical and some are non technical in the fest all of enjoyed so much and in the fest some big celebrity came on band night or edm night and in the fest time college looks like a fairy place and workshop is also held like after 15 days there is a workshop in our college.Extracurricular activities is compulsory course in every semester for the personal development of the students. Sports is also happen from college level to national level institutes wise. Gender ratio is not good at this college it is about 100:12 means for 100 boys there is only 12 girls is present but now a days it will improve. So many cultural society is present in the college like dance club,drama club,music club and etc

TECHNOZION, SPRINGSPREE, YOUTH FEST, AYODHYAN are the major fests and many many small events regularly.Technozion is south india's second largest fest.Spring spree is cultural fest.Youth fest is for motivation and ethics.Ayodhyan is related to sports.Many other regular events will be organized by different clubs of college.One of the top institutions of india in engineering. Very good college because people from different parts of india gather at one place. Not only academics it also provides alround development of the students. Placements and packages are also pretty good. We leave the college with the best memories in life.

I got in VIT by appearing in the VITEEE entrance exams conducted by VIT. The exam was held in January 2018 and it consisted of four subjects, namely, English, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology. After the exam was conducted, the results came out by the beginning of May 2018 and according to the rank I was called in for admissions at the Chennai campus wherein according to the ranks, we had different courses available for us to choose one from the extensive list.The course curriculum for me follows that of B.tech Biotechnology and it comprises of various subjects which will further help us in the required fields of expertise. The exam structure comprises two CAT exams and one FAT exam per semester and it also includes three Internals which either includes quizzes or digital assignments.

college_name: Indian Institute of Engineering Science and Technology - [IIEST] Shibpur
review_text: I qualified the Joint Entrance Examination ( Main) with a rank.By the end of June counselling process start which last upto 3rd week of July.Through counselling i was alloted a seat in department of Civil Engineering.After few reported physically and submit and verify all the documents and then institute accepted it and alloted a hostel.Finally i got admission.Placement and job scenario in Computer Science Department, Information Technology, Electrical Engineering, Electronics an Telecommunication Engineering, Mining Engineering is more than 80%.And in all othet departments its swinging around 60%.Yes, institute provides interships.Overall experience is average.

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP]
review_text: Here the course curriculum is very good.Exams are conducted in the way such that we have two quizzes and one final end semester.1st quiz will be on one month after the sem get started and after 45 days quiz will be conducted and after another 45 days final end sem will be conducted.you need not to bother more about exams even if you read a night before the exam you can pass.And labs will also be conducted in a good and fair manner.It is based on annual income of the parents(separately).If yours parent income is below one lakh then you should pay only 9k to 10k for institute fees and if income is between 1 to 5 lakhs then an extra amount of 30k should be paid.If income is greater than 5lakhs then beisde that 9k they should pay 1lakh.And hostel fees will be 34k which is equal to all.All this is for each semester.

There are so many activities option in this like sports labs social society and many option are available in sports there is national cricket stadium and international hockey stadium and also available football stadium and moreIn this course you have many options of engineering in this College all branch of engineering is available in this College you can choose your best in your basis of entrence exam result and and core branch is also available.

college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]
review_text:
There is three main event every year

The total fees of 8 semesters is approximately 10 Lakhs.The fee of the semsters in Birla Institute of Technology Mesra varies from 1.5 lakhs in semesters 7 to 1.2 lakhs in semester 2.Overall, the facilities and placement opportunities provided by the college compensate the fees.Apart from this we have to pay mess and hostel fee as well which is 15000 ruppees.Birla Institute of Technology Mesra is amongst the most reputed college of India and pursuing your graduation from here is a matter of pride, nd the 4 years one spends here will surely make a huge impact on your life and of you are sincere and honest enough, you will surely get a good job and be ready to face challenges ahead.

college_name: IIT Hyderabad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITH]
review_text: The techno cultiral fest of the colleage os called elan and and vision with elan being the cultural fest and nvision the technical one.its a 3 day fest with a lot of competions and fun and celebrities coming in and out of the campus.We have a lot of club which compete in kost of the competitions around india and has secured prizes.We have a rich alumni network with nearly 8 baches of students passing out and most of them workinh or running premier companies and MNCs.Their is an alumni cell especially formed to deal with the alumni affairs and their intraction with the students for oppurtunities in their respective fields or companies.

college_name: Vel Tech Rangarajan Dr. Sagunthala R and D Institute of Science and Technology - [Vel Tech]
review_text: Ordinary free structure in our college is about 2.5 lacs there will be a scholarship for the students from here the college management if we get above 90 percentage in MPC subjects then we will get 75% scholarship and if you right face 1 exam you will get extra 25000 scholarship and if you write phase 2 exam then you will get 20,000 scholarship extraYeah The main advantage in our college when compared other colleges have a scholarship facility provided by the college management as before I already said.If you get above 90 percentage in maths physics chemistry subjects in inter MPC then you will get 70% scholarship if we get above 80% then you get 50% scholarship

Gravitas,Rivervale,VPL,etc. In gravitas, various types of technical and fun full events are conducted in the college to increase the knowledge for the students in many aspects and In Rivera In this event, we to create a 4-day show with the biggest stars of our nation and help students to release theirs for few days.The VIT University is place where we learn all aspects of life in many ways such as education, activities, careers etc. As well as seniors also are very helpful by guideing us through their own experience that thry have undergone and this place is wonderful in all aspects.

As far as Sports is concerned it is not the quality is not high, The facilities are not of the top quality.
Labs and research works are too good in the university and the guidance by the staffs will be also of very high quality.
Cultural teams are very good here, they perform well and it is a organized structure here.The admission of seats splits into two, one- based on the board marks which accounts to 25% of the seats and the second one is based on the combined score of boards and JEE mains, which accounts to the 75% of the seats, students will be ranked based on the stream and seats are allotted in the counselling

college_name: IIT Kanpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITK]
review_text: The alumni network is great. They are very helpful. You can easily contact them using messenger or ping them on linkedin. They will be able to provide you guidance based on their experiences. College seniors also provide valuable guidance and most of them are willing to help you as much as they can.There are mainly three festivals. The technical fest is Techkriti, sports fest is Udghosh and the cultural fest is Antaragni. Apart from these three sessions on entrepreneurship is held by E-cell. Various different events are organized by different student bodies based on your interest

Freshers happen ever year to give welcome party to students who came new to College,fests take place,dj nights are best of them all,nascent programs are there,fashion club is also there,you can expect to have much awaited fun in College fest when celebrities come and entertain us.Placement and job opportunities are really impressive for computer science,mechanical,information technology students,we get enough campus placements every year,can say anything about stipend though.you can expect high grade companies to come in College too

Club general secretaries and so many other benefits where students can learn to rule

Course gives a good in depth knowledge of the subjects and also helps you to prepare for GATE examinations. college_name: Indian Institute of Information Technology Sri City - [IIIT]
review_text: Our college have some pannel to provide students internships and jobs but most of the students here are very intelligent and hard working that they dont require it by the way.Last years highest package was 44 lakhs and four of our senior s got placed in microsoft 2 in facebook and 1 in IBM and furthermore.To get admission in this college one should have cleared 12th class and secured atleast 75 percentage of marks and also cleared JEE mains exam with about 20 thousand ranking or better.Only CSE and ECE is available in this college so you should be willing to take one of these to bein this college.

college_name: IIT Patna - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITP]
review_text: The extracurricular activities are not that great here.They provide with very little of that but the quality is good.Gender Ratio is around 23 Girls every 100 Boys in total(currently).It varies for different courses.We are provided with different options for workshops, Cultural festival, sports to choose from.Some are for boys only or Girls only and some are available to everyone.There are people from different part of India, so definitely students of many ethnicity, race, religion are present here which can bring difficulties at first but once you get familiar here it provides a lot of scope to learn about different Cultures and parts of India which is quite interesting.The placemnet and Job opportunities are great here.Every year many students get thier preferred job directly from the college visit of companies.Companies like Google, Infosys, Microsoft, Citigroup, Eaton Ltd, ITC, John Deere, Microsoft, Morgan Stanley, MU Sigma, Nvidia Corporation, etc.The stipend vary from companies to companies for different course.It ranges from 5-6 lalh rupees yearly to 50 lakh rupees yearly.Sadly, there is no internship program provided by college which can be a little sad for those students who do not get job directly and have to find job by themselves.But there are many few of them and I guess they are responsible for ot themselves.

The faculty is ok.
Some faculties have no idea about the subject and just dictate everything they read from the textbook itself.Some faculties are really good and have a lot of experience.They make sure that each and every student gets the concept and then proceed.
In general faculties just do their work and leave the rest on students.Many teachers are just MTech graduates and doesn't have an actual teaching degree.These teachers are only 3-4 years older than the students.They are highly unqualified.
Some teachers are really good and have deep knowledge of subject.These are well qualified.In college all the marks depends upon the faculty.The total marks out of 100 are divided into two exams ( Mid semester and End semester ), lab practicals ( not compulsory for every subject ) and internal marks ( based on projects and assignments ).These are assessed by the subject teacher itself.The research opportunities are ok.Interested students often contact the professors of their respective departments and work as research intern under them.The labs are chilled for some courses but are really hectic for others.

Yes the main college event that is KIIT FEST have more than 4000 events to participate for both cultural and technical.Except that celebration of ganesh puja,saraswati puja, diwali is done.And sports events also happens individual branch wise.Also republic day is celebrate with huge enthusiasm with all branch doing a parade and culture event.About this I would say no other college have a better facilities than us.we have student activity center in almost each campus with basketball, football, ten is court, gym, swimming pool and all other activity are their.We have a big campus with cricket stadium and Kit student activity center where cultural practice goes on.

There are both negative and positive remarks for this college. The positive aspect is the placements and internships are very good and the negative aspect is the syllabus is outdated. The second thing is there is no brand outlet for food. Local canteen which are running by their shoo owners holds the place.The college curriculum is outdated. The college follows a very comprehensive study course. The syllabus for first semester is same for all the respective branches. college_name: Dhirubhai Ambani Institute of Information and Communication Technology - [DA-IICT]

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text:

college_name: IIT Tirupati - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITTP]

The fees are a bit expensive, but the facilities are also pretty good. The fees are expensive because it also gets used for the funding of underprivileged tribal students who are taught at KISS. They study, eat, and wear everything free of cost, which is a very good initiative, but it's the reason why our fees are a bit high compared to other private colleges in the same league.There are labs for each and every department and in multiple numbers with the latest of equipment and technology. Our University also has great activity and enthusiasm in various sports like cricket, football, basketball, hockey, etc and has several courts, grounds, gyms, swimming pools, etc.

The ffcs system is used in this university where a student can study their interesting courses. EXAM STRUCTURE-- there are 3 exams per semester. The first two are 50 marks exam and last is 100 marks exam. where the second exam called CAT 2 is an opened notebook exam. students can bring their notes to the exam. There are 60 percent theory weightage and 40 percent lab weightage.I have given an online-based entrance exam called viteee, the rank list was then declared and I was selected so I had attended counseling and confirmed my seat. At the time of counseling, they will call students which course they are interested in and provided that course according to their rank if there are any seats available.

There are two cycles for 1st year:- Physics cycle and chemistry cycle There are two labs for the physics cycle that is physics lab and mechanical workshop lab and for the chemistry cycle, there are 3 labs that are chemistry lab, computer programming lab, and AutoCAD lab. For sports, there are basketball court one football/cricket ground, volleyball ground, lawn tennis ground, squash, table tennis, gymnasium, etc.There are 10-12 branches that are taught in this college which i am talking only for engineering and there are other streams also such as pharmacy, dental science, B-com, BBA,MTECH,MBA. The exam structure is like that there will be 3 internal exams in one semester which carries of 50% weightage and which will be conducted each month and at last there will be external exams which carries of 50% weightage.

First you have to give entrance exam which is free, if you clear it you are called for counseling which is held in june-july and at the time of counseling you have to pay the admission fee for seat booking. And once counseling is over you can apply for course upgradation if eligible. And after that Academic fee along with Hostel and Mess charges has to be paid ,if taken. And once you have paid the fees you are officially a KIITian.Extracurricular activities are organised throughout the year some by the college and some by the students societies. Laboratories are well equipped and there are large number laboratories meeting the need of the students. College has more than one cricket and football stadium. And almost every sports equipment is available. Including gym and swimming pool and tennis courts also.

I am a lateral entry student. i got merit seat. my college fees is Rs. 17000. Here there is another kind fees structure also existing that self finance which is offered for students who joined via counseling. Their fees structure is upto Rs.60000. Comparing to other colleges the fees structure is very feasible and worth.Placement and job opportunities are satisfactory. But mainly most of the students got placed only in IT companies. Now there is very small job opportunities for civil engineering and Mechanical engineering they mostly got selected in software companies. Core companies will also hire some number of students

Every year many events will be conducted in VIT. And 2 events particularly get the attention of every student. One is a technical fest and the other one is a cultural fest. And when it comes to Riviera many cultural events and pro shows are conducted and the fest also invites the presence of many celebrities.Actually speaking the fee structure in VIT is expensive compared to the NIT's and IIT's Unless you get a very high rank in the VITEEE exam you'll have the burden of paying a huge fee for the course. And also the intermediate state board topper of each state will be exempted with 75 percent of the fee. Except for these VIT is very expensive

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text:
The course curriculum is very decent and covers the basics of every branch in 1st year of the college and then from 2nd year the course consists of the subject teaching how are different branches work together for development.
The exam structure is also optimised as the college have evaluations on the basis of quiz, class tests, viva, semester exams and also through different types of assignments.The packages of tge placements vary from branch to branch. The best packages are from CS branch with a highest of 33 l.p.a in 2016 and in year 2018 also the average was around 11 l.p.a. The examination pattern in our college is very well planned. We have three internal exams in which the best of two results are selected. After qualifying for the internal examination we are then eligible for semester exams. The two internals have 50% weightage and the semester exam is the other 50%. Combining both the internals and semester results our CGPA is calculated.Our college has various events celebrated throughout the year. The main event of our college is called as UTSAV. It's a week long event. Our college also has a big technical event called phaseshift in which many technical companies do come to showcase the new technologies and every department also puts up the work they have done so far.

It encourages students to join societies and take part in various projects, programs.in each campus there is a sports complex which includes gym,table tennis,lawn tennis, badminton, volleyball, basketball,chess etc.

there is a huge alumni network for senior students in a university alumni are connected to the university through a portal called alumini portal where the students from the 3rd year and 4th year are registered before their placements this will help them communicate with university for various purposes including if they need any help with their documents or if they want to come and visit their university. other than that students from the first to third year are also works as an alumni coordinator.Extracurricular activities are arranged by the division of student welfare. Various sports competitions are arrange every month of the year including racing events and basketball competitions. The conditions of lab also good.

college_name: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology - [Thapar University]
review_text: Admission in Thapar college is on the basis of the JEE main entrance exam. Then a list was prepared by the Thapar University on the basis of the rank we have in our JEE examination. Only those students are applicable who has filled the form for the Thapar University before the results.In Thapar University every year a lot of companies come for the recruitments of the students in the placement cell. And Thapar starts providing internship from third year onwards. The average is also good than other institute's

It's a expansive but for the money they balance the feds he have a smart class room with wifi facility and We have fresher's, hackathon, culture fest, tech fest, traditional day,class mb and more events in our college All the students are participate and also other colleges also be participate in the events.We have positive and negative is that all are some good and not sure.we have a good All are well done in PhD on there courses they are researchers and be friendly to all students. We have 100% PhD and all are be an equal are greater than iit(Indian institute of technology) Faculty.

If you have more than 80% in your 12th you can get 25% scholarship.
If you have more than 88% you can get 50% scholarship. And if you have more than 92% you can get 100% scholarship by the college which would be continuing if you are in top 20 percentile,10 percentile and 5 percentile respectively.They provide internship to only 3rd year students, if other's are interested they can go for internship and College will appreciate that but they are not going to provide any support in finding a company for internship. Placements are good but not best. Average package is nearly 3 to 3.5 LPA.

We had to write an entrance exam called kcet, our rank should be below 1500 to get the top branch in this college, the procedures were like in every other college. We had to do verification through kcet first and then on a particular day off allotment we should come to college and get our verification done. On the same day we should pay our hostel and college fees and the admission will be done.There is mainly 1 fest every semester. odd sem gets one and even sem gets one. Apart from these there are many small fests and events held by clubs in the college. There IEEE organises a fest. other clubs organise events that have different games which are both physical games and mental games. There are several other competitions held.

Department fests for each department occurs frequently. CARPEDIUM(Cultural), UTSAV(Technical), FEURA(Sports and Games) are some of the intra college events.There is DAKSH - Inter college Technical fest, one of the Top 5 in South India, There is COLOSSEUM - Inter college Sports and Games fest, Last but not the least KURUKSASTRA - an enigmatic inter college cultural fest !Our college has a curriculum on par to that of IIT syllabus. Three Cumulative Internal Assessment (CIAs) each of 50 marks is conducted for each semester and the best 2 out of the 3 CIAs are considered for Internals 50 marks along with 10 marks for assignment. Remaining 50 marks is from Final Semester exam (100 converted to 50)

If it comes positive it's college is good. It has a bakery , icecream parlor and Maggie center inside a campus. and teaching is also good. placement is also good if you compare other colleges
Negative thing is that they don't push you to compete some hackathons or they don't make you to participate.During odd sem there is a fest called inception , celebrities come and give stage performance and also there will be ethnic day during inception. This is also coolest thing. and during even sem there is a event called anadyanta. this is a big fest in which great great singer comes and performs

college_name: IIT BHU - Indian Institute of Technology
review_text: We have 5 Councils- Games and Sports Council, Science and Technology Council, Cultural Council, Social Welfare Council, Film and Media council. Each council has its own college festa. The KASHI YATRA is one of the oldest fests of IIT BHU. It is organized by the Cultural Council and is a Cultural Fest of the college. Events like singing, dancing, debates take up in this event with prize money of over 3 lakhs. It happens between 15-20 January every year and is a 3 day event. Next fest is Technex- the technical fest of the college- organized by Science and Technology Council. It is a 3 day event taking place in Mid February every year having a prize over 3 lakhs with events and competitions related to Aero Modelling, Robotics, Coding, Startups, Astronomical and Core Engineering Events. Then comes Spardha- the sports fest happening in the end of October. It is 3 day event and the Asia's Largest Sports Festival. Film and Media council has an event called FMC WEEKEND- a 3 day event with competitions like Film Making, Photographies and many others. Each council depends on other councils and helps each other in their respective events.The course structure is well planned and curriculum is also well maintained such that the students get a good time for extra-curricular acrivities and also for studies. We have a credit and CGPA system as in US and other top foreign universities. We have each course of a pparticular credit. Lab courses are usually of 13 credits. The credits are divided as follows-3-1-2, 3 is the number of credit for each class. Each week we have 3 classes so we have 9 credits, 1 credit for tutorial and 1 for self study and 2 for lab. So in this way we have 13 credits of lab courses. We have mid semester and end semester examination. Mid semester examinations carry 30% weightage and end semwstwr examination have 60% weightage. The rest 10% is of Class Assignment/Attendance/Class Tests and Quizzes. Grading system is as follows- A* hrade for outstanding performance and A for excellent. Both have 10 pointer. A- has 9, B has 8, B- has 7, C has 6, C- has 5 and after that it is F or Fail grade. So CGPA is calculated as- Sum of grade??credits of each subject divided by total number of credits.

The job opportunities are not that great however in this scenario of joblessness you would be lucky to land with a substandard job.Many software companies visit here and even tech giants like IBM come here so it provides offcampus for other colleges of West Bengal as well.So you may know that getting a job is a surety but the quality is not too good.You may manage somewhere an average package of 4 lacs per annum.But the college does not provide any internships.You have to get an internship from outside and the college may give approval in this regard.No I don't know of any scholarship as provided by the college however students can avail scholarships from their state governments.Students from different states can easily manage to cover a major portion of their fees as the state government gives enough scholarship and the fees is low enough.

college_name: ISM Dhanbad - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITISM]
review_text: As we are talking about job oppotunities, being an IIT & one of tje old college of India this college provide many job opportunities & students also get high annual package at placements.Insternship oppotunities are also good as it also provide you with abroad internship oppotunities.Course curriculum is decent, a normal student would easily get used to it.Exams takes places 2 times in a semester, one is mid-semester exam which consists of 30-32% of total GPA of that semester & other is End-semester exam which consists of 48-50% of total GPA of that semester.

In fact, VIT is ranked no. 1 private institution in India. With directional efforts, you can easily get a super dream job offer with more than 10 lakh per annum. review_text:

college_name: MNNIT Allahabad - Motilal Nehru National Institute of Technology - [MNNIT]
review_text:
1. Curriculum in our institute is a bit outdated and needs to be updated very quickly. Practices like lab file record and tutorial assignments are still prevailing here.
2. This kind of situation is there in almost every NIT system institute and graduates from these instituitions lack up to date knowledge of industrial needs.
3. There is semester system in our institute with each semester lasting about 5-6 months and two major examinations held in each semester.
Mid-Semester Exams - 20 Marks End-Semester Exams - 60 Marks
Teacher's Evaluation - 20 Marks
That makes total of 100 marks.
No. Of subjects in each semester is usually 9-10.
About 6 theory subjects and 3-4 practical subjects depending upon your branch.1. Science Stream students like me who had Mathematics as their 3rd major subject along with Physics and Chemistry, give the National Engineering Entrance Exam known as Joint Entrance Examination or JEE.
2. JEE comprises of two exams ie JEE Main and JEE Advanced. On the basis of JEE Main scores ranks are given to students and on the basis of these ranks students can take admissions in NITs, IIITs, GFTIs , etc. Similarly on the basis of ranks in JEE Advanced students can take admissions in IITs.
3. I gave JEE in 2018 and got decent ranks in both JEE Mains as well Advanced, but rank in JEE Advanced was not enough to get my preferred branch in IITs so I went with MNNIT Allahabad which is best NIT in North India.

There are numerous event held in our campus some of them are spectra ,one india etc where different types of cultural shows are held apart from that there are many sports event .Last year it also host All india science Congress meet ,which is one of the biggest event.Feasible as compared to other universities and it provide scholarship also on the basis of marks secured in the entrance exam,it also provide scholarship on the basis of class 12 marks and JEE mains and advance marks on the basis of different criteria.

college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text: We have one of the best opportunities in India in terms of Placement and Internship. We have 150+ companies visiting campus during Internship and 300+ companies during placement. International companies like Amazon Robotics pay as huge as 8000$ monthly stipend as well. Also Microsoft Redmond, WA pay similarly.We have numerous scholarships as mentioned earlier. Some which I can recall are MCM Scholarship which pays ? 70k annually to students who maintain a certain GPA and are not financially well-off. Then there is Earn-Learn-Return which offers ? 1lac annually to toppers of JEE Advanced. There is also another scholarship which is offered to toppers of every department. Moreover the SBI Bank Kharagpur branch offer educational loan at no interest or reduced rate.

Each semester has 2 exams

In end sem, there is only one subject each day with 3:00 hours duration per subjectI got admission in this college through a counselling process. college_name: IIT Kharagpur - Indian Institute of Technology - [IITKGP]
review_text: Course curriculum mostly consist of theoretical part. That's one big issue. To clear JEE advanced many students just mug up the things. They are just worried about marks not about learning things. Course curriculum is right but the exam pattern should be changed. Exam paper contains multiple choice questions that can be single correct or multi correct. There are paragraphs also. There are some integer based questions and some Match the column questions are also there. Exam pattern is not same for every exam. It keep changing every year. There are two paper of JEE advanced, paper 1 and paper 2, there is a 2 hour gap between both the exams. For clearing exam you have to appear in both the paper.The two major events celebrated here are Spring fest(Cultural fest), Kshitiz(tech management fest). Kshitiz is known to Asia largest techno management fest. So many workshops are conducted during Kshitiz which help in building profiles in tech. So many competetion like robo war etc. It's a pool for tech. In spring fest so many bollywood stars come here, perform here, enjoy the culture of IIT Kgp. So many stand up comedies happen here. There are also competetion related to dancing, dramatics etc. From all over India people visit IIT Kharagpur to have fun in Spring fest.

All in all, I love my college. It has everything I would need to go forward in my life. The seniors guide us well for us to know what to do next. The infrastructure provided by the college is always there to help us with our studies. The only issue I would say is the location of our college. Since it's in a town in Assam, few companies never come here for recruitment, if they would've then NIT Silchar would've been one of the most highest package placement college in India.When it comes to festivals like Diwali, Holi etc. All are celebrated with excitement. Our college has 2 main events: Tecnoesis, our tech fest and Incandesence, our cultural fest. These 2 are some of the biggest fests in North East India. All the students help with the preparations, participate and enjoy it wholeheartedly. We also have another cultural fest called Posua which is for the Assamese people in the institution where they can enjoy their own Bihu.
